Purchase of T Co 421,000 收购子公司带进来的子公司 PPE 的价值
Cash purchase(Balancing figure) 697,660 CFI - Cash purchased
PPE @x5.12.31 3,668,900 期末合并资产负债表信息
a(ii) 上述报表调整项的文字论述
The consolidated statement of cast flows categorises inflows and outflows of cash
and cash equivalents from the group's perspective between operating, investing, and
financing activities.
指出合并现金流量表是站在集团角度将现金流入流出按照经营性、投资性、融资
性活动进行分类。
The acquisition of Trudos Co should be included as an outflow in investing activities.
It is important that the effects of non-cash transactions are eliminated.
指出现金收购子公司的花费需要作为现金流出的部分计入投资性活动现金流；同
时任何非现金的部分需要在经营性活动现金流中进行调整，还原非现金费用、收
入对于利润的影响。
The consideration paid as a share for share exchange must not be included since it is
not a cash flow. Since the assets and liabilities of Trudos Co will be consolidated from
30 June 20X5, the cash balance existing on acquisition is effectively a cash addition to
the group. The cash paid to acquire Trudos Co is therefore shown net of the cash and
cash equivalents acquired on acquisition. Significant non-cash transactions are
otherwise disclosed in a note to the financial statements.
结合案例进行分析，以股换股的部分不涉及现金流因此不被包含。同时由于
x5.6.30 已经收购，子公司单家报表上的现金流金额也会被合并加总进来，因此
体现在合并现金流量表的现金收购金额需要将子公司的现金流信息剔除后的净
值列示，也就是答案中看到的$375,100.
It is also necessary to adjust the calculations for the other assets and liabilities
acquired when gaining control over Trudos Co, such as inventories, trade receivables
and trade payables. Since the net assets of Trudos Co will be consolidated, the effects
of the acquisition must be eliminated so that the movements on the assets and
liabilities reflect only cash flows incurred by the group during the year. The impact of
the acquisition of a subsidiary on the statement of cash flow is included in a note to
the financial statements.
指出由于收购了 80%的股份，获得了控制权，因此子公司的资产负债需要全额合
并进入合并资产负债表。但由于期初数字未包含子公司数据，因此当考虑集团当
年资产负债变动情况对于现金流影响的时候，需要将期末子公司的影响剔除之后
再进行计算。
Care must also be undertaken to identify any goodwill impairments arising in the year.
Cash generated from operations is calculated by adjusting the group's profits for
non-cash flows which have impacted upon operating profit. Impairments of goodwill
must be added back to the profit before taxation since they would reduce group
profits but without impacting on the group's cash flows.
商誉减值损失减少了集团利润，但并没有带来任何的现金流出，因此需要在经营
性活动现金流中将这部分的减值损失作为非现金的费用加回处理，还原费用对于
PBT 的影响。
The cash paid to acquire property, plant and equipment during the year must exclude
the effects of the acquisition of Trudos Co. The acquisition of Trudos Co will increase
the non-current assets by their fair value at acquisition, being the carrying amount of
$421,000 plus the fair value adjustment of $50,000 needed at the date of acquisition.
The initial recognition of the non current assets of Trudos Co is not a cash flow for
the kabelo Group.
针对采购 PPE 的现金支付，剔除掉子公司本身的 PPE 以及收购日当天公允价值调
整的影响，得到集团 cash purchase 的金额。
The taxation figure should be the cash paid by the group to the tax authorities during
the year. It is important therefore that the impact of deferred tax is adjusted for in
calculating the figure since movements in deferred tax are not a cash flow. The fair
value increase of $50,000 to the property, plant and equipment increases the
carrying amount of property, plant and equipment for consolidation purposes
without a corresponding change to the tax base of the assets. A deferred tax liability
arises on acquisition equal to $15,000 being 30% of the fair value adjustment. This
must be excluded from the consolidated statement of cash flows so that only the
cash transaction for tax paid is recognised.
记录在合并现金流量表里的 tax paid 是集团层面实际支付给税务局的税金，同时
计算 tax paid 需要结合 current tax payable 以及 deferred tax liability 的期初期末变
动情况以及当期新增的税费(包含当期所得税费用以及递延所得税费用)倒推得出。
因此结合案例信息，收购日当天公允价值调增 50,000，账面价值增加税基不变，
因此根据税率 30%可以得出产生了 15,000 的 DTL，在计算 tax paid 的时候需要将
此影响考虑进去。
题干解析:
2. Financial instruments
The Kabelo Group has a number of financial instruments presented within its
consolidated statement of financial position.
Kabelo 集团有很多金融工具且已经列示在合并资产负债表上。
Some group entities which had surplus cash resources had acquired debentures in
other non-group entities to increase returns. Other group entities had raised finance
by issuing bonds.
集团内部分公司使用盈余现金购买集团其他公司的债券以增加投资回报。其他的
一些公司也通过发行债券而进行融资。
Kabelo Co financed the acquisition of Trudos Co by acquiring a bank loan. Kabelo Co
also had an overdrawn bank balance as at 31 December 20X5. The overdrawn bank
balance fluctuates regularly from an in-funds balance to an overdrawn balance.
Kabelo 公司通过申请银行贷款辅助完成 Trudos 公司的收购。Kabelo 公司在年底
x5.12.31 存在银行透支。银行余额有规律地波动。
Requirements 解析:
(b) Using exhibit 2, advise the financial controller as to how the various financial
instruments, including the overdraft, should be presented in the consolidated
statement of cash flows. (6 marks)
使用 exhibit 2 案例信息，针对不同的金融工具包括银行透支改如何在合并现金流
量表中列示，给财务负责人提出建议。
答案解析:
(b)金融工具列报
Investing activities include expenditures intended to generate future income and
cash flows. Consequently, the acquisition of the debentures should be presented
within investing activities as they were acquired to increase returns.
投资性活动主要包含购买非流动资产或者投资资产，其目的是为了未来能够产生
相应的收益以及现金流，比如通过非流动资产的使用源源不断地创造收益。因此
购买债券属于买金融资产的范畴，属于投资性活动。
Financing activities are activities which result in changes in the size and composition
of the contributed equity and borrowings of the entity. Issues of bonds are similar in
nature to bank borrowings since they typically are used to finance the long-term
activities of the entity.
融资性活动主要来源于公司的融资途径，包含股权融资以及债券融资，而这些行
为都会影响公司的融资结构。因此发行债券类似于银行贷款，也是向债权人借钱，
且目的也是为了公司长期的经营活动。
IAS 7 Statement of Cash flows states that bank borrowings are generally considered
to be financing activities. The acquisition of Trudos Co was financed by a bank loan. It
would therefore seem appropriate that both the proceeds of the loan and from the
issue of the bonds should be included as a financing activity.
结合上段内容，向银行贷款则也属于融资性活动。
The bank overdraft should, however, be classified as cash and cash equivalents.
Where the bank overdraft is repayable on demand and forms an integral part of the
entity's cash management, then it should be included as a component of cash and
cash equivalents.
Bank overdraft 通常是以流动负债列示的，因此其一般作为现金及现金等价物的
一部分。
Since the bank balance fluctuates between an in-funds balance and an overdraft on a
regular basis, it would seem to be integral to the cash management of the business.
Should the overdraft be more of a permanent nature, then it would be correctly
presented within financing activities.
但案例信息有提到公司的现金正向余额以及银行透支额是有规律的波动着的，因
此如果银行透支的目的是为了长期不断地支持企业的资金需求，则列入融资性活
动也是合理的。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:2023 SD 真题解析.pdf#1

stem_zh:
Q2
题干
explanation_zh:
1. Ethical considerations
The finance director (FD) of Dario Co is not a permanent employee but is paid on a
consultancy basis. He is currently on a six-month contract. The chief executive officer
(CEO) is a personal friend of the FD and she recommended him to the board of Dario
Co. The FD and the CEO are both ACCA members. FD 并不是永久员工(临时工)，只
是以咨询顾问的身份被雇佣，且目前合约还有 6 个月。CEO 与 FD 是好朋友，两
人都是 ACCA members。(作为高管但工作不稳定，并且与公司 CEO 是好朋友，这
些会影响 FD 的 independence，使其面临 familiarity 以及 self-interest 的 threats。)
Dario Co currently has a cash flow problem, and the CEO has asked the FD if he is
prepared to reduce his current fee by 50%. The FD’s contract is due for renewal in the
next month. The CEO has recently spoken to the FD and stated that his contract is
more likely to be renewed if he is prepared to take a fee reduction. She has also said
that if he needs support, then as a friend, she will help him.
D 公司目前存在现金流问题，因此 CEO 询问 FD 是否愿意降低 50%的费用。FD 的
合同将在下个月续签。CEO 告知 FD 如果想要续约则需要接受降薪。但同时还告
知 FD，如果需要帮助她会给到支持。(面临降薪的风险，且被告知不降薪则不续
签，使得 FD 面临 intimidation 的风险，进而也可能会影响到 FD 后续的工作态度。)
The FD is upset by the fee request as no other director has taken a cut in their salary.
He is currently undertaking a going concern review of Dario Co, but has decided not
to complete his report until he has considered his position with Dario Co.
FD 决定直到合同问题解决之前他都不准备完成当前的可持续经营报告。
The FD’s findings will also form part of a submission to the government for funding
and will be shared with the external auditors. He knows that the current cash flow
position would indicate that the company is not a going concern and that if he
submitted his report, there would be no government funding. There is also the
possibility that the financial statements would receive a qualified audit report. He is
considering overstating future potential returns on investments.
公司的可持续状态也会影响申请政府的资助。由于公司目前面临现金流困境，因
此 going concern 不一定满足，一旦他提交此报告给到政府则公司申请不到任何
的补助。同时公司还有可能收到一份有保留意见的审计报告，对于公司经营都是
不利的。因此他决定夸大(高估)公司预计收到的投资回报。(此举动反映出 FD 违
背了 integrity 的基本原则，作为 ACCA 会员需要保有职业操守)
In addition, many of the accounting staff are remote employees, meaning that they
are now working at home rather than working in the office. The FD is worried about
the possibility of data theft. Unfortunately, he does not have the skills, nor does
Dario Co have the capacity, to undertake a widespread investigation into the issue.
Given the current request to reduce his fee and the cash flow situation, he also does
not have the incentive to deal with the potential data theft or inform the board to
enable them to address any potential problem. However, he has asked the
information technology department to add productivity monitoring software to all
remote employees’ computers without their permission or knowledge and any
employee who spends time on non-business websites will be immediately
disciplined.
内部数据有可能被盗窃，反映出公司内控流程有问题，而 FD 自身没有能力进行
解决，需要对其 competence 以及 due care 的能力表示怀疑。同时为了解决此情
况，在未经员工同意的情况下将监测软件安装在员工的电脑上。(虽然不存在道
德问题，但未经员工允许的行为在职场中并不合适。)
Requirements 解析:
(a) Using exhibit 1, discuss the ethical issues caused by the current business situation.
(10 marks)
Professional marks will be awarded in part (a) for the identification of relevant ethical
principles and the application of these to the scenario. (2 marks)
题目要求结合当前公司现状讨论潜在的道德问题。
答案解析:
One area of concern for the finance director (FD) is that of maintaining
independence in his decision making. His relationship with the chief executive officer
(CEO) can create familiarity and self-interest threats. These threats have manifested
themselves by the CEO’s request and pressure to reduce his fee. The CEO has stated
that the FD’s contract is more likely to be renewed if he is prepared to take a fee
reduction This is effectively an intimidation threat which can affect competence and
due care obligations.
结合上述案例信息进行分析，先识别出面临的道德威胁，以及违背的道德原则。
这里由于与 CEO 的朋友关系，导致 FD 的独立性受到影响，使其面临 familiarity and
self-interest threats。同时由于合同续签的问题，也同时面临 intimidation threat，
这些都会影响 FD 的 competence 以及 due care。
This pressure has resulted in the FD not submitting his going concern review to the
board. This could result in a lack of professional skepticism by the FD and a delayed
review of the situation by the board. The consequence is that the subsequent
disclosure of the going concern issue may be too late to save the company. This is not
because the FD is unaware of the need for disclosure, but because his non-disclosure
is due to the pressure which he is under. His personal friendship with the CEO has
resulted in the offer of support and this may have compromised his objectivity. The
going concern review is likely to have created some stress in his business relationship
with the CEO.
在面临合同续签压力的情况下，FD 的职业怀疑态度以及客观性都会受到负面影
响，使得他拖延审核可持续的情况，而这些都有可能会影响公司的后续经营。
The pressures on cash flow cannot be allowed to cloud the FD’s judgement when
preparing and presenting information accurately, without misleading in any way. This
challenge has arisen because of the need to present a submission to the government
for funding. The advocacy threat can create the potential for biased information as
the FD is considering overstating future potential return on investments. Information
must represent the true nature of business activities, and the fundamental principle
of integrity of the professional accountant is critical.
FD 不准确披露可持续发展报告，考虑夸大潜在的投资回报的行为存在着 advocaty
threat，从而导致存在偏见性的信息。而这依然会影响其 integrity，因为准确呈
现商业活动的真实性质是作为专业会计师必须要保守的。
The risk of data theft through the remote working of employees should have been
anticipated. IT and data risk assessments should be periodically carried out. However,
there is a capacity issue within Dario Co in dealing with data theft. In the FD’s case,
there are competence and due care issues which have potentially led to a lapse in
the assessment of internal controls and governance structures. Also, personal
integrity is extremely important, when dealing with such challenges. It is also vital for
him to recognise the importance of sharing his concerns with the board to enable
the decision makers to address the problems.
数据被盗风险体现了公司内控的问题，作为公司高管理应对于此风险问题进行定
期地评估核查，因此 FD 的 competence 以及 due care 存在问题。同时，个人诚信
也非常重要，FD 应该向董事会汇报此事项的重要性，使得决策者能够更好地解
决问题。
The FD wishes to implement productivity monitoring software to determine how
much the employees are working. This is not of itself an unethical practice, but he
wishes to implement this change without the knowledge or agreement of the
employees. When the employees are confronted, it will send a message to the
employees that he does not trust them and shows a lack of integrity on his part.
给员工电脑安装检测软件的出发点没有问题，也没有违背职业道德，但是在未通
知员工，未与员工进行有效沟通的情况下，直接安装，会让员工觉得并不被信任，
也是缺乏 integrity 的体现。
He should remind the employees that they should use company-issued equipment
for business purposes only, and not spend time on non-business websites. This is
simply good business practice and is not unethical on the part of the FD. However, to
threaten the employees with immediate disciplinary action does not seem to be a
considered approach and further emphasises the FD’s need for training and
professional development.
FD 应该在安装之前知会员工，并解释这么做的必要性。这虽不是职业道德问题，
但加强沟通，与员工建立相互信任的关系，而不是偏激地采取纪律处分其实是更
合适地举动。
题干解析:
2. Operating segments
Dario Co has four divisions (A, B, C and D). Divisions A and B produce and sell to third
parties. Division A produces vaccines and sells them to governments, generating 60%
of Dario Co’s total revenue. Division B produces allergy testing kits and sells them to
pharmacy wholesalers, generating 20% of Dario Co’s total revenue. Division B’s
long-term average gross profit margin is significantly higher than division A’s.
D 公司目前有四个 divisions，ABCD。Division A 以及 B 生产及销售商品给第三方。
Division A 主要销售疫苗给政府，产生的收入占 Dario 公司总收入的 60%。Division
B 主要生产过敏检测试剂盒，并卖给药品批发商，产生的收入占 Dario 公司总收
入的 20%。Division B 的长期平均毛利率远高于 Division A。
Divisions C and D undertake research and development. Division C does not provide
any research and development services to third parties and only conducts activities
for divisions A and B. Division C does not generate revenue from any internal or
external source. It is purely a cost centre operating from division D’s premises.
Division D performs contract investigation activities for other laboratories and
pharmaceutical companies. The external revenues of this division represent 20% of
Dario Co’s total revenues.
Division C 以及 D 主要负责研发工作。Division C 并不向第三方提供任何调差研发
服务，它仅支持 Division A 以及 B 的研发需求。因此 Division C 也不会产生任何内
外部的收入，仅仅只是 Division D 的一个成本中心。Division D 主要为其他的实验
室或者医药公司开展调查研发工作，其创造的外部收入占 D 公司总收入的 20%。
The operating results for divisions A, B, and D are regularly reviewed by the CEO.
These results include separate divisional operating profit or loss statements, from
which operational decisions are made. The costings and budgets for division C are
also reviewed by the CEO. There are four divisional heads who are directly
accountable to the CEO and these heads regularly discuss the operating activities
including the research and development activities, financial results, forecasts and
plans for their division. The CEO is the chief operating decision maker.
CEO 会定义审核 Division ABD 的经营结果，包含单个部分的经营损益表并从中做
出经营决策。Division C 的成本及预算也由 CEO 审查。目前四个 division 的负责人
都直接向 CEO 汇报，并定期讨论部门的经营活动，包括研发、财务、预测以及
计划。CEO 是首席运营决策者。
Requirements 解析:
(b) Explain, in accordance with the principles of IFRS 8 Operating Segments, whether
divisions A, B, C and D should be treated as separate reportable segments. Your
explanation should cover:
• the definition of an operating segment;
• the aggregation of operating segments; and
• the identification of reportable segments. (8 marks)
结合 IFRS 8 准则，解释 ABCD 是否应该作为单独的报告分部。且答案中需要包括
operating segment 经营分部的定义，经营分部的相加合并条件，已经报告分部的
划分条件。
答案解析:
Operating segment:
IFRS 8 Operating Sesments states that an operating segment is a component of an
entity which engages in business activities from which it might earn revenues or
incur expenses(including revenues and expenses relating to transactions with other
components of the same entity), whose operating results are regularly reviewed by
the entity's the chief operating decision maker (CODM), to make decisions about
resources to be allocated to the segment and assess its performance, and for which
discrete financial information is available.
结合准则，先将经营分部定义及确认条件列示清楚。
Division C is not an operating segment. This is because division C does not generate
revenue from any intenal or external source. It is purely a cost centre.
结合案例分析，Division C 不符合经营分部定义，因为 Division C 其实是不存在利
润中心的，它只是为了 Division A 以及 B 提供研发活动，不创造任何内外部收入，
因此仅仅只是一个成本中心。
Divisions A,B and D all generate revenues. The divisions have heads who are directly
accountable to the CEO and these heads regularly discuss the operating activities
including the research and development activities, financial results, forecasts and
plans for their division. It therefore seems reasonable to conclude that divisions A,B
and D are separate operating segments.
继续分析案例，Division ABD 都创造收入，且定期被 CODM 审查经营财务情况，
也都有自己独立的财务数据，是符合经营分部的划分条件的。
Aggregation of operating segments
Two or more operating segments may be aggregated into a single operating segment
if aggregation is consistent with the core principle of IFRS 8, the segments have
similar economic characteristics and the segments are similar in each of the following
respects:
- the nature of the products and services;
- the nature of the production processes;
- the type of customer for the products and services;
- the distribution methods for the products;and
- the nature of the regulatory environment.
符合经营分部定义后，下一步需考虑是否有部分经营分部可以被相加合并在一起，
因此先放相加的准则定义。
Divisions A and B both produce and sell pharmaceutical products and the nature of
the products could be deemed to be similar, although the production processes for
vaccines and allergy testing kits are likely to be different. Also, the type of customer
for these products is very different, being governments compared to pharmaceutical
wholesalers.
结合案例进行分析，Division A 以及 B 都是生产销售医药商品，因此产品性质是
类似的，仅管生产过程不同。但同时 Division A 以及 B 的客户群里也是不同的，
一个是政府，而另一个则是医药批发商。
In addition, operating segments often display similar long-term financial
performance if they have similar economic characteristics. Divisions A and B report
significantly different long-term average gross margins. Therefore, it would be
inappropriate to aggregate divisions A and B into one operating segment for
reporting purposes.
除此之外，如果经营分部存在类似的经济特征的情况下，其实他们的长期经济绩
效也应该是类似的，比如利润率。但是结合案例信息，Division A 以及 B 的平均
长期毛利率差异非常大，因此综合看来他们是不适合相加为一个单一的经营分部
的。
Reportable segments
An entity should report separately the information about an operating segment
which meets any of the following quantitative thresholds:
(a) Its reported revenue, including both sales to external customers and
inter-segment sales or transfers, is 10% or more of the combined revenue (internal
and external) of all operating segments.
(b)The absolute amount of its reported profit or loss is 10% or more of the greater, in
absolute amount, of
(i)the combined reported profit of all operating segments which did not report a loss,
and
(ii)the combined reported loss of all operating segments which reported a loss.
(c)Its assets are 10% or more of the combined assets of all operating segments.
考虑完加总后，就需要考虑报告分部的事项，因此先放准则关于报告分部的划分
条件。
If total external revenue reported by operating segments constitutes less than 75% of
the entity’s revenue, additional operating segments must be identified as reportable
segments.
除了上述三个划分条件外，还需要额外考虑外部收入 75%测试。
The revenues of divisions A, B and D exceed the 10% quantitative threshold for total
revenues. Because this first threshold is met, it is not necessary to apply the
remaining threshold tests. Divisions A, B and D will be reported separately.
结合案例进行分析，Division ABD 都符合 10%总收入的划分条件，因此都可以被
单独划分为报告分部，就无需再考虑其他的划分条件了。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:2023 SD 真题解析.pdf#2

stem_zh:
Q3
题干
explanation_zh:
Jacinta Co is the parent company of a group. Its main business is in the telecommunications
sector but, with the continued expansion of products and services it has also developed a credit
risk management business.The financial year end is 31 December 20X6. The functional and
presentation currency of Jacinta Co and Jacinta Group is the dollar.
Jacinta 公司是一个集团的母公司。它的主要业务是电信行业，但随着产品和服务的不断扩张，
它也发展了信贷风险管理业务。财政年度截止日期为 2016 年 12 月 31 日。Jacinta 公司和
Jacinta 集团的功能货币和报表货币是美元。
1. Loan portfolio
Jacinta Co purchases credit-impaired loans and measures these at amortised cost. Jacinta Co
correctly calculates the loss allowance as the changes in lifetime expected credit losses since
initial recognition of the asset which is effectively the changes in the estimated cash flows. On
1January 20X6, Jacinta Co bought credit-impaired loans with a remaining term of four years.
Jacinta Co paid $32 million, which is equivalent to the fair value. At 1 January 20X6,the remaining
contractual cash flows expected to be received on 31 December each year are:
Jacinta 公司购买信用减值的贷款，按照摊销成本衡量。Jacinta Co 正确地记录了减值准备，
自初始确认资产以来计提全部生命周期的预期信贷损失的变化，这实际上是估计现金流量的
变化。2016 年 1 月 1 日，Jacinta Co 购买了剩余期限为四年的信用减值贷款。Jacinta 公司支
付了 3200 万美元，与公允价值相当。截至 2016 年 1 月 1 日，预计每年 12 月 31 日收到的
剩余合同现金流量如下:
The credit-adjusted effective interest rate on the above cash flows is 3.9% per annum.
During the year ended 31 December 20X6, $8.8 million was collected. At 31 December 20X6 the
credit worthiness of the loans has improved. Jacinta Co now expects the following cash flows to
be collected:
信用调整的有效利率是 3.9%，16 年年底，$8.8m 被收回，在 16 年 12 月 31 日，信用提升，
Jacinta 公司预期未来现金流会被收回。
2. Revenue and inventories
Revenue from Sear Co
On 1January 20X6, Jacinta Co entered into a contract to provide Sear Co with an internet service
for two years. The contract price was agreed at $2 million which was received in full on 1January
20X6. At the same date, Jacinta Co sold equipment to Sear Co at its fair value of $500,000. At the
end of the internet service contract,on 31 December 20X7, Sear Co has a right to return the
equipment to Jacinta Co and receive $20,000. Jacinta Co anticipates that the equipment's fair
value will be $75,000 in two years' time.
2016 年 1 月 1 日，Jacinta Co 签订了一份合同，为 sear.co 提供为期两年的互联网服务。合同
价格商定为 200 万美元，并于 2016 年 1 月 1 日全额收到。在同一日期，Jacinta Co 以 50 万
美元的公允价值将设备出售给 sear.co。在互联网服务合同结束时，即 2017 年 12 月 31 日，
sear.co 有权将设备退还给 Jacinta Co，并获得 20,000 美元。Jacinta 公司预计，该设备的公允
价值将在两年后达到 7.5 万美元。
The market for second-hand equipment is very strong and Sear Co would be able to sell the
equipment easily at the end of the two-year agreement. Jacinta Co does not want to deal in
second-hand equipment and therefore always prices equipment returns at an amount
substantially below fair value. Sear Co has never returned equipment to Jacinta Co in the past.
二手设备的市场非常强劲，在两年的协议结束时，希尔公司可以很容易地卖掉这些设备。
Jacinta 公司不希望交易二手设备，因此总是以远低于公允价值的价格为设备回报定价。西尔
公司过去从未将设备退还给 Jacinta 公司。
Sale of inventories to overseas subsidiary
On 1 September 20X6, Jacinta Co sold inventories costing $1.35 million to a wholly-owned
overseas subsidiary for $2 million. There is no right of return.The subsidiary has the euro as its
functional currency.The exchange rate on the date of the sale from Jacinta Co to the overseas
subsidiary was $1= €1.25. At 31 December 20X6,the subsidiary still holds the goods in inventories
and the exchange rate is $1 = €1.30. There is no inter-company debt outstanding at the year end.
2016 年 9 月 1 日，Jacinta Co 以 200 万美元的价格将价值 135 万美元的库存出售给一家全资
海外子公司。没有返回的权利。该子公司以欧元作为其职能货币。Jacinta Co 出售给海外子
公司之日的汇率为 1 美元= 1.25 欧元。在 2016 年 12 月 31 日，子公司仍持有存货，汇率为
1 美元= 1.30 欧元。年底没有公司间未偿债务。
Requirements 解析:
(a) Explain how Jacinta Co should account for the credit-impaired loans in the financial
statements for the year ended 31 December 20X6. Your answer should include journal entries.(8
marks)
Jancinta 公司怎么在 2016 年 12 月 31 日计量信用减值的贷款【需要写分录】
对于账务记录相关的题目，首先要解释准则本身对于该事项的规定，再结合题干信息，记性
具体的计量和会计处理，本题要求写分录，因此需要在作答时通过计算，写出会计分录。
答案解析:
(a) Financial asset
IFRS 9 financial instruments requires recognition of impairment losses on a forward-looking basis,
which means that impairment loss is recognised before the occurrence of any credit event. These
impairment losses are referred to as expected credit losses(ECL). IFRS 9 requires that a loan be
reported with its risk of expected loss, regardless of whether the risk has already materialised or
will materialise in the future.
IFRS9 号准则要求金融资产需要采用预期减值法，出于谨慎性和对未来不确定性的考量，需
要在 stage 1 的时候针对未来 12 个月提信用减值准备，在 stage 2(风险显著上升的时候）和
stage 3（明显证据证明已发生减值时）针对未来剩余期限提信用减值准备。
因此这里需要阐述，IFRS 9 要求买来的贷款应该体现预期损失，无论这个风险是重大还是非
重大。
The purchased credit-impaired financial assets are recorded on initial recognition at the
transaction price without presentation of an allowance for expected contractual cash shortfalls.
This is because the expected shortfalls are implicit in the purchase price.On initial recognition,
the following journal entry is required:
结合题干进行具体的计量和确认：在初始计量的时候，因为买入价格（金融资产定价时）中
已经包含了未来可能的预期减值，所以这里不需要提减值准备。
但是在后续，按照摊余成本法，按有效利率计息（32*3.9%=1.25）,按照实际收款的现金流调
减债券价值，Dr cash $8.8m Cr loan asset $8.8m.年底该债务资产的账面金额按照摊余成本法
应该是$24.45m.
The amount recognised as a loss allowance for these assets is not the total amount of lifetime
ECL,but instead the change in lifetime ECLs since initial recognition of the asset.Any favourable
changes in lifetime ECL are recognised as an impairment gain.
对于信用减值准备，作为资产负债表科目，我们需要基于上年记录的减值准备调整当年的变
动额，从而得到年底时应计的减值准备。
The revised expected cash flows should be discounted using the original EIR and compared to the
original expected cash flows as at 31 December 20X6:
算摊余成本法计量的金融资产，年底的 Recoverable amount 等于新估计的现金流，按照原本
的有效利率（3.9%）折现。因此期初该资产的可赎回金额从$24.47m 变为$28.8m,，资产价
值增加，因此调减减值准备，差额计入当期损益。
The resulting favourable change in lifetime ECLs is therefore $3.34 million($27.8m-$24.47m).An
impairment gain of $3.34 million is recognized in profit or loss at 31 December 20X5.
Dr Loss allowance $3.34m
CR Profit or loss $3.34m
Tutorial note: An alternative approach is to calculate that the revised cash flows show in increase
or $1.2 million per annual from 20X7 onwards and discount this change to calculate$ 3.34
million($1·2m/039+$1·2m/1·039^2+$1.2m/1.39^3)
方法二，也可以直接计算现金流差额的现值和，即$(10-8.8)m 在未来三年的现值和。
At 31 December 20X6, the following amounts will be recognised in the statement of financial
position:
Loan carrying amount will be $24.45 million
Loas allowance will be a debit balance of $3.34 million
Requirements 解析:
(b)(i) Explain how the revenue from the internet service contract and the sale of equipment to
Sear Co should be accounted for in accordance with IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with
Customers. (10 marks)
请解释对于互联网服务合同与卖设备应该怎样记录
本题考察的是对收入准则的理解，收入确认的时点，以及对于售后回购的会计处理。因此首
先需要阐述在 IFRS15 收入准则的内容。再结合题干进行解释。
答案解析:
b)(i) Internet service contract
In accordance with IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers,each performance obligation
requires the determination of whether it is satisfied by transferring the control of goods or
services over time or at a point in time.
收入准则要求收入应当在相应的履约义务被完成，也就是商品或者服务的控制权发生转移的
时候，可能是某个时点，也可能是一段时间，才可确认。
With regards to the internet service contract, the customer simultaneously receives and
consumes the economic benefits of the provided service as the jacinta Co performs it. Revenue
from this is therefore recognised over time.
互联网那个服务合同，是一个典型的服务类的履约义务，服务类即为顾客同时收到对方提供
的经济利益并消费了该利益。因此收入应该按照时间段确认。
Revenue of $1 million will be recognised in each year of the contract. At 31December 20X6,a
contract liability of $1 million will be presented as a current liability on the statement of financial
position.
而因为货款在第一时间全部收到，相应履约义务并未完成，因此应当确认为递延收益（负债）
在 16 年年底的时候，只提供了一年的服务，只可以确认$1m 为收入，另外$1m 为短期负债
Equipment
IFRS 15 states that if a customer has a right to require the entity to repurchase the asset at a
price which s lower than the original selling price, then the entity must assess whether the
customer has a significant economic incentive to exercise the right. The key factor to consider is
the relationship between the repurchase price and the expected fair value of the asset at the
date of repurchase.
IFRS 15 规定，若顾客有权利要求卖方公司以一个低于原本售价的金额回购该商品，那么公
司需要评估顾客有无较大的经济动机行使该权利（即以固定价格卖出，put option）。卖方
公司需要考虑该回购价格和回购日该资产的公允价值
lf the customer has a significant economic incentive to require the entity to repurchase the asset,
then the entity accounts for the agreement as a lease, unless the contract is part of a sale and
leaseback transaction. If the customer does not have a significant economic incentive. then the
entity accounts for the agreement as the sale of a product with a right of return.
若顾客有极大的经济动机要求公司买回该商品，那么相当于公司“卖”出资产收到一笔对价，
并在回购后继续拥有该资产，且支付一定的对价。即相当于这是一个租赁活动，卖方公司其
实是出租该资产，收取租金。
如果顾客没有强大的动机去要求卖方买回该资产，那么公司可以将该交易看做是有退货权的
销售。
Sear Co does not have a significant economic incentive to require Jacinta Co to repurchase the
asset because the repurchase price of $20,000 is lower than the expected fair value of $5,000.
Additionally, the market for second-hand equipment very strong and Sear Co would be able to
sell the equipment easily at the end of the two-year agreement.
S 公司没有强烈的动机要求 H 公司买回该资产，因为该资产的回购价格为$20000 比回购时
的公允价值要低$5000.且题干说了，该设备的二手市场很火爆，S 公司可以轻松的在二手市
场上以更高的价格快速卖出该设备。
Therefore, Jacinta Co has transferred control of the equipment to Sear Co on 1 January 20X6.This
is because Sear Co is not limited in its ability to direct the use of the equipment, or ts ability to
obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from the equipment. Jacinta Co will account for
the transaction as a sale with a right of return.
因此 J 公司在 2016,1,1 完成了该资产控制权的转移. 因为 S（未被限制）可以主导该资产的
用途，且能够获取该设备后续全部的经济利益流入。因此 J 公司是卖出了该资产的，应该当
成有退货权的销售。
Revenue will be measured at the gross transaction price of $500,000,less the expected level of
returns, which, in this case, will be nil. This means no refund liability or return asset will be
recognised. Jacinta Co will update its measurement of the refund liability and return asset at
each reporting date for changes in expectations about the amount of the refunds.
收入应该按照该资产的卖价$500,000 减掉可能退回的金额。但是目前根据已知信息是 0.所
以 J 公司应该按照$500,000 确认收入，且在后续每年估计可能退款的情况，按照预期调整
refund liability 的金额。
Requirements 解析:
(b)(ii) Explain, with calculations, how the sale of inventories would be accounted for in the
individual financial statements of both Jacinta Co and the overseas subsidiary at 1 September
20X6 and in the consolidated financial statements of the Jacinta Group at 31 December 20X6. (7
marks)
请计算辅助解释买存货在 1 Sep 20X6 Jacinta 应该在公司和海外子公司，以及 X6.12.31 年底合
并报表上如何计量。
解析：内部交易，母子公司双方单价报表正常按照采购和销售进行记账。
答案解析:
(ii) Sale of inventories
Jacinta Co at 1 September 20X6
When recording the sale of $2 million as revenue on l September 20X6, and the cost of the
inventories in cost of sales of $1.35 million, Jacinta Co has realised profit of
$650,000($2m-$1.35m) in its individual financial statements.
对于母公司 Jacinta, 在发生销售的时候，按照$2m 记录销售收入，并按照存货的成本记录售
出存货成本，差额即为当期的利润
The subsidiary at 1 September 20X6
The subsidiary has recorded the cost of inventories at €2.5 million (€2m*1.25）At 1 September
20X6, the cost of inventoriess includes the orginal cost to Jacinta Co of €1,687,500(1.35mx1.25)
plus the intra-group profit of €812,500(€650,000x1.25) charged by Jacinta Co
对于子公司，因为按照欧元记账，需要按照采购日当天的汇率进行换算，因此应当计入存货
成本€2.5m, 这里考官答案对该存货成本进行了拆分解释，€2.5m 的金额包含了该批存货在
母公司处的成本（$1,687,500）及母公司赚取子公司的利润（$812,500）
Consolidated financial statements at 31 December 20X6
Jacinta Co consolidation at 31 December 20X6, the intragroup revenue and cost of sales should
be eliminated. The intragroup profit of $650,000 (€812,500)is also eliminated as the inventories
have not been sold and the profit is not realised outside the group.
When the subsidiary's financial statements are translated into the presentation currency of the
group, the cost of the inventories of €1,687,500 is translated at the current exchange rate at 31
December20X6 and inventories of $1,298,077 (€1,687,500÷1.3) are recognised in the group
statement of financial position.Therefore,at 31 December 20X6, Jacinta Group will recognise a
loss on exchange on inventories of $51,923 ($1.35m-$1,298,077) as part of the translation
reserve associated with the subsidiary.
Because the subsidiary is wholly-owned, there is no non-controlling interest.
合并报表上，则需要对内部交易进行冲回。内部交易，母公司记账的收入，子公司记账的成
本，都应当冲回。
且该批存货全部都属于内部交易且没有被卖出集团，因此所有的利润都属于未实现利润，该
存货应当按照成本记账。因为 9 月子公司买入时的真正的成本是€1,687,500，按照年底的汇
率换算成母公司的报表货币$，也就是$1298077. 因此消除内部交易 ，该批存货在年底的价
值为 $1,298,077。
而原本在母公司处该存货的进货成本（真正成本为$1350000）,差额为汇兑损失，计入子公
司报表翻译时产生的合并报表的汇兑损益
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:2023 SD 真题解析.pdf#3

stem_zh:
Q4
Benito Co is in the real estate industry. The financial year end of Benito Co is 31 December 20X7.
Benito 是一家房地产公司，财年年底为 31,12,20X7,。
题干
explanation_zh:
1. Sale and leaseback
On 1 January 20X7, Benito Co sold its head office which had a carrying amount of $3.75 million to
Otine Co for cash of $7·5 million. On the same date Benito Co has entered into a contract with
Otine Co to use the head office for 20 years, with annual payments payable at the end of each
year.Both companies have correctly accounted for the transaction as a sale and leaseback.
At 1 January 20X7, the fair value of the head office is $6·75 million. The present value of the
annual lease payments is $5.47 million.
2007 年 1 月 1 日，Benito Co 以 750 万美元的现金将其账面金额为 375 万美元的总部大楼出
售给了 Otine 公司。在同一天，Benito Co 与 Otine 公司签订了一份使用总部 20 年的合同，
每年年底付款。两家公司都正确地将这笔交易记录为售后回租。
2007 年 1 月 1 日，总部大楼的公允价值为 67.5 万美元。每年租金的现值为 547 万美元。
Key performance indicators (KPIs)
A sale and leaseback transaction allows selling companies to free up cash, improve liquidity and
redeploy capital into core activities. The decision as regards whether the sale and leaseback can
be treated as a sale under IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers can significantly
affect Benito Co's KPls and profitability, therefore affecting an investor's view of the assets which
Benito Co is using and the liabilities which it has. The following KPls of Benito Co wil l be affected
by this decision:
售后回租交易使得卖方公司释放现金，改善流动性，并将资本重新配置到核心业务中。根据
《国际财务报告准则第 15 号》，关于出售和回租是否可以被视为出售的决定可能会显著影
响 Benito Co 的 kpl 和盈利能力，从而影响投资者对 Benito Co 正在使用的资产及其所拥有的
负债的看法。Benito Co 的以下 kpl 将受到此决定的影响:
Requirements 解析:
(a) In accordance with IFRS Accounting Standards:
(i) describe how an entity assesses whether the transfer of an asset qualifies as a ‘sale’ in sale
and leaseback accounting;
请解释公司如何判断资产属于售后回租中出售的状态
(ii) explain how sale and leaseback transactions are dealt with by the seller-lessee in accordance
with IFRS 16 Leases, if the transfer of an asset qualifies as a ‘sale’;
若满足售后回租的出售定义，请解释售后回租交易如何被卖方（承租人）计量。
(iii) explain how sale and leaseback transactions are dealt with by the seller-lessee, if the transfer
of an asset does not qualify as a ‘sale’; and
若不满足售后回租的出售定义，请解释售后回租交易如何被卖方（承租人）计量。
(iv) explain the effect on the accounting treatment if a sale and leaseback agreement contains a
call option under which the seller-lessee can, at its option, repurchase the property.
请解释若售后回租合同包含了一个看涨期权，卖方（承租人）有权利回购该商资产，应当如
何计量售后回租
Note: There is no need to refer any exhibit to answer part (a). (9 marks)
(b) Using exhibit 1, explain in accordance with IFRS 16, the accounting entries for the sale and
leaseback transaction in the financial statements of Benito Co on 1 January 20X7 if the head
office transfer:
• qualifies as a ‘sale’; and
• does not qualify as a ‘sale’. (7 marks)
结合信息 1，请解释 IFRS 16 对于售后回租的规定在 20X7 年 1 月 1 日应分别如何记账：
如果满足出售的判定；若不满足出售的判定
(c) Using exhibit 1, compare the impact of the head office transfer qualifying as a ‘sale’ and not
qualifying as a ‘sale’ on Benito Co’s financial statements and key performance indicators (KPIs).
(7 marks)
结合信息 1，对比总部大楼满足出售和不满足出售，对公司 KPI 的影响
Two professional marks will be awarded in part (c) for the quality of the discussion regarding the
effects on the financial statements and the specific KPIs. (2 marks)
本题完全围绕售后回租进行考察，需要对相关准则定义有较深刻的理解。
答案解析:
4 (a)(i) Assessing whether the transfer qualifies as a sale
The entity assesses whether the transfer qualifies as a sale by using the guidance in IFRS 15
Revenue from Contracts with Customers. A sale has occurred when the seller has satisfied a
performance obligation by transferring control of the asset. Control refers to the ability to direct
the use of the asset and to obtain substantially all of its remaining benefits.
关于是否转让满足出售的定义，我们需要结合 IFRS 15 收入准则进行解释，销售意味着履约
义务的完成和控制权的转移，控制的转移在新准则中定义为：是否有能力主导该资产的用途，
并且能够获得该资产后续所有的经济利益流入。
(ii) Accounting treatment if the transfer QUALIFIES as a sale
The seller-lessee derecognises the asset.
A right-of-use(Rou) asset is recognised, and is measured at the proportion of the previous
carrying amount of the asset which relates to the rights retained by the seller-lessee.
A lease liability is recognised, equal to the present value off the lease payments to be paid. A
profit or loss is reported in the statement of profit or loss, based on the actual part transferred.
若转让满足出售：应当确认使用权资产，并按照作为出售方回租比例的账面价值进行计量。
同时应当确认租赁负债，等于未来要支付的租金现值和。对于实际卖出的部分资产，记录处
置损益。
Note that if the consideration received for the asset exceeds its fair value this is accounted for as
additional financing. The consideration is below the assets fair value, then this is accounted for
as a prepayment of lease payments.
若出售对价高于资产的公允价值，视同额外融资。若出售对价低于公允价值，视同租金的预
付。
Subsequently, the Rou asset is depreciated over the lease term. interest is charged on the lease
liability using the rate implicit in the lease.
后续使用权资产应当在租期内进行折旧，利息按照租赁合同的默含利率乘租赁负债计算。
iii)Accounting treatment if the transfer does not qualify as a sale
若不满足出售的定义
The seller-lessee continues to recognise the asset on its statement of financial position. The
seller lessee accounts for proceeds from the sale and leaseback as a financial liability in
accordance with IFRS 9 Financial Instruments.
作为出售方，应当继续在资产负债表上确认该资产，将出售对价按照 IFRS9 金融工具准则计
做金融负债
对于不满足售后回租的交易，相当于是抵押贷款 secured loan ,所以记做负债，在后续支付的
租金，相当于是分期还款。
(iv)The effect if an agreement contains a call option
If the transaction contains a call option under which the seller-lessee can, at its option,
repurchase the property, then such an option generally precludes sale accounting under IFRS
15.This is because the existence of the call option means that the seller-lessee retains control of
the property. Therefore, sale and leaseback accounting does not apply, with the asset remaining
on the statement of financial position and the cash received treated as a financial liability.
如果该交易包含一项看涨期权，根据该期权，卖方-承租人可以自行选择回购该财产，则该
期权通常排除了 IFRS 15 下的出售会计。这是因为看涨期权的存在意味着卖方-承租人保留对
财产的控制。因此，售后回租会计不适用，资产保留在财务状况表上，收到的现金被视为金
融负债。
(b) Head office transfer qualifies as a sale
Because the consideration for the sale of the head office is not at fair value, Benito Co recognises
the $0.75 million excess over fair value($7.5m-$6.75m) as additional financing provided by Otine
Co. The present value of the annual payments amounts to $5.47 million of which $0.75
million($7.5m-$6.75m) relates to the additional financing and $4.72 million($5.47m- $0.75m)
relates to the lease.
At 1 January 20X7,Benito Co measures the Rou asset arising from the leaseback of the building at
the proportion of the previous carrying amount of the head office which relates to the right of
use retained. This is calculated as $3.75 million (the carrying amount of the head office)÷$6.75
million(the fair value of the building) $4.72 million(the discounted lease payments for the RoU
asset), that is $2.62 million.
Benito Co recognises only the amount of the gain which relates to the rights transferred to Otine
Co. The gain on sale is $3 million ($6.75m-$3.75m), of which $2.09 million($3m÷$6.75mx$4.72m)
relates to the right to use the head office retained by Benito Co. The rights transferred to Jtine
Co are measured at $0.9 million ($3m-$6·75mx($6.75m-$4.72m)).
若转让满足销售，本题销售对价不等于公允价值，因此对于多卖出的钱，相当于是在处置日
当天进行了一笔额外融资，金额为 7.5-6.75=$0.75m,未来租金的总现值和在今天为$5.47m,
其中有$0.75m 是在还这笔额外融资，因此剩余的$4.72m 是真正的调整后的租赁负债。
2017/1/1 Benito 公司应当按照回租比例的账面价值计算使用权资产，回租比例为 4.72/6.75,
使用权资产的价值为$3.75m*4.72/6.75=$2.62m.
看 似 该 笔 出 售 的 处 置 收 益 为 $3 million ($6·75m-$3.75m), 其 实 有 $2.09
million($3m÷$6·75mx$4·72m)属于租回的部分资产，所以不是真正的处置收益，只有$0·9
million ($3m-$6·75mx($6·75m-$4.72m))应当计入损益，是卖出部分的资产的处置收益。
At 1 January 20X7,Benito Co accounts for the transaction as follows.
若不属于真正的销售，则 Benito 应当继续确认固定资产账面价值为$3.75m,同时确认金融负
债$7.5m.
(c) Financial statements
The decision as regards whether the sale and leaseback can be treated as a sale under IFRS 15
can significantly affect performance ratios. If the sale and leaseback cannot be treated as a sale
under IFRS 15,the gross assets and liabilities will be greater for the seller-lessee as the asset
remains on the statement of financial position and the cash received is treated as a financial
liability. Total debt will be higher and this may have an impact with loan covenants based on
total debt levels as it may lead to breaches.
关于售后回租是否可被视为 IFRS 15 项下的销售的决定会显著影响业绩表现。
如果根据国际财务报告准则第 15 号，该售后回租不能被视为销售，则卖方（承租人）的总
资产和负债将更大。因为该资产仍在财务状况表中，“处置”收到的所有现金对价的被视为金
融负债。总债务将可能会更高，这可能会对基于总债务水平的贷款契约产生影响，高因为债
务存在着未来违约的不确定性，因此可能会影响公司的后续借贷能力。
The difference in treatment will also affect some profitability ratios because of two elements: the
depreciation and the interest charge. The depreciation and interest charge will be higher in this
case as the carrying amount of the property is recorded at a higher value than the RoU asset and
the interest charge will be higher as the financial liability is higher than the lease liability
Also, there will be no gain recorded on the sale of the asset,thus reducing profit.
Key performance indicators (KPIs)
会计处理对利润指标也会有影响，主要因素是利息费用和折旧费用。折旧和利息费用。对于
该题，不能确认为出售的话，折旧和利息费用会更高一些，因为资产的账面价值比 ROU asset
更高，所以折旧更多；其次利息费用是基于全部的 financial liability 计算的，而不是租赁负
债为技术计算，因此公司的利润会被降低。
KPI Calculation Effect of sale and leaseback
compared with the
transaction of fully sale
Gearings Debt/Equity Gearing will be higher because
the financial liability of $7.5
million is greater than the
combined sales and leaseback
liability of $5.47 million.
杠杆增加的原因是金融负债
的金额比租赁负债的金额要
大。
EBIT Earnings before interest and
tax
EBIT will be lower because of
a higher depreciation
charge( based on original
carrying amount of $3·75
million rather than RoU asset
of $2·62 million)
and no gain on sale.
息税前利润要扣除折旧，而原
账面价值计算的折旧费用更
高，因此息税前利润较低。
Asset turnover Sales/total assets Interest has no impact on
EBIT. Asset turnover will be
lower because the property
remains on SOFP at its
carrying amount of $3·75
million. This is higher than the
RoU asset of $2·62 million if
the transfer is a sale.
资产周转率会降低，因为销售
额不变的情况下，（处置资产
不计入主营业务收入）账面价
值比使用权资产更高
Return on capital employed
、ROCE)
EBIT/equity plus financial
liabilities
ROCE will be lower because
EBIT will be lower. Also,
financial liabilities will be
higher if the asset is not sold.
If the transfer is a sale, equity
will be higher because of the
gain on sale and the reduced
deprecation but also EBIT will
be higher.
ROCE 会降低，因为不卖掉该
资产的 EBIT 降低，（折旧大，
而且不计处置收益），且负债
更高，所以 ROCE 降低。
反之若是销售，存在处置收
益，和较小的折旧费用，因此
equity 和 EBIT 也同等变大，
且租赁负债比金融负债小，
ROCE 一 般 是 一 个 小 于 1 的
数，小于 1 的分数分子分母
同增相同金额，分数变大。
.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:2023 SD 真题解析.pdf#4

stem_zh:
Q1
背景分析:
Articat 公司是集团母公司，年底为 X5.6.30。合并利润表存在错报及漏报的情况(因此需
要改错)。T 以及 F 两个被投资方，今年 Articat 准备进行股份处置。
题干
explanation_zh:
1. Thuddle Co
On 1 October 20X4, Articat Co sold 50,000 of its equity shares in Thuddle Co for proceeds of
$465,000. Articat Co debited cash and credited investments with the proceeds.
X4.10.1，A 公司卖掉了 50,000 支 T 公司的股票，销售对价为$465,000。A 公司按照此金
额将其借方记入 Cash，贷方计入 Investment in T。
Articat Co had acquired 400,000 of the 500,000 $1 equity shares of Thuddle Co several years
ago. Goodwill on acquisition was correctly calculated as $68,000 and has not been impaired.
The fair value of the net assets of Thuddle Co (excluding goodwill) on 1 October 20X4 were
$4·8 million.
A 公司在多年前购买了 T 公司 500 支股票当中的 400 支(股本$1)，持股 80%。收购日当
天商誉正确计算为$68,000，且没有发生减值。X4.10.1 处置日当天 T 公司净资产的公允
价值(不包括商誉)为$4.8m。(通过案例可以得知，处置的是收购的 400 支股票当中的 50
支，持股比例从 80%降为 70%，属于 control to control，控制权并未丧失。)
On 30 June 20X5, Thuddle Co sold inventory at a mark-up of 25% to Articat Co for $30,000.
Articat Co still had all of these goods in closing inventory. The sale and the purchase were
both recorded correctly in the individual financial statements, but no adjustments have yet
been made to the consolidated financial statements for this transaction.
T 公司在 X5.6.30 卖了批存货给 A 公司，售价$30,000，并按照 mark-up on cost 25%定价。
年底这批存货依然留在 A 公司的仓库里。买卖交易都已正确记录在母子公司各自的单家
报表中了，但并未进行合并抵消处理。
Articat Co valued the non-controlling interest of Thuddle Co at acquisition as its
proportionate share of the fair value of the identifiable net assets.
A 公司按照 partial goodwill 的方式对 NCI 进行计量。
Articat Co has included 100% of the revenue and expenses of Thuddle Co for the year ended
30 June 20X5 in the draft statement of profit or loss.
A 公司已经将 T 公司截止到 X5.6.30 全年的收入费用 100%合并进合并利润表里了。
Requirement 解析:
(a)(i) Using exhibit 1, explain and calculate the accounting treatment in the consolidated
financial statements of the disposal of shares in Thuddle Co. (5 marks)
根据案例 1，解释并计算合并报表层面处置 T 公司股份的会计处理。
答案解析:
The sale by Articat Co reduces its ownership in Thuddle Co from 80% to
70%((400,000-50,000)/500,000). Since control was not lost, Articat Co correctly included
100% of the income and expenses of Thuddle Co for the year in the consolidated statement
of profit or loss.
持股比例从 80%减持到 70%，控制权未丧失，因此 T 公司全年利润可以 100%被合并，
处理没有问题。
The proceeds received should not have been credited to investments. Instead, the reduction
in Articat Co's ownership should have been treated as an equity transaction.The carrying
amount of the non-controlling interests (NCl) is adjusted to reflect the change in its interest
in Thuddle Co. Since the NCl at acquisition was measured at its proportionate share of the
fair value of the subsidiary's identifiable net assets, no goodwill is attributable to the NCl.
The NCl will increase by $480,000, being 10% of the net assets of $4.8 million at disposal.
Since this is greater than the proceeds of $465,000, a debit of $15,000 should be recorded
within equity.
控制权不变的情况下，不应该减少 investments 而是考虑 NCI 的变动情况，因为属于股
东间的交易(NCI 从 20%增加到了 30%)。同时 NCI 按照 partial goodwill 的方式进行计量，
因此无需考虑商誉的影响。根据处置日当天集团净资产的 FV $4.8m 得出处置掉的 10%
的价值为$480,000(也就是 NCI 增加了$480,000)。由于是股东间的交易，因此处置利得计
入 Equity。
Tutorial note: The entry should have been:
Dr Cash $465,000
Dr Equity $15,000
Cr NCl $480,000
正确的会计分录如上所示，因此需要根据正确的及错误的推导出调整分录。
2. Findler Co
On 1 July 20X2, Articat Co had acquired 900,000 of the one million $1 ordinary shares of
Findler Co. The carrying amount of the identifiable net assets on acquisition was equal to
their fair value of $7,950,000. Articat Co valued the non-controlling interest at acquisition at
fair value. This was deemed to be $870,000.
A 公司在 X2.7.1 购买了 F 公司 90%的股份。收购日当天 F 公司净资产的公允价值为
$7,950,000。A 公司按照 full goodwill 的方法核算 NCI，同时收购日当天 NCI 的价值为
$870,000。
On 31 March 20X5, Articat Co sold 500,000 of its equity shares in Findler Co for cash of $10
per share. This was the fair value of one Findler Co share on 31 March 20X5. Articat Co has
included the entire proceeds within investment income in the consolidated statement of
profit or loss. The fair value of the net assets of Findler Co on 31 March 20X5 were
$9,048,000. The draft statement of consolidated profit or loss only includes nine months of
Findler Co’s profit for the year. The profit for the year was $504,000 and it accrued evenly.
在 X5.3.31，A 公司按照每股$10 处置了 900,000 支股票当中的 500,000 支股票(持股比例
从 90%减少到了 40%)。A 公司将整个处置所得包含进合并利润表下的投资收益当中去(处
理错误)。处置日当天 F 公司净资产的公允价值为$9,048,000，当前草拟的合并利润表里
仅包含了 F 公司 9 个月的损益。全年损益为$504,000 且均匀发生。
Plant owned by Findler Co on 31 March 20X5 had a fair value of $20,000 above the carrying
amount and a remaining useful life of two years. It can be assumed that Articat Co has
significant influence following the disposal.
在处置日当天，F 公司的某个 plant 公允价值比账面价值多$20,000，剩余可使用年限为
2 年的时间。假设处置后 A 公司对于 F 公司具有重大影响(即 F 公司为 associate 联营企
业)。
Goodwill of Findler Co of $1,020,000 had been impaired by 25% in the year ended 30 June
20X4. Since the sales proceeds were above the original cost, it was decided to reverse the
impairment in the current year which has been credited to administrative expenses in the
draft consolidated statement of profit or loss for the Articat group.
截止到上年底 X4.6.30，F 公司$1,020,000 的商誉减值了 25%。由于处置股份的对价高于
成本，F 公司决定在当年进行商誉的减值损失转回，并作管理费用减记(Dr Goodwill 255 Cr
Admin exp 255，做错了需要改错，商誉不允许进行减值损失转回)
On 30 June 20X5, Articat Co sold a property to Findler Co for $1·5 million. The proceeds were
considered to be equal to the fair value of the property on 30 June 20X5. Articat Co has
included the proceeds in group revenue. The carrying amount of the property was $1·6
million. The property, which has not been derecognised by Articat Co, was used for
administrative purposes.
A 公司在 X5.6.30 将一个 property 卖给了 F 公司，卖了$1.5m，销售对价与公允价值相等，
A 公司将此金额包含进了 Revenue(做错了需要调整，不属于主营业务应计算处置利得)。
处置当天资产的账面价值为$1.6m，同时 A 公司并未将此资产终止确认(处置当下账面价
值高于公允价值，资产存在减值损失，根据准则要求资产处置前的减值损失由母公司全
额承担)。
Requirement 解析:
(a)(ii) Using exhibit 2, explain, without calculations, the accounting treatment in the
consolidated financial statements of the Articat Group for each of the following issues:
• The reversal of the impairment of goodwill of Findler Co;
• The disposal of shares in Findler Co; and
• The sale of the property by Articat Co to Findler Co. (11 marks)
针对商誉减值损失转回、处置 F 公司股份以及 A 销售给 F 的 property 的会计处理，做出
解释，无需任何计算。
答案解析:
The reversal of the impairment of goodwill of Findler Co
It is incorrect to reverse the impairment of goodwill of Findler Co. This is because the
reversal would represent an increase in internally generated goodwill rather than a reversal
of the impairment loss recognised for the acquired goodwill. IAS 38 Intangible Assets
explicitly prohibits the recognition of internally generated goodwill since goodwill is not an
identifiable resource controlled by the entity which can be reliably measured. The reversal of
the impairment of $255,000 must be added back to administrative expenses.
商誉不可以进行减值损失转回，准则是禁止的。因此进行减值损失转回的处理是错误的，
需要将错误记账反向冲销，即 Dr Admin exp 255 Cr Goodwill 255。
The disposal of shares in Findler Co
The sale by Articat Co reduces its ownership in Findler Co from 90% to 40%((900,000
-500,000)/1,000,000). Control has been lost, meaning that Articat Co should only consolidate
the revenue and expenses for the first nine months of the year. The profit attributable to the
NCl will be calculated on just nine months of profits. The net assets, goodwill and NCl should
be derecognised from the consolidated statement of financial position.
A 公司将其在 F 公司的所有权从 90%减少到 40%((90-50)/100), 丧失了控制权，这意味着
F 公司应该只合并今年前 9 个月的收入和费用。可归属于 NCl 的利润同样需要按 9 个月
的利润计算。净资产、商誉和 NCI 都应从合并资产负债表中终止确认。
The proceeds of $5 million will need to be removed from the investment income of the
group. Instead, the difference between Articat Co's net investment in Findler Co (net assets
plus goodwill minus NCl) and the proceeds and fair value of the remaining 40% equity
interest should be recorded as a profit or loss on disposal in the consolidated statement of
profit or loss.
$5m 的处置对价不应该计入 investment income，相反应该根据处置的 proceeds+剩余股
份的 FV-(处置日当天的净资产+商誉-NCI)的差得出处置利得的部分。
Since Articat Co retains significant influence after the sale, an investment in an associate
should be recognised at its fair value on this date. The equity method should be used for the
last three months of the year. This means that Articat Co should recognise 40% of the profits
of Findler Co for the last three months of the year as its share of the profit of the associate.
This amount will be reduced for Articat Co's share of the additional depreciation arising from
the difference between the fair value of Findler's depreciable assets and their carrying
amounts.
由于 A 公司在出售后仍具有重大影响，因此应该在剩余的 3 个月按照权益法对于 F 公司
(Inv in A)进行会计处理。也就是说 A 公司有权利分享 F 公司今年最后三个月的 40%利润。
根据准则要求，在收购日当天如果联营企业净资产的 FV 大于账面价值，则也需要进行
公允价值调整，同时调整完毕后续对于利润表的影响，需要确认进 Share of profit of
associate 中(准则原文截图如下)。
The sale of the property by Articat Co
The sale of the property by Articat Co is a downstream transaction with its associate, Findler
Co.The proceeds of $1.5 million should be removed from revenue as the property should
have been derecognised instead. Losses on downstream transactions with an associate are
usually recognised in the consolidated financial statements only to the extent of unrelated
investors' interests in the associate. However, the proceeds received by Articat Co of $1.5
million are said to be the fair value of the property sold. The carrying amount of the property
was $1.6 million. This means that the loss of $0.1 million is evidence of an impairment at the
point of sale. In such circumstances, Articat Co should recognise the loss of $0.1 million in
full in profit or loss.
A 公司卖 property 给 F 属于逆流交易，$1.5m 不应该确认为 revenue，同时 PPT 所有权转
移需要终止确认。资产发生的减值损失需由 A 公司 100%承担，中间的差值作为处置利
得确认，因此调整分录应为：
Dr Revenue 1.5m
Dr Admin exp 0.1
Cr PPE 1.6
3. Draft consolidated SOPL (Refer spreadsheet)
Requirements 解析:
(b) Using the pre-populated spreadsheet response option and exhibits 1 and 2, revise the
draft consolidated statement of profit or loss for the Articat Group for the year ended 30
June 20X5. (Ignore any tax implications arising from the adjustments. Calculation of the
profit attributable to the non-controlling interest is not required). (14 marks)
根据上述情况调整预编制报表。(忽略税的影响吗，归属于 NCI 的利润无需计算)

URP=30,000/125%*25%=6,000

stem_zh:
要求：计算收购日当天的商誉，A 公司应该如何根据 IAS36 号准则评估商誉是否有没有减
值，不需要计算。
Suggested answer:
(a)(i)Goodwill
The goodwill of Trailer Co should be calculated as follows:
$m
Cost of investment
– cash 45
– shares ((15m x 80%) /10x 1 x $5) 6
+ NCI at acquisition (20% x $40m) 8
- net assets at acquisition (40)
Goodwill at acquisition date 19
收购日当天商誉金额为 19m。
(a)(ii)Goodwill impairment
IFRS Accounting Standards require an annual test for goodwill impairment. An asset is
impaired if its carrying amount exceeds its recoverable amount. The RA is the higher of its
fair value less costs of disposal and its value in use. Value in use is calculated as the
discounted cash flows which are expected to be obtained from using the asset in the
business.
【商誉需要每年做减值测试，如何去做减值测试】
Goodwill cannot be sold separately and so has no fair value. It does not generate cash
flows for the business separately from other assets of the business. In order to test
goodwill arising from a business combination for impairment, it therefore must be
allocated to a cash-generating unit or units. This is the smallest group of assets which
generate independent cash flows.
【商誉没有交换价值和使用价值，没法单独做减值测试，需要引入 CGU 的概念，什么是
CGU】
IAS 36 Impairment of Assets requires goodwill to be allocated to the cash-generating units
which are expected to benefit from the synergies of the combination. The impairment test
will then take place by comparing the carrying amount of the cash-generating unit with the
recoverable amounts.
【将商誉分摊至单个单个 CGU，如何对 CGU 去做减值测试】
A complication will arise since the goodwill of Trailer Co was calculated by measuring the
non-controlling interest at acquisition at its proportionate share of the fair value of the
subsidiary’s identifiable net assets. This means that no goodwill has been attributed to the
non-controlling interest. This goodwill figure needs to be grossed up by 100/80 for the
purpose of conducting an impairment test.
【如果是 partial goodwill，那么在做减值测试的时候需要对 partial goodwill 进行 gross up】
2. Division Zed
The activities of Trailer Co differed significantly from the rest of the group. To enable the
group to focus upon its core activities, Auto Co decided to sell the majority of its shares in
Trailer Co.
T 公司的经营活动和集团中其他公司的经营活动大大不同，为了让集团集中精力发展核心业
务，A 公司已经决定卖掉所持有的 T 公司的绝大多数股票。【看到公司决定卖掉子公司就要
联想到是否满足划分为持有待售类别】
Until recently, Trailer Co consisted of three divisions: Tuc, Fos and Zed. On 30 September
20X5, a third-party, Wheel Co, agreed to purchase 90% of Auto Co's investment in Trailer
Co. The agreement stated the purchase would not go ahead until Auto Co had disposed of,
or closed down, division Zed. There was no obvious acquirer for the assets of Zed at this
time and it was uncertain how long this process would take. Consequently, Auto Co began
to sell the individual assets of Zed to different buyers. None of the individual disposals
were material to the group. On 31 March 20X6, unsold non-current assets were
abandoned or transferred to other divisions, outstanding liabilities were settled, and the
trade of Zed ceased. On this date, Auto Co and Wheel Co continued negotiations for the
sale of the investment in Trailer Co and finalised the agreement. Trailer Co met the criteria
to be classified as a disposal group held for sale.
直到最近，T 公司由三个分部构成： Tuc, Fos and Zed。在 20x5 年 9 月 30 号，W 公司要
购买我们 A 公司所持有的 T 公司的股份的 90%，即 T 公司 72%的股份。协议当中说了这次
收购要一直到 A 公司处置掉 division Zed 才开始。但是截止到现在没有人愿意买 Zed。A 公
司准备把 division Zed 的资产单个单个出售给不同的买方。这些单个单个的资产对于集团而
言均不重大。截止到年底没有出售的长期资产已经遗弃或者转给其他的 division，也清偿到
所有的债务，终止了 Zed 的交易。在年底，A 公司和 W 公司继续谈判关于 T 公司股份的出
售，T 公司满足持有待售的处置组。
Requirements:
(b) Using exhibit 2, and in accordance with IFRS 5 Non-current Assets Held for Sale and
Discontinued Operations:
 consider whether the reclassified as held for sale during the year ending 31 March
20X6; and
 discuss the impairment principles which should be applied on the reclassification of
Trailer Co as held for sale. Calculations are not required.(6 marks)
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:2023 Sept - Sample 2.pdf#1

stem_zh:
要求：根据 IFRS5 号准则思考，在年底 Zed 的资产是否应该划分为持有待售，讨论 IFRS5
号下的减值原则。
Suggested answer:
(b) Zed division
On disposal of the assets of the Zed division, it would be necessary to consider whether
any of the assets should be reclassified at 30 September 20X5 as held for sale in
accordance with IFRS 5 Non-current Assets Held for Sale and Discontinued Operations.
This would be required, for example, if they were being actively marketed at a reasonable
price and the sale was deemed to be highly probable. Since there was no obvious buyer
for the assets and it was uncertain how long the process would take, it is unlikely that
many, if any, of the assets would have met the criteria. Further evidence that the criteria
would probably not have been met is that some of the unsold non-current assets were
either transferred to the remaining divisions of Trailer Co or abandoned. Abandoned
assets cannot be classified as held for sale as their carrying amount is not being
recovered principally through a sale.
【主要围绕持有待售的定义和确认条件来进行分析】
Reclassification of Trailer Co as held for sale
Impairment must be considered both at the time of classification as held for sale and
subsequently.
【划分成为持有待售之前要做减值测试，划分成为持有待售之后也要做减值测试】
At the time of classification as held for sale:
 immediately prior to classifying an asset or disposal group as held for sale,
impairment is measured and recognised in accordance with the applicable IFRS
Accounting Standards; and
 any impairment loss is recognised in profit or loss unless the asset had been
measured at revalued amount under IAS16 Property, Plant and Equipment or IAS 38
Intangible Assets, in which case the impairment is treated as a revaluation decrease.
【划分为持有待售前要立即做一次减值测试，这个时候的减值按照 IAS36 号准则进行账务
处理，这属于课本准则当中的原话】
After classification as held for sale:
 an entity should calculate any impairment loss based on the difference between the
adjusted carrying amounts of the asset/disposal group and fair value less costs to sell;
and
 any impairment loss which arises by using the measurement principles in IFRS 5
must be recognised in profit or loss even for assets previously carried at revalued
amounts
【划分为持有待售之后做减值是按照 IFRS5 号准则的规定，比较 CV 和 FV-CTS，任何的减
值损失直接计入当期损益，这属于课本准则当中的原话】
3. Disposal of Trailer Co
Auto Co originally acquired 80% of the ordinary shares of Trailer Co, measuring
non-controlling interests using the proportionate share of net assets method. The net
assets of Trailer Co have been included on a line-by-line basis in the draft consolidated
statement of financial position. Once the trade of Zed had ceased on 31 March 20X6, Auto
Co and Wheel Co continued negotiations for the sale of the investment in Trailer Co. At
this date, Trailer Co met the criteria to be classified as a disposal group held for sale.
The carrying amount of the net assets of the remaining parts of the business of Trailer Co,
a cash-generating unit, on 31 March 20X6 were as follows:
A 公司原先收购的是 T 公司 80%的股份，按照 proportionate method 核算 NCI 的价值，T
公司的净资产与集团的净资产一行一行合并进了合并资产负债表。处置掉 division Zed 之后
T 公司剩余组成部分的账面价值如下：
$`millions
PPE 51.8
Intangible asset 14.6
Inventory 7.1
T/R 3.3
Cash and cash equivalent 1.6
Long-term borrowings -18.5
T/P -2.3
Total 57.6
The intangibles do not include the goodwill on acquisition of Trailer Co which had a
carrying amount of $8 million as at 31 March 20X6. None of the goodwill related to Zed as
it had ceased to trade.
【T 公司划分为 disposal group held for sale 之前要立马去做一次减值测试，即比较 T 公司
的 CV 和 RA，CV=100% net asset+100% goodwill】
The fair value of the net assets of Trailer Co and transaction costs for the sale of the
shares were $51.5 million and $1 million respectively at 31 March 20X6.
【RA=FV-CTS =51.5-1=50.5】
Negotiations on the sale between Auto Co and Wheel Co delayed the sale of the shares
until 30 June 20X6. Tuc and Fos continued to trade up to 30 June 20X6 and earned profits
of $1.5 million from 1 April 20X6 until 30 June 20X6.
A 公司和 W 公司之间的谈判一直持续到了 x6 年 6 月 30 号，从 4 月 1 号到 6 月 30 号这一

stem_zh:
Q2
题干分析：
The accountant of Abby Co is concerned about the disclosure of trading that has occurred
between Abby Co and a company that a director of Abby Co is associated with. This director is
also attempting to influence how the accountant should account for two issues associated with
the consolidated .
1.Related party transactions 关联方交易
The accountant has discovered that the finance director of Abby has purchased goods from a
company, Arwight, which the director jointly owns with his wife and the accountant believes that
this purchase should be disclosed. However, the director refuses to disclose the transaction as in
his opinion it is an‘arm’s length’ transaction. He feels that if the transaction is disclosed, it will be
harmful to business and feels that the information asymmetry caused by such non-disclosure is
irrelevant as most entities undertake related party transactions without disclosing them. Similarly,
the director felt that competitive harm would occur if disclosure of operating segment profit or
loss was made. As a result, the entity only disclosed a measure of total assets and total liabilities
for each reportable segment.
When preparing the financial statements for the recent year end, the accountant noticed that
Arwight has not paid an invoice for several million dollars and it is significantly overdue for
payment. It appears that the entity has liquidity problems and it is unlikely that Arwight will pay.
The accountant believes that a loss allowance for trade receivables is required. The finance
director has refused to make such an allowance and has told the accountant that the issue must
not be discussed with anyone within the trade because of possible repercussions for the credit
worthiness of Arwight.
会计师发现，Abby 的财务总监从 Arwight 公司购买了商品，Arwight 公司是该董事与其妻子
共同拥有的公司，会计师认为该购买行为应予以披露。然而，董事拒绝披露该交易，因为他
认为这是一笔“公平交易”。他认为，如果交易被披露，将对业务有害，并认为这种不披露导
致的信息不对称是无关紧要的，因为大多数企业在进行关联方交易时都没有披露。同样的，
该董事认为，如果披露经营分部的利润或亏损，将造成竞争损害。因此，该实体仅披露了每
个可报告分部的总资产和总负债。
在编制最近一个年末的财务报表时，会计师注意到 Arwight 没有支付数百万美元的发票，而
且已经严重逾期付款。该企业似乎存在流动性问题，Arwight 不太可能支付。会计师认为，
应收账款需要计提及减值准备。而财务总监拒绝这样做，并告诉会计师，由于可能会对
Arwight 的信用价值产生影响，因此不得与业内任何人讨论该问题。
分析：关联方关系的披露，披露内容，经营分部的判定，披露的内容，以及隐瞒关联方关系
的原因，怀疑，道德威胁
2.Subsidiary fair value adjustments 子公司公允价值的调整
Additionally, when completing the consolidated financial statements, the director has suggested
that there should be no positive fair value adjustments for a recently acquired subsidiary and has
stated that the accountant’s current position is dependent upon following these instructions. The
fair value of the subsidiary is $50 million above the carrying amount in the financial records. The
reason given for not fair valuing the subsidiary’s net assets is that goodwill is an arbitrary
calculation which is meaningless in the context of the performance evaluation of an entity
在完成合并财务报表时，董事建议对最近收购的子公司不进行正向公允价值调整，并声明会
计师的当前状况取决于以下指示。子公司的公允价值比财务记录中的账面价值高出 50m。
对子公司净资产进行不公平估值的原因是，Goodwill 是一种任意计算，在企业绩效评估中毫
无意义。
分析：商誉的计算，资产金额低估，PPE 估值技术，这样做的动机：对于报表以及财务指标
的影响
3.Goodwill impairment 商誉的减值
Finally, when preparing the annual impairment tests of goodwill arising on other subsidiaries, the
director has suggested that the accountant is flexible in the assumptions used in calculating
future expected cash flows, so that no impairment of goodwill arises and that the accountant
should use a discount rate which reflects risks for which future cash flows have been adjusted. He
has indicated that he will support a salary increase for the accountant if he follows his
suggestions.
在准备 Goodwill 对其他子公司的年度减值测试时，董事建议会计在计算未来预期现金流量
时采用灵活的假设，以便 Goodwill 不会发生减值，并且会计应使用反映未来现金流已调整
风险的折现率。他表示，如果听从他的建议，他将支持增加会计的工资。
分析：商誉的减值，折现率的选择，对报表的影响，动机，道德威胁
Requirement
explanation_zh:
Discuss the ethical and accounting implications of the above situations from the perspective of
the reporting accountant. (18 marks)
Professional marks will be awarded in question 2 for the application of ethical principles. (2 marks)
(20 marks)
题目要求：从会计师的角度讨论上述情况对职业道德和会计记账的影响。（18 分）
应用道德原则的专业分数。（2 分）（20 分）
解题思路：
首先问题的要求是从会计影响和职业道德的影响两个方面回答问题，所以三段话都要回答这
两个方面。同时在读题时就可以先找出不符合会计准则要求的做法，进行合理的分值分配。
关于会计做账问题，首先回答会计准则要求，再指出题目中不恰当的处理，接着站在会计师
的角度给出修改的建议，从题干分析来看，一共有五个问题需要讨论，分别为: 关联方交易
的披露，经营分部的报告披露，应收账款的减值，子公司公允价值的调整以及商誉的减值测
试。
关于职业道德的问题，首先指出题目职业道问题，再给出合理的建议去解决道德冲突。
答案解析：
The objective of IAS 24 Related Party Disclosures is to ensure that an entity's financial statements
contain the disclosures necessary to draw attention to the possibility that its financial position
and profit or loss may have been affected by the existence of related parties and by transactions
and outstanding balances with such parties. If there have been transactions between related
parties, there should be disclosure of the nature of the related party relationship as well as
information about the transactions and outstanding balances necessary for an understanding of
the potential effect of the relationship on the financial statements.
【准则 IAS 24 关联方交易披露的原因以及披露内容】
IAS 24 要求财务报表包含必要的披露，以说明其财务状况和损益可能受到关联方的存在以及
与关联方的勒索和未偿余额的影响。如果关联方之间存在交易，则应披露关联方关系的性质
以及了解该关系对财务报表的潜在影响所需的交易和未清余额信息。
The director is a member of the key management personnel of the reporting entity and the entity
from whom the goods were purchased is jointly controlled by that director. Therefore a related p
arty relationship exists and should be disclosed.
结合题意，董事是企业的关键管理人员之一，采购的企业由该董事共同控制。因此，存在关
联方关系，应予以披露。
IFRS 8 Operating Segments requires an entity to report financial and descriptive information
about its reportable segments. Reportable segments are operating segments or aggregations of
operating segments which meet specified criteria. IFRS 8 does not contain a 'competitive harm'
exemption and requires entities to disclose the financial information which is provided by the
chief operating decision maker (CODM). The management accounts reviewed by the CODM may
contain commercially sensitive information, and IFRS 8 might require that information to be
disclosed externally. Under IFRS 8, firms should provide financial segment disclosures which
enable investors to assess the different sources of risk and income as management does. This
sensitive information would also be available for competitors. The potential competitive harm
may encourage firms to withhold segment information. However, this is contrary to IFRS 8 which
requires information about the profit or loss for each reportable segment, including certain
specified revenues and expenses such as revenue from external customers and from transactions
with other segments, interest revenue and expense, depreciation and amortisation, income tax
expense or income and material non-cash items.
【准则 IAS 8 经营分部的定义以及准则要求的信息的披露】
IFRS 8 经营分部要求报告分部的财务和描述性信息。可报告分部是指符合规定标准的经营分
部或经营分部的集合。IFRS8 不包含“竞争损害”豁免，并要求各披露由首席运营决策者（CODM）
提供的财务信息。CODМ审查的管理账户可能包含商业敏感信息，IFRS 8 可能要求对外披露
该信息(确实以管理法编制的分布报告有利于报表使用使用者以以管理层的角度去看待的经
营情况，但也会披露企业的商业秘密，如企业的内部交易定价原则）。
公司应提供分部的财务信息，使投资者能够像管理层那样评估不同的风险和收入来源。竞争
对手也可以获得这些敏感信息。潜在的竞争危害可能会鼓励公司隐瞒分部信息。然而，这违
反了 IFRS 8 的相关规定，IFRS 8 要求提供每个可报告分部的损益信息，包括特定的收入和费
用，如来自外部客户和与其他分部交易的收入、利息收入和费用、折旧和摊销、所得税费用
或收入和重大非现金项目。
Areas such as impairments of financial assets often involve the application of professional
judgement. The director may have received additional information, which has allowed him to
form a different opinion to that of the accountant. The matter should be discussed with the
director to ascertain why no provision is required and to ask whether there is additional
information available. However, suspicion is raised by the fact that the accountant has been told
not to discuss the matter. Whilst there may be valid reasons for this, it appears again that the
related party relationship is affecting the judgement of the director.
【不进行信息披露的原因】
金融资产减值等领域通常涉及专业判断，董事可能收到了额外的信息，这使得他形成了与会
计师不同的意见。这些事件应与会计师进行讨论，以确定原因并询问是否有其他可用信息。
然而，会计师被告知不要讨论此事，这很值得怀疑，即使可能有合理的理由，但关联方关系
是影响了董事的判断。
Positive fair value adjustments increase the assets of the acquired company and as such reduce
the goodwill recognised on consolidation. However, the majority of positive fair value
adjustments usually relate to items of property, plant and equipment. As a result, extra
depreciation based on the net fair value adjustment reduces the post-acquisition profits of the
subsidiary. This has a negative impact on important financial performance measures such as EPS.
公允价值向上调整增加了被收购公司的资产，因此减少了合并时确认的商誉。然而，大多数
公允价值调整(公允价值大于账面价值）通常与 PPE 相关。因此，基于净公允价值调整的额
外折旧会减少子公司的收购后利润，这对 EPS 等重要财务绩效指标有负面影响。
Therefore, by reducing fair value adjustments it will improve the apparent performance of new
acquisitions and the consolidated financial statements. Accountants should act ethically and
ignore undue pressure to undertake creative accounting in preparing such adjustments. Guidance
such as IFRS 3 Business Combinations and IFRS 13 Fair Value Measurement should be used in
preparing adjustments and professional valuers should be engaged where necessary.
因此，通过减少公允价值调整，将改善新收购和合并财务报表的表面绩效。会计人员在准备
此类调整时，应采取合乎道德的行为，忽视这种创造性会计的压力。编制报表要依据准则要
求，必要时应聘请专业估价师。
In measuring value in use, the discount rate used should be the pre-tax rate which refiects
current market assessments of the time value of money and the risks specific to the asset. The
discount rate should not reflect risks for which future cash flows have been adjusted and should
equal the rate of return which investors would require if they were to choose an investment
which would generate cash flows equivalent to those expected from the asset. By reducing the
impairment, it would have a positive impact on the financial statements.
【折现率的选择】【不计提减值的动机：对报表和财务指标的影响】
在衡量使用价值时，使用的贴现率应为税前利率，反映当前市场对货币时间价值和资产特定
风险的评估。折现率不应反映未来现金流已调整的风险，而应等于投资者在选择产生与资产
预期现金流相等的现金流的投资时所需的回报率。通过减少减值，将对财务报表产生积极影
响。
The offer of a salary increase is inappropriate and no action should be taken until the situation is
clarified. Inappropriate financial reporting raises issues and risks for those involved and others
associated with the company. Whilst financial reporting involves judgement, it would appear that
this situation is related to judgement.
加薪的是不适当的，在情况澄清之前，不应采取任何行动。不当的财务报告会给相关人员和
与公司相关的其他人员带来问题和风险。虽然财务报告涉及判断，但这种情况似乎与判断有
关。
The director is trying to coerce the accountant into acting unethically. IAS 1 Presentation of
Financial Statements requires all standards to be applied if fair presentation is to be obtained.
Directors cannot choose which standards they do or do not apply.
董事会有几起潜在的违反会计准则和不道德行为。主任试图强迫会计做出不道德的行为。
IAS1 如果要获得公允列报，就必须采用所有标准。董事不能选择他们适用或不适用的标准
It is important that accountants identify issues of unethical practice and act appropriately in
accordance with ACCA's Codes of Ethics. The accountant should discuss the matters with the
director. The technical issues should be explained and the risks of non-compliance explained to
the director. If the director refuses to comply with accounting standards, then it would be
appropriate to discuss the matter with others affected such as other directors and seek
会计师应识别不道德行为的问题，并根据 ACCA 的道德规范采取适当行动。会计应与主管讨
论此事。应向董事解释技术问题和不合规风险。如果董事拒绝遵守会计准则，则宜与其他受
Legal advice should be considered if necessary. An accountant who comes under pressure from
senior colleagues to make inappropriate valuations and disclosures should discuss the matter
with the person suggesting this. The discussion should try to confirm the facts and the reporting
guidance which needs to be followed.
如有必要，应考虑法律建议。如果一名会计师受到高级同事的压力，要求其做出不适当的估
值和披露，那么他应该与提出这一建议的人讨论这一问题。讨论应尽量确认需要遵循的事实
和报告指南。
Financial reporting does involve judgement but the cases above seem to be more than just
differences in opinion. The accountant should keep a record of conversations and actions and
discuss the matters with others affected by the decision, such as directors. Additionally,
resignation should be considered if the matters cannot be satisfactorily resolved.
财务报告确实涉及主观判断，但上述案例似乎不仅仅是意见分歧。会师应记录对话和行动，
与受决策影响的其他人（如董事）讨论相关事宜。此外，如果无法令人满意地解决问题，应
考虑辞职。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:2023改版后 Specimen 1真题解析.pdf#1

stem_zh:
Q3
a(i)
题干
explanation_zh:
Africant Co owns several farms and also owns a division which sells agricultural vehicles. It is
considering selling this agricultural retail division and wishes to measure the fair value of the
inventory of vehicles for the purpose of the sale. The directors are also considering several
options to value some farmland that it owns. Finally, the directors are preparing for a
forthcoming meeting with shareholders and thinking about questions they might be asked
regarding a mixed measurement approach to valuing assets and liabilities.
1.Vehicles
Three markets currently exist for the vehicles. Africant Co has transacted regularly in all three
markets.At 31 December 20X5, Africant Co wishes to find the fair value of 150 new vehicles,
which are identical. The current volume and prices in the three markets are as follows:
Africant Co wishes to value the vehicles at $39,100 per vehicle as these are the highest net
proceeds per vehicle, and Europe is the largest market for Africant Co’s product.The directors of
Africant Co are unclear about the principles behind the valuation of the new vehicles and also
whether their valuation would be acceptable under IFRS 13 Fair Value Measurement.
这个公司主要是来卖一些农业用车的。现在打算卖掉某个 agricultural retail division 某个农业
零售区域，所以希望能够对于它库存里的一批农用车估计一下他们的公允价值，因为想要把
他们卖掉。对于现有的三个市场，欧洲市场、亚洲市场以及非洲市场，选择哪个市场作为公
允价值就看有无信息能指出哪个市场是主要市场，如果没有就看哪个市场是最有利市场。
At 21 Dec 20x5，A 公司想知道它手里 150 台 vehicles 的公允价值是多少。后面这个 identical
的意思是说，这 150 台 vehicles 其实是一模一样的，所以只要找到其中一台的单价就可以代
表全部了。
Requirement 解析：
Advise Africant Co on the appropriate accounting treatment of (i) its vehicle. (8 marks)
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:2023改版后 Specimen 1真题解析.pdf#2

stem_zh:
要求基于 IFRS 13 准则的要求，分析下新车估值背后的原理是什么，并进行相应的计量。答
题主要围绕 FV 定义，如何选择市场，如何计量公允价值并结合案例有效分析即可。
答案
explanation_zh:
答题逻辑：
1/ 先放相关概念：什么是公允价值，什么是主要市场，什么是最有利市场。
2/ 一般是先找主要市场，主要市场找不到了退而求其次找最有利市场。
3/ Apply to case，在这道题目里面 Africant 这个公司错误地在主要市场存在的情况下，直接
选择了最有利市场；并且最终选定的 fair value 的价值也是错误的，因为将交易手续费的部
分也包含进去了。
4/ 给出正确的做法，并进行相应的计算。
那么三个不同的市场，每台车的 sale price 售价，historical volume 历史上 A 公司在对应市场
上卖了多少台，total volume 这个市场一共卖掉的车的数量，以及 transaction costs 交易手续
费，transport cost 运输费这些基本的信息都给到了。如果是需要去选择 principal market 主要
市场的话，主要是看 total volume 这个市场上的总的一个交易量。
A 公司希望按照每台 39100 的价格去估值，这个 39100 是每台车能够获得的 highest net
proceeds 净利得，同时它说 Europe 欧洲市场是承载 A 公司产品最大量的一个市场。所以实
际上它是用 40000-500 的交易手续费-400 的运输费得来 39100 的。那么通过比较，其实也能
够看出来 selling price – transaction cost – transport cost 得到的金额最大的也就是这个 Europe
欧洲市场，因此可以确定的是，欧洲市场相当于最有利的市场。
但是当要去决定公允价值的时候，首先应该要看 principal market 主要市场，也就是成交量
最大的，如果这个市场获得不了的话，退而求其次才能够选择最有利市场。所以这里直接跳
过主要市场选择最有利市场，肯定是不合适的。这里的主要市场是总交易量达到 75w 的亚
洲市场，所以说亚洲市场才是主要市场，接着需要确定一下亚洲市场的公允价值，公允价值
是用 selling price – transport cost 运输费，所以真正的公允价值=38000-700=37300 每台，一
共是打算卖 150 台，因此最终的总价值=37300*150。
a(ii)
题干解析：
2.Land
Africant Co uses the revaluation model for its non-current assets. Africant Co has several plots of
farmland which are unproductive. The company directors of Africant Co believe that the land
would have more value if it were used for residential purposes. There are several potential
purchasers for the land but planning permission has not yet been granted for use of the land for
residential purposes. However, preliminary enquiries with the regulatory authorities seem to
indicate that planning permission may be granted. Additionally, the government has recently
indicated that more agricultural land should be used for residential purposes.
Africant 采用重估模型对于非流动资产进行后续计量。A 公司有几块没有生产力的农田，公
司认为如果这块土地被用于住宅用途，它将更有价值。土地有几个潜在买家，但尚未授予将
该土地用于住宅用途的规划许可。但是，监管当局的初步调查表明，规划许可应该是可以授
予的。除此之外，政府最近还表示，更多的农业用地应该用于住宅用途。（看到 non-financial
asset 以及用途转变讨论的，基本都是与 highest and best use 最佳用途假设相关这个知识点
相关。
Africant Co has also been approached to sell the land for commercial development at a higher
price than that for residential purposes and understands that fair value measurement of a
non-financial asset takes into account a market perspective. The directors of Africant Co are
unclear about what is meant by a ‘market perspective’ and how to measure the fair value of the
land in its financial statement.
A 公司也在试图以高于住宅用途的价格出售土地用于商业开发，并且明白非金融资产的公允
价值计量是以市场为导向的。
Requirement 解析：
Advise Africant Co on the appropriate accounting treatment of (ii) its land with reference to
relevant International Financial Reporting Standards.
A 公司就如何在其财务报表中衡量土地的公允价值提出建议。其实就是结合 highest and best
use 进行分析。
答案解析：
A fair value measurement of a non-financial asset takes into account a market participant’s ability
to generate economic benefits by using the asset in its highest and best use or by selling it to
another market participant who would use the asset in its highest and best use. The maximum
value of a non-financial asset may arise from its use in combination with other assets or by itself.
IFRS 13 requires the entity to consider uses which are physically possible, legally permissible and
financially feasible. The use must not be legally prohibited. For example, if the land is protected
in some way by law and a change of law is required, then it cannot be the highest and best use of
the land. In this case, Africant’s land for residential development would only require approval
from the regulatory authority and as that approval seems to be possible, then this alternative use
could be deemed to be legally permissible. Market participants would consider the probability,
extent and timing of the approval which may be required in assessing whether a change in the
legal use of the non-financial asset could be obtained.
结合准则进行分析，首先放准则，点出非金融资产的公允价值计量需要考虑 highest and best
use 最佳使用用途假设，并放出最佳用途假设的定义：非金融资产的公允价值计量考虑了市
场参与者通过将资产用于最高和最佳用途或将其出售给将资产用于最高和最佳用途的另一
个市场参与者来产生经济利益的能力。
非金融资产的最大价值可能产生于与其他资产结合使用或本身使用。IFRS 13 要求考虑物理
上的可能性、法律上的不禁止和经济上的可行性。（点出最佳用途假设的确认条件）在法律
上的不禁止，例如，如果土地以某种方式受到法律的保护，那么它就不可能是对土地的最高
和最佳的利用。结合案例，A 公司用于住宅开发的土地只需要得到监管当局的批准，而且这
种批准是可能的，那么这种替代用途可以被认为是法律允许的。市场参与者将考虑在评估是
否可以获得非金融资产的合法使用方面的变化时，可能需要获得的批准的可能性、范围和时
间。
Africant would need to have sufficient evidence to support its assumption about the potential for
an alternative use, particularly in light of IFRS 13’s presumption that the highest and best use is
an asset’s current use. Africant’s belief that planning permission was possible is unlikely to be
