sufficient evidence that the change of use is legally permissible. However, the fact the
government has indicated that more agricultural land should be released for residential purposes
may provide additional evidence as to the likelihood that the land being measured should be
based upon residential value. Africant would need to prove that market participants would
consider residential use of the land to be legally permissible. Provided there is sufficient evidence
to support these assertions, alternative uses, for example, commercial development which would
enable market participants to maximise value, should be considered, but a search for potential
alternative uses need not be exhaustive. In addition, any costs to transform the land, for example,
obtaining planning permission or converting the land to its alternative use, and profit
expectations from a market participant’s perspective should also be considered in the fair value
measurement.
继续结合案例进行分析。A 公司需要有足够的证据来证明替代用途符合最佳用途假设。公司
认为规划许可是可能的，但这并不足以证明法律上是允许的。不过政府已经表示，应该将更
多的农业用地用于住宅用途，这其实可以提供额外的证据，证明土地的适用应该以住宅为基
础。A 公司需要证明，市场参与者也同样认为土地的住宅使用用途，是法律上允许的。如果
有足够的证据支持，就可以考虑土地的替代用途，比如能够使市场参与者的价值最大化的商
业开发。此外，改造土地的任何成本，比如获得规划许可或将土地改造为替代用途的转换成
本，以及市场参与者预期期待的利益，都应该在计量公允价值的时候考虑进去。
If there are multiple types of market participants who would use the asset differently, these
alternative scenarios must be considered before concluding on the asset’s highest and best use. It
appears that Africant is not certain about what constitutes the highest and best use and
therefore IFRS 13’s presumption that the highest and best use is an asset’s current use appears to
be valid at this stage.
如果有多种类型的市场参与者会以不同的方式使用该资产的话，在考虑最佳用途假设的时候
需要将这些可能性考虑进去。案例中表示 A 公司似乎不确定什么构成了最佳用途，那么按
照 IFRS 13 的规定，如果这个资产的用途发生了改变，除非你有证据表明这个改变可以使得
资产得到物尽其用，否则的话可以假设这个资产现在的用途 current use 就是最佳使用用途。
(b)
题干解析：
3.Mixed measurement approach
Africant is about to hold its annual general meeting with shareholders and the directors wish to
prepare for any potential questions which may be raised at the meeting. There have been
discussions in the media over the fact that the most relevant measurement method should be
selected for each category of assets and liabilities. This ‘mixed measurement approach’ is used by
many entities when preparing financial statements. There have also been comments in the media
about the impact that measurement uncertainty and price volatility can have on the quality of
financial information.
Africant 公司即将东举行年度股东大会。媒体认为每一类资产和负债，都应该选择最相关的
计量方法。“混合计量方法”被广泛使用。媒体也有评论说，计量的不确定性和价格波动都会
对财务信息质量有影响。
Requirement 解析：
Discuss the impact which the above matters may have on the analysis of financial statements by
investors in Africant. (8 marks)
题目要求讨论一下，上述事项可能对 A 公司投资者进行财报分析产生哪些影响。(本题结合
不同计量基础的优缺点，考核计量方式组合问题，对考生概念性框架下相关知识点的掌握，
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:2023改版后 Specimen 1真题解析.pdf#3

stem_zh:
要求较高。）
答案
explanation_zh:
Some investors might argue in favour of a single measurement basis for all recognised assets and
liabilities as the resulting totals and subtotals can have little meaning if different measurement
methods are used. Similarly, profit or loss may lack relevance if it reflects a combination of flows
based on historical cost and of value changes for items measured on a current value basis.
有一些投资者可能会支持对所有已确认的资产和负债建立单一的计量基础，因为如果使用不
同的计量方法，所得出的总额和小计可能没有什么意义（投资者认为汇总的资产和负债没有
意义是因为对应的资产和负债计量口径不一致）。同样的，如果损益是基于历史成本法以及
当前价值的话，有可能会降低其相关性。
However, the majority of investors would tend to favour a mixed measurement approach,
whereby the most relevant measurement method is selected for each category of assets and
liabilities. This approach is consistent with how investors analyse financial statements. The
problems of mixed measurement are outweighed by the greater relevance achieved if the most
relevant measurement basis is used for each class of assets and liabilities. The mixed
measurement approach is reflected in recent standards; for example, IFRS 9 Financial
Instruments and IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers.Historical cost would not have
been relevant for all financial assets and has severe limitations for many liabilities; hence, the
only viable single measurement method would have been fair value.
但是大多数投资者还是倾向于采用混合计量方法，也就是每一类资产和负债选择最相关的计
量方法。这种方法与投资者分析财务报表的方式是一致的。如果对每一类资产和负债都使用
最相关的测量基础，那么其相关性就会更高。混合计量方法在准则当中的反映，比如 IFRS 9
金融工具和 IFRS 15 收入。历史成本不会与所有金融资产相关，并且使用历史成本法对很多
负债都有严重的局限性(例如,期权的出售方)；因此,(在这些情形下)唯一可行的单一计量方法
应该是公允价值。
IFRS 9 requires the use of cost in some cases and fair value in other cases, while IFRS 15
essentially applies cost allocation.The Conceptual Framework does not propose a single
measurement method for all assets and liabilities, and instead supports the continued use of a
mixed measurement approach.
IFRS 9 要求在某些情况下使用成本，在其他情况下使用公允价值,而 IFRS 15 本质上是按照成
本分配对价。概念性框架并没有为所有资产和负债提出一种单一的计量方法，而是支持继续
使用混合计量方法。
Most accounting measures of assets and liabilities are uncertain and require estimation. While
some measures of historical cost are straightforward as it is the amount paid or received, there
are many occasions when the measurement of cost canbe uncertain – particularly recoverable
cost, for which impairment and depreciation estimates are required. In a similar vein, while some
measures of fair value can be easily observed because of the availability of prices in an actively
traded market (a so-called ‘Level 1’ fair value), others inevitably rely on management estimates
and judgements (‘Level 2’ and ‘Level 3’).
大多数资产和负债的会计计量方法都是不确定的，需要进行会计估计。虽然一些历史成本的
计量相对是比较直接的，因为就是看支付或收到的金额，但在很多情况下，成本的计量可能
是不确定的——特别是可回收成本，需要考虑减值和折旧的影响。同样地，虽然一些公允价
值的指标很容易被获取到，因为当交易市场存在的时候，可以直接选取 level-1 层级的数据，
但其他的则需要依赖于管理层的估计和判断，参考 level 2 以及 3 层级的参数。
High measurement uncertainty might reduce the quality of information available to investors.
High price volatility may make analysing an investment in that entity more challenging. If a
relevant measure of an asset or liability value is volatile, this should not be hidden from investors.
To conceal its volatility would decrease the usefulness of the financial statements. Of course,
such volatile gains and losses do need to be clearly presented and disclosed, because their
predictive value may differ from that provided by other components of performance.
先回顾一下题目的最后一句话，即计量的不确定性和价格波动都会对财务信息质量有影响，
首先，要了解这两者不是同一个东西，高的价格波动并不是财务报告的问题，例如购买的期
权具有较高的价格波动本身就体现该资产的性质（高风险，高收益），那就应该披露体现出
来。当然估计的不确定性确实会降低投资者获得的信息质量。
较高的计量不确定性可能会降低投资者所能获得的信息的质量。高的价格波动可能会使投资
决策分析更具有挑战性。如果资产或负债价值的相关计量标准不稳定，则不应该向投资者隐
瞒。隐瞒价值波动性将会降低财务报表的有用性。因此价值波动所带来的损益，需要清楚地
呈现和披露给投资者，因为它们的预测价值可能不同于其他业绩组成部分所提供的预测价值。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:2023改版后 Specimen 1真题解析.pdf#4

stem_zh:
Q4
题干
explanation_zh:
The directors of Rationale Co are reviewing the published financial statements of the group which
include performance measures, such as ‘underlying profit’. This information is additional to that
which is required by International Financial Reporting Statements (IFRS).
1. Financial statement extract – this exhibit provides a detailed analysis of Rationale Co’s costs
for the years ended 31 December 20X5 and 20X6 and explains the directors’ use of “underlying
profit”.
1. Financial statement extract
The following is an extract of information to be found in the financial statements.
The directors use ‘underlying profit’ to comment on its financial performance. Underlying profit
is a measure normally based on earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortisation
(EBITDA). However, the effects of events which are not part of the usual business activity are also
excluded when evaluating performance. The following items were additionally excluded from net
profit to arrive at ‘underlying’ profit. In 20X6, the Rationale Co had to write off a property due to
subsidence and the insurance recovery for this property was recorded but not approved until
20X7, when the company’s insurer concluded that the claim was valid. In 20X6, Rationale Co
considered issuing loan notes to finance an asset purchase, however, the purchase did not go
ahead. Rationale Co incurred costs associated with the potential issue and so these costs were
expensed as part of net profit before taxation. The directors of Rationale Co felt that the
share-based payment was not a cash expense and that the value of the options was subjective.
Therefore, the directors wished to exclude the item from ‘underlying profit’. Similarly, they
wished to exclude restructuring charges incurred in the year, and impairments of acquired
intangible assets.
该企业试图使用 EBITDA 来展示业绩表现，关于披露 additional performance measure，根据 IAS
1，额外的信息应当是能够使得报表使用者对于企业的财务状况和财务表现有更好的认识。
但要注意的是管理层使用 APM 展现的额外信息受否通过消除负面的影响导致过于乐观或者
有误导报表使用者的可能。而在这个案例显然管理层是有意图使用 underlying profit 是为了
掩盖今年糟糕的业绩表现。
Requirement 解析：
(a) (i) Discuss the possible concerns where an entity may wish to disclose information that is in
addition to that required by IFRS standards in its financial statements and whether the
Conceptual Framework for Financial Reporting (2018) helps in determining the boundaries for
disclosure. (8 marks)
讨论企业可能希望在其财务报表中披露除国际财务报告准则要求的信息之外的信息的情况，
以及《财务报告概念框架(2018)》是否有助于确定披露的界限。
答案解析：
There is no specific guidance on information which is not required by an IFRS being disclosed in
financial statements. IFRS requires an entity to disclose additional information which is relevant
to an understanding of the entity's financial position and financial performance.
对于额外的信息，如果能够对报表使用者对于企业的财务状况以及财务表现有更好的理解，
即该信息是相关的，呢么 IFRS 是要求披露的，只是目前并没有对额外信息的披露有一个详
细的指引。
A company may disclose additional information where it is felt that an entity's performance may
not be apparent from accounts prepared under IFRS. A single standardised set of accounting
practices can never be sufficient information to understand an entity's position or performance.
Additional information can help users understand management's view of what is important to
the entity and the nature of management's decision.
这里想表达的意思是，毕竟每一个企业的情况都是不一样的，用一套单一的会计标准是不太
可能了解一个企业的状况或业绩的充分信息。因此，附加信息可以帮助使用者了解管理层对
实体的重要性和管理层决策的性质的看法。
There are concerns relating to the disclosure of additional information. Such information may not
readily be derived or reconciled back to financial statements. There is also dificulty comparing
information across periods and between entities because of the lack of standardised approaches.
Also the presentation of additional information may be inconsistent with that defined or
specified in IFRS and the entity may present an excessively optimistic picture of an entity's
financial performance. Non-IFRS information may make it difficult to identify the complete set of
financial statements, including whether the information is audited or not. Additionally, the
information may be given undue prominence or credibility merely because of its location within
the financial statements. Non-IFRS financial information should be clearly labelled in a way that
distinguishes it from the corresponding IFRS financial information. Any term used to describe the
information should be appropriate having regard to the nature of the information. The term or
label should not cause confusion with IFRS information and should accurately describe the
measure.
对于披露额外的信息有几点是值得担忧的。首先，这些信息可能没办法跟财务报表对照。以
及由于缺少标准的方法，是很难比较同个企业各个时期所披露的额外信息，也不好在同一时
期比不同企业的额外信息。此外，这些额外信息可能和 IFRS 所规定的准则不一致，管理层
容易过度乐观去披露企业的财务表现。非国际财务报告准则信息可能使识别整套财务报表变
得困难，包括这些信息是否经过审计。此外，这些信息可能仅仅因为其在财务报表中的位置
而被给予不适当的突出或可信性。非国际财务报告准则财务信息应以一种与相应的国际财务
报告准则财务信息区分开来的方式清晰地标记。考虑到信息的性质，用于描述信息的任何术
语都应该是适当的。术语或标签不应与国际财务报告准则信息混淆，并应准确描述计量。
Effective disclosure can enhance the usefulness of financial information. However, the
Conceptual Framework states that disclosure decisions should be based on whether the benefits
provided to users of financial statements by presenting or disclosing particular information are
likely to justify the costs of providing and using that information.
对于额外信息，由于缺少具体的指引和规则。因此根据概念框架，有效的披露额外信息应该
是能够提高财务信息的有用性且提供该信息报表使用者所获得的利益应当要超过披露该信
息所需要的成本。
Requirement 解析：
(ii) Discuss the use and the limitations of the proposed calculation of ‘underlying profit’by
Rationale.
Note: Your answer should include a comparative calculation of underlying profit for the years
ended 31 December 20X5 and 20X6. (9 marks)
R 公司用 underlying profit 的好处和缺点。
答案解析：
The directors of Rationale are utilising a controversial figure for evaluating a company’s earnings.
Depreciation and amortisation are non-cash expenses related to assets which have already been
purchased and they are expenses which are subject to judgement or estimates based on
experience and projections. The company, by using EBITDA, is attempting to show operating cash
flow since the non-cash expenses are added back.
R 公司的董事们正在利用一个有争议的数字来评估他们公司的收益。折旧和摊销是与已购买
的资产有关的非现金费用，它们是根据经验和预测进行判断或估计的费用。该公司通过使用
EBITDA 试图显示经营活动现金流，因为他们将这些非现金的费用全部加回去了。
However, EBITDA can also be misused and manipulated. It can be argued that because the
estimation of depreciation, amortisation and other non-cash items is vulnerable to judgement
error, the profit figure can be distorted but by focusing on profits before these elements are
deducted, a truer estimation of cash flow can be given. However, the substitution of EBITDA for
conventional profit fails to take into account the need for investment in fixed capital items.
然而，EBITDA 也可以被滥用和操纵。我们可以这样认为，由于对折旧、摊销和其他非现金
项目的估计容易出现判断错误，利润数字可能会被扭曲，但通过关注在扣除这些要素之前的
利润，可以估计出更真实的现金流量。然而，用 EBITDA 替代常规利润没有考虑到固定资本
项目的需要。
There can be an argument for excluding non-recurring items from the net profit figure. Therefore,
it is understandable that the deductions for the impairment of property, the insurance recovery
and the debt issue costs are made to arrive at ‘underlying profit’. However, IAS 1 Presentation of
Financial Statements states ‘An entity shall present additional line items, headings and subtotals
in the statements presenting profit and loss and other comprehensive income when such
presentation is relevant to an understanding of the entity’s financial performance.’ This
paragraph should not be used to justify presentation of underlying, adjusted and pre-exceptional
measures of performance on the face of the income statement. The measures proposed are
entity specific and could obscure performance and poor management.
从净利润中排除非经常性项目可能存在争议。可以理解的是，扣除固定资产的减值损失、保
险补偿和债券发行成本都是为了获得“基本利润”。虽然，IAS1 号准则中说了：一个公司应在
综合收益表中列示其他的行项目、标题和小计，只要这些陈述与财报预期使用者了解该公司
的财务业绩相关。”但是，这一内容不应被用来证明在利润表上列示基本的、经调整的和预
先例外的业绩指标的合理性。这些业绩衡量方式对于该公司而言是特定的，可能会达到掩盖
业绩和管理不善的目的。
Share-based compensation may not represent cash but if an entity chooses to pay equity to an
employee, that affects the value of equity, no matter what form that payment is in and therefore
it should be charged as employee compensation. It is an outlay in the form of equity. There is
therefore little justification in excluding this expense from net profit. Restructuring charges are a
feature of an entity’s business and they can be volatile. They should not be excluded from net
profit because they are part of corporate life. Severance costs and legal fees are not non-cash
items. Impairments of acquired intangible assets usually reflect a weaker outlook for an acquired
business than was expected at the time of the acquisition, and could be considered to be
non-recurring. However, the impairment charges are a useful way of holding management
accountable for its acquisitions. In this case, it seems as though Rationale has not purchased
wisely in 20X6.
股份支付虽然不是真正的现金流，但是如果公司选择用权益结算，那么分录是借方费用贷方
权益，是会影响权益的金额的。而且不管是用权益结算还是用现金结算都是借方费用，作为
公司的一项费用。因此，将其从净利润中排除没有什么理由说得通。重组其实是一个公司整
个生命周期中不可避免的一个环节，而且重组相关的支出也是具有波动性的。既然它们是企
业生命周期中的一个组成部分，那么将重组相关的支出排除在净利润之外就不应该。遣散费
和律师费也不是非现金项目。企业并购时获得的无形资产后续出现了减值，这通常反映了被
收购业务的前景低于收购时的预期，这一情况可以被认为是非经常性的。然而，减值损失应
该由管理层负责任，因为这代表他们收购的时候预期的不准确。在这种情况下，R 公司在 20X6
的收购似乎并不是明智的。
It appears as though Rationale wishes to disguise a weak performance in 20X6 by adding back a
series of expense items. EBITDA, although reduced significantly from 20X5, is now a positive
figure and there is an underlying profit created as opposed to a loss. However, users will still be
faced with a significant decline in profit whichever measure is disclosed by Rationale. The logic
for the increase in profit is flawed in many cases but there is a lack of authoritative guidance in
the area. Many companies adopt non-financial measures without articulating the relationship
between the measures and the financial statements.
R 公司希望通过在净利润的基础上加回去一些费用，然后扭亏为盈去掩盖 20X6 糟糕的业绩。
和 20x5 年相比，20x6 年的 EBITDA 虽然大幅下降，但至少是一个正数，而不像净利润是一
个负数，即净损失。虽然是计算出来的结果最终是个正数，但是和去年相比还是大幅度下降。
从净亏损 5m 到最终的 underlying profit35m。这其中的有些调整其实是缺乏逻辑的，也缺乏
权威的指导。许多公司采用非财务衡量指标来说明公司的情况，但没有明确说明这些计量方
法与财务报表之间的关系。
Requirement 解析：
(b) The directors of Rationale are confused over the nature of a reclassification adjustment and
understand that the IASB has issued pronouncements on the subject.
Required: Discuss, with examples, the nature of a reclassification adjustment and the arguments
for and against allowing reclassification of items to profit or loss.
Note: A brief reference should be made in your answer to the Conceptual Framework for
Financial Reporting (2018). (8 marks)
本题问的是考官文章中的 reclassification adjustment 这一问题，即有的交易计入 OCI 中之后
可以从 OCI 中转入 PL 有的交易则不可以转。
答案解析：
Reclassification adjustments are amounts recycled to profit or loss in the current period which
were recognised in OCI in the current or previous periods.所谓 Reclassification adjustments 就是
有些计入 OCI 当中的金额可以转入 PL An example of items recognised in OCI which may be
reclassified to profit or loss are foreign currency gains on the disposal of a foreign operation and
realised gains or losses on cash flow hedges. 比如 IAS21 号准则中所说的海外子公司外币报表
折算时产生的汇兑损益是要计入 OCI 但是在将海外子公司处置掉的时候原先计入 OCI 的金额
需要转入 PL 去影响 group profit or loss on disposal；再比如现金流套期当中有效套期的部分
产生的时候计入 OCI，在交易发生的时候可以从 OCI 中转入 PL。Those items which may not be
reclassified are changes in a revaluation surplus under IAS 16 Property, Plant and Equipment, and
actuarial gains and losses on a defined benefit plan under IAS 19 Employee Benefits. 但是有些
准则中规定原先计入 OCI 当中的金额后续不可以转入 PL，比如固定资产的重估增值发生时
计入 OCI，在固定资产处置时是不能从 RS
转入 PL，再比如 IAS19 号准则养老金计算当中 remeasurement component 发生时是要计入
OCI 的后续也是不可以转入 PL。
There are several arguments for and against reclassification.有些人支持转，有些人不支持转 If
reclassification ceased, then there would be no need to define profit or loss, or any other total or
subtotal in profit or loss, and any presentation decisions can be left to specific IFRSs. It is argued
that reclassification protects the integrity of profit or loss and provides users with relevant
information about a transaction which occurred in the period. Additionally, it can improve
comparability where IFRS permits similar items to be recognised in either profit or loss or OCI.
支持转的人认为：将 OCI 转入 PL 这种重新分类保护了损益的完整性，并向用户为当期发生
的交易提供了相关的信息。此外，它也提高可比性。
Those against reclassification argue that the recycled amounts add to the complexity of financial
reporting, may lead to earnings management and the reclassification adjustments may not meet
the definitions of income or expense in the period as the change in the asset or liability may have
occurred in a previous period.
那些反对重新分类的人认为，从 OCI 当中结转进 PL 的金额增加了财务报告的复杂性，可能
导致利润金额不准确和且该调整可能不符合收入或费用的定义，因为资产或负债的变化可能
发生在前一个时期，譬如你持有的按照 FVTOCI 进行核算的债权性质的金融资产，去年持有
的过程中产生的增值咱们去年计入了 OCI，去年调整的资产，结果你在今年将其从 OCI 转入
PL 这就不匹配。
The lack of a consistent basis for determining how items should be presented has led to an
inconsistent use of OCI in IFRS. Opinions vary but there is a feeling that OCI has become a home
for anything controversial because of a lack of clear definition of what should be included in the
statement. Many users are thought to ignore OCI, as the changes reported are not caused by the
operating flows used for predictive purposes.
由于本身哪些项目要计入 OCI 就具有争议，缺乏一致性。这导致 OCI 在 IFRS 中的使用不一
致。现阶段人们的做法似乎是，一旦一个项目计入 PL 具有争议就认为将其计入 OCI，这种
做法其实本身就不具有说服性。
The Conceptual Framework presumes that income and expenses included in OCl in one period
will be reclassified in a future period. Reclassification should occur in the period when it would
enhance the relevance of the statement of profit or loss. The presumption can be rebutted if
there is no clear basis for identifying the period in which the reclassification would enhance the
relevance of the information in the statement of profit or loss.
现在概念框架推定有些进入 OCI 的 income 和 expense 是会在未来重分类到 PL，如果是可以
提高 SOFP 的相关性。但这个推定是可以被质疑的，因为确实没有一个明确的依据去重分类
到 PL。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:2023改版后 Specimen 1真题解析.pdf#5

stem_zh:
Q1
Hill Co is the parent company of a group. All entities in the group have a financial year end of
30 September 20X6.
H 公司是集团的母公司，会计年度的年底是 20x6 年的 9 月 30 号。
1. Disposal of Doyle Co
On 1 October 20X4, Hill Co purchased 60% of the ordinary shares of Doyle Co. At this date,
the fair value of Doyle Co’s identifiable net assets was $510 million.
20x4 年 10 月 1 号，H 公司购买了 D 公司 60%的股份，在收购日当天子公司净资产的公
允价值是$510m。
The non-controlling interest at acquisition was measured at its fair value of $215 million.
Goodwill arising on the acquisition of Doyle Co was $50 million and had not been impaired
prior to the disposal date.
收购日当天少数股东权益的公允价值是 215m，商誉是 50m，一直到处置日商誉都没有
出现减值损失。
On 1 April 20X6, Hill Co disposed of a 20% holding in the shares of Doyle Co for cash
consideration of $140 million, losing control. At this date, the net assets of Doyle Co,
excluding goodwill, were carried in the consolidated financial statements at $590 million.
20x6 年 4 月 1 号，H 公司卖掉了 20%的 G 公司的股份，之前持有 60%的股份，卖掉 20%，
还剩 40%的持股比例。20%的股份出售价格为 140m，在当天也失去了控制。在这一天，
D 公司净资产的在合并报表上的账面价值是 590m。
From 1 April 20X6, Hill Co has the ability to appoint two of the six members of Doyle Co’s
board of directors. The fair value of Hill Co’s 40% shareholding was $300 million at that date.
Doyle Co reported a profit for the year ended 30 September 20X6 of $123 million.
从 20x6 年 4 月 1 号起，H 公司只有能力任命 D 公司董事会 6 个成员当中的 2 个。当天 H
公司所持的 D 公司的 40%的股份的公允价值是 300m。D 公司今年的税后利润是 123m。
2. Further information
The group’s financial controller has produced draft extracts of the consolidated statement of
cash flows and has not yet considered the impact of the disposal of Doyle Co (exhibit 1).
There are also some errors. All other consolidated financial statements have been correctly
prepared. The draft extracts have been included in the spreadsheet response option.
集团的财务负责人有编制出如下的合并现金流量表，但是在编表的过程中没有考虑卖掉
所持 D 公司的股份对现流表的影响。而且如下的合并现流表当中有一些错误，需要考生
们去更正。
The carrying amount of the net assets of Doyle Co on 1 April 20X6 were as follows:
20x6 年 4 月 1 号，D 公司的净资产在合并报表当中的账面价值如下：
$m
Property,plant and equipment 311.9
Inventories 200.5
Trade and other receivables 181.3
Cash and cash equivalents 20.9
Trade and other payables (124.6)
Total 590
The statement of cash flows figures for inventories, trade and other receivables, trade and
other payables and property, plant and equipment have been calculated by considering only
the movement in the balances between the consolidated statement of financial position
from 30 September 20X5 to 30 September 20X6.
合并现流表当中的应收账款，应付账款，存货，固定资产的数据都是直接用期末数和期
初数之间相减得到的，即没有考虑处置子公司对其的影响。
Hill Co exchanged property, plant and equipment with one of its suppliers on 1 November
20X5. It is estimated that the fair value of both assets exchanged was $50 million although
the carrying amount of the asset transferred by Hill Co was $45 million at the date of transfer.
There were no other disposals of non-current assets by the group during the year other than
the disposal of Doyle Co. The group also acquired property, plant and equipment during the
year for cash. The depreciation charge of the group for the year ended 30 September 20X6
was $775.1 million.
H 公司在 20x5 年 11 月 1 号，通过以物易物的方式获得了一个固定资产，不管是换入资
产还是换出资产其 FV 都是 50m，换出资产的账面记账是 45m。集团今年有用现金采购
固定资产，今年固定资产的折旧金额为 775.1m。
3. Deferred tax asset
Hill Co has made a loss in the year ended 30 September 20X6 in its separate financial
statements, as well as in the previous two financial years. In the consolidated statement of
financial position, it proposes to recognise a deferred tax asset in respect of the
carry-forward of unused tax losses. These losses cannot be surrendered to other group
companies.
H 公司在今年出现了亏损，且之前的两个会计年度也是亏损。在合并资产负债表当中，
公司针对亏损确认了递延所得税资产，而且 H 公司出现的亏损不能给到集团当中其他的
公司去抵税。
On 30 September 20X6, Hill Co breached a covenant attached to a bank loan which is due for
repayment in 20X9. The loan agreement terms state that a breach in loan covenants entitles
the bank to demand immediate repayment of the loan. Hill Co and its subsidiaries do not
have sufficient liquid assets to repay the loan in full.
在 20x6 年 9 月 30 号，H 公司违背了贷款合同的契约，该贷款本身应该在 20x9 年去偿
还，但是贷款合同当中写着公司违约了那么银行就有权利要求企业立即还钱，而 H 公司
及其子公司没有足够多的资产去全额偿还贷款。
Hill Co has produced a business plan which forecasts significant improvement in its financial
situation over the next three years as a result of the launch of new products which are
currently being developed.
H 公司的商业计划书当中说公司未来三年的财务状况会有大大的改善，因为公司将要发
布一款新产品，虽然这款产品还处于研发过程当中。
4. Convertible bond issue
On 1 October 20X6, Hill Co issued a convertible bond at par value of $20 million. The bond is
redeemable for cash on 30 September 20X8 at par. Bondholders can instead opt for
conversion in the form of a fixed number of shares in Hill Co. Interest on the bond is payable
at a rate of 4% a year in arrears. The interest rate on similar debt without a conversion
option is 10%.
在 20x6 年 10 月 1 号，H 公司发行了面值为 20m 的可转债，债券的到期日为 20x8 年 9
月 30 号，到期持有人可以选择按照面值拿钱回去，也可以选择将其转换成为固定数量
的股票。可转债每年年底支付利息，票面利率为 4%，实际利率为 10%。
Discount factors:
Year Discount rate Discount rate
4% 10%
1 0.952 0.909
2 0.907 0.826
5. Draft consolidated SOCF
The draft extracts have been replicated in the spreadsheet response option.
Draft extracts from the consolidated statement of cash flows for the Hill group for the year
ended 30 September 20X6:
Cash flows from operating activities: $m
Profit before taxation 1,581.7
Adjustments for:
Increase in inventories -842.4
Increase in trade and other receivables -379.2
Increase in trade and other payables 224.2
Cash generated from operations 584.3
Interest paid -126.8
Income taxes paid -424.6
Net cash from operating activities 32.9
Cash flows from investing activities
Purchase of property, plant and equipment -733.8
Net cash used in investing activities -733.8
Extracts from the consolidated statement of financial position of the Hill group:
30 September 20X6 30 September 20X5
$m $m
Property, plant and equipment 4,953.0 4,219.2
Requirements
explanation_zh:
(a) Using exhibit 1, explain, with suitable calculations, how the investment in Doyle Co
should be dealt with in the consolidated financial statements for the year ended 30
September 20X6. (7 marks)
对 D 公司的投资应该如何在 20x6 年的合并报表当中进行账务处理。
(b) Using the pre-populated spreadsheet response option and exhibits 1 and 2, update the
draft extracts of the consolidated statement of cash flows to adjust for the disposal of Doyle
Co shares and to amend for any errors/omissions made by the directors. (13 marks)
调整合并现金流量表。
(c) Using exhibit 3, evaluate the proposed treatment of Hill Co's deferred tax asset. (4 marks)
评估 H 公司针对亏损确认 DTA 的账务处理。
(d) Using exhibit 4, discuss, with suitable calculations, how the convertible bond should be
dealt with in the consolidated financial statements for the year ended 30 September 20X7. (6
marks)
可转债应该如何在 20x7 年的合并报表当中进行账务处理。
答案解析:
(a) Doyle Co
The sale of shares results in Hill Co losing control over Doyle Co. The goodwill, net assets and
non-controlling interest (NCI) of Doyle Co must be derecognised from the consolidated
statement of financial position. The difference between the proceeds from the disposal
(including the fair value of the shares retained) and these amounts will give rise to a $47
million profit on disposal. This is calculated as follows:
H 公司卖掉 20%的持股比例，那么就失去了对 D 公司的控制，那么 D 公司的商誉，少数
股东股权，净资产就不再需要合并到财务报表当中了，即需要从合并报表当中终止确认。
并且需要计算处置掉子公司产生的利得或损失。计算如下：
$m
Sales proceeds 140
FV of remaining 40% interest 300
减去：原先所持有的 60%的股份在处置日当天在合并报表上的账面价值=处置日当天的
Net asset+处置日当天的 goodwill-处置日当天的 NCI
Net asset at disposal date 590
Goodwill at disposal date 50
Less：NCI at disposal date Less: 247=215+(590-510)40%
Gain on disposal 47
After the disposal of shares, Hill Co owns 40% of Doyle Co's shares and has the ability to
appoint two of the six members of Doyle Co's board of directors. IAS 28 Investments in
Associates and Joint Ventures states that an associate is an entity over which an investor has
significant influence. Significant influence is presumed when the investor has a shareholding
of between 20 and 50%. Representation on the board of directors provides further evidence
that significant influence exists.
卖掉股份之后，H 公司只持有 D 公司 40%的持股比例，且有权利任命董事会当中的 2 名
成员，根据 IAS28 号准则，如果投资方能够对被投资方产生重大影响，那么被投资方即
为投资方的联营公司，所谓重大影响一般是持股比例在 20%到 50%之间。能够在董事会
当中派代表即表明重大影响存在。
Therefore, the remaining 40% shareholding in Doyle Co should be accounted for as an
associate. It will be initially recognised at its fair value of $300 million and accounted for
using the equity method. This means that the group recognises its share of the associate’s
profit for the year, which equates to $24·6 million ($123m x 6/12 x 40%). As at the reporting
date, the associate will be carried at $324·6 million ($300m + $24·6m) in the consolidated
statement of financial position.
剩下 40%的持股比例应该是看成对联营公司的投资，其初始入账金额为当中股票的公允
价值即 300m，后续按照权益法在合并报表当中进行账务处理。这意味着成为 H 公司的
联营公司之后 D 还是产生的利润 H 公司能够分享其中的 40%的比例。即 24.6m，这会使
得对联营公司的投资价值增长到 324.6m。
(b)The consolidated statement of cash flows:
W1 W2 W3 W4
Cash flows from operating activities: $m $m $m $m $m $m
Profit before taxation 1,581.7 1581.7
Adjustments for:
Depreciation 775.1 775.1
Profit on disposal of Doyle Co -47 -47
Profit on disposal of PPE -5 -5
Share of profit of associate -24.6 -24.6
Increase in inventories -842.4 -200.5 -1042.9
Increase in trade and other receivables -379.2 -181.3 -560.5
Increase in trade and other payables 224.2 124.6 348.8
Cash generated from operations 584.3 1025.6
Interest paid -126.8 -126.8
Income taxes paid -424.6 -424.6
Net cash from operating activities 32.9 474.2
Cash flows from investing activities
Purchase of property, plant and equipment -733.8 -1082 -1815.8
Proceeds on disposal of Doyle Co 119.1 119.1
Net cash used in investing activities -733.8 -1696.7
Working1：
Dr Cash 140 Investing activity 调增
Dr Investment in Associate 300
Dr NCI 247
Cr Goodwill 50
Cr Net asset of Doyle Co 590
Cr Gain on disposal 47 Operating activity 调减
卖掉所持有的 G 公司 20%的股份所收到的钱，计入合并现流表投资活动中。今年处置掉
了子公司 Doyle Co，D 公司处置日的 cash 余额为 20.9，卖掉子公司也会使得这部分现金
流出集团，所以合并现流表当中处置子公司收到的现金净额为 140-20.9=119.1；卖到子
公司所产生的的处置收益会使得 PBT 增加，但是不会影响经营活动现金流，所以要从
PBT 当中调减。
Working2：
Inventory B/F a
Movement of inventory 1042.9 Operating activity 调减
Disposal of Doyle Co (200.5)
Inventory C/F a + 842.4
存货总共增加 1042.9，所以在经营活动中要调减 1042.9，由于合并现流表中已经调减了
842.4，所以只需要再调减 200.5 就好了。
T/R B/F a
Movement of T/R 560.5 Operating activity 调减
Disposal of Doyle Co (181.3)
T/R C/F a + 379.2
应收账款总共增加 560.5，所以在经营活动中要调减 560.5，由于合并现流表中已经调减
了 379.2，所以只需要再调减 181.3 就好了。
T/P B/F a
Movement of T/P 348.8 Operating activity 调增
Disposal of Doyle Co (124.6)
T/P C/F a + 224.2
应付账款总共增加 348.8，所以在经营活动中要调减 560.5，由于合并现流表中已经调减
了 379.2，所以只需要再调增 124.6 就好了。
Working3：
Dr PPE exchange received 50
Cr Gain on disposal 5 Operating activity 调减
Cr PPE exchange given up 45
固定资产以物易物时产生的利得 5 会使得 PBT 增加，对经营活动现金流没有影响，所以
应该在经营活动现金流当中调减。
PPE B/F 4219.2
Cash purchased y = 1815.8 Investing activity 调减
PPE exchange received 50
PPE exchange given up 45
Depreciation expense 775.1 Operating activity 调增
Disposal of Doyle Co 311.9
PPE C/F 4953
现金购买固定资产使得现金总共减少 1815.8，题目已知现流表当中已经记录了 733.8，
所以只需要补记 1082 就好了。今年固定资产折旧费用金额为 775.1，会使得 PBT 减少，
对经营活动现金流没有影响，所以应该在经营活动现金流当中调增。
Working4：
从 20x6.4.1 到年底 20x6.9.30 这六个月对 D 公司的投资变成了联营公司，所以按照权益
法核算，产生 share of profit of associate 24.6，会使得 PBT 增加，对经营活动现金流没有
影响，所以应该在经营活动现金流当中调减。
(c) Deferred tax
According to IAS 12 Income Taxes, an entity should recognise a deferred tax asset in respect
of the carry-forward of unused tax losses to the extent that it is probable that future taxable
profit will be available against which the losses can be utilised. IAS 12 stresses that the
existence of unused losses is strong evidence that future taxable profit may not be available.
For this reason, convincing evidence is required about the existence of future taxable profits.
IAS 12 states that entities should consider whether the tax losses result from identifiable
causes which are unlikely to recur. Hill Co has now made losses in three consecutive financial
years, and therefore significant doubt exists about the likelihood of future profits being
generated.
根据 IAS12 号准则的规定，要想根据亏损确认递延所得税资产必须是公司在未来有足够
多的应税利润且该应税利润出现在亏损能够抵税的时效性之内。H 公司连续三年都是亏
损其实就是强有力的证据表明公司在未来出现应税利润的可能性没有达到很可能。而且
公司要分析为什么公司连续三年都是亏损的原因。
Although Hill Co is forecasting an improvement in its trading performance, this is a result of
new products which are currently under development. It will be difficult to reliably forecast
the performance of these products. More emphasis should be placed on the performance of
existing products and existing customers when assessing the likelihood of future trading
profits.
虽然 H 公司预测未来三年公司的财务状况能够得到很大的改善，因为公司将要发布一款
新产品，虽然这款产品还处于研发过程当中。说实话这个是很难说服大家的，因为这款
产品还处于研发当中，能够研发成功都是一个问号，而且就算研发成功了新产品发行到
市场当中也不是一定会得到市场的认可。
Finally, Hill Co breached a bank loan covenant and some uncertainty exists about its ability to
continue as a going concern. This, again, places doubts on the likelihood of future profits and
suggests that recognition of a deferred tax asset for unused tax losses would be
inappropriate.
最后，公司违反了贷款协议导致银行在年底有权利要求企业立即偿还贷款，而公司又没
有能力全额偿还，这就导致了持续经营问题的出现。
Based on the above, it would seem that Hill Co would be incorrect to recognise a deferred
tax asset in respect of its unused tax losses.
综上所述，公司针对亏损全额确认递延所得税资产是不正确的。
(d) Convertible bond
Hill Co has issued a compound instrument because the bond has characteristics of both a
financial liability (an obligation to repay cash) and equity (an obligation to issue a fixed
number of Hill Co’s own shares). IAS 32 Financial Instruments: Presentation specifies that
compound instruments must be split into:
H 公司发行了可转债，应该一部分列示到金融负债一部分列示到权益当中。其中列示到
金融负债当中的金额是未来所需要支付的利息和偿还的本金的现值的合计数，列示到权
益当中的金额为发行可转债收到的钱和列示到金融负债当中的金额之间的差额。
 a liability component (the obligation to repay cash) measured at the present value of
the cash repayments, discounted using the market rate on non-convertible bonds; and
 an equity component (the obligation to issue a fixed number of shares) measured as the
residual amount (difference cash received and the liability component).
The initial carrying amount of the liability will be measured at $17·9 million, calculated as
follows:
The equity component should be initially measured at $2.1 million ($20m – $17.9m). The
equity component will remain unchanged.
根据上述理论方法得知，列示到金融负债当中的金额为 17.9，列示到权益当中的金额为
2.1.
After initial recognition, the liability will be measured at amortised cost, as follows:
The finance cost to be recorded in profit or loss for the year will be $1·8 million.
The liability will have a carrying amount of $18·9 million as at the reporting date.
The interest paid of $0.8 million will be a cash outflow presented in the operating activities
section of the statement of cash flows.
列示到金融负债当中的金额 17.9 需要进行后续计量，即年底需要对其计息和付利息。
今年的利息费用为 1.8，今年实际支付的利息为 0.8.负债的期末数为 18.9.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:2023改版后 Specimen 2真题解析.pdf#1

stem_zh:
Q2
题干
explanation_zh:
Gustoso Co 是一家上市公司，生产一系列豪华食品。它根据 IFRS 编制财务报表。Gustoso
Co 的董事们每年都会收到一笔现金奖金，前提是报告的利润超过了预先设定的目标。
截至 20X7 年 12 月 31 日, Gustoso 公司的一年表现超出了目标，但 20X8 年的财务预测并
不乐观的。【因此对于 G 公司的管理层来说，就会产生 Self-interest 的道德威胁，管理层
为了拿到这笔现金奖励（因为和自身的利息相挂钩），因此往往会操纵报表以此来达到
设定的目标。】
Exhibit 1 Provisions 预计负债
A new accountant has recently started work at Gustoso Co. She noticed that the provisions
balance as at 31 December 20X7 is significantly higher than in previous years. She made
enquiries of the finance director, who explained that the increase was due to substantial
changes in food safety and hygiene laws which become effective during 20X8. As a result,
Gustoso Co must retrain a large proportion of its workforce. This retraining has not taken
place yet, so a provision has been recognised for the estimated cost of $2 million. The
finance director then told the accountant that such enquiries were a waste of time and
would not be considered favourably when deciding on her future pay rises and bonuses.
一名新会计最近开始在 Gustoso 公司工作。她注意到截至 20X7 年 12 月 31 日的预计负
债的余额明显高于前几年。她询问了财务总监，财务总监解释说，这一增长是由于食品
安全和卫生法的重大变化，这些法律将于 2008 年生效。因此，Gustoso Co 必须对其大
部分员工进行再培训。由于再培训尚未进行，因此先确认了一笔 200 万美元的预计负债。
财务总监随后告诉会计师，这种询问是浪费时间，在决定她未来的加薪和奖金时不会得
到有利的考虑。
Additionally in November 20X7, the board of directors discussed a potential restructure of
Gustoso Co. The restructuring plans included an analysis of long term cost savings but,
should the restructure take place, there will be significant short term costs which would be
necessary. These include professional fees, penalties for cancelling leases and also
redundancy costs for a number of employees. Even if the restructure did not take place
exactly as planned, alternative plans will need to be explored to ensure Gustoso Co
remains a going concern. The finance director has therefore included a restructuring
provision arguing that this is prudent. A final decision and announcements to staff and
lessors are likely to be made prior to the authorisation of the financial statements which is
expected in April 20X8.
此外，在 20X7 年 11 月，董事会讨论了 Gustoso 公司的潜在重组。重组计划包括对长期
成本节约的分析，但如果进行重组，将需要大量的短期成本，其中包括专业费用、取消
租赁的罚款以及一些员工的裁员费用。即使重组没有完全按计划进行，也需要探索其他
计划，以确保 Gustoso 公司哪个继续经营下去。因此，财务总监加入了一项重组条款，
认为这是谨慎的。最终决定可能会在财务报表发布之前向员工和出租人发布公告，预计
财务报表将于 20X8 年 4 月发布。
Exhibit 2 Wheat contract 小麦合同
Gustoso Co purchases significant quantities of wheat on which Gustoso Co records significant
profit margins. The price of wheat is volatile and so, on 1 November 20X7, Gustoso Co
entered into a contract with a supplier to purchase 500,000 bushels of wheat in June 20X8
for $5 a bushel. The contract can be settled net in cash. Gustoso Co has entered into similar
contracts in the past and has always taken delivery of the wheat. By 31 December 20X7 the
price of wheat had fallen. The finance director recorded a derivative liability of $0·5 million
on the statement of financial position and a loss of $0·5 million in the statement of profit or
loss. Wheat prices may rise again before June 20X8. The accountant is unsure if the current
accounting treatment is correct but feels uncomfortable approaching the finance director
again.
Gustoso 公司购买了大量小麦，并因此获得了可观的利润。小麦价格波动很大，因此，
2007 年 11 月 1 日，Gustoso 公司与一家供应商签订了一份合同，于 2008 年 6 月以每蒲
式耳 5 美元的价格购买 500,000 蒲式耳小麦。这份合同可以用现金结算。Gustoso 公司
过去也签订过类似的合同，并且总是接收小麦。到 2007 年 12 月 31 日，小麦价格已经
下跌。财务主任在财务状况表中记录了 50 万美元的衍生负债，在损益表中记录了 50 万
美元的损失。小麦价格可能在 6 月 20X8 之前再次上涨。会计师不确定当前的会计处理
是否正确，但又不愿意去找财务总监。
答案解析:
预计负债：
G 公司的会计人员发现 G 公司 20X7 年的预计负债金额会高于往年。而 G 公司管理层给
出的理由是，因为食品行业的安全条例/法律将会在 20X8 年发生重大的变革，因此公司
需要重新培训员工以便于遵循最新的法律/法规，因此在 20X7 年确认了预计负债金额 2m。
但是根据 IAS 37 的规定，员工培训的费用是不可以确认为预计负债的，因此该笔费用是
和企业未来的经营活动是有关联的。因此确认员工培训费用作为预计负债，会涉及到盈
余管理 (Earnings management)，即管理层通过设定预计负债来操纵企业当期的利润。并
且管理层告知给财务人员询问此类问题完全是浪费时间并且对未来自己的薪酬会产生
不利的影响，因此在此产生了 Intimidation 的道德威胁，威胁会计人员的薪资以让会计
人员屈服。
小麦合同：
G 公司通常会采购大量的小麦用于生产面包和意大利面，面包和意大利面会给 G 公司带
来很高的利润率。然而小麦的价值波动性很高，因此对于 G 公司来说如果原材料(小麦)
价格一旦上涨，势必很影响到本公司的利润率。因此为了降低小麦价格波动对于公司所
造成的影响，G 公司和供应商签订了远期合约，约定好在 20X 年 6 月从供应商那以$5/
蒲式耳的单件去购买 500,000 蒲式耳(一种计量单位，不用纠结)，该合约最终以现金做
结算。同时 G 公司也有一份购买小麦的合约。但是该公司签订该合约的目的是要预计购
买小麦为目的而签订的一份非金融项目的合约，因此针对于该笔合约(非金融项目的买
卖合同)实际符合”自用豁免”(own-use contract)的规定，因此该笔合约是不要作为衍生合
约做处理。
因此对于该类合同是不需要确认金融负债 0.5m，因此最终做处理的时候需要减少进入
负债，增加企业当期的利润 0.5m。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:2023改版后 Specimen 2真题解析.pdf#2

stem_zh:
Q3
背景
explanation_zh:
Calendar has a reporting date of 31 December 20X7. It prepares its financial statements in
accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards. Calendar develops biotech
products for pharmaceutical companies. These pharmaceutical companies then manufacture
and sell the products. Calendar receives stage payments during product development and a
share of royalties when the final product is sold to consumers. A new accountant has
recently joined Calendar’s finance department and has raised a number of queries.
Calendar 是一个制药公司，在产品的开发阶段收到阶段性的付款，在最终产品卖给消费
者的时候收到版税款。
题干解析：
(a) (i) During 20X6 Calendar acquired a development project through a business combination
and recognised it as an intangible asset. The commercial director decided that the return
made from the completion of this specific development project would be sub-optimal. As
such, in October 20X7, the project was sold to a competitor.
16 年的时候 Calendar 通过收购，获得了一个已经完成开发的项目。并把这个项目确认
为无形资产。但是因为认为这个项目的回报一般，所以十月份把他卖掉了
The gain arising on derecognition of the intangible asset was presented as revenue in the
financial statements for the year ended 31 December 20X7 on the grounds that
development of new products is one of Calendar’s ordinary activities. Calendar has made
two similar sales of development projects in the past, but none since 20X0.
处置无形资产的收益计入了主营业务收入，因为开发是公司的主营业务。Calendar 在 10
年前有过两笔相似的交易
Requirement 解析：
(a)The accountant requires advice about whether the accounting treatment of the sale of the
development project is correct. (6 marks)
问题解析：从财务的角度分析上述销售的会计处理是否正确
答案解析：
(a) Sale of intangible
IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers defines revenue as income arising from an
entity’s ordinary activities. Calendar’s ordinary activities do not involve selling development
projects. In fact, Calendar has made no such sales since 20X0. It would seem that Calendar’s
business model instead involves developing products for its customers, who then take over
its production, marketing and sale. Stage payments and royalties are the incomes which arise
from Calendar’s ordinary activities and should be treated as revenue.
从 IFRS 15 展开，什么是公司的主营业务。
Calendar 的主营业务并不是卖开发的项目，而且最近的两笔交易是在 10 年之前，所以
并不是主要的日常活动，stage payments and royalties 的收益才是主营业务收入。
Based on the above, Calendar is incorrect to recognise the gain as revenue. In fact, IAS 38
Intangible Assets explicitly prohibits the classification of a gain on derecognition of an
intangible asset as revenue.
因此，卖项目的收益不属于主营业务，不可以确认为收入，只能作为处置利得确认。
IAS 38 defines an intangible asset as an identifiable non-monetary asset without physical
substance. Intangible assets held for sale in the ordinary course of business are outside the
scope of IAS 38 and are instead accounted for in accordance with IAS 2 Inventories. The fact
that the development project was classified as an intangible asset upon initial recognition
further suggests that it was not held for sale in the ordinary course of business.
If the development was incorrectly categorised in the prior year financial statements as an
intangible asset, then, as per IAS 8 Accounting Policies, Changes in Accounting Estimates and
Errors, this should be corrected retrospectively.
However, based on the infrequency of such sales, it seems unlikely that the development
was misclassified.
讨论 project 作为无形资产是否是正确的分类，这里考官进行了延伸，考虑了这个项目
既然可以卖，是不是可以作为存货。然后根据销售的频率进行了否定，因此项目作为无
形资产是 ok 的。
题干解析：
(b) While searching for some invoices, the accountant found a contract which Calendar had
entered into on 1 January 20X7 with Diary, another entity. The contract allows Calendar to
use a specific aircraft owned by Diary for a period of three years. Calendar is required to
make annual payments.
根据发票，发现 17 年 1 月 1 日 Calendar 和 Diary 签了合同，Calendar 可以在三年里用 D
的飞机，每年付款。
On 1 January 20X7, costs were incurred negotiating the contract. The first annual payment
was made on 31 December 20X7. Both of these amounts have been expensed to the
statement of profit or loss.
支付的金额被费用化了
There are contractual restrictions concerning where the aircraft can fly. Subject to those
restrictions, Calendar determines where and when the aircraft will fly, and the cargo and
passengers which will be transported.
合同现值了飞行的区域，在这个前提下，C 可以决定什么时候飞去哪里，以及确定乘客
和货物
Diary is permitted to substitute the aircraft at any time during the three-year period for an
alternative model and must replace the aircraft if it is not working. Any substitute aircraft
must meet strict interior and exterior specifications outlined in the contract. There are
significant costs involved in outfitting an aircraft to meet Calendar’s specifications.
D 公司被允许在三年内替换掉飞机，如果飞行器不能正常飞行的话。替换的飞机都要严
格在合同里规定，装备飞机是 C 的成本
The accountant requires advice as to the correct accounting treatment of this contract. (9
marks)
Requirement 解析：
(b) The accountant requires advice as to the correct accounting treatment of the aircraft
contract. (9 marks)
问题解析：从财务的角度分析上述销售的会计处理是否正确
答案解析：
(b) Contract
IFRS 16 Leases says that a contract contains a lease if it conveys the right to control the use
of an identified asset for a period of time in exchange for consideration. When deciding if a
contract involves the right to control an asset, the customer must assess whether they have:
– The right to substantially all of the identified asset’s economic benefits;
– The right to direct the asset’s use.
Calendar has the right to use a specified aircraft for three years in exchange for annual
payments. Although Diary can substitute the aircraft for an alternative, the costs of doing so
would be prohibitive because of the strict specifications outlined in the contract.
Calendar appears to have control over the aircraft during the three-year period because no
other parties can use the aircraft during this time, and Calendar makes key decisions about
the aircraft’s destinations and the cargo and passengers which it transports. There are some
legal and contractual restrictions which limit the aircraft’s use. These protective rights define
the scope of Calendar’s right of use but do not prevent it from having the right to direct the
use of the aircraft.
Based on the above, the contract contains a lease. IFRS 16 permits exemptions for leases of
less than 12 months or leases of low value. However, this lease contract is for three years, so
is not short term, and is for a high value asset so a lease liability should have been
recognised at contract inception. The lease liability should equal the present value of the
payments yet to be made, using the discount rate implicit in the lease. A finance cost accrues
over the year, which is charged to profit or loss and added to the carrying amount of the
lease liability. The year-end cash payment should be removed from profit or loss and
deducted from the carrying amount of the liability.
A right-of-use asset should have been recognised at the contract inception at an amount
equal to the initial value of the lease liability plus the initial costs to Calendar of negotiating
the lease. The right-of-use asset should be depreciated over the lease term of three years
and so one year’s depreciation should be charged to profit or loss.
根据案例背景，考虑飞机应该归属于租赁准则。
先复述一下租赁准则的主要内容。再贴合案例进行展开。
虽然有限制性条款，但是 C 其实还是在合同范围能能够控制飞机的，能够主导他的使用
用途，并能够获得后续的利益流入。
租赁三年属于长期租赁，因此要记入租赁负债，使用权资产，而不应该费用化。接下来
就是用文字描述一下使用权资产的初始和后续计量
题干解析：
(c) The new accountant has been reviewing Calendar’s financial reporting processes. She has
recommended the following:
– All purchases of property, plant and equipment below $500 should be written off to profit
or loss. The accountant believes that this will significantly reduce the time and cost involved
in maintaining detailed financial records and producing the annual financial statements.
– A checklist should be used when finalising the annual financial statements to ensure that
all disclosure notes required by specific IFRS and IAS Standards are included.
新会计被建议按照如下的提议来处理账务
$500 以下的固定资产采购应该计入费用，会计认为这样做可以节约很多记账的时间。
准备检查清单，去确保所有的披露都按照准则的规定
Requirement 解析：
(c) With reference to the concept of materiality, discuss the acceptability of the above two
proposals.
Note: Your answer should refer to the Exposure Draft on the IFRS Practice Statement:
Application of Materiality to Financial Statements. (10 marks)
问题解析：结合重要性原则，讨论上述两种提议的可接受性
答案解析：
(c) Materiality
Calendar’s financial statements should help investors, lenders and other creditors to make
economic decisions about providing it with resources. An item is material if its omission or
misstatement might influence the economic decisions of the users of the financial
statements. Materiality is not a purely quantitative consideration; an item can be material if
it triggers non-compliance with laws and regulations, or bank covenants. Calendar should
consider materiality throughout the process of preparing its financial statements to ensure
that relevant information is not omitted, misstated or obscured.
答案解析：因为提议 1 想要简化会计处理，节约记账的成本和时间，因此其实这里涉及
到了重要性水平的判定。所以要说一下重要性水平的内容和定义。
Property, plant and equipment (PPE)
IAS 16 Property, Plant and Equipment states that expenditure on PPE should be recognised
as an asset and initially measured at the cost of purchase. Writing off such expenditure to
profit or loss is therefore not in accordance with IAS 16.
因为毕竟是在买固定资产，因此要结合 IAS 16 阐述准则对固定资产计量和确认的要求
According to IAS 8 Accounting Policies, Changes in Accounting Estimates and Errors, financial
statements do not comply with International Financial Reporting Standards if they contain
material errors, or errors made intentionally in order to present the entity’s financial
performance and position in a particular way. However, assuming that the aggregate impact
of writing off small PPE purchases to profit or loss is not material, then the financial
statements would still comply with International Financial Reporting Standards. Moreover,
this decision seems to be a practical expedient which will reduce the time and cost involved
in producing financial statements, rather than a decision made to achieve a particular
financial statement presentation.
If implemented, this policy must be regularly reassessed to ensure that PPE and the
statement of profit or loss are not materially misstated.
这里不需要定论，而是要充分展开讨论，从不同的角度讨论，企业需要对固定资产计量
的调整是否会造成信息使用者结论的变更，并且需要有充分的证据证明这样做可以节约
时间和成本，并不会影响投资者的决策，如果要调整，则应该追溯调整。
Disclosure notes
IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements states that application of IFRS Standards in an
entity’s financial statements will result in a fair presentation. As such, the use of a checklist
may help to ensure that all disclosure requirements within IFRS Standards are fulfilled.
However, IAS 1 and the Exposure Draft on the Practice Statement on Application of
Materiality to Financial Statements both specify that the disclosures required by IFRS
Standards are only required if the information presented is material.
The aim of disclosure notes is to further explain items included in the primary financial
statements as well as unrecognised items (such as contingent liabilities) and other events
which might influence the decisions of financial statement users (such as events after the
reporting period). As such, Calendar should exercise judgement about the disclosures which
it prepares, taking into account the information needs of its specific stakeholders. This is
because the disclosure of immaterial information clutters the financial statements and
makes relevant information harder to find.
Calendar may also need to disclose information in addition to that specified in IFRS
Standards if relevant to helping users understand its financial statements.
披露应该要遵守准则规定，因此 checklist 应该是有用的。但是也不需要一一记录，因为
IAS 1 也说了重要的信息才披露出来。因此在披露的时候，要考虑和报表主体信息的关
联性，且不需要事无巨细。其他对投资者有帮助的信息也需要披露
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:2023改版后 Specimen 2真题解析.pdf#3

stem_zh:
Q4
背景信息:
Kiki is a public limited entity. It designs and manufactures children’s toys. It has a reporting
date of 31 December 20X7 and prepares its financial statements in accordance with
International Financial Reporting Standards. The directors require advice about the following
situations.
Kiki 是一家上市公司。它主要的经营范围是设计和制造儿童玩具（指明了业务模式）。其
报告日期为 20X7 年 12 月 31 日，并根据 IFRS 编制财务报表。董事会要求就以下情况提
供建议。
Requirement
explanation_zh:
(a)The directors require advice about how revenue from the following sources should be
accounted for in the financial statements for the year ended 31 December 20X7:
(i) gift cards (6 marks)
(ii) royalty (6 marks)
针对 gift cards 给出相应的会计处理。
题干解析:
(a)(i) Kiki sells $50 gift cards. These can be used when purchasing any of Kiki’s products
through its website. The gift cards expire after 12 months. Based on significant past
experience, Kiki estimates that its customers will redeem 70% of the value of the gift card
and that 30% of the value will expire unused. Kiki has no requirement to remit any unused
funds to the customer when the gift card expires unused.
Kiki 出售 50 美元的礼品卡。当通过 Kiki 的网站购买 Kiki 的任何产品时，都可以使用这些
工具。礼品卡在 12 个月后到期。根据以往的重要经验，Kiki 估计，其客户将赎回礼品卡
价值的 70%，30%的价值将过期而不使用。Kiki 没有要求在礼品卡到期未使用时将任何
未使用的资金退给客户。董事们不确定如何对礼品卡进行会计处理。（6 分）
解题思路：
本题是对 IFRS 15 收入准则的具体应用。这个 case 主要考察顾客未行使的权利存在的情
况下收入的确认。当卖出去礼品卡的时候，履约义务 PO 并没有实现，所以不能确认收
入，只能当成是预收账款，同时确认合同负债。当顾客用礼品卡买商品的时候才可以确
认收入。Variable consideration 可变对价，可以可靠估计的时候，可以进行确认。估计
的方法可以根据 expected value 未来预期法也就是加权平均来计算，也可以用 most likely
amount，最有可能发生的金额来确认。但是在确认的时候要保证，极有可能累计确认的
额收入不会发生重大逆转。这个案例给到了以往的重要经验，30%的部分是顾客不会用，
Kiki 也不会退，所以每当顾客花了 35，其实就可以直接确认 50 的收入，这样可以可以
计算出来一个比例，就是每兑换 1，其实就可以确认 1.43 的收入。如果收入的确认再保
守一些的话，Kiki 也可以等到这些礼品卡过期的时候再去确认收入。
答案解析：
(a)(i) IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers says that revenue should be
recognised when or as a performance obligation is satisfied by transferring the promised
good or service to the customer. When a customer buys a gift card they are pre-paying for a
product. Revenue cannot be recognised because the entity has not yet transferred control
over an asset and so has not satisfied a performance obligation. As such, cash received in
respect of gift cards should be initially recognised as a contract liability.
根据 IFRS 15，当履约义务满足时，应确认收入。当客户购买礼品卡时，他们是在为产品
预付款。无法确认收入，企业尚未转移对资产的控制权，因此未履行履约义务。礼品卡
收到的现金最初应确认为合同负债。
IFRS 15 refers to a customer’s unexercised rights as breakage. The guidance for variable
consideration is followed when estimating breakage. In other words, the expected breakage
is included in the transaction price if it is highly probable that a significant reversal in the
amount of cumulative revenue recognised will not occur once the uncertainty is
subsequently resolved. This means that if the company is unable to reliably estimate the
breakage amount, then revenue for the unused portion of the gift card is recognised when
the likelihood of the customer exercising their remaining rights becomes remote.
However, if an entity is able to reliably estimate the breakage amount, then it recognises the
expected breakage amount as revenue in proportion to the pattern of rights exercised by the
customer. In relation to Kiki, it appears that the amount of breakage can be reliably
determined and so this should be recognised in revenue as the gift card is redeemed. For
every $1 redeemed, Kiki should recognise $1·43 ($1 x 100/70) in revenue.
IFRS 15 将客户未行使的权利称为 breakage。Breakage 可以参考可变对价。当不确定性得
以解决，也就是极有可能不会出现已确认累计收入金额的重大逆转，那么可以将预期未
行使将包含在交易价格中。这意味着，如果公司无法可靠地估计金额，则当客户行使其
剩余权利的可能性变得非常低的时候，才能确认礼品卡未使用部分的收入。然而，如果
企业能够可靠地估计金额，那么它会根据客户行使的权利比例将预期未行使的权利确认
为收入。就 Kiki 而言，breakage 似乎可以可靠地确定，因此在兑换礼品卡时应在收入中
予以确认。每兑换 1 美元，Kiki 应确认 1.43 美元（1x100/70 美元）的收入。
(a)(ii) 题干解析
Kiki’s best-selling range of toys is called Scarimon. In 20X6 Colour, another listed company,
entered into a contract with Kiki for the rights to use Scarimon characters and imagery in a
monthly comic book. The contract terms state that Colour must pay Kiki a royalty fee for
every issue of the comic book which is sold. Before signing the contract, Kiki determined that
Colour had a strong credit rating. Throughout 20X6, Colour provided Kiki with monthly sales
figures and paid all amounts due in the agreed-upon period. At the beginning of 20X7,
Colour experienced cash flow problems. These were expected to be short term. Colour made
nominal payments to Kiki in relation to comic sales for the first half of the year. At the
beginning of July 20X7, Colour lost access to credit facilities and several major customers.
Colour continued to sell Scarimon comics online and through specialist retailers but made no
further payments to Kiki.
Kiki 销量最好的玩具系列是 Scarimon。20X6 年，另一家上市公司 Colour 与 Kiki 签订了一
份合同，获得在每月一本漫画书中使用 Scarimon 角色和图像的权利。合同条款规定，
Colour 必须为出售的每期漫画向 Kiki 支付版税。在签署合同之前，Kiki 确定 Colour 的信
用评级很高。在整个 20X6 期间，Colour 向 Kiki 提供了每月的销售数据，并在商定的期
限内支付了所有到期款项。20X7 年初，Colour 遇到了现金流问题。预计这些都是短期的。
Colour 就今年上半年的漫画销售向 Kiki 支付了名义上的款项（名义款项可能时很低的金
额，但是还是有支付的）。20X7 年 7 月初，Colour 失去了信贷服务和几个主要客户。Colour
继续在网上和通过专业零售商销售 Scarimon 漫画，但没有向 Kiki 支付更多款项。董事不
确定如何在截至 20X7 年 12 月 31 日的财务报表中处理上述问题。（6 分）
答题思路：
题目明确的分了三个时间：在整个 20X6 期间， 20X7 年初， 20X7 年 7 月初，所以要立
足这三个时间判断能否确认收入，以及收入确认的金额。所以可以先把收入确认的五步
法写出来，然后代入案例来分别讨论。
整个 20X6 期间，满足收入确认条件，可以确认收入。
20X7 年上半年，虽然 Colour 信用情况不好，但是仍有支付款项，所以可以继续确认收
入，应收账款要作减值测试；
20X7 年下半年，根据 case，Colour 信用恶化，没有任何支付，很可能收不回款项，不
满足收入确认条件。
答案解析：
(a)(ii) According to IFRS 15, an entity should only account for revenue from a contract with a
customer when it meets the following criteria:
– The contract has been approved;
– Rights regarding goods and services can be identified;
– Payment terms can be identified;
– It is probable the seller will collect the consideration it is entitled to.
At inception of the agreement, Kiki and Colour entered an explicit contract which specified
payment terms and conditions. Moreover, Colour had a strong credit rating and so payment
was probable. As such, it would seem that the above criteria were met. IFRS 15 says that
revenue from a usage-based royalty should be recognised as the usage occurs. Whether a
contract with a customer meets the above criteria is only reassessed if there is a significant
change in facts and circumstances.
在协议开始时，Kiki 和 Colour 签订了一份明确的合同，其中规定了付款条款和条件。此
外，Colour 的信用评级很高，因此付款是可能的。因此，似乎符合上述标准。IFRS 15 表
示，基于使用的特许权使用费收入应在使用发生时予以确认。只有在事实和情况发生重
大变化时，才重新评估与客户签订的合同是否符合上述标准。
In July 20X7, Colour lost major customers and sources of finance. As such, it was no longer
probable that Kiki would collect the consideration it was entitled to. From July 20X7, no
further revenue from the contract should be recognised. According to IFRS 9 Financial
Instruments, non-payment is an indicator that the outstanding receivables are credit
impaired. A loss allowance should be recognised equivalent to the difference between the
gross carrying amount of the receivables and the present value of the expected future cash
flows receivable from Colour. Any increase or decrease in the loss allowance is charged to
profit or loss.
20X7 年 7 月，Colour 失去了主要客户和资金来源。因此，Kiki 不再可能收取其有权获得
的对价。自 20X7 年 7 月起，不应确认合同的进一步收入。根据 IFRS 9 Financial Instruments，
未付款是未偿应收款发生信用减值的一个指标。损失备抵应确认为应收账款账面总额与
Colour 预计未来应收现金流现值之间的差额。损失准备金的增加或减少均计入损益。
(b) 题干解析
As a result of rising property prices, Kiki purchased five buildings during the current period in
order to benefit from further capital appreciation. Kiki has never owned an investment
property before. In accordance with IAS 40 Investment Property, the directors are aware that
they can measure the buildings using either the fair value model or the cost model. However,
they are concerned about the impact that this choice will have on the analysis of Kiki’s
financial performance, position and cash flows by current and potential investors.
Requirement 解析:
(b)Discuss the potential impact which this choice in accounting policy will have on investors’
analysis of Kiki’s financial statements. Your answer should refer to key financial performance
ratios. (11 marks)
由于房地产价格上涨，Kiki 在本期购买了五栋建筑，以从进一步的资本增值中获益。Kiki
以前从未拥有过投资性房地产。根据 IAS 40，董事们知道，他们可以使用公允价值模型
或成本模型对建筑物进行计量。然而，他们担心这种选择将对当前和潜在投资者分析
Kiki 的财务业绩、头寸和现金流产生影响。要求：讨论这种会计政策选择对投资者分析
Kiki 财务报表的潜在影响。答案应参考关键财务绩效比率。（11 分）
解题思路：
要非常熟悉两种计量模式，写出来初始计量和后续计量的账务处理方法，以及对于报表
数据的影响。因为题目要求计算关键的财务比率，但是没有指明具体是哪些，所以可以
挑选一些投资者重点关注的，比如 operating profit margin, ROCE, gearing，以及 AFM 中
的一些指标 EBITDA 等，
答案解析：
(b) Investment properties
In accordance with IAS 40 Investment Properties, the buildings should be initially measured
at cost. If the cost model is applied, then the buildings will be recognised at cost less
accumulated depreciation and impairment losses. If the fair value model is applied, then the
buildings will be remeasured to fair value at each reporting date. Gains and losses on
remeasurement are recognised in the statement of profit or loss. No depreciation is charged.
准则运用：具体的计量方式
如果采用成本模型，则建筑物将按成本减去累计折旧和减值损失进行确认。如果采用公
允价值模型，则建筑物将在每个报告日重新计量为公允价值。重新计量的损益在损益表
中确认。不计提折旧费
Statement of financial position
Assuming that property prices rise, the fair value model will lead to an increase in reported
assets on the statement of financial position. In contrast, investment property measured
using the cost model is depreciated, which reduces its carrying amount. This means that the
fair value model may make Kiki appear more asset-rich.
假设房地产价格上涨，公允价值模型将导致财务状况表中报告的资产增加。相比之下，
使用成本模型计量的投资性房地产被折旧，从而减少其账面价值。这意味着公允价值模
型可能会让 Kiki 看起来更富有。
Some stakeholders may place importance on an entity’s asset base, as it can be used as
security for obtaining new finance. However, reporting higher assets can sometimes be
perceived negatively. For example, asset turnover ratios will deteriorate, and so Kiki may
appear less efficient.
一些利益相关者可能会重视企业的资产基础，因为它可以作为获得新资金的保障。然而，
报告较高的资产有时会被认为是负面的。例如，资产周转率将恶化，因此 Kiki 的效率可
能会降低。
If assets increase, then equity also increases. As such, the fair value model may lead to Kiki
reporting a more optimistic gearing ratio. This may reduce the perception of risk,
encouraging further investment.
如果资产增加，那么所有者权益也会增加。因此，公允价值模型可能会导致 Kiki 报告更
乐观的杠杆比率。这可能会降低风险感知，鼓励进一步投资。
Statement of profit or loss
In times of rising prices, the use of the fair value model will lead to gains being reported in
the statement of profit or loss. This will increase profits for the period. In contrast, the
depreciation charged under the cost model will reduce profits for the period. Therefore,
earnings per share, a key stock market and investor ratio, is likely to be higher if the fair value
model is adopted.
价格上涨时，使用公允价值模型将导致在损益表中报告收益。这将增加该期间的利润。
相反，根据成本模型收取的折旧将减少该期间的利润。因此，如果采用公允价值模型，
每股收益（一个关键的股票市场和投资者比率）可能会更高。
However, it should be noted that fair values are volatile. In some years, fair value gains may
be much larger than in other years. If property prices decline, then the fair value model will
result in losses. As such, reported profits are subject to more volatility if the fair value model
is adopted. This may increase stakeholders’ perception of risk. In contrast, the depreciation
expense recorded in accordance with the cost model will be much more predictable,
meaning that investors will be better able to predict Kiki’s future results.
然而，应注意的是，公允价值具有波动性。在某些年份，公允价值收益可能比其他年份
大得多。如果房地产价格下跌，那么公允价值模型将导致损失。因此，如果采用公允价
值模型，报告的利润会更加波动。这可能会增加利益相关者对风险的感知。相比之下，
根据成本模型记录的折旧费用将更加可预测，这意味着投资者将能够更好地预测 Kiki 的
未来业绩。
Many entities now present alternative performance measures (APMs), such as EBITDA
(earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortisation). Other entities present
‘underlying profit’ indicators, which strip out the impact of non-operating or non-recurring
gains or losses (such as the remeasurement of investment properties). Although the use of
APMs has been criticised, Kiki may consider them to be useful in helping investors to assess
underlying business performance through the eyes of management and to eliminate the
impact of certain accounting policy choices.
许多企业提出了其他绩效指标（APM），如息税折旧摊销前利润（息税折旧摊销前利润）。
尽管 APM 的使用受到了批评，Kiki 可能认为它们有助于帮助投资者从管理层的角度评估
潜在的业务绩效，并消除某些会计政策选择的影响。
Statement of cash flows
Accounting policy choices have no impact on the operating, investing or financing cash flows
reported in the statement of cash flows.
现金流量表：会计政策选择对现金流量表中报告的经营、投资或融资现金流量没有影响。
Disclosure
It should be noted that entities using the cost model for investment properties are required
to disclose the fair value. Such disclosures
披露应注意，使用投资性房地产成本模型的实体需要披露公允价值。此类披露使得在不
同模型下对投资性房地产进行会计核算的实体之间能够进行更为准确的比较。
