stem_zh:
1.企业编制财务报告的目标：提供有用的财务信息，根据这些信息做决策。
The objective of general purpose financial reporting is to provide financial
information about the reporting entity that is useful to existing and potential investors,
lenders and other creditors(users) in making decisions(buying or selling shares or debt
instruments) about providing resources to the entity.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#1

stem_zh:
2.Management's stewardship of the entity's resources
历年真题 2020-3-Q2(a)(ii)
Requirement：Discuss what is meant by good stewardship of a company and whether
the restructure and the recognition of a restructuring provision in the financial
statements are examples of good stewardship. (4 marks)
Answer：Stewardship is an ethical principle which embodies the responsible planning
and management of resources. The directors of Bagshot Co perform a stewardship
role in that they are appointed by the shareholders to manage Bagshot Co on their
behalf. The directors therefore assume responsibilities to protect the entity’s resources
from unfavourable effects of economic factors such as price and technological
changes and to ensure that Bagshot Co complies with all laws, regulations and
contractual obligations.
Group results have been disappointing in recent years although no specific causes
have been identified. It could be argued, therefore, that the restructure is acting in
good faith and reflecting good principles of stewardship. It is anticipated that
long-term shareholder value will be enhanced from the proposals.
A second factor of good stewardship is that it is important that investors, both existing
and potential, and lenders have reliable and accurate information about the entity’s
resources so that they can assess how efficiently and effectively the entity’s
management and governing board have discharged their responsibilities. It is
important therefore that the financial statements are transparent, objective and comply
fully with International Financial Reporting Standards. Mrs Dawes wants Bagshot Co
to include a restructuring provision as at 31 December 20X5 even though no
obligation arises. Whilst prudence is a guiding principle when dealing with issues of
uncertainty, excessive prudence cannot be justified. As a qualified member of ACCA,
it should be apparent to Mrs Dawes that no provision should be recognised and to
include one would be misleading to the stakeholders of Bagshot Co.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#2

stem_zh:
3.财务信息的质量特征
2 个基础+4 个加强型质量特征都要理解，其中重点掌握相关性、可比性、可理解
性。
基础 1: Relevance
Financial information is relevant if it is capable of making a difference in the
decisions made by users. Relevant information has predictive value, confirmatory
value or both. The more material the information, the more relevant it is.
基础 2: Faithful representation
Financial information must faithfully represent the substance of what it purports to
represent. A faithful representation is complete, neutral and free from error.
加强 1: Comparability
Compared with similar information about other entities and about the same entity for
another period.
Consistency helps to achieve Comparability. Consistency refers to the use of the same
methods for the same items.
加强 2: Verifiability
Different knowledgeable and independent observers could reach consensus.
加强 3: Timeliness
Having information available to decision-makers in time. The older the information
the less useful it is.
加强 4: Understandability
Classifying, characterizing and presenting information clearly and concisely. Complex
information should also be included.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#3

stem_zh:
4.Going concern 持续经营假设
Going concern assumption
The reporting entity is a going concern if it will continue in operation for the
foreseeable future. It is assumed that the entity has neither the intention nor the need
to liquidate to cease trading.
If going concern assumption is not satisfied, the financial statements may have to be
prepared on a “break-up basis”.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#4

stem_zh:
5.财务报表五要素的定义
Asset: A present economic resource(a right that has the potential to produce economic
benefits) controlled by the entity as a result of past events.
Liability: A present obligation of the entity to transfer an economic resource as a
result of past events.
Equity: Residual interest.
Income: Increases in assets, or decreases in liabilities.
Expense: Decreases in assets, or increases in liabilities.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#5

stem_zh:
6.Recognition 确认条件
(a) Relevant information about the elements of financial statements;
(b) A faithful representation of the elements of financial statements; and
(c) Benefits exceeds the cost of providing information.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#6

stem_zh:
7.Reclassification
Gains and losses that may be reclassified to profit and loss
- Gain / loss on remeasurement of financial assets (debt) classified as FVTOCI (IFRS
9)
- Gain / loss on derivatives classified as cash flow hedge (IFRS 9)
- Group foreign exchange translation differences (IAS 21)
Gains and loss that may not be reclassified to profit and loss
- Gain / loss on revaluation of property plant and equipment (IAS 16)
- Gain / loss on remeasurement of defined benefit pension schemes (IAS 19)
- Gain / loss on remeasurement of financial assets (equity) classified as FVTOCI
(IFRS 9)
IAS16 Property, plant, and equipment 固定资产
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#7

stem_zh:
1. Definition: Item for use in production, for rental to others, or for administrative
purposes, and are used for more than one period. (主要用途是为企业生产经营使用)
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#8

stem_zh:
2. Recognition:
- probable of future economic benefits
- cost can be measured reliably
 Large and complex assets should be broken down into composite parts and each
depreciated separately, if the parts have differing patterns of benefits and the cost
of each is significant.
 If subsequent expenditure can improve the economic benefits from the asset, the
expenditure should be capitalised (e.g. regular replacement/major overhaul).
 If it just maintains the economic benefits from the asset, the expenditure should
be treated as an expense (e.g. repairs/maintenance).
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#9

stem_zh: 3. Measurement:
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#10

stem_zh:
3.1 Initial measurement - At cost, which includes all costs necessary to bring the
asset to working condition for its intended use.
Cost includes:
• Purchase price of an asset (less any trade discount and import duty &
non-refundable purchase taxes )
• Directly attributable costs (直接归属成本) such as:
- Cost of site preparation
- Initial delivery and handling costs
- Installation and assembly costs
- Testing expense
- Professional fees for architects and engineers
• Borrowing costs 可被资本化的借贷成本
• Dismantling, removing and restoring cost 弃置成本
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#11

stem_zh:
3.2 Subsequent measurement
- Cost model 成 本 模 型 : Cost less accumulated depreciation and accumulated
impairment loss.
- Revaluation model 重 估 模 型 : Revalued amount(fair value) less subsequent
accumulated depreciation & accumulated impairment loss. (需要重估时，entire class
of assets 都要进行重估)
 Revaluation increases credit to OCI and accumulated in RS (will not be recycled
to profit or loss).
 Revaluation decreases debit to P/L as an expense
 先减值再增值: Revaluation increases are recognised in OCI and accumulated in
equity or profit or loss to the extent that it reverses a revaluation decrease of the
same asset previously recognised in profit or loss.
 先增值再减值: Revaluation decreases should be recognised in P/L as an expense
to the extent that it exceeds any amount previously credited to the revaluation
surplus relating to the same asset.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#12

stem_zh:
4. Depreciation
- Depreciation begins when the asset is available for use and continues until the asset
is derecognised, even if it is idle. (即使闲置也要折旧, 但土地不折旧)
- The depreciation method, residual value, and useful life should be reviewed annually
and revised prospectively if necessary, and the adjustments are accounted for as a
change in accounting estimates.
- Depreciation is charged to profit or loss, unless it is included in the carrying amount
of another asset.【e.g:折旧和生产直接相关，计入存货的成本】
- Depreciation must be allocated on a systematic basis, reflecting the pattern in which
the asset’s future economic benefits are expected to be consumed.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#13

stem_zh:
5. Derecognition
- An asset should be derecognised when disposal occurs, or if no further economic
benefits are expected from the asset's use or disposal.
- The gain or loss should be recognised(i.e. difference between the net disposal
proceeds and the carrying amount of the item).
- When a revalued asset is disposed of, any RS may be transferred directly to RE.
IAS 40 Investment Property 投资性房地产
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#14

stem_zh:
1. Definition
IAS 40 Investment Property relates to property (land or buildings) held (by the owner
or by the lessee as a right-of-use asset) to earn rentals (under operating lease) or for
capital appreciation or both.
 属于 IP:
- Land held for long-term capital appreciation
- Undetermined future use
- Buildings leased out under an operating lease
- Vacant buildings held to be leased out under an operating lease.
 不属于 IP:
- Property held for use in production or supply of goods or for administrative purposes;
- Property held for sale in the ordinary course of business or in the process of
construction of development for such sale;
- Owner-occupied property
- Property leased to another entity under a finance lease.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#15

stem_zh:
2. Recognition
- it is probable that the future economic benefits will flow to the entity, and
- the cost of the property can be reliably measured.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#16

stem_zh:
3. Measurement
初始计量: Initially measured at cost, including transaction costs.
后 续 计 量 : Entities can choose either the fair value model or the cost model and
apply that policy to all its investment properties.
- Cost model: 同 PPE 固定资产处理方式
- Fair value model:
 remeasured to fair value at each reporting date;
 with gains and losses recognised in profit or loss;
 not depreciated
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#17

stem_zh:
4. Accounting for transfers between categories
(1) Transfer from IP to PPE: Use the fair value at the date of change for the
subsequent accounting under IAS16. 使用转换日当日的公允价值用作固定资产的
后续计量。
(2) Transfer from PPE to IP:
 Normal accounting under IAS 16 (cost less depreciation) will have been applied
up to the date of the change.
 On adopting fair value, there is normally an increase in value.This is recognised
as other comprehensive income and credited to the revaluation surplus in equity
in accordance with IAS 16-Revaluation model. If the fair valuation causes a
decrease in value, then it should be charged to profits.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#18

stem_zh:
5. Derecognition
An investment property should be de-recognised on disposal or when the investment
property is permanently withdrawn from use and no future economic benefits are
expected from its disposal.
IAS 38 Intangible Assets 无形资产(常考，重难点)
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#19

stem_zh:
1.Definition
An intangible asset is defined as ‘an identifiable non-monetary asset without physical
substance.
The asset must be:
(a) Controlled by the entity as a result of events in the past; and
(b) Something from which the entity expects future economic benefits to flow.
 Identifiable: separable or arises from contractual or other legal rights.能够单独买
卖，有存在性，或是能够收到法律保护的权利。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#20

stem_zh:
2.Recognition
An intangible asset must be recognised only when:
 It meets the definition of IA; and
 An inflow of expected future economic benefits is probable; and
 Cost of the asset must be reliably measured.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#21

stem_zh:
3.Measurement
(a) Initial measurement: Measured at cost.
(b) Subsequent measurement:
- Cost model:
Original cost less accumulated amortization and i mpairment.
- Revaluation model:
Revalued amount (based on fair value) less any subsequent accumulated amortization
and impairment losses only if fair value can be determined. If there is no active
market, use cost model. 无形资产经常没有活跃市场，因此很难获得 FV。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#22

stem_zh:
4.Useful life
- Indefinite life 不 可 确 定 的 可 使 用 年 限 : no foreseeable limit to the period over
which the asset is expected to generate net cash inflows for the entity.
 No need to amortise
 Useful life should be reviewed at each reporting period (the change in the useful
life assessment from indefinite to finite should be accounted for as a change in an
accounting estimate)
 Tested for impairment annually
- Finite life 可确定的可使用年限: a limited period of benefit to the entity.
 Amortized on a systematic basis over that life (一般按照直线法摊销).
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#23

stem_zh:
5.Research and development expenditure
- Research costs: shall be expensed when it is incurred.
- Development costs: can be capitalised only if following conditions are met：
 Probable future economic benefits
 Intention to complete and use/sell
 Resources adequate to complete and use/sell
 Ability to use/sell
 Technical feasibility
 Expenditure can be measured reliably
If an entity cannot distinguish the research phase from the development phase, the
entity treats the expenditure for that project as if it were incurred in the research phase
only.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#24

stem_zh:
6.Derecognition
- When an asset is permanently withdrawn from use, or sold or scrapped, and no
future economic benefits are expected from its disposal.
- Gains or losses on disposal should be recognised in profit or loss.
IAS 36 Impairment of Assets 资产减值(常考，重难点)
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#25

stem_zh:
1. Indicators of impairment 先识别是否存在重大内外部减值迹象
 External sources of information
(a) A fall in the asset's market value that is more significant than would normally be
expected from passage of time over normal use.
(b) A significant change in the technological, market, legal or economic environment
of the business in which the assets are employed.
(c) An increase in market interest rates or market rates of return on investments likely
to affect the discount rate used in calculating value in use.
(d) The carrying amount of the entity's net assets being more than its market
capitalisation.
 Internal sources of information
(a) Obsolescence or physical damage.
(b) Significant adverse changes have taken place during the period, or are expected to
take place in the near future, the extent to which, or manner in which, an asset is used
or is expected to be used.
(c) Economic performance of an asset is, or will be, worse than expected (e.g.
operating losses or maintenance costs higher than budgeted).
 No indications of impairment 特殊项，无论是否存在减值迹象都必须每年进行
减值测试
Even if there are no indications of impairment, the following assets must always be
tested for impairment annually.
(a) An intangible asset with an indefinite useful life
(b) Goodwill acquired in a business combination
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#26

stem_zh:
2. Impairment test 出现重大减值迹象后，即需要进行减值测试
Impairment is determined by comparing the carrying amount of the asset with its
recoverable amount.
The recoverable amount of an asset should be measured as the higher of:
(a) the asset's fair value less costs to sell; and
(b) its value in use.
 Fair value is determined in accordance with IFRS 13
 Costs to sell are the direct added costs only (not existing costs or overhead) which
bring an asset into the condition for sale(e.g. legal cost, transaction taxes, costs of
removing the assets, selling commission, etc.).
 Value in use is calculated by estimating present value of future cash inflows and
outflows from the use of the asset and its ultimate disposal, and applying a
suitable discount rate to these cash flows. The cash flow projections should relate
to the asset’s current condition and should exclude expenditure to improve or
enhance it
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#27

stem_zh:
3. Recognition of impairment loss
1) Impairment loss should be recognized whenever recoverable amount is below
carrying amount.
2) Impairment loss is an expense in the income statement (unless it relates to a
revalued asset where the value changes are recognized directly in other
comprehensive income).
3) Adjust depreciation for future periods.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#28

stem_zh:
4. Reversal of impairment loss
1) The increased carrying amount due to reversal should not be more than what the
depreciated historical cost would have been if the impairment had not been
recognized.
2) Reversal of an impairment loss is recognized as income.
3) Adjust depreciation for future periods.
4) Reversal of an impairment loss for goodwill is prohibited.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#29

stem_zh: 5. Cash-Generating Units (CGU)
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#30

stem_zh:
5.1 Definition
A cash-generating unit is the smallest identifiable group of assets that generates cash
inflows that are largely independent of the cash inflows from others.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#31

stem_zh:
5.2 Allocation of impairment losses for a CGU
1) to any assets that are obviously damaged or destroyed
2) to any goodwill allocated to the unit
3) to the other assets of the unit on a pro-rata basis based on the carrying amount of
each asset in the unit
However, the carrying amount of an asset cannot be reduced below the highest of:
• fair value less costs to sell
• value in use
• Nil
IFRS 5 Non-current Assets held for sale and Discontinued
Operations 非流动资产持有待售以及终止经营
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#32

stem_zh:
1. Definition
A non-current asset or disposal group should be classified as ‘held for sale' if carrying
amount will be recovered primarily through a sale transaction rather than through
continuing use.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#33

stem_zh:
2. Classification
• The asset is available for immediate sale in its present condition
• The sale is highly probable
 For the sale to be highly probable, the following must apply：
• Management is committed to a plan to sell
• An active programme to locate a buyer is initiated
• Within 12 months of classification as held for sale
• The asset is being actively marketed for sale at a sales price reasonable in relation to
its fair value
• It is unlikely that plan will be significantly changed or withdrawn
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#34

stem_zh:
3. Measurement
- Before classification: the asset (or disposal group) is measured in accordance with
the applicable IFRS
- On classification: the asset (or disposal group) is measured at the lower of its
carrying amount and fair value less costs to sell.(impairment loss shall be recognised
and charged to profit or loss)
- After classification: not to be depreciated
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#35

stem_zh:
4. Discontinued operations
A discontinued operation is a component of an entity that either has been disposed of
or is classified as held for sale and:
(a) represents a separate major line of business or geographical area of operations, or
(b) is part of a single coordinated plan to dispose of a separate major line of business
or geographical area of operations, or
(c) is a subsidiary acquired exclusively with a view to resale
A component of an entity is one that has operations and cash flows that can be
clearly distinguished, operationally and for financial reporting purposes, from the rest
of the entity.
IAS 37 Provisions, contingent liabilities and contingent
assets 预计负债，或有负债与或有资产
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#36

stem_zh: 1. Provision
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#37

stem_zh:
1.1 Definition
Provision is a liability of uncertain timing or amount.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#38

stem_zh:
1.2 Recognition
An entity must recognize a provision if, and only if:
- A present obligation (legal or constructive) has arisen as a result of a past event;
- Outflow of economic benefits to settle the obligation is probable,;
- The amount can be estimated reliably.
* 考试重点集中在是否存在 obligation，无论法定的还是推定的，是否是无法避
免的义务。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#39

stem_zh:
1.3 Measurement
- Provisions for one-off events (restructuring, dismantling, and settlement of a lawsuit)
are measured at the most likely amount.
- Provisions for large populations of events (warranties, customer refunds) are
measured at a probability-weighted expected value.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#40

stem_zh: 2. Specific types of provision
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#41

stem_zh:
2.1 Future operating losses 未来经营损失
- not recognised as no obligation (没有义务，不确认)
- relate to future events not past
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#42

stem_zh:
2.2 Onerous contracts 亏损合同
- Definition:
A contract in which the unavoidable costs of meeting the contract exceed the
economic benefits expected to be received under it.
- Accounting treatment
 If an entity has an onerous contract, a provision should be recognised for the
present obligation under the contract.
 The provision is measured at the lower of the cost of fulling the contract and the
cost of terminating it and suffering any penalties.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#43

stem_zh:
2.3 Dismantling, removing and restoring 弃置成本
Recognise a provision for removal costs arising from the construction of the oil rig as
it is constructed, and add to the cost of the asset (discounted cash flows using the
pre-tax rate)
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#44

stem_zh:
2.4 Restructuring 重组
- Restructuring by sale of an operation: Recognize a provision only when the entity is
committed to a sale, i.e. there is a binding sale agreement.(legal obligation)
- Restructuring by closure or reorganization: Recognize a provision only when a
detailed form plan is in place and the entity has started to implement the plan, or
announced its main features to those affected. A Board decision is
insufficient.(constructive obligation)
Restructuring provisions should include only direct expenditures caused by the
restructuring, not costs that associated with the ongoing activities of the entity. Direct
expenditure is mainly redundancy expenditure.
 Ongoing activities includes:
- Retraining, relocating staff - Marketing
- Investing in new system, distribution network
- Future operating loss
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#45

stem_zh:
3. Contingent liabilities 或有负债
- Definition: Contingent liability is a possible obligation depending on whether some
uncertain future event occurs, or a present obligation but payment is not probable or
the amount cannot be measured reliably.
- Accounting treatment: It requires that entities should not recognize contingent
liabilities, but it should disclose them, unless the possibility of an outflow of
economic resources is remote.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#46

stem_zh:
4. Contingent assets 或有资产
- Definition: Contingent asset is a possible asset that arises from past events, and
whose existence will be confirmed only by the occurrence or non-occurrence of one
or more uncertain future events not wholly within the control of the entity.
- Accounting treatment: Contingent assets should not be recognized, but should be
disclosed where an inflow of economic benefits is probable. When the realization of
income is virtually certain, then the related asset is not a contingent asset and its
recognition is appropriate.
 Reimbursements 赔偿:
Some of the expenditure to settle a provision may be expected to be recovered from a
third party. The reimbursement should be recognised only when it is virtually
certain that it will be received, and should be treated as a separate asset with the
amount recognised not being greater than the provision itself.
IFRS 15 Revenue(常考，重难点)
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#47

stem_zh:
1. 控制的定义
新收入准则更加强调 control 控制权的转移。
Transfer control of an asset refers to:
• customer has the ability to direct the use of, and
• obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits (inflows or savings in outflows)
from the asset;
• Customer has the ability to prevent other entities from obtaining benefits from an
asset.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#48

stem_zh:
2. 收入的确认(五步法)
Definition: Revenue is income arising from the course of an entity’s ordinary
activities (公司的主营业务 normal trading and operating activities).
Step 1: Identify the contract with the customers 识别与客户间的合同
(a) the parties to the contract have approved the contract and are committed to
perform their respective obligations
(b) the entity can identify each party’s rights regarding the goods or services to be
transferred
(c) the entity can identify the payment terms for the goods or services to be
transferred
(d) the contract has commercial substance, and
(e) it is probable that the entity will collect the consideration(考试重点集中在此点
考核, 判断是否很有可能会收到对价)
 Contract modification 合同变更: 经合同各方同意，对原合同范围或价格作
出变更.
① Modification accounted for as a separate contract 合同变更部分作为单独合
同
The modification is accounted for as a separate contract if:
- The scope of the contract increases because of the addition of distinct goods or
services; and
- The price increases reflects the stand-alone selling prices of the additional goods or
services.
合同变更增加了可明确区分的商品及合同价款，且新增合同价款反映了新增商品
的单独售价，应当将该合同变更作为一份单独的合同进行会计处理。
② Modification not accounted for as a separate contract 合同变更部分不作为单
独合同(指未满足上述条件时)
The modification is accounted for as an adjustment to the existing contract, either
prospectively or through acumulative catch-up adjustment.
Modification accounted for prospectively 原合同的终止，新合同的建立
If the remaining goods are distinct from the goods transferred before the modification
but the consideration for these goods does not reflect their stand-alone prices. This
type of modification is treated as termination of the original contract and creation of a
new contract.
The amount of consideration allocated to the remaining performance obligations after
the modification is the sum of any consideration originally included but not been
recognised and consideration promised as part of the modification.合同变更不属于
第一种情况，且在合同变更日未转让商品与已转让商品之间可明确区分，但原合
同对价并未反映各自的单独售价。该合同变更视为原合同终止，创立新合同。新
合同交易价格为两项金额之和：原合同交易价格中尚未确认为收⼊的部分和合同
变更中客户承诺的对价金额。
Modification accounted for through a cumulative catch-up adjustment 合同变更
作为原合同的组成部分
If the remaining goods in the modification are not distinct, this modification is
treated as if it were part of the original contract, and the effect is recognised as an
adjustment to revenue at the date of modification.
合同变更不属于第⼀种情况，且在合同变更日已转让商品与未转让商品之间不可
明确区分，应将该合同变更视为原合同额组成部分，在变更日当天，重新计算履
约进度从而调整当期收入。
Contract costs 合同成本: The costs of obtaining a contractand fulfilling it must be
capitalised, and then will be amortised to P/L as revenue is recognised.
① 合 同 取 得 成 本 : Incremental costs of obtaining a contract (such as sales
commission) are capitalised if the entity expects to recover those costs. (不包含 legal
fees costs travelling to a tender 投标费, 因为无论合同是否 obtain 都会发生)
② 合 同 履 约 成 本 : Costs of fulfilling a contract are capitalised if meeting all the
following(若为了履约而购买的 Inventory/PPE/IA，遵循自身准则做账；若不符合
的，则需要同时满足以下三个条件方可按照合同履约成本的要求，资本化处理):
- The costs relate directly to an identifiable contract. 该成本与当前或预期取得的合
同直接相关。
- The costs generate or enhance resources used in satisfying performance obligations.
该成本增加了企业未来⽤于履约的资源。
- The costs are expected to be recovered. 该成本预期能被收回。
Presentation:
① 收到对价前确认收⼊，应确认如下: A receivable if the right to the consideration
is unconditional, or a contract asset.
② 收到对价时未确认收⼊，应确认如下: A contract liability.
Step 2: Identify the separate performance obligations within the contract 识别合
同中(可明确区分的)单项履约义务
A good or service is distinct if the customer can benefit from it (either on its own or
with other readily available resources) and the entity’s promise to transfer it is
separately identifiable from other promises in the contract.
Factors that indicate that a promise to transfer a good/service is separately identifiable
include that the good/service: 承诺可以被明确区分的情形:
① is not integrated with other goods/services in the contract (i.e. it is not an input to
deliver a combined output); or 合同中承诺的商品与服务无需与合同中承诺的其
他商品进行整合才能让客户受益。
② does not significantly modify or customise other goods/services promised in the
contract. 该商品对合同中承诺的其他商品不会予以重大修改或定制。
③ does not highly depend on or relate to other goods/services promised in the
contract. 该商品与合同中承诺的其他商品不具有高度关联性。
Step 3 Determine the transaction price 确定交易价格
The transaction price (sales tax 销项税 is excluded) is the amount of consideration
the entity expects in exchange for satisfying a performance obligation.
 Variable consideration 可变对价 (expected value & the most likely outcome)
Constraint on including variable consideration into transaction price 计⼊交易价格
的可变对价⾦额的限制:
An entity includes the amount of variable consideration only to the extent that it is
highly probable that a significant reversal in the amount of cumulative revenue
recognised will not occur when the uncertainty is subsequent resolved.
可变对价计量 Measure variable consideration at:
- Probability-Weighted expected value 大 批 量 事 件 按 照 期 望 值 法 (e.g. if large
number of contracts with similar characteristics); or
- Most likely outcome 两 种 情 况 下 取 最 大 可 能 性 (e.g. if only two possible
outcomes).
③ Volume discounts 数量折扣
④ Sale with a right of return 售后退回: An entity should not recognise revenue for
goods that it expects to be returned. 【背: This will be shown as a refund liability and
a deduction from revenue. The entity also recognises an asset against costs of sales.】
 Significant financing component 具有重大融资成分的合同
Indications of a significant financing component:
- The difference between promised consideration and the cash selling price.
- The length of time between the transfer of promised goods and the payment date.
*If there is a financing component, the consideration receivable should be discounted
to present value using the customer’s borrowing rate.
* If there is a financing component, revenue is recognised at an amount that reflects
the price that a customer would have paid if the customer had paid cash when they
transfer the goods to the customer.以现销价格确认
 Non-cash consideration 非现金对价(如股份支付)
Any non-cash consideration is measured at fair value, and if fair value cannot be
estimated reliably, then use stand-alone prices. 按照公允价值计量
- The fair value of the non-cash consideration may vary because of the form of the
consideration (for example, a change in the price of a share to which an entity is
entitled to receive from a customer). 因对价形式本身而发生的变动，比如以股票为
对价，股价波动的部分不计入收入，而是作为金融工具的价值波动来计量。
- If the fair value of the non-cash consideration promised by a customer varies for
reasons other than only the form of the consideration (for example, the fair value
could vary because of the entity’s performance). 因对价形式以外的因素而发生价
值的变动，比如因为公司业绩表现而导致收入变化，按照 variable consideration
可变对价来处理
 Consideration payable to a customer
- If consideration is paid to a customer in exchange for a distinct good, then it should
be accounted for as a purchase transaction.
- If not distinct, it should be accounted for as a reduction of transaction price.
Step 4 Allocate the transaction price to the separate performance obligations 将
交易价格分摊至各单项履约义务
The total transaction price should be allocated to each performance obligation in
proportion to stand-alone selling price.
If the stand-alone selling price is not directly observable, then it must be estimated
and observable inputs must be maximised:
 Market-based approach 市场调整法: 根据某商品或类似商品的售价确定该合
同商品的单独售价。
 Cost-plus approach 成本加成法: 根据商品预计成本加上合理⽑利后的价格。
 Residual value approach 余值法(常用): 根据合同价格减去合同中其他商品可
观测的单独售价后的余值。
Step 5 Recognise revenue when the entity satisfies a performance obligation 履
行各单项履约义务时确认收⼊
An entity must determine at contract inception whether it satisfies the performance
obligations over time or at a point in time. 收入确认条件
 Over time if meeting one of the following:
① The customer simultaneously receives and consumes the benefits. 客户在企业履
约的同时即取得并消耗所带来的经济利益。
② The entity’s performance creates or enhances an asset (such as WIP) that the
customer controls in the process. 客户能够控制企业履约过程中的在建商品。
③ The entity’s performance does not create an asset with an alternative use to itself
and has an enforceable right to payment for performance completed to date. 企业履
约过程中产出的商品具有不可替代用途，且在整个合同期内有权就累计至今已完
成的履约部分收取款项。
注: 如果客户缴纳定金，只有公司违约时定金才可退回，而若顾客违约则定金不
予退回，因此不算 satisfy over time。
If satisfying over time, then revenue is recognised over time based on progress
towards the satisfaction:
① Output method 产出法: such as surveys of performance, time elapsed.
② Input method 投⼊ 法 : such as costs incurred as a proportion of total expected
costs.
If progress cannot be reliably measured, then revenue can only be recognised up to
the recoverable costs incurred (因此不产生毛利).
 If at a point in time, the entity must determine the point at which a customer
obtains control of the asset.
An entity controls an asset if it can direct its use and obtain most of its remaining
benefits (also including the ability to prevent others from obtaining benefits from the
asset).
 Indicators of transfer of control:
- The entity has a present right to payment. 该公司有权取得对价
- The customer has legal title to the asset. 客户拥有该资产的所有权
- The entity has transferred physical possession of the asset. 该公司已物理上转交该
资产
- The customer has significant risks and rewards of ownership of the asset. 客户承担
所有权的风险与回报
- The customer has accepted the asset. 客户已验收
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#49

stem_zh: 3. Specific transactions
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#50

stem_zh:
3.1 Warranties 产品质量保证金:
 If the customer has the option to purchase the warranty separately, it should be
treated as a distinct performance obligation and a portion of the transaction price
must be allocated to it.
 If the customer cannot purchase separately (normally required by law) is not a
performance obligation. It might be result in the recognition of a provision.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#51

stem_zh:
3.2 Principals and agents
 Principal
- A supplier is a principal if it controls a promised good or service before it is
transferred to a customer. A principal is responsible for satisfying a performance
obligation.
- A principal must recognise the gross amount of revenue to which it is entitled for
goods and services transferred.
 Agents
- An agent’s performance obligation is to arrange for the provision of goods or
services by another party (the principal). The agent is providing a selling service to
the principal.
- An agent must recognise revenue as the amount of any fee or commission to which it
is entitled in exchange for arranging for the principal to provide its goods or services.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#52

stem_zh:
3.3 Consignment arrangements
When a product is delivered to a customer under a consignment arrangement, the
customer (dealer) does not obtain control of the product at that point in time, so no
revenue is recognised upon delivery.委托代销: 寄售后，若未卖出, 则不构成控制
权转移，不能确认收入，卖出后才构成。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#53

stem_zh:
3.4 Sale with a right to return
 Revenue would not be recognised for the products expected to be returned.预计
要被退回的货物，不确认收入；
 A refund liability 根据退货率确认应计退货款
 An asset for its right to recover products from customers 预计会退回来的货物
确认为资产
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#54

stem_zh:
3.5 Repurchase agreement
An entity sells an asset and promises, or has the option, to repurchase it.
Repurchase agreements generally come in three forms:
(a) An entity has an obligation to repurchase the asset (a forward contract)
(b) An entity has the right to repurchase the asset (a call option)
(c) An entity must repurchase the asset if requested by the customer (a put option).
 In the case a) and b), a forward or a call option the customer does not obtain
control of the asset, even if it has physical possession. The entity will account for the
contract as:
- A lease in accordance with IFRS 16, if the repurchase price is below the original
selling price; (Repurchase price < Original selling price)
- A financing arrangement if the repurchase price is equal to or greater than the
original selling price. In this case the entity will recognise both the asset and a
corresponding liability. (Repurchase price ≥ Original selling price)
 In the case of c), the entity is obliged to repurchase at the request of the customer
(a put option), it must consider whether or not the customer is likely to exercise that
option.
- Repurchase price < Expected market value → no significant economic incentives
→ sale of a product with a right of return
- Repurchase price ≥ Expected market value → significant economic incentives
• Original selling price > Repurchase price → lease
• Original selling price ≤ Repurchase price → financing arrangement
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#55

stem_zh:
3.6 Options for additional goods or services
Customer options to acquire additional goods or services for free or at a discount. The
option give rise to a performance obligation in the contract only if the option provides
a material right to the customer. 当客户拥有一项重大的权利，在购买额外的商品/
服务时，有 option 选择免费或是折扣价购买的情况下，此 option 构成一个单项
的履约义务。且需要根据不同履约义务的单独售价，将交易价格进行分配。
Entity recognizes revenue when those future goods or services are transferred or when
the option expires. 当额外的商品或服务被转移，或者当 option 到期的时侯，方可
确认收入。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#56

stem_zh:
3.6 Customers’ unexercised rights
Receive a prepayment from a customer, an entity shall recognise a contract liability.
收到预付款时，确认为合同负债。
An entity shall derecognise that contract liability (and recognise revenue) when it
transfers those goods or services and, therefore, satisfies its performance obligation.
当客户行使了权利(比如使用了礼品卡购买商品)，企业履约义务履行了，才可以
确认收入。因此判断重点在于，客户行使权利的可能性有多大，是否能够合理判
断 breakage。
If the company is able to reliably estimate the breakage amount: recognise the
expected breakage amount as revenue in proportion to the pattern of rights exercised
by the customer.
If the company is unable to reliably estimate the breakage amount: revenue for the
unused portion of the gift card is recognised when the likelihood of the customer
exercising their remaining rights becomes remote.
会计处理参考可变对价的处理方式：
- 如果可以可靠估计客户行权使用的比例，那么就按照客户行使权利的情况比例
来确认收入；
- 如果不可以可靠估计，则需要等到客户基本不可能去行权的时候，比如卡到期
了，才可以将 unused portion 的部分确认收入。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#57

stem_zh:
3.7 Non-refundable upfront fees
In many cases upfront fees do not relate to the transfer of any promised good or
service but are simply for future goods or services(an advance payment for future
goods or services). In this case revenue is recognised when the future goods or
services are provided.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#58

stem_zh:
3.7 Licensing
If the promise to grant the license is not distinct from the promised goods or services,
this is treated as a single performance obligation. 首先判断授权本身是否能与提供
的商品服务明确区分开，如果不能则需要捆绑在一起，确认为单一的履约义务。
Examples of licences that are not distinct from other goods or services promised in the
contract include the following:
- a licence that forms a component of a tangible good and that is integral to the
functionality of the good; and
- a licence that the customer can benefit from only in conjunction with a related
service (such as an online service provided by the entity that enables, by granting a
licence, the customer to access content).
考试核心点：判断授权承诺是否可明确区分。如果不满足上述条件，符合 distinct
定 义 ， 则 构 成 separate performance obligation 。 接 着 则 需 判 断 whether the
performance obligation is satisfied ‘at a point in time’ or ‘over time’.
 Satisfied over time 以下三个条件同时满足:
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#59

stem_zh:
1. The licensor will undertake activities that will significantly affect(make changes)
the IP.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#60

stem_zh: 2. The customer is exposed to the positive or negative effects of these activities.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#61

stem_zh:
3. The activities do not result in the transfer of a good/service to the customer.
若上述条件不满足，则属于 satisfied at a point in time.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#62

stem_zh:
3.7.1 Royalty
Revenue from a usage-based or sales-based royalty promised in exchange for licence
is recognised on the later of the following events:
- Usage or sales occurs; or
- The performance obligation to which the royalty has been allocated has been
satisfied.
IAS 20 政府补助
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#63

stem_zh:
1. Definition
Government grants are assistance by government in the form of transfers of resources
to an entity in return for past or future compliance with certain conditions relating to
the operating activities of the entity.
Grants related to assets: Government grants whose primary condition is that an entity
qualifying for them should purchase, construct or otherwise acquire non-current assets.
(会对于资产的 type or location, or the periods to be acquired or held. )
Grants related to income: Government grants other than those related to assets.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#64

stem_zh:
2. Recognition
A grant is recognized only when there is reasonable assurance that:
- The entity will comply with any conditions attached to the grant; and
- The grant will be received.
 Grants relating to income
The grant is recognized as income over the period necessary to match them with the
related costs, for which they are intended to compensate, on a systematic basis.
 Grants relating to assets
A grant relating to assets may be presented in one of two ways:
- As deferred income; or
- By deducting the grant from the asset's carrying amount;
IAS 19 员工福利
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#65

stem_zh:
1. Short-term benefits
Expected to be settled wholly within12 months after the end of the period (年底后 12
个月之内全额支付)
Accounting treatment:
The expense must be accounted for on an accruals basis and any unpaid entitlement
should be recognised as a short-term liability.
 Short-term paid absences
a) Non-accumulating paid absences: unused amounts cannot be carried forward
b) Accumulating paid absences: can be carried forward
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#66

stem_zh:
2. Post-employment benefits
Benefits that are payable after the completion of employment.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#67

stem_zh:
2.1 Defined contribution plans(DCP)
Entity pays fixed contributions into a separate entity and will have no legal or
constructive obligation to pay further contributions. Employee bears the risk. 定期
向养老金账户转入固定金额的养老金即可, 企业不承担后续义务，员工承担所有
风险。
会计处理: The contributions payable for the reporting period are charged to profit or
loss as an expense.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#68

stem_zh:
2.2 Defined benefits plans(DBP)高频，常考
除了 DCP, 其他的都属于 DBP.
FV of plan asset( 池 子 里 的 钱 ) 和 PV of obligation( 员 工 应 得 的 钱 ) 的 差 额 (net
obligation)均由企业承担, 企业承担后续义务及风险。养老金最终也以 net postion
净值列示:
- 若 obligation>asset -- plan deficiti
- 若 asset>obligation -- plan surplus
【裁员 redundancy 属于养老金计划的 curtailment，并不是 re-measurement】裁
员导致公司的员工减少，因此之前计提的养老金负债会因此而减少，即为养老金
负债缩减。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#69

stem_zh:
2.2.1 Accounting treatments:
- Current service cost: increase in the present value of the obligation arising from
employee service in the current period. 当期确认的服务成本，作为 pension expense
计入 profit or loss。
- Net interest expense(finance cost) is calculated by multiplying the opening defined
pension liability by the discount rate at the start of the annual reporting period, 根据
期初数及期初利息率计算，利息费用进损益。
- Net interest income is calculated by multiplying the opening plan assets by the
discount rate at the start of the annual reporting period, 根据期初数及期初利息率计
算，利息收入进损益。
- Re-measurement component is taken to OCI and this gain can not be reclassified to
profit or loss (精算差异全部计入 OCI, 且之后不可 reclassify 到 PL, 而是直接进
RE).
- Benefits paid have no effect on the net obligation as both plan assets and obligations
are reduced by the same amount. Pension fund 支付 benefits 时，不影响 PL。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#70

stem_zh:
2.2.2 Asset ceiling test
If the net item is a surplus it is subject to a ‘asset ceiling’ test. 池子里的金额付完养
老金之后还有多的.
A surplus is measured at the lower of:
- the surplus in the defined benefit plan; and
- the asset ceiling (which is the present value of any economic benefits available in
the form of refunds from the plan or reductions in future contributions to the plan).(要
么退款要么通过精算减少未来的投入)
Any impairment loss is charged immediately to other comprehensive income
immediately.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#71

stem_zh:
2.2.3 Past service costs
Past service cost, which is the change in the present value of the obligation for
employee service in prior periods, resulting from a plan amendment or curtailment.
 A plan amendment occurs when an entity introduces, or withdraws, a defined
benefit plan or changes the benefits payable under an existing defined benefit
plan.
 A curtailment occurs when an entity significantly reduces the number of
employees covered by a plan.
All past service costs to be recognised as an expense at the earlier of the following
dates：
a) when the plan amendment or curtailment(缩减) occurs
b)when the entity recognises related restructuring costs or termination benefits
These costs should be recognised regardless of the vesting requirements.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#72

stem_zh:
2.2.4 Settlement
Gains or losses on settlement are recognised in profit or loss when the settlement
occurs.
If a curtailment and a settlement occur together, there is no need to distinguish
between a past service cost and gain or loss settlement.
Dr Present value of obligation (advised by actuary) X
Cr Fair value of plan assets (any asset transferred) X
Cr Cash (paid directly by the entity as compensated 若还需支付现金赔偿) X
Dr/Cr Profit or loss (difference) X
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#73

stem_zh:
3. Other long-term employee benefits
Other long-term employee benefits are all employee benefits other than short-term
employee benefits, post-employment benefits and termination benefits.
The accounting treatment for other long-term benefit plans follows the treatment for
defined benefit pension plans, but remeasurements are not recognised in OCI.
The net total of the following amounts is recognised in profit or loss:
a) Service cost
b) Net interest on the defined benefit liability/asset
c) Remeasurement of the defined benefit liability/asset
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#74

stem_zh:
4. Termination benefits
Termination benefits are employee benefits provided in exchange for the termination
of an employee’s employment. They are benefits paid when employment is
terminated at the request of the employer.
确认条件:
An entity must recognise a liability and expense for termination benefits at the earlier
of the following dates:
- when the entity can no longer withdraw the offer of those benefits; and
- when the entity recognises costs for a restructuring within the scope of IAS 37.
IFRS 2 Share based payment 股权支付
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#75

stem_zh:
1. Types of transaction:
 Equity-settled share-based payment: The entity receives goods as
consideration for equity instruments of the entity.
 Cash-settled share-based payment: The entity acquires goods by incurring
liabilities to the supplier for amounts that are based on the price of the entity’s
shares or other equity instruments.
Transactions with a choice of settlement: The entity acquires goods and the terms
provide either the entity or the supplier with a choice of whether the entity settles in
cash or by issuing equity instruments.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#76

stem_zh:
2. Recognition: Where performance is not immediate, the expense is spread over the
period (the vesting period) until the counterparty becomes entitled to receive the
share-based payment.
Dr Expense
Cr Equity (if equity-settled)
Cr Liability (if cash-settled)
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#77

stem_zh:
3. Measurement of expense:
(1) Direct method: Fair value of goods received.
(2) Indirect method: By reference to the fair value of the equity instruments.
Equity-settled: Use fair value at grant date and do not update.
Cash-settled: Update fair value at each year end and changes recognised in P/L.
Any changes in estimates of expected number of employees are treated as a change in
accounting estimate and recognised in the period of the change.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#78

stem_zh:
4. Share-based payment with a choice of settlement
The entity has the choice: If there is a present obligation to settle in cash, treat as
cash-settled transaction. If not, treat as equity-settled transaction.
The counterparty has the choice: The entity has issued a compound financial
instrument. Account for debt component as cash-settled transaction, and equity
component measured at the residual fair value at grant date.
IFRS 13 Fair Value Measurement 公允价值计量
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#79

stem_zh:
1. Definition 定义
Fair value is the price that would be received to sell an asset or paid to transfer a
liability in an orderly transaction between market participants at the measurement
date.
公允价值是市场参与者在计量日发生的有序交易中，出售一项资产所能收到或者
转移一项负债所需支付的价格。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#80

stem_zh:
2. Principal market VS the most advantageous market 主要市场和最有利市场
(1) Principal market: The market with the greatest volume and level of activity for
the asset or liability.
主要市场：相关交易量最大和交易活跃程度最高的市场。(计算 FV 时考虑交通
费用，不考虑 transaction cost)
(2) In the absence of a principal market, it is assumed that the transaction would
occur in the most advantageous market. Most advantageous market is the market
that maximises the amount that would be received to sell the asset or minimises the
amount that would be paid to transfer the liability, after taking into account
transaction costs and transport costs.
最有利市场：不存在主要市场的，企业以最有利市场的价格计量相关资产和负债
的公允价值。在考虑交易费用和运输费用后，能够以最高金额出售相关资产或以
最低金额转移相关负债的市场。
注意: 在寻找最有利市场时，要考虑交易费用和运输费用，但是最终在计算公允
价值时，只减去运输费用。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#81

stem_zh:
3. Valuation technique 估值技术
(1) market approach
(2) cost approach
(3) income approach
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#82

stem_zh:
4. Inputs 输入值
Valuation techniques used to measure fair value shall maximise the use of relevant
observable inputs and minimise the use of unobservable inputs.
优先使用可观察输入值，在可观察输入值无法取得或取得不切实可行的情况下，
才可以使用不可观察输入值。
Level 1: Quoted prices (unadjusted price) in active markets for identical assets or
liabilities that the entity can access at the measurement date.相同资产或负债+活跃
市场+未经调整的报价
Level 2: Inputs other than quoted prices included within Level 1 that are observable
for the asset or liability, either directly or indirectly.
- 活跃市场中类似资产或负债的报价
- 非活跃市场中相同或类似资产或负债的报价
- 除报价以外的其他直接可观察输入值
- 市场验证输入值
Level 3: Unobservable inputs for the asset or liability
例如：未来现金流折现
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#83

stem_zh:
5. Application to non-financial asset
For non-financial assets, fair value should be measured by consideration of the
highest and best use of the asset. There is a presumption that current use would be
the highest and best use.
非金融资产的公允价值应当考虑市场参与者的角度确定非金融资产的最佳用途。
通常情况下，企业对非金融资产的当前用途视为最佳用途。
The highest and best use of a non- financial asset takes into account the use of the
asset that is physically possible, legally permissible and financially feasible.
最佳用途要考虑实物上是否可能，法律上是否允许，财务上是否可行。
Fair value measurement for a liability assumes that the liability remain outstanding
and the market participant transferee would be required to fulfill the obligation。
负债的公允价值计量假定负债未清偿，市场参与者被要求履行义务。
Financial instruments 金融工具
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#84

stem_zh:
1. Definition:
A financial instrument is any contract that gives rise to a financial asset of one entity
and a financial liability or equity instrument of another entity.
金融工具是一种合同，使得合同一方持有金融资产，合同另一方持有金融负债或
权益工具。
A financial asset is any asset that is:
(a) cash;
(b) an equity instrument of another entity;
(c) a contractual right:
(i) to receive cash or another financial asset from another entity; or
(ii) to exchange financial assets or financial liabilities with another entity under
conditions that are potentially favourable to the entity; or
(d) a contract that will or may be settled in the entity’s own equity instruments and is:
(i) a non- derivative for which the entity is or may be obliged to receive a variable
number of the entity’s own equity instruments; or
(ii) a derivative that will or may be settled other than by the exchange of a fixed
amount of cash or another financial asset for a fixed number of the entity’s own equity
instruments.
金融资产：
(a)现金
(b)其他方的权益工具
(c)一项合同权利：
(i)从其他方收取现金或其他金融资产的合同权利；
(ii)在潜在有利条件下，与其他方交换金融资产或金融负债的合同权利
(d)将来须用或可用企业自身权益工具结算的合同：
(i)将来须用或可用企业自身权益工具结算的非衍生工具合同，且企业根据该合同
将收到可变数量的自身权益工具；
(ii)将来须用或可用企业自身权益工具结算的衍生工具合同，但以固定数量的自
身权益工具交换固定金额的现金或其他金融资产的衍生工具合同除外。
A financial liability is any liability that is:
(a) a contractual obligation:
(i) to deliver cash or another financial asset to another entity; or
(ii) to exchange financial assets or financial liabilities with another entity under
conditions that are potentially unfavourable to the entity; or
(b) a contract that will or may be settled in the entity’s own equity instruments and is:
(i) a non- derivative for which the entity is or may be obliged to deliver a variable
number of the entity’s own equity instruments; or
(ii) a derivative that will or may be settled other than by the exchange of a fixed
amount of cash or another financial asset for a fixed number of the entity’s own equity
instruments.
金融负债：
(a)一项合同义务：
(i)向其他方交付现金或其他金融资产的合同义务；
(ii)在潜在不利的条件下，与其他方交换金融资产或金融负债的合同义务
(b)将来须用或可用企业自身权益工具进行结算的合同：
(i)将来须用或可用企业自身权益工具进行结算的非衍生工具合同，且企业根据该
合同交付可变数量的自身权益工具；
(ii)将来须用或可用企业自身权益工具进行结算的衍生工具合同，如果是以固定
数量的自身权益工具交换固定金额的现金或其他金融资产的衍生工具，那么它是
权益工具，除此之外是金融负债
An equity instrument is any contract that evidences a residual interest in the assets of
an entity after deducting all of its liabilities.
权益工具：能证明拥有某个企业在扣除所有负债后的资产中的剩余权益的合同。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#85

stem_zh:
2. Classification of financial liability and equity instruments
套话：A financial instrument should be classified as equity or financial liability not on
the basis of its legal form but on the substance.
金融负债和权益工具的分类应当秉持经济实质重于法律形式, 根据各自的定义
进行判断。主要集中于 Financial liability, 看下公司是否存在契约约定的无法避免
的付钱的义务，如果是则属于 Financial liability, 不是则是 equity instruments.
例子：
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#86

stem_zh: 1. Contingent payment 属于 financial liabilities
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#87

stem_zh:
2. Redeemable preference share 属于 financial liability；Irredeemable preference share
属于 equity instrument.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#88

stem_zh:
3. Convertible bond 可转债
Convertible bond (属于 compound instrument) has characteristics of both a financial
liability and equity.
Initial measurement: Convertible bond must be split into a liability component and
an equity component. The fair value of the liability component is the present value
of cash flow. The remaining of the convertible bond belongs to equity instrument.
Subsequent measurement: The liability component should be measured under
amortised cost method, but the equity component remains unchanged.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#89

stem_zh:
3. Recognition
Financial asset and financial liabilities is recognized in the statement of financial
position only when the entity becomes one party of the contract of the financial
instrument.
只要成为金融工具合同的一方就必须在资产负债表中确认金融资产和金融负债。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#90

stem_zh:
4. Classification
Classification of financial assets
(a) Amortised cost
A financial asset is measured at amortised cost if both of the following conditions
are met:
(1) the asset is held within a business model whose objective is to hold assets in order
to collect contractual cash flows; and
(2) the contractual terms of the financial asset give rise on specified dates to cash
flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount
outstanding.
分类为摊余成本法：持有金融资产的目的是为了获取合同现金流且符合利息加本
金的合同现金流量特征。
(b) FVTOCI
A financial asset is measured at fair value through OCI if both of the following
conditions are met:
(1) the asset is held within a business model whose objective is achieved by both
holding and collecting contractual cash flows and selling the financial assets; and
(2) the contractual terms of the financial asset give rise on specified dates to
cashflows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the principal amount
outstanding.
分类为以公允价值计量且其变动计入其他综合收益：持有持有金融资产的目的是
为了获取合同现金流和出售且符合利息加本金的合同现金流量特征。(交易频率
高、金额大)
(c) FVTPL
All other financial assets must be measured at fair value through P&L.
不能分类为(a)和(b)的，那么就分类为 FVTPL
 Irrevocable designations
(1) On initial recognition of a financial asset that would otherwise be measured at
amortised cost or at fair value through OCI a company can make an irrevocable
decision to designate them as at fair value through P&L. This is only allowed where it
eliminates or significantly reduces a measurement or recognition inconsistency.
(2) Investments in equity instruments are measured at fair value through P&L.
However, on initial recognition an entity is allowed to make an irrevocable decision to
measure an investment in equity at fair value with movements reported in OCI.
两个强行指定：
(1)为了减少会计错配，企业可以将分类为摊余成本或 FVTOCI 的金融资产指定
为 FVTPL，且一经指定不得撤销。(debt instrument)
(2)企业可以将非交易性的权益工具指定为 FVTOCI，且一经指定不得撤销。
(equity instrument)
Financial asset - investment in debt instrument
Amortised
cost
Business model: held to collect contractual cash flow
Cash flow characteristics: interest + principal
FVTOCI Business model: held to collect contractual cash flow and sell
Cash flow characteristics: interest + principal
FVTPL Irrevocable election (to eliminate accounting mismatch)
Financial asset - investment in equity instrument
FVTPL Default (默认)
FVTOCI Irrevocable election
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#91

stem_zh:
5. Classification of financial liability
Financial liabilities are classified as measured at either:
(1) Financial liabilities at amortised cost;
(2) Financial liabilities at fair value with gains and losses recognised in profit or loss
(FVTPL).(held for trading or designation)
金融负债可分类为以摊余成本计量的金融负债和 FVTPL。以交易为目的持有金
融负债分类为 FVTPL，也可以为消除会计错配而强行指定金融负债分类为
FVTPL。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#92

stem_zh:
6. Reclassification
If an entity changes its business model for managing financial assets, all affected
financial assets are reclassified (e.g. from fair value through profit or loss to amortised
cost).
This only applies to investments in debt instrument. Reclassification is prospective,
no restatement is required. Investment in equity instrument cannot by reclassified.
当企业的管理金融资产的业务模式发生变更，企业应当重分类金融资产。(业务
模式变更一般指的是开始或终止对经营影响重大的活动，比如收购、处置、终止
某一业务线)
持有的债权工具可以进行重分类，但是持有的权益工具不能重分类。重分类采用
未来适用法。
Reclassification of financial liabilities is not allowed.
金融负债不得重分类。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#93
