stem_zh:
8.Impairment - Expected credit loss
定义:
Credit loss: The difference between all contractual cash flows that are due to an entity
in accordance with the contract and all the cash flows that the entity expects to receive
(i.e. all cash shortfalls), discounted at the original effective interest rate.
Expected credit losses: The weighted average of credit losses with the respective risks
of a default occurring as the weights.
Life-time expected credit losses: The expected credit losses that result from all
possible default events over the expected life of a financial instrument.
12-month expected credit losses: The portion of lifetime expected credit losses that
represent the expected credit losses that result from default events on a financial
instrument that are possible within the 12 months after the reporting date.
会计处理:
Initial
measurement
Subsequent measurement
Stage 1 Stage 2 Stage 3
Impairment 12 month expected credit
losses Lifetime expected credit losses
Interest
income
Effective interest on the gross carrying amount
(before deducting expected losses)
Effective interest
on the net amount
 Initial measurement
At initial recognition of a financial asset, a loss allowance equal to 12-month
expected credit losses must be recognised.
 Subsequent measurement
Stage 1: 12-month expected credit losses
Credit risk has not increased significantly since initial recognition
Effective interest calculated on gross carrying amount of financial asset.
Stage 2: Lifetime expected credit losses
Credit risk has increased significantly since initial recognition
Effective interest calculated on gross carrying amount of financial asset.
No actual default has yet occurred
Stage 3: Lifetime expected credit losses
Objective evidence of impairment exists at the reporting date
Effective interest calculated on net carrying amount of financial asset.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#95

stem_zh:
9. Financial liability - modification
A substantial modification of the terms of the existing financial liability is accounted
for by extinguishing the original financial liability and recognising a new financial
liability.终止原负债合同，并确认新的金融负债。
The terms are substantially different if the present value of the cash flows under the
new terms (including any fees paid net of any fees received) when discounted using
the original effective interest rate is at least 10% different from the present value of
the remaining cash flows of the original financial liability.
The difference between the carrying amount of a financial liability extinguished or
transferred to another party 终止确认的 FL and the consideration paid 支付出去的对
价 is recognised in profit or loss.
Where the terms are not substantially different the original liability is not
derecognised. The liability is restated to the present value of the revised cash flows.
Any increase or decrease in carrying amount is presented in the statement of profit or
loss.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#96

stem_zh:
10. Hedge
Hedge accounting is a method of managing risk by designating one or more hedging
instruments so that their change in fair value is offset, in whole or in part, by the
change in fair value or cash flows.
套期会计：当财报中的项目(item)或现金流受制于潜在的价值波动时，公司可以
通过套期来对冲公允价值波动或现金流波动。
Adopting the hedging accounting is mandatory where the hedging relationship
meeting all of the following:
(1) The hedging relationship consists only of eligible hedging instruments and eligible
hedged items.
(2) It was designed at its inception as a hedge with full documentation.
(3) The hedging relationship meets all the hedge effectiveness requirements:
①There is an economic relationship between the hedged item and the hedging
instrument.
② The effect of credit risk does not dominate the value changes that result from the
economic relationship.
③ The hedge ratio (quantity of hedging instrument to quantity of hedged item) is the
same as that resulting from the quantity of the hedged item the entity actually hedges
and the quantity of the hedging instrument the entity actually uses.
采用套期会计的强制性
(1) 套期关系包含合格(eligible)的套期工具和被套期项目
(2) 初始就设计为套期，并有完整的文件记录。
(3)满足套期有效性
①套期工具与被套期项目之间存在经济关系
②信用风险的效应不主导价值变化。 
③被套期项目的数量与套期工具的数量一致
Fair value hedge: a hedge of the exposure to changes in fair value of a recognised
asset or liability (已确认的资产或负债) or an unrecognised firm commitment (尚
未确认的确定承诺) that is attributable to a particular risk and could affect profit or
loss (or other comprehensive income for equity investments measured at fair value
through other comprehensive income)
Accounting treatment of FV hedge:
(1) The hedging instrument will be remeasured to fair value;
(2) The carrying amount of the hedged item will be adjusted for the change in fair
value since the inception of the hedge.
(3) The gain (or loss) on the hedging instrument and the loss (or gain) on the hedged
item will be recorded:
①in profit or loss in most cases, but
②in other comprehensive income if the hedged item is an investment in equity that is
measured at fair value through other comprehensive income.
公允价值套期的账务处理：
(1)套期工具按照 FV 计量
(2)被套期项目的账面价值要调整至 FV，并记录其 FV 的变动
(3)套期项目 FV 的变动导致的收益(损失)和被套期项目 FV 的变动导致的损失(收
益)：
①计入当期损益
②如果被套期项目是分类为 FVTOCI 的权益工具投资，那么套期项目和被套期项
目的 FV 变动都计入 OCI
Cash flow hedge: a hedge of the exposure to variability in cash flows that is
attributable to a particular risk associated with a recognised asset or liability (已确认
的资产或负债) or a highly probable forecast transaction (极可能发生的预期交易)
and that could affect profit or loss.
For cash flow hedges, the hedging instrument will be remeasured to fair value at the
reporting date. The gain or loss is recognised in other comprehensive income. –
Effective portion
However, if the gain or loss on the hedging instrument since the inception of the
hedge is greater than the loss or gain on the hedged item then the excess gain or loss
on the instrument must be recognised in profit or loss. – Ineffective portion
对于现金流量套期，套期工具按照 FV 计量，属于有效套期的部分的计入 OCI(可
转入当期损益)；属于无效套期的部分计入当期损益。
IFRS 8 Operating Segment
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#97

stem_zh:
1. Definition
An operating segment is a component of an entity:
• that engages in business activities from which it may earn revenues and incur
expenses; and
• whose operating results are reviewed regularly by the entity's chief operating
decision maker to make decisions about resources to be allocated to the segment and
assess its performance; and
• for which discrete financial information is available.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#98

stem_zh:
2. Aggregation criteria
Two or more operating segments may be aggregated into a single operating segment if
aggregation is consistent with the core principle of this IFRS, the segments have
similar economic characteristics, and the segments are similar in each of the following
respects:
• The nature of the products and services;
• The nature of the production processes;
• The type or class of customer for their products and services;
• The methods used to distribute their products or provide their services; and
• If applicable, the nature of the regulatory environment, for example, banking,
insurance or public utilities.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#99

stem_zh:
3. Reportable segments
IFRS 8 requires an entity to report financial and descriptive information about its
reportable segments.
Reportable segments are operating segments or aggregations of operating segments
that meet specified criteria:
• its reported revenue, from both external customers and inter-segment sales or
transfers, is 10% or more of the combined revenue, internal and external, of all
operating segments; or
• the absolute measure of its reported profit or loss is 10% or more of the greater, in
absolute amount, of
(i) the combined reported profit of all operating segments that did not report a loss
and
(ii) the combined reported loss of all operating segments that reported a loss; or
• its assets are 10% or more of the combined assets of all operating segments.
If the total external revenue reported by operating segments constitutes less than 75%
of the entity's revenue, additional operating segments must be identified as reportable
segments (even if they do not meet the quantitative thresholds set out above) until at
least 75% of the entity's revenue is included in reportable segments.
IFRS 16 Lease 租赁
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#100

stem_zh:
1. Definition
Lease: A contract, or part of a contract, that conveys the right to use an asset, for a
period of time in exchange for consideration.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#101

stem_zh:
2. Recognition
IFRS 16 introduces a single lessee accounting model and requires a lessee to
recognise assets and liabilities for all leases with a term of more than 12 months
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#102

stem_zh:
2.1 确认豁免:
a) short-term lease(within 12 months)
b) lease for low value
例: IT equipment, Servers including many individual modules, Office equipment.
注: 若包含 purchase option，则不能豁免确认。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#103

stem_zh:
3. Identifying a lease
For a contract that contains a lease component and non-lease components, a lessee
shall allocate the consideration in the contract to each lease component on the basis of
the relative stand-alone price of the lease component and the aggregate stand-alone
price of the non-lease components.
 Lease component: 按照 IFRS 16 的要求处理
 Non-lease component: 单独作为服务合同，确认 expense
The right to control the use depends on the lessee having both:
① The right to obtain substantially all of the economic benefits from the use of the
identified asset.
② The right to direct the use if either:
 The lessee has the right to direct how and for what purpose the asset is used, or
 The decisions about use are pre-determined and the lessee can operate asset
without the lessor having the right to change those operating instructions.
 A lessee does not control the use if the lessor could substitute the underlying asset
for another and would benefit from doing so.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#104

stem_zh:
4. 初始计量
Initial measurement of right-of-use assets: At cost, which includes:
 The amount of the initial measurement of the lease liability.
 Any lease payments made before the commencement date less any lease
incentives received.
 Any initial direct costs.
 Any costs for dismantling and removing the underlying asset or restoring the site
at the end of the lease term.

Initial measurement of lease liability:
At present value of future lease payments discounted at the interest rate implicit in
the lease (use lessee’s incremental borrowing rate if the rate cannot be determined).
Dr ROU asset
Cr Lease liability PV of lease payment
Cr cash initial payment+direct cost-incentive
Cr Provision PV of dismantling cost
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#105

stem_zh:
5. 后续计量：
Subsequent measurement of right-of-use assets: Using cost model unless it is an
investment property or belongs to assets using revaluation model.
 If the lease transfers ownership at the end of the lease - depreciated over the
useful life;
 If not - depreciated using the shorter of useful life and lease term.
Subsequent measurement of lease liability: The carrying amount is increased by
interest charges on the outstanding liability and then reduced by lease payments. (最
好在年底区分 current 与 non-current 部分)
Reassessment of lease liability
租赁期间发生变化——新 discount rate
租赁金额发生变化——旧 discount
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#106

stem_zh:
6. Apportionment of rental payments: an interest charge and a repayment of part of
the capital cost of the asset.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#107

stem_zh:
7. Lessor accounting
A lessor must classify its leases as finance leases or operating leases.
Finance lease - a lease where the risks and rewards of the underlying asset
substantially transfers to the lessee.
Operating lease - a lease that does not meet the definition of a finance lease.
Indicators that a lease is a finance lease:
• Ownership is transferred to the lessee at the end of the lease
• The lessee has the option to purchase the asset for less than its expected fair value at
the date the option becomes exercisable and it is reasonably certain that the option
will be exercised
• The lease term (including any secondary periods) is for the major part of the asset's
economic life
• At the inception of the lease, the present value of the lease payments amounts to
at least substantially all of the fair value of the leased asset
• The leased assets are of a specialised nature so that only the lessee can use them
without major modifications being made
• The lessee will compensate the lessor for their losses if the lease is cancelled
• Gains or losses from fluctuations in the fair value of the residual fall to the lessee
(for example, by means of a rebate of lease payments)
• The lessee can continue the lease for a secondary period in exchange for
substantially lower than market rent payment
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#108

stem_zh:
7.1 Accounting for Finance Lease
For a finance lease, the lessor derecognises the underlying asset and instead
recognises a lease receivable (“net investment in the lease”) equal to the present value
of lease payments to be received plus any unguaranteed residual value accruing to the
lessor.
These amounts are discounted at the interest rate implicit in the lease. Rentals
received by the lessor are split into repayment of capital and interest elements.
Interest is calculated to give a constant periodic rate of return on the lessor's
investment in the lease.
The interest income credited to profit or loss decreases each year as the lease
receivable is reduced. IFRS 9 impairment requirements apply to the lease receivable.
If the lessor is a manufacturer/dealer, gross profit (loss) on the sale of the asset is
recognised at the inception of the lease.
进入租赁：
Dr Lease receivable
Cr Asset
计息：
Dr Lease receivable
Cr Interest income
收租金：
Dr Cash
Cr Lease receivable
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#109

stem_zh:
7.2 Accounting for Operating Lease
- Lessor continues to recognise the underlying asset which is depreciated on the same
basis as similar assets.
- Operating lease rentals are credited to profit or loss on a straight line basis.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#110

stem_zh:
7.3 Sublease
定 义 : A transaction for which an underlying asset is re-leased by a lessee
(‘intermediate lessor’) to a third party, and the lease (‘head lease’) between the head
lessor and lessee remains in effect.
会计处理:
As separate contracts, the head lease and a sublease are accounted for separately. A
sublease should be classified by an intermediate lessor as follows:
- if the head lease is a short-term lease and the entity has elected to apply the
recognition exemption, as an operating lease;
- otherwise, by reference to the right-of-use asset arising from the head lease (not by
reference to the underlying asset), as an operating lease or finance lease
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#111

stem_zh:
8. Sale and leaseback
IFRS16 requires an initial assessment whether the transfer constitutes a sale.
 Transfer is not sale: If the transfer does not satisfy IFRS15 to qualify as a
sale( 控 制 权 / 风 险 报 酬 未 转 移 ), then the seller continues to recognise the
transferred asset and the transfer proceeds are treated as a financial liability (more
in nature of a secured loan).
 Transfer is a sale: The seller/lessee measures the right-of-use asset from the
leaseback at the rights retained of its previous carrying amount, and only
recognises gain/loss on the sale that relates to the rights transferred to the buyer
Dr Cash (sale proceeds)
Dr ROUA (CA*LL/FV)
Cr PPE (CA)
Cr Lease liability (PV of future lease payments)
Dr/Cr Gain or loss (1-LL/FV)*(sale proceeds-carrying amount))
当 SP(selling price)>FV,retain%=(lease liability-additional financing)/FV
当 SP(selling price)<FV,retain%=(lease liability+prepayment)/FV
IAS 12 Income tax
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#112

stem_zh:
1. Current tax 当期所得税(不太考)
Income taxes payable or recoverable in respect of taxable profit or loss for a period.
Current tax unpaid for current and prior periods is recognised as a liability.
Amounts paid in excess of amounts due are shown as an asset.
The benefit relating to a tax loss is recognised as an asset.
Dr income tax expense
Cr current tax liability
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#113

stem_zh:
2. Deferred tax 递延所得税
Deferred tax is an accounting measure, used to match the tax effects of transactions
with their accounting effect.
Why recognise deferred tax?
由于账项处理不同，会计准则计量下的 carrying amount 和税法计量下的 tax base
可能不同，导致同一笔费用/收入确认进 accounting profit 以及 taxable profit 的期
间不同，产生了 Temporary difference 暂时性差异。
The tax base of an asset is the amount that will be deductible for tax purposes against
any taxable economic benefits that will flow to an entity when it recovers the carrying
amount of the asset.
The tax base of a liability is its carrying amount, less any amount that will be
deductible for tax purposes in future periods.
Temporary difference 暂时性差异：
 Taxable temporary differences - generate a deferred tax liability.
 Deductible temporary differences - generate a deferred tax asset.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#114

stem_zh:
3. 常考例子：
(1) Development costs: capitalised but are often tax deductible in the period
incurred.
Temporary differences is the full carrying amount of the development expenditure
since the tax base is nil, generating a deferred tax liability when multiplied by the tax
rate.
(2) Impairment losses (inventory losses): If the loss is not tax deductible until later,
a deductible temporary difference arises because the tax base does not change and so
a deferred asset arises based on the loss multiplied by the tax rate.
(3) Financial assets: Gains on financial assets held at fair value are either recognised
in P/L or OCI. If the gain is not taxable until sale, a taxable temporary difference
generates a deferred tax liability.Losses tax deductible on sale generates a deferred tax
asset.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#115

stem_zh:
4. Unused tax losses and unused tax credits
Tax losses and credits may result in tax saving if they can be carried forward to reduce
future tax payments.
A deferred tax asset is recognised to the extent that it is probable that future taxable
profit will be available against which the unused tax losses and credits can be used.
Deferred tax assets arising from unused tax losses or tax credits are recognised only to
the extent that:
• the entity has sufficient taxable temporary differences(same tax authority & the
same taxable entity), or
• there is convincing other evidence that sufficient taxable profit will be available
against which the unused tax losses or unused tax credits can be utilised by the entity
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#116

stem_zh: 5. Deferred tax: Group financial statements
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#117

stem_zh:
5.1 Fair value adjustment on consolidation
(a) FV>CA: 子公司净资产公允价值调增，TB 不变，产生应纳税暂时性差异，
形成 DTL，负债增加从而减少 the fair value of net assets of the subsidiary；
(b) FV < CA : 子公司净资产公允价值调减，TB 不变，产生可抵扣暂时性差异，
形成 DTA，资产增加 increases the fair value of net assets of subsidiary.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#118

stem_zh:
5.2 Provisions for unrealised profit
Accounting adjustment reduces the carrying amount of the inventory in the
consoldiated financial statements but the tax base of the inventory remains the same.
This creates a deductible temporary difference, giving rise to a deferred tax asset in
the consolidated financial statements.
SME 中小型企业会计准则
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#119

stem_zh:
1. Definition：
Entities that do not have public accountability and publish general purpose financial
statements for external users. (SME 的判断和规模无关 no size test.)
 It comprises a single standard divided into simplified sections for each relevant
IFRS standard but which have also omitted certain IFRS standards such as EPS.
 The SME standard is naturally a modified version of full IFRS standards and not
an
independently developed standard.
 SME 如果要变成 public listed entity 转变是很方便的，因为准则只是精简版，
但内容并未更改
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#120

stem_zh:
2. 掌握一些例子，在考试中可以运用
 Intangible asset：所有 IA(包括商誉)都摊销，年限不确定，就按 10 年做摊销，
不可以用重估模型，所有的内部研发成本全部费用化。
 Borrowing costs ：直接费用化
 折旧摊销的会计估计 ：不再每年进行 review
 Financial instrument：债务类工具基本都用摊余成本；权益类工具默认 FVTPL。
Business combination 企业合并
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#121

stem_zh:
1. 识别谁是 acquirer 收购方。
这类主要是判断谁能够控制被收购方，要记住 control 的定义。
An investor controls an investee when:
 the investor has power over the investee, and
 the investor is exposed, or has rights, to variable returns from its involvement
with the investee, and
 the investor has the ability to affect those returns through its power over the
investee.
其中关于 power 的判断，只能考虑实质性权利。
In this assessment, it is important to distinguish between substantive rights and
protective rights. Only rights which are substantive are said to give the investor
control. 保护性权利(比如更改公司章程，或是任命审计师团队)更多是保护利益
者自身利益。
*判断 power 的思路:
- 先根据持股比例判断，是否是 majority of voting rights(超过 50%)；
- 若无法清晰判断，结合案例判断是否存在 power。是否拥有实质性的决策权；
如果是，则存在 power，投资方可以确认为 acquirer；
- 确认为 acquirer 后，锁定收购日计算商誉。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#122

stem_zh:
2. 关于被收购方是不是构成一项业务的判断
Business combination: A transaction or other event in which an acquirer obtains
control of one or more business. 企业合并的定义建议背诵
Business: an integrated set of activities and assets that is capable of being conducted
and managed for the purpose of providing goods or services to customers, generating
investment income (e.g. dividends or interest) or generating other income from
ordinary activities. 关于业务的定义建议背诵
If an acquired group of assets and liabilities meets the definition of a business, the
transaction is a business combination and is accounted for under IFRS3. if not, then it
is an asset acquisition and is accounted for as such.
如果被收购方构成一项业务，那么就按照企业合并去进行账务处理且计算商誉，
如果被收购方不构成一项业务，那么就只能将该交易看成是一项资产的采购，不
需要去计算商誉不需要去合并。
The three elements of a business are defined as follow：
 Inputs are economic resources that can create outputs once processes are applied
to them. Examples include property, plant and equipment, intangible assets, raw
materials, and employees.
 Processes are ‘any system, standard, protocol, convention or rule that when
applied to an input or inputs, creates outputs or has the ability to contribute to the
creation of outputs’. Processes are normally documented. However, employee
knowledge and experience in following rules and conventions can also constitute
a process.
 Outputs result from inputs and the processes applied to inputs. Outputs include
goods, services and income.
要想构成一项业务，被收购方要有 input，process，output。这三个概念定义建议
Although businesses usually have outputs, outputs are not required for an integrated
set of activities and assets to qualify as a business.
被收购方一般存在 outputs，但是是否存在 outputs 也不是被收购方构成一项业务
的必要条件。
Determining whether a particular set of activities and assets is a business shall be
based on whether the integrated set is capable of being conducted and managed as a
business by a market participant. Thus, in evaluating whether a particular set is a
business, it is not relevant whether a seller operated the set as a business or whether
the acquirer intends to operate the set as a business.
企业在判断组合是否构成业务时，应当从市场参与者角度考虑可以将其作为业务
进行管理和经营，而不是根据合并方的管理意图或被合并方的经营历史来判断。
因为判断被收购方是不是一项业务是一项比较困难的问题，所以 IASB 又更新了
准则，加入了一个集中度测试来帮助判断被收购方是不是一项业务，集中度测试
的内容需要背诵。
There is an optional concentration test that preparers of financial statements can use to
assess whether an acquired set of assets is not a business.
 The concentration test is met (i.e the acquired assets are not a business) if
substantially all of the fair value of the total assets acquired is concentrated in a
single identifiable asset or group of similar identifiable assets.
 If the concentration test is not met, or if the test is not applied, a more detailed
assessment of the facts and circumstances is required to ascertain if a business has
been acquired.
如果集中度测试满足，那么被收购方就不构成一项业务，那么就不需要合并不需
要去计算商誉，当成是单项资产的采购。
如果集中度测试不满足，或者没有进行集中度测试，那么我们需要进行更加细节
的分析。看看被收购方是不是存在 inputs 和实质性的 process。
To meet the definition of a business, there must be inputs and processes that, when
applied to acquired inputs, are capable of producing outputs. The processes acquired
must be important and substantive.
 If there are no outputs at the acquisition date then an acquired process is only
substantive if:
it is critical to convert an input to an output, and
 inputs acquired include a knowledgeable, skilled, organised workforce able to
perform that process on other acquired inputs to produce outputs.
该组合在合并日无产出的，同时满足下列条件的加工处理过程应判断为是实质性
的;
第一，该加工处理过程对投入转化为产出至关重要;第二，具备执行该过程所需
技能、知识或经验的有组织的员工，且具备必要的材料、权利、其他经济资源等
投入，例如技术、研究和开发项目、房地产或矿区权益等。
If there are outputs at the acquisition date then an acquired process is substantive if it:
 is critical to continuing to produce outputs, and the inputs acquired include an
organised workforce with the skills and knowledge to perform that process, or
 significantly contributes to the ability to continue producing outputs and is either
rare or not capable of easy replacement.
该组合在合并日有产出的，满足下列条件之一的加工处理过程应判断为是实质性
的：第一，该加工处理过程对持续产出至关重要，且具备执行该过程所需技能、
知识或经验的有组织的员工;第二，该加工处理过程对产出能力有显著贡献，且
该过程是独有、稀缺或难以取代的。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#123

stem_zh:
3. 商誉的计算
X1：收购对价
收购对价一定要用公允价值入账，其形式有现金对价，以股换股，递延对价，或
有对价，股份支付合同的替换等等。其中重点掌握递延对价，或有对价。
Deferred Consideration
 If cash is to be paid in the future, the amount will need to be discounted to
Present Value. The increase in present value, through the passage of time, will be
a finance cost charged to profit or loss.
Dr Investment in Subsidiary
Cr Deferred consideration (liability)
Dr Finance cost / Group Retained Earning
Cr Deferred consideration(liability)
 Deferred shares should be measured at the fair value at the acquisition date with
subsequent changes in fair value ignored.
Dr Investment in Subsidiary
Cr Other component of equity (equity)
Contingent Consideration
 If the contingent consideration will be settled in cash, a liability will be
recognised;
 If the contingent consideration will be settled through the issue of additional
equity instruments, the credit entry will be to equity(OCE).
Contingent consideration is included even if payment is not deemed probable. Its Fair
value will incorporate the probability of payment occurring.
Any change to contingent consideration that arises within the measurement period and
that are due to additional information obtained relating to the acquisition date result in
the remeasurement of goodwill is reflected in goodwill;
Any change that arises after the measurement period does not affect the original
calculation of goodwill:
 If settlement will be in cash, the consideration will be remeasured at each
reporting date and any changes recognised in profit or loss.
 If settlement will be through an equity issue, the consideration is not remeasured
and any change in value on eventual issue will be included within equity.
Goodwill $`000
FV of purchase consideration X1
Value of NCI X2
Less: FV of identifiable net assets of S at acquisition
date (AD)
(X3)
Goodwill at AD X4
Impairment to date (X5)
Goodwill at reporting date (RD) X6
X2：少数股东权益
FV method: NCI at AD=number of share of S * NCI% * SP of S at AD
Proportion method: NCI at AD=FV of identifiable net asset of S at AD* NCI%
收购日当天少数股东权益价值的计算有两种方法，公式如上，如果是用公允价值
的方法计算 NCI，那么计算出来的是 full goodwill，即子公司 100%的商誉；如果
是用 proportion method 去计算 NCI，那么计算出来的是 partial goodwill，即子公
司的商誉当中归母公司的部分。
年底少数股东权益期末数的计算公式：
NCI at Reporting date=NCI at AD+post-acquisition equity of Subsidiary * NCI%
X3：收购日当天子公司净资产的公允价值
FV of identifiable net asset of S at AD=CV of identifiable net asset of S at AD+FV
adjustment
CV of identifiable net asset of S at AD=CV of equity of S at AD=share capital+share
premium+revaluation surplus+retained earning+other component of equity.
X4=X1+X2-X3=商誉
 如果 X4 大于 0，那么将其作为一项资产确认在合并资产负债表的长期资产
当中，后续每年对商誉进行减值测试。
 如果 X4 小于 0，企业需要重新计算一次，如果还是负数，那么将其作为 other
income 计入损益表。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#124

stem_zh:
4. 商誉的减值
商誉没有办法单独去做减值测试，所以必须引入 CGU 的概念，这边建议 CGU
的定义背诵，因为有出现在考官答案当中，一般这个 CGU 也就是子公司自身。
所以对商誉去做减值测试的问题就转换成对子公司去做减值测试的问题。
对子公司去做减值测试是比较子公司的账面价值即子公司 100%的净资产+子公
司 100%的商誉和子公司 100%的 Recoverable amount。如果 CV 大于 RA，那么
子公司就出现了减值损失，再将该减值损失总金额分摊给子公司的各资产，按照
合适的顺序。
 首先分摊给明显损坏的资产
 其次分摊给商誉
 再者分摊给其他未分摊过减值损失的长期资产，按照其账面价值的比例。
如果当初计算 NCI 用的是 FV method，那么计算出来的是 full goodwill，所以直
接将合并报表当中的商誉加进去就好了。减值分录如下：
Dr Group R/E (total impairment * P%)
Dr NCI (total impairment * NCI%)
Cr Goodwill
Cr Other assets
如果当初计算 NCI 用的是 proportion method，那么计算出来的是 partial goodwill，
所以在做减值测试的时候需要先将 partial goodwill 先进行 gross up，先还原成
100%的商誉，即计算出 notional goodwill。减值分录如下：
Dr Group RE (impairment loss of goodwill*P% + impairment loss of other asset
* P%)
Dr NCI (impairment loss of other asset * NCI%)
Cr Goodwill
Cr Other assets
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#125

stem_zh:
5. 计算商誉时对子公司的净资产进行的公允价值调整所产生出来的递延所得税。
资产负债在合并报表当中的 CV 都是其收购日当天的 FV，但是 tax base 是税务
局所认可的子公司单体报表上的数据，所以只要进行了公允价值调整，那么就会
产生暂时性差异，乘上税率，就会形成递延所得税。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#126

stem_zh:
6. 分步收购
从金融资产到子公司，从联营公司到子公司：
 都需要在实现控制之时进行商誉的计算，其公式如下：
 都需要将原先所持有的股份从账面价值调整至公允价值。
从 60%的持股比例到 70%的持股比例：
- In substance, there has been no acquisition because the entity is still a subsidiary.
- Instead this is a transaction between group shareholders.点出属于股东间的交易，
只会影响 equity.
- Note that no profit or loss arises on the purchase of the additional shares. Goodwill
is not recalculated.
分录如下：
Dr NCI (NCI at date of step acquisition * % purchased/NCI% before step acquisition)
Cr Cash
Dr/Cr Equity (β)
年底合并资产负债表当中少数股东权益的金额计算如下：

stem_zh:
7. 分步处置
从子公司到联营公司，从子公司到金融资产：
 都需要进行处置利得或损失的计算，公式如下：
$ $

lost:

从 70%的持股比例到 60%的持股比例：
- There has been no disposal because the entity is still a subsidiary, so no profit on
disposal should be recognised.
- Instead this is a transaction between the equity holders of the group.
- Note that no profit or loss arises on the sale of the shares. Goodwill is not
recalculated.
分录如下：
Dr Cash
Cr NCI

【NCI= (在处置日当天合并报表中子公司净资产账面价值+在处置日当天合并报
表中商誉的账面价值) * 股权变动的百分比】 或者 【NCI= 在处置日当天合并
报表中 NCI 的账面价值 * 股权变动的比例/NCI 本身的持股比例】

stem_zh:
1. 联营公司的判断和定义建议背诵
Associate is an entity over which the investor has significant influence.
Significant influence is power to participate in the financial and operating policy
decisions of the investee but is not control or joint control of those policies.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#129

stem_zh:
2. 对联营公司的投资在合并报表上的账务处理---权益法
在合并资产负债表当中只记录 investment in associate 这一个科目。
在合并综合收益表当中存在 share of profit of associate 和 share of OCI of associate
这两个科目。
The equity method is a method of accounting whereby the investment is initially
recognised at cost and adjusted thereafter for the post-acquisition change in the
investor’s share of the investee’s net assets.
- Investment in associate
Dr Investment in associate
Cr Cash, Share capital, Share premium, Financial liability
- Associate’s profit and loss sharing
Dr Investment in associate Cr Share of profit of associate (P/L)
- Associate’sOCI sharing
Dr Investment in associate
Cr Share of OCI of associate (OCI)
- Unrealised profits (URP)
Dr Cost of sales of P Cr Investment in Associate URP*P% (Downstream P to

["A. Dr Share of profits of A Cr Inventory URP*P% (Upstream A to P)
- Impairment
Dr Impairment loss (P/L) Cr Investment in associate
- Dividend receipt
Dr Cash or Dividend receivable Cr Investment in associate
- De-recognition
Dr Cash Cr Investment in associate Cr/Dr P/L (β)
Dr/Cr OCI Cr/Dr P/L
IFRS 11 joint arrangement"]
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#130

stem_zh:
1. 合营安排的定义：
Joint arrangement is an arrangement of which two or more parties have joint control.
Joint control is the contractually agreed sharing of control of an arrangement, which
exists only when decisions about the relevant activities require the unanimous
consent of the parties sharing control.
Unanimous consent means that any party with joint control of the arrangement can
prevent any of the other parties from making unilateral decisions (about the relevant
activities) without its consent.
分享控制权的参与方都具有一票否则权。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#131

stem_zh:
2. 合影安排分为共同经营和合营企业两种类型。
A joint operation is a joint arrangement whereby the parties that have joint control of
the arrangement have rights to the assets, and obligations for the liabilities, relating to
the arrangement. Those parties are called joint operators.
A joint venture is a joint arrangement whereby the parties that have joint control of
the arrangement have rights to the net assets of the arrangement. Those parties are
called joint venturers.
关于如何区分合营企业和共同经营要理解，共同经营是对被投资方的资产享有权
利，对负债承担义务。而合营企业是对被投资方的净资产享有权利。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#132

stem_zh:
3. 对共同经营和合营企业的账务处理。
A joint operator accounts for the assets, liabilities, revenues and expenses relating to
its involvement in a joint operation with proportion in accordance with the relevant
IFRSs. No adjustments are necessary on consolidation as the figures are already
incorporated correctly into the separate financial statements of the joint operator.
共同经营下，只要被投资方存在资产负债收入费用，投资方都需要将该资产负债
收入费用乘上持股比例之后加到自己的单体报表当中即可。
A joint venture recognizes its interest in a joint venture as an investment and shall
account for that investment using the equity method in accordance with IAS 28
合营企业也是按照权益法进行账务处理，其跟对联营公司的权益法一模一样。
IAS 21 The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#133

stem_zh:
1. Functional currency determination
定 义 : Functional currency is the currency of the primary economic environment in
which the entity operates.
判定条件:
First indicators:
- That mainly influences sales prices for goods and services and
- The currency that mainly influences labor, material and other costs of providing
goods or services.
- The currency of the country whose competitive forces and regulations mainly
determine the sales prices of its goods and services.
Secondary indicators:
- The currency in which funds from financing activities are generated and
- The currency in which receipts from operating activities are retained.
*Other factors include the autonomy of a foreign operation from the reporting entity.
若子公司高度自治，并不依赖于母公司，则可以自行选择 functional currency。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:objective:1.24年9月考纲更新 SBR必背准则.pdf#134

stem_zh:
1. Definition 定义
Sublease – a transaction for which an underlying asset is re-leased by a lessee (“intermediate lessor”)
to a third party, and the lease (“head lease”) between the head lessor and lessee remains in effect.
转租指的是由承租人（“中间出租人”）将标的资产重新出租给第三方，而主出租人和承租
人之间的租赁（“主租赁”）仍然有效的一种交易。
2. Accounting treatment 会计处理
As separate contracts, the head lease and a sublease are accounted for separately. A sublease should
be classified by an intermediate lessor as follows:
- if the head lease is a short-term lease and the entity has elected to apply the recognition exemption,
as an operating lease;
- otherwise, by reference to the right-of-use asset arising from the head lease (not by reference to the
underlying asset), as an operating lease or finance lease (see Example 1).
原本的租赁与转租，按照各自独立的合同分开计量。
出现转租的时候，对于中间出租人来说:
- 如果是短期租赁且公司也选择适用确认豁免，则直接按照 operating lease 经营租赁进行会计
处理；
- 否则，中间出租人将参照主租赁产生的 ROUA 使用权资产（而非标的物资产），将转租作
为经营租赁或融资租赁进行会计处理。(参考 Example 1)
Example 1 Sublease Classification
Head lease: Lin Co (the head lessor 主出租人) enters into a five-year lease for 5,000 square metres of
office space (the head lease 主租赁) with Kor Co(中间出租人).
Sublease examples
(a) At commencement of the head lease, Kor
Co subleases the office space for two years to
Met Co.
主租赁开始时，Kor Co 转租了办公空间给
Met Co，租期 2 年。
(b) At the beginning of year 3, Kor Co subleases
the office space for the remaining three years of
the head lease to Met Co.
在 5 年租期的第 3 年年初，Kor Co 将办公空间
转租给了 Met Co，租期为主租赁的剩余 3 年。
Reference to the right-of-use asset
 Sublease is for two years
 ROU arising from head lease has
useful life of five years
 Conclusion: Sublease is an operating
lease because none of the indicators
of a finance lease are present.
 只租了资产使用年限 5 年中的 2 年，不符
合融资租赁的划分，租期并未占据资产绝大
部分的使用年限，因此划分为经营租赁。
 Sublease is for three years
 ROU arising from head lease has
remaining useful life of three years
 Conclusion: Sublease is a finance
lease after considering the indicators of a
finance lease.
资产剩余可使用年限还剩 3 年，而转租期也为
3 年，租期占据了资产绝大部分的使用年限，
因此划分为融资租赁。
Accounting by Kor Co when entering into sublease
进入转租时的会计处理
 Retain the lease liability and the ROU asset
relating to the head lease in the statement of
financial position.
 经营租赁下，原租赁产生的租赁负债以及
使用权资产继续确认在 SOFP 下。
 Derecognise the ROU asset relating to the head
lease that has been transferred to Met Co and
recognise the net investment in the sublease;
 融资租赁下，需要将原租赁产生的使用权
资产终止确认，因为此资产相关的风险报酬已
经转移给了 Met Co；同时需要确认转租产生的
net investment in lease 转租赁投资净额。
 Recognise any difference between the ROU asset
and the net investment in the sublease in profit
or loss; and
 将使用权资产以及转租赁投资净额之间的
差额计入 SOPL。
 Retain the lease liability relating to the head lease
in the statement of financial position, which
represents the lease payments owed to Lin Co.
 在 SOFP 中保留原租赁产生的租赁负债，
此负债金额依然代表着应付原租赁出租方(Lin
Co)的租赁负债金额。
Accounting by Kor Co during the term of the sublease
转租期内的会计处理
Recognise:
 a depreciation charge for the ROU asset and
interest on the lease liability; and
 经营租赁下，原租赁产生的租赁负债以及
使用权资产正常计算利息费用以及折旧费
用；
 lease income from the sublease.
 同时确认转租产生的租赁收入。
Recognise:
 finance income on the sublease; and
 确认转租产生的租赁收入；
 interest expense on the head lease.
 确认原租赁产生的利息费用；
Following on from Example 1, sublease example (b) :转租下构成融资租赁案例的继续考核
Kor Co pays $80,000 annually in advance to Lin Co, under the head lease commencing 1 January
20X1. The interest rate implicit in the lease is 5% and the initial measurement of the lease liability is
$283,676. Depreciation is calculated on straight line basis over the term of the lease.
From 1 January 20X3, Met Co pay Kor Co $85,000 annually in advance, under the sublease.
Requried:
Explain how Kor Co should account for the sublease.
When entering into the sublease, 1 January 20X3
Derecognise the ROU asset relating to the
head lease that has been transferred to Met Co
and recognise the net investment in the
sublease;
The ROU asset is initially recognised at $363,676
($283,676 + $80,000 租赁负债+初始支付的租
金 ); therefore, its carrying after two years ’
depreciation is $218,206 (3/5 × $363,676). This
must be derecognised.
The net investment in the sublease is $243,050
(W1). 转租赁投资净额其实就是转租下，未
来应收的租金折现值。
Recognise any difference between the ROU
asset and the net investment in the sublease in
profit or loss; and
Dr Net investment in lease $243,050
Cr ROU asset $218,206
Cr Profit or loss $24,844
Retain the lease liability relating to the head
lease in the statement of financial position,
which represents the lease payments owed to
Lin Co.
The lease liability on 1 January 20X3
immediately before the payment due on that
date is $228,753 (W2).
During the term of the sublease, 20X3 - 20X5 转租期内的会计处理
Recognise
 finance income on the sublease; and
 interest expense on the head lease.
- Finance income on the sublease is $7,902 in 20X3
and $4,048 in 20X4 (W3). 转租下的转租赁投资净
额产生的利息收入。
- Interest expense on the head lease is $7,438 in
20X3 and $3,809 in 20X4 (W2). 主租赁的租赁负
债产生的利息费用。
WORKINGS (in $)
 Net investment in the sublease
If the interest rate implicit in the sublease cannot be readily determined, an intermediate lessor may
use the discount rate used for the head lease. The net investment in the sublease is therefore
calculated as:
Date Receivable Discount factor Present value
1-Jan 20X3 85,000 1 85,000
1-Jan 20X4 85,000 1/1.05 80,952
1-Jan 20X5 85,000 ​ 1/1.05^2​ 77,098
243,050
 Lease liability
Year 1 January Payment (1 Jan) Interest @5% 31 December
20X1 283,676 Note 14,184 297,860
20X2 297,860 (80,000) 10,893 228,753
20X3 228,753 (80,000) 7,438 156,191
20X4 156,191 (80,000) 3,809 80,000
20X5 80,000 (80,000)
Tutorial note: The first payment of $80,000 on 1 January 20X1 is not included in the calculation of
the lease liability.
 Finance income
As a lessor, Kor Co recognises finance income over the lease term using the interest rate implicit in
the lease (again using 5% as in W1):
Year 1 January Receipt (1 Jan) Interest income @5% 31 December
20X3 243,050 (85,000) 7,902 165,952
20X4 165,952 (85,000) 4,048 85,000

stem_zh:
IFRS Sustainability Disclosure Standards
From September 2024, the SBR exam will test candidates on the IFRS
Sustainability Disclosure Standards. Candidates will need to be able to use
their knowledge of IFRS S1 and IFRS S2 to assess disclosures of
climate-related risks and opportunities. Candidates may also have to appraise
the factors which impact the usefulness of sustainability-related disclosures.
This article highlights some of the key principles within IFRS S1 and IFRS S2,
which will aid candidates with their understanding of this topic.
- 能够运用 IFRS S1 以及 S2 准则，评估与气候相关的风险和机会的披露情况；
- 能够评估影响可持续相关披露有用性的因素。
Background
In recent years, there has been a growing awareness of sustainability-related
risks and the potential impact which these may have on an entity’s current and
future financial position, performance, and cash flows. Investors require
high-quality information about these risks, as well as potential
sustainability-related opportunities, when making investment decisions.
Although sustainability reporting has become increasingly prominent, the
proliferation of different, voluntary reporting standards and frameworks has
caused fragmentation, and this has limited the usefulness of the information
provided.
近年来，越来越多人意识到与可持续性相关的风险，以及这些风险可能会对企业
当前和未来的财务状况(资产负债表)、业绩(综合性收益表)和现金流(现金流量表)
产生的潜在影响。投资者在做出投资决策时，需要有关这些风险的高质量信息，
以及潜在的与可持续性相关的机会。虽然可持续性报告变得日益突出，但不同的
自愿报告标准以及框架的激增使得信息碎片化，限制了所提供信息的有用性。
The IFRS Foundation believed that the information needs of investors were not
being met. In response to this, they founded the International Sustainability
Standards Board (ISSB), which has the aim of developing a global baseline of
sustainability standards.
IFRS 基金会认为，投资者的信息需求并没有得到满足。因此成立了国际可持续
发展准则委员会(ISSB)，旨在制定可持续发展标准的全球基准。
In 2023, the ISSB issued its first two sustainability disclosure standards. These
are:
 IFRS S1 General Requirements for Disclosure of Sustainability-Related
Financial Information; and;
 IFRS S2 Climate-related Disclosures.
These two standards are included on the list of Examinable Documents for
Strategic Business Reporting (SBR) exams from September 2024 onwards.
23 年 6 月，ISSB 正式发布了首批全球可持续披露准则，分别是:
-《国际财务报告可持续披露准则第 1 号 - 可持续相关财务信息披露一般要求》;
及
-《国际财务报告可持续披露准则第 2 号 - 气候相关披露》。
General requirements 一般要求
Entities apply IFRS S1 when preparing and reporting sustainability-related
financial disclosures. IFRS S1 refers to four areas of core content that entities
should provide in their sustainability-related financial information:
 Governance – the governance processes used to monitor and manage
sustainability-related risks and opportunities.
 Strategy – the approach to managing sustainability-related risks and
opportunities.
 Risk management – the processes used to identify, assess, and monitor
sustainability-related risks and opportunities.
 Metrics and targets – the entity’s performance in relation to
sustainability-related risks and opportunities, including progress towards
any targets set.
企业在编制及报告与可持续相关的财务披露时适用 IFRS S1。IFRS S1 涵盖了四
个核心领域:
(1) 治理 – 用于监测和管理与可持续发展相关的风险和机遇的治理流程。
(2) 战略 – 管理可持续发展相关风险和机遇的方法。
(3) 风险管理 – 用于识别、评估和监控可持续发展相关的风险和机遇的流程。
(4) 指标和目标 – 企业在可持续性相关的风险和机遇方面的表现，包括实现任
何既定目标的进展。
As well as applying the principles in IFRS S1, entities must refer to other
issued IFRS Sustainability Disclosure Standards when disclosing information
about specific sustainability-related risks and opportunities. This means that
entities must use IFRS S2 when preparing disclosures of climate-related risks
and opportunities.
除了应用 IFRS S1 中的原则外，企业在披露与可持续性相关的特定风险、机遇
的信息时，还必须参考其他已发布的 IFRS 可持续性披露标准。这就意味着，在
准备气候相关风险和机遇的披露时，企业也必须使用 IFRS S2。
As no further IFRS Sustainability Disclosure Standards have yet been issued,
sustainability-related disclosures related to non-climate issues must be
prepared using the principles in IFRS S1 only. The ISSB will eventually draft,
consult on, and issue a suite of other sustainability disclosure standards but, in
the meantime, IFRS S1 requires users to consider the disclosure topics
covered in standards issued by the Sustainability Accounting Standards Board
(referred to as the SASB Standards) and to assess whether they are
applicable.
由于尚未发布进一步的可持续性披露标准，与非气候问题相关的可持续性披露则
只能使用 IFRS S1 中的原则进行编制。ISSB 最终将起草、咨询并发布一套其他
可持续性披露准则，但与此同时, IFRS S1 要求用户考虑可持续性会计准则委员
会发布的标准(SASB 标准)中涵盖的披露主题，并评估这些标准是否适用。
IFRS S1 does permit entities to omit information about sustainability-related
opportunities from sustainability-related financial disclosures if that information
is commercially sensitive. However, the exemption does not apply to any
information that is already publicly available, neither does it apply to
sustainability-related risks.
IFRS S1 确实允许企业在与可持续性相关的财务披露中省略与可持续性相关的
机遇信息，如果这些信息是商业敏感的。然而，这项豁免并不适用于任何已经公
开获得的信息，也不适用于与可持续发展相关的风险。
Interconnectedness 互联性
There are several similarities between the principles and concepts in IFRS
Sustainability Disclosure Standards and in IFRS Accounting Standards.
IFRS 可持续性披露准则和 IFRS 国际会计准则，在原理及概念方面，是有一些
相似之处的。
Both sets of standards use the same two fundamental characteristics of useful
information (relevance and faithful representation), as well as the same four
enhancing qualitative characteristics (comparability, verifiability, timeliness,
and understandability).
两套准则都使用同样的质量性特征：判断信息有用性的基础属性(相关性及公允
陈述)；以及加强属性(可比性、可验证性、及时性以及可理解性)
The definition of materiality across the two sets of standards is also the same:
‘Information is material if omitting, misstating or obscuring that information
could reasonably be expected to influence decisions that the primary users of
general-purpose financial reports make on the basis of those reports.’
两套准则对于重要性的定义也是相同的：
“如果可以合理地预期漏报、错报或模糊/掩盖信息会影响财务报告使用者做决策，
则该信息是重要的。”
Both sets of standards identify the same primary user of financial reports:
current and potential investors, lenders, and other creditors.
两套准则也都针对的是同样的财报使用者: 当前及潜在的投资者、贷款人以及其
他的债权人。
IFRS S1 specifies that sustainability-related financial disclosures must be
reported at the same time as the related financial statements. The reporting
period for the sustainability-related information must also be the same as for
the financial statements. Both sets of standards require the disclosure of
comparative information.
IFRS S1 规定，与可持续性相关的财务披露必须与相关财务报表同时对外报告。
与可持续性有关的资料的报告区间也必须与财务报表相同。两套准则都要求披露
可比较的信息。
The treatment of material, prior period errors in sustainability-related financial
information is also based on the same principle as in IFRS Accounting
Standards: these errors must be corrected retrospectively by restating
comparative amounts for the prior period, unless this is impractical.
针对可持续性相关的财务信息中的重大前期错误的处理，采用与 IFRS 会计准则
相同的原则：除非不切实际，否则必须追溯调整这些错误。
Climate-related disclosures 与气候相关的披露
Risks and opportunities 风险与机遇
The SBR syllabus for exams from September 2024 onwards requires
candidates to assess disclosures of climate-related risks and opportunities.
Entities apply IFRS S2 when producing climate-related disclosures. The
purpose of these disclosures is to provide information about climate-related
risks and opportunities that the primary users of its financial reports would find
useful.
企业在进行气候相关的披露时适用 IFRS S2。与气候相关的披露的目的：是为了
给财报使用者提供有用的气候相关的风险及机遇的信息。
Climate-related risks can be separated into two broad categories:
 physical risks, and
 transition risks.
气候相关的风险主要分为两大类：物理风险以及转型风险。
Physical risks result from weather-related events, such as floods or droughts.
They also result from longer term climatic changes; for example, rising
temperatures may lead to rising sea levels as well as a loss of biodiversity.
物理风险来自与天气相关的事件，比如洪水或是干旱。它们也是长期气候变化的
结果；例如，气温上升可能导致海平面上升以及生物多样性的丧失。
Transition risks result from the move towards a lower-carbon economy.
转型风险则源于向低碳经济的转变。
Entities are required to provide disclosures about its governance, so that the
users of its financial reports can understand how climate-related risks and
opportunities are identified, monitored, and managed.
企业被要求披露其治理情况，以便其财务报告使用者能够了解如何识别、监测以
及管理与气候相关的风险和机遇。
When identifying sustainability-related risks and opportunities, entities must
use all reasonable and supportable information that is available to them
without undue cost of effort. Data sources can be internal or external, and
might include risk management processes, external reports or statistics, and
industry experience. Assessments of what constitutes undue cost or effort will
change over time and depend on the benefits of the information provided to the
users.
在识别与可持续性相关的风险和机遇时，各企业必须充分利用其可获得的所有合
理和可支持的信息，且无需付出过度努力。数据来源可以是内部的，也可以是外
部的，可能包括风险管理流程、外部报告或统计数据以及行业经验。对所谓过度
成本或努力的评估将随着时间的推移而变化，并取决于向用户提供的信息的益处。
The following is an example of a disclosure of some of the climate-related risks
and opportunities facing an international restaurant business:
以下是国际餐饮企业面临的一些与气候相关的风险和机遇的披露示例:
Physical risks 物理风险
Our business model relies on large quantities of raw materials. Increased
levels of flooding due to climate change has affected the quantity and quality of
several key ingredients. This leads to price volatility, which reduces profit
margins.
我们的商业模式依赖于大量的原材料。由于气候变化导致洪水泛滥，影响了几种
关键成分的数量和质量。这将导致价格波动，从而使得利润率降低。
Restaurants in some locations are heavily impacted by water shortages and
chronic weather events. These events may cause temporary closures but can
also increase the costs of insurance premiums.
一些地域的餐馆则受到缺水和长期天气事件的严重影响。这些事件可能会导致餐
馆的临时关闭，从而导致保险费用的增加。
Transition risks 转型风险
Climate-related laws and regulations are regularly changing. Our restaurants
operate in more than 90 countries around the world. Compliance costs are
high. Moreover, increased regulation can result in higher operational costs,
such as in relation to the cost of energy.
与气候相关的法律法规定期会发生变化。我们的餐厅遍布全球 90 多个国家。合
规成本非常高。此外，增加监管可能导致更高的运营成本，例如与能源相关的运
营成本。
Many of our customers pay close attention to our climate-related strategies
and targets. If these are deemed insufficient, then our brand value will decline.
Reputational damage will impact our market share and our share price.
我们的许多客户都在密切关注我们与气候相关的战略和目标。如果这些被认为不
够充分的话，我们的品牌价值则会随之下降。声誉受损会直接影响我们的市场份
额以及股价。
Opportunities 机遇
To achieve our goals, we are pursuing more efficient operations through
investment in new technologies. In the long-term, we expect a significant
decline in energy-usage, with a resulting increase in reported profit margins.
为了实现我们的目标，我们正在通过投资新技术来实现更高效的运营。长远看来，
预计能源使用量将会大幅下降，从而提高利润率。
With regards to identified risks and opportunities, the entity would need to
disclose the impact on its strategy and decision making. It must also outline the
effect of the risks and opportunities on its financial position, financial
performance, and cash flows in the current reporting period, as well as the
anticipated effect in the short, medium, and long term. To help users assess
the adaptability of the entity, information must also be provided about the
resilience of the entity’s strategy and business model to climate-related
changes. This includes providing information about climate-related scenario
analysis that the entity has carried out.
针对已识别的风险和机遇，企业需要披露其对战略和决策的影响。此外还必须概
述风险和机遇对于当期财务状况、财务业绩和现金流的影响，以及短期、中期和
长期的预期影响。为了帮助使用者评估企业的适应程度，还必须提供有关企业战
略和商业模式对气候相关变化的适应性的信息。这包括提供有关企业已进行的气
候相关情景分析的信息。
Metrics 指标
Entities are required to disclose metrics and targets that will help the primary
users understand its performance in relation to climate-related risks and
opportunities. IFRS S2 states that this involves providing information about
any climate-related targets that the entity has set, as well as information about
its greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions.
企业需要披露与气候相关的风险和机遇的绩效指标以及目标，使其有助于主要使
用者了解相关信息。IFRS S2 指出，这涉及到提供企业已经设定的气候相关目标
的信息，包括对于其温室气体排放的信息。
Greenhouse gas emissions 温室气体排放
In accordance with IFRS S2, entities are required to disclose gross GHG gas
emissions. This disclosure must be broken down into Scope 1, Scope 2, and
Scope 3 emissions. These are defined as follows:
根据 IFRS S2，各企业都被要求披露 GHG 温室气体排放总量。此披露必须细分
为范围 1、范围 2 以及范围 3 排放。
 Scope 1 – direct GHG emissions that occur from sources that are owned or
controlled by an entity.
范围 1 - 由企业拥有或控制的来源产生的直接温室气体排放
 Scope 2 – indirect GHG gas emissions from the generation of purchased or
acquired electricity, steam, heating, or cooling consumed by an entity.
范围 2 - 企业购买或获得的电力、蒸汽、加热或冷却消耗所产生的间接温室气体
排放
 Scope 3 – indirect GHG emissions (not included in Scope 2) that occur in
the value chain of an entity.
范围 3 - 企业价值链中产生的间接温室气体排放(不包含在范围 2 中)
An example disclosure of gross GHG emissions is provided below:
GHG 总排放量披露示例如下:
GHG emissions (metric ton CO2e) 20X6 20X5
Scope 1 GHG Emissions 1,153,352 1,045,243
Scope 2 GHG Emissions 4,534,212 4,734,243
Scope 3 GHG Emissions 7,432,435 7,875,432
This disclosure is important because it helps users of the sustainability-related
financial information assess the entity’s exposure to climate-related risks and
opportunities. Entities with higher GHG emissions are potentially more
exposed to climate-related transition risk because they will be more heavily
impacted by legal, technological, market and reputational risks. In particular,
shifting consumer demands could lead to severe reputational damage for
entities that do not make sufficient progress in reducing their GHG emissions.
此披露很重要，因为它有助于可持续性相关的财务信息使用者评估企业对于气候
相关风险和机会的曝光情况。GHG 排放量较高的企业可能更容易面临与气候相
关的转型风险，因为它们会受到法律、技术、市场和声誉风险的更大影响。特别
是，消费者需求的变化可能会对那些在减少 GHG 排放方面没有取得足够进展的
企业造成严重的声誉损害。
For some industries, Scope 3 emissions can account for almost 90% of total
GHG emissions. For this reason, Scope 3 emissions are sometimes perceived
as a hidden risk – especially if undisclosed. The requirement in IFRS S2 to
disclose Scope 3 GHG emissions therefore gives a more complete picture of
an entity’s overall carbon impact, and it also enables investors to compare the
carbon-impact of entities with different business models (such as those which
heavily outsource activities and those that do not).
对于某些行业，范围 3 的排放几乎占 GHG 总排放量的 90%。因此，范围 3 的
排放有时被视为隐藏的风险 - 特别是在未披露的情况下。因此, IFRS S2 要求披
露范围 3 - GHG 排放量，这才能更全面地反映一个企业的总体碳影响，也使投
资者能够针对具有不同业务模式的企业的碳影响进行相互比较(如大量外包活动
的企业和没有外包活动的企业)。
It can be difficult to measure GHG emissions, particularly those falling into
Scope 3, and many different methodologies exist. For this reason, IFRS S2
requires entities to measure GHG emissions in accordance with Greenhouse
Gas Protocol: A Corporate Accounting and Reporting Standard (2004), unless
an entity is legally required to use a different method. This consistent approach
reduces the risk of bias, helping to ensure a faithful representation of the
underlying phenomena. There are also some exemptions and simplifications
for entities in the first year of applying IFRS S2.
GHG 排放量并不容易测量，特别是属于范围 3 的排放量，而且存在许多不同的
方法。因此, IFRS S2 要求各企业按照《温室气体协议:公司会计和报告标准(2004
年)》进行温室气体排放量的测量，除非法律要求某个企业使用不同的方法。这
种一致的方法降低了产生偏见的风险，有助于确保对潜在现象的公允陈述。在应
用 IFRS S2 的第一年，对企业也会有一些豁免和简化。
Targets 目标
IFRS S2 requires entities to provide information about the climate-related
targets it has set. Below is an example of an extract from a disclosure note for
an international hotel business:
IFRS S2 要求各企业提供有关其设定的气候相关目标的信息。以下是一家国际酒
店企业的披露说明示例:
The company aims to reduce water consumption per occupied room by 20%
from a 20X0 baseline by the end of 20X9. By the end of 20X6, the reduction in
water consumption per occupied room since 20X0 was 8.4%. We are behind
schedule on this goal but continue to explore opportunities to reduce water
consumption.
公司目标是在 20X9 年底的时候，将每个被占用房间的用水量从 20X0 年的基准
线上减少 20%。截止到 20X6 年底，每个被占用房间的用水量自 20X0 年减少了
8.4%。公司在这一目标上落后于计划，但仍将继续探索减少用水量的机会。
The company plans to reduce net carbon dioxide emissions by 30% from a
20X0 baseline by the end of 20X9. To achieve this, we will reduce gross
emissions by 10%, while also making use of carbon credits. These credits are
generated from projects that use technology to capture carbon dioxide from
the atmosphere before storing it safely underground. The carbon credits have
been independently verified by the Global Carbon Council. By the end of 20X6,
net and gross carbon dioxide emissions had reduced by 24% and 2%
respectively. We are behind schedule on our gross target but have
implemented a new executive management share-based payment scheme
which will vest if the goal is achieved.
公司计划到 20X9 年底，将二氧化碳净排放量从 20X0 年的基准线上减少 30%。
为了实现这一目标，将减少 10%的总排放量，同时也会利用碳信用额度辅助达
成目标。这些碳信用额度来源于一些项目，这些项目利用技术从大气中捕捉二氧
化碳，然后将其安全地储存在地下。碳信用额度已经由全球碳理事会独立验证。
截至到 20X6 年底，二氧化碳净排放量和总排放量分别减少了 24%和 2%。公司
的总目标落后于计划，但我们已经实施了一个新的基于高管持股的薪酬计划，如
果目标实现，将授予该计划。(拓展: 碳信用 carbon credit 又称碳权，指在经过
联合国或联合国认可的减排组织认证的条件下，国家或企业以增加能源使用效
率、减少污染或减少开发等方式减少碳排放，因此得到可以进入碳交易市场的碳
排放计量单位。)
If entities disclose a net GHG emissions target, IFRS S2 also requires
disclosure of the gross target. This is because a reduction in net GHG
emissions can be achieved without making changes to the entity’s business
model, for instance, through the purchase of carbon credits.
如果企业披露净 GHG 排放目标，IFRS S2 也要求披露总目标。这是因为 GHG
净排放量的减少可以在不改变实体商业模式的情况下实现，例如，通过购买碳信
用额度。
IFRS S2 defines a carbon credit as ‘an emissions unit that is issued by a
carbon crediting programme and represents an emission reduction or removal
of greenhouse gases.’ IFRS S2 requires entities to disclose their planned use
of carbon credits to achieve any net greenhouse gas emissions target.
Disclosure of the planned use of carbon credits in achieving climate-related
goals is important because some carbon capture technologies may prove
ineffective, or there might be a change in regulations that ban entities from
using certain schemes. The prices of carbon credits may also change
significantly, which will impact these plans and, therefore, the entity’s ability to
meet its targets.
IFRS S2 将碳信用定义为“由碳信用计划发布的排放单位，代表着温室气体的减
排或清除。” IFRS S2 要求各企业披露其为实现温室气体净排放目标而计划使用
的碳信用额。披露碳信用额在实现气候相关目标方面的计划使用非常重要，因为
一些碳捕获技术可能被证明是无效的，或者禁止企业使用某些方案的法规可能会
发生变化。碳信用的价格也可能会发生重大变化，这将影响这些计划，并因此影
响企业实现其目标的能力。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:3.SBR-可持续发展披露相关的考官文章解析（新）.pdf

stem_zh:
内部资料，仅供交流学习
For the SBR INT, UK and IRL exam, knowledge of the differences between
IFRS Sustainability Disclosure Standards and European Sustainability
Reporting Standards is required in the following areas:
IFRS Sustainability
Disclosure Standards (ISSB

stem_zh:
Management commentary - Definition
Management commentary: a narrative report that relates to financial statements that
have been prepared in accordance with IFRSs. Management commentary provides
users with historical explanations of the amounts presented in the financial
statements, specifically entity's financial position, financial performance and cash
flows. It also provides commentary on an entity's prospects and other information
not presented in the financial statements. Management commentary also serves as a
basis for understanding management's objectives and its strategies for achieving those
objectives.
IFRS Practice Statement
The IFRS Practice Statement is a non -binding guidance document rather than an IFRS.
It is intended to be applied by entities that present management commentary that
relates to financial statements prepared in accordance with IFRSs. It is designed for
publicly traded entities, but it is left to regulators to decide which entities are required to
publish management commentary and how frequently they should report.
This approach avoids the adoption hurdle, i.e that the perceived cost of applying
IFRSs increases. This perceived extra cost could dissuade jurisdictions/ countries
that have not already adopted IFRS from requiring its adoption, especially where
IFRS requirements differ significantly from existing national requirements.
Principles for the preparation of management commentary
Management should present commentary that is consistent with the following
principles from the IFRS Practice Statement:
- To provide management's view of the entity's performance, position and progress;
and
- To supplement and complement information presented in the financial statements.
In aligning with these principles, management commentary should include:
- Forward-looking information (including prospective results); and
- Information that possesses the qualitative characteristics described in the
Conceptual Framework for Financial Reporting.
Presentation
The form and content of management commentary will vary between entities,
reflecting the nature of their business, the strategies adopted by management
and the regulatory environment in which they operate.
Therefore, the Practice Statement does not require a fixed format, nor does it
provide application guidance or illustrative examples, as this could be interpreted as a
floor or ceiling for disclosures.
Elements of management commentary
The particular focus of management commentary will depend on the facts and
circumstances of the entity. However, the Practice Statement requires a management
commentary to include information that is essential to an understanding of:
- The nature of the business
- Management's objectives and its strategies for meeting those
objectives
- The entity's most significant resources, risks and relationships
- The results of operations and prospects
- The critical performance measures and indicators that management uses to
evaluate the entity's performance against stated objectives.
Link to Conceptual Framework for Financial Reporting
The Practice Statement states that management commentary should include
information that possesses the qualitative characteristics of useful financial
information. In other word, the information should be relevant, faithfully
represented, comparable, timely, verifiable and understandable.
Management commentary provides users with information about risk management,
as well as the extent to which current performance may be indicative of future
performance. This forward-looking information is relevant because it helps users
to make decisions about whether to hold or sell investments in an entity.
To enhance relevance, management commentary should include material
information and should focus on the most important information. Generic
information is not relevant and should be avoided.
To maximize understandability, management commentary should be presented in a
clear and straightforward manner.
When selecting key performance measures, management should use those that are
accepted and used widely within the industry. This will enable users to draw
comparisons between entities. Management should calculate and report performance
measures consistently over time, thus enabling users to compare the performance of
the entity year -on-year.
If information from the financial statements is adjusted for inclusion in
management commentary, then this fact should be disclosed. Financial performance
measures should be reconciled to the figures in the financial statements. Users are
therefore able to verify the nature of the calculations. They can also assess whether
the performance measures included offer a faithful presentation of the entity’s
financial performance and position.
PAST EXAMS: 2018-12-Q4(a)
The IFRS Practice Statement Management Commentary provides a broad, non -
binding framework for the presentation of management commentary which
relates to financial statements which have been prepared in accordance with IFRS
Standards. The management commentary is within the scope of the Conceptual
Framework and, therefore, the qualitative characteristics will be applied to both
the financial statements and the management commentary.
Required:
(i)Discuss briefly the arguments for and against issuing the IFRS Practice Statement
Management Commentary as a non -binding framework or as an IFRS Standard. (4
marks)
(ii)Discuss how the qualitative characteristics of understandability, relevance and
comparability should be applied to the preparation of the management commentary. (5
marks)
Suggested answer:
(a) (i) The IFRS Practice Statement Management Commentary provides a broad, non -
binding framework for the presentation of management commentary. The Practice
Statement is not an IFRS Standard. Consequently, entities applying IFRS Standards
are not required to comply with the Practice Statement, unless specifically required by
their jurisdiction. Furthermore, non -compliance with the Practice Statement will
not prevent an entity’s financial statements from complying with IFRS Standards.
It can be argued that the International Accounting Standards Board’s (the Board)
objectives of enhancing consistency and comparability may not be achieved if the
framework is not mandatory. A standard is more likely to guarantee a consistent
application of the principles and practices behind the management commentary.
However, it is difficult to create a standard on the MC which is sufficiently detailed
to cover the business models of every entity or be consistent with all IFRS Standards.
Some jurisdictions take little notice of non -mandatory guidance but the Practice
Statement provides regulators with a framework to develop more authoritative
requirements.
The Practice Statement allows companies to adapt the information provided to
particular aspects of their business. This flexible approach could help generate
more meaningful disclosures about resources, risks and relationships which can affect
an entity’s value and how these resources are managed. It provides management with
an opportunity to add context to the published financial information, and to explain
their future strategy and objectives without being restricted by the constraints of a
standard.
If the MC were a full IFRS Standard, the integration of management commentaries
and the information produced in accordance with IFRS Standards could be
challenged on technical grounds, as well as its practical merits. In addition, there
could be jurisdictional concerns that any form of integration might not be accepted by
local regulators.
(ii) The Framework states that ‘an essential quality of the information provided in
financial statements is that it is readily understandable by users’. The MC should be
written in plain language and a style appropriate to users’ needs. The primary
users of management commentary are those identified in the Conceptual Framework.
The form and content of the MC will vary between entities, reflecting the nature of
their business, the strategies adopted and the regulatory environment in which they
operate. Users should be able to locate information relevant to their needs.
Information has the quality of relevance when it has the capacity to influence the
economic decisions of users by helping them evaluate past, present or future events
or confirming, or correcting, their past evaluations. Relevant financial information
is capable of making a difference to the decision made by users. In order to make a
difference, financial information has predictive value, confirmatory value or both. The
onus is on management to determine what information is important enough to be
included in the MC to enable users to ‘understand’ the financial statements and
meet the objective of the MC. If the entity provides too much information, it could
reduce its relevance and understandability. If material events or uncertainties are not
disclosed, then users may have insufficient information to meet their needs.
However, unnecessary detail may obscure important information especially if entities
adopt a boiler -plate approach. If management presents too much information about,
for example, all the risks facing an organisation, this will conflict with the relevance
objective. There is no single optimal number of disclosures but it is useful to
convey their relative importance in a meaningful way.
Comparability is the qualitative characteristic which enables users to identify and
understand similarities and differences amongst items. It is important for users to be
able to compare information over time and between entities. Comparability between
entities is problematic as the MC is designed to reflect the perspectives of
management and the circumstances of individual entities. Thus, entities in the same
industry may have different perceptions of what is important and how they measure
and report it. There are some precedents on how to define and calculate non -
financial measures and financial measures which are not produced in accordance
with IFRS Standards but there are inconsistencies in the definition and
calculation of these measures.
It is sometimes suggested that the effectiveness of the overall report may be
enhanced by strengthening the links between financial statements and the MC.
However, such suggestions raise concerns about maintaining a clear distinction
between the financial statement information and other information. An entity should
ensure consistency in terms of wording, definitions, segment disclosures, etc between
the financial statements and the MC to improve the understanding of financial
performance.
