都不能重分类。但是，境外业务净投资套期或是现金流套期当中的有效部分，包
括处置外币子公司时，前期累积的汇兑损益，则可以重分类到 PL。
Arguments for and against reclassification
There are arguments for and against reclassification. If reclassification ceased, then
there would be no need to define profit or loss, or any other total or subtotal in profit
or loss, and any presentation decisions could be left to specific IFRS Standards. It is
argued that reclassification protects the integrity of the statement of profit or loss
and provides users with relevant information about a transaction that occurred in the
period. Additionally, it can improve comparability where IFRS Standards permit
similar items to be recognised in either profit or loss or OCI.
对于是否需要重分类，其实是存在争议的。如果停止重分类，那么就没必要去界
定利润或损失，或利润或损失的任何其他总额或小计了，任何列报决定都可以留
给具体的国际会计准则来决定。有人认为，重分类是为了保护损益表的完整性，
并为用户提供该期间发生的交易的相关信息，同时可以增加可比性。
Arguments against reclassification include that the reclassified amounts add to the
complexity of financial reporting, may lead to earnings management, and the
reclassification adjustments may not meet the definitions of income or expense in the
period as the change in the asset or liability may have occurred in a previous period.
持反对意见的人则认为，重分类增加了财务报告的复杂性，可能会引起盈余管理，
同时重分类调整可能并不符合该期间收入或费用的定义，因为资产或负债的变化
可能发生在以前的期间。
The original logic for OCI was that it kept income-relevant items that possessed low
reliability from contaminating the earnings number. Markets rely on profit or loss and
it is widely used. The OCI figure is crucial because it can distort common valuation
techniques used by investors, such as the price/earnings ratio. Thus, profit or loss
needs to contain all information relevant to investors. Misuse of OCI would
undermine the credibility of net income.
OCI 最初的逻辑其实是为了防止可靠性低的相关收入项目污染收益数据。市场依
赖于利润或损失数据，它被广泛使用着。OCI 数据至关重要，因为它会扭曲投资
者常用的估值技术，比如市盈率。因此，损益需要包含与投资者相关的所有信息。
错误使用 OCI 会破坏净收入的可信度。
Accounting mismatches
An accounting mismatch occurs when assets and liabilities that are economically
related are treated inconsistently for financial reporting purposes. For example, this
could occur when an investment in another entity’s debt instruments is held in a
business model whose objective is achieved by both collecting contractual cash flows
and selling some of the debt instruments, with changes in fair value recognised
directly in OCI, while a related liability is measured at amortised cost with changes in
fair value not recognised. In such circumstances, an entity may conclude that its
financial statements would provide more relevant information if both the asset and the
liability were measured at fair value through profit or loss.
当出于财务报告目的而对相关的资产和负债进行不一致的处理时，就会出现会计
错配。比如，当投资某个债务工具获得了金融资产，并且商业模型的目的是通过
收集合同现金流和出售一些债务工具来实现的，那么应该按照 FVTOCI 来计量，
而相关负债以摊余成本法计量，公允价值的变化不被确认。在这种情况下，公司
可以得出结论，如果资产和负债都以 FVTPL 计量，财务报表可以提供更多相关信
息。
A mismatched remeasurement arises where an item of income or expense represents
an economic phenomenon so incompletely that presenting that item in profit or loss
would provide information that has little relevance in assessing the entity’s financial
performance. An example of this is when a derivative is used to hedge a forecast
transaction as part of a cash flow hedge; changes in the fair value of the derivative
may arise before the income or expense resulting from the forecast transaction are
recognised. Before the results of the derivative and the hedged item can be matched
together, any gains or losses resulting from the remeasurement of the derivative, to
the extent that the hedge is effective and qualifies for hedge accounting as a cash flow
hedge, should be reported in OCI. Subsequently, those gains or losses are reclassified
into profit or loss when the forecast transaction affects profit or loss. This allows users
to see the results of the hedging relationship.
会计错配的重新计量通常是由于收入或者费用呈现的经济现象不完整，以至于列
示在损益表当中的项目与企业业绩评估相关性并不大。比如，当衍生工具作为现
金流对冲的一部分用于对冲预测交易时；在确认预测交易产生的收入或费用之前，
衍生工具的公允价值可能会发生变化。在套期工具和被套期项目的结果可以匹配
在一起之前，如果套期有效且符合作为现金流套期进行套期会计的条件，则衍生
工具重新计量产生的任何收益或损失应记录在 OCI 中。之后，当预测交易影响损
益的时候，这些收益或损失可以被重分类为损益。这样可以允许用户查看套期关
系的结果。
Users’ views
Some users think that OCI is confusing and see it as a black hole or ‘dumping ground’
for anything that is an accounting issue. There is a lack of clarity among users about
the roles of profit or loss and OCI, and when OCI items should or should not be
reclassified to profit or loss. A common misunderstanding is that the distinction is
based upon realised versus unrealised gains. This lack of a consistent basis for
determining how items should be presented has led to an inconsistent use of OCI in
IFRS Standards. It may be difficult to deal with OCI on a conceptual level since the
IASB themselves are finding it difficult to find a sound conceptual basis.
一些用户认为 OCI 令人困惑，并将其视为一个黑洞或任何会计问题的“垃圾场”。
用户不太清楚损益和 OCI 的作用，也不清楚 OCI 项目何时应该或不应该重新归类
为损益。一个常见的误解是，这种区别是基于已实现收益和未实现收益。由于缺
乏项目应该如何列报的一致基础，导致国际会计准则中，对其他综合性收益的使
用存在不一致。在概念层面上处理 OCI 可能很困难，因为 IASB 自己也发现找到
一个可靠的概念基础是比较困难的。
Many users do not analyse OCI items in detail because of a lack of understanding of
OCI or because they do not consider them to be operating cash flows from which they
can predict long-term trends. As a result, it can be argued that improving the
presentation of OCI would not provide additional relevant information for their
analysis.
许多用户不详细分析 OCI 项目，因为缺乏对 OCI 的了解，或者由于他们不认为
OCI 可以用来预测长期的经营现金流走向。因此，可以认为，改进 OCI 的列报并
不会为他们的分析提供额外的相关信息。
There is a general lack of agreement about which items should be presented in profit
or loss and in OCI. This is especially true of the principles behind reclassification
which includes the logic of when and which OCI items should be reclassified. Users
are confused by the lack of consistency and of a conceptual basis for the use of OCI in
IFRS Standards. As a result, some users may feel that OCI is used to report
controversial items. Users may pay less attention to OCI because some preparers give
it less prominence than profit or loss as some entities provide less disaggregation and
explanatory disclosures about items of OCI than items of profit or loss. This results in
poor presentation and insufficient disclosures for OCI items.
对于哪些项目应在损益和 OCI 中列报，普遍缺乏一致意见。重分类背后的原则尤
其如此，特别是何时以及哪些 OCI 项目应该重新分类的逻辑。由于国际会计准则
中使用 OCI 缺乏一致性和概念基础，用户对此感到困惑。因此，一些用户可能会
觉得 OCI 是用来报告有争议的项目的。用户可能不太关注其他综合收益，因为一
些编制者对其的重视程度低于损益，部分企业提供的其他综合性收益项目的分类
和解释性披露也少于损益项目，这导致 OCI 项目的列报和披露都存在不足。
Many users are thought to ignore OCI as the changes reported are generally not
caused by the operating cash flows which can be assessed from other parts of the
financial statements. Similarly, users may not analyse OCI items in detail either
because of a lack of understanding of OCI or because they do not consider them to be
operating cash flows from which they can infer long-term trends.
许多使用者被认为忽略了 OCI，因为所报告的变化通常不是由经营现金流引起的，
而经营现金流可以从财务报表的其他部分进行评估。同样，用户可能不会详细分
析 OCI 项目，要么是因为缺乏对 OCI 的了解，要么是因为他们不认为它们是可以
从中推断长期经营现金流的走向的。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:Section B - Profit, loss and other comprehensive income.pdf

stem_zh:
Deferred tax
The basics
Deferred tax is accounted for in accordance with IAS® 12, Income Taxes. In FR, deferred tax
normally results in a liability being recognised within the Statement of Financial Position.
IAS 12 defines a deferred tax liability as being the amount of income tax payable in future
periods in respect of taxable temporary differences. So, in simple terms, deferred tax is tax
that is payable in the future. However, to understand this definition more fully, it is necessary
to explain the term ‘taxable temporary differences’.
递延所得税是根据 IAS 12 所得税准则进行计量的。在 FR 当中，递延所得税通常作为
负债确认进资产负债表里。IAS 12 将递延所得税负债定义为由于应纳税暂时性差异引起
的未来需要支付的税项。因此，简单来说，递延税是在未来应付的税。但是，为了更全
面地理解这一定义，有必要先解释一下什么是“应纳税暂时性差异”。
Temporary differences are defined as being differences between the carrying amount of an
asset (or liability) within the Statement of Financial Position and its tax base ie the amount at
which the asset (or liability) is valued for tax purposes by the relevant tax authority.
暂时性差异被定义为财务状况表中资产(或负债)的账面金额与其税基(即相关税务机关
出于税务目的对资产(或负债)进行估价的金额)之间的差异。
Taxable temporary differences are those on which tax will be charged in the future when the
asset (or liability) is recovered (or settled).
应纳税暂时性差异是指未来收回(或结算)资产(或负债)时将被征税的暂时性差异。
IAS 12 requires that a deferred tax liability is recorded in respect of all taxable temporary
differences that exist at the year-end – this is sometimes known as the full provision method.
IAS 12 要求在年末记录所有应纳税暂时性差异引起的递延所得税负债入账，该方法也被
称为全额计提法。
All of this terminology can be rather overwhelming and difficult to understand, so consider it
alongside an example. Depreciable non-current assets are the typical deferred tax example
used in FR.
所有这些术语都可能会让人不知所措，难以理解，所以结合例子更有助于理解。需折旧
的非流动资产是 FR 中递延所得税会使用到的典型案例。
Within financial statements, non-current assets with a limited useful life are subject to
depreciation. However, within tax computations, non-current assets are subject to capital
allowances (also known as tax depreciation) at rates set within the relevant tax legislation.
Where at the year-end the cumulative depreciation charged and the cumulative capital
allowances claimed are different, the carrying amount of the asset (cost less accumulated
depreciation) will then be different to its tax base (cost less accumulated capital allowances)
and hence a taxable temporary difference arises.
在财务报表中，使用寿命有限的非流动资产需要折旧。但是，在税务计算中，非流动资
产按照相关税法规定的利率享受资本减免(也称为税务折旧)。如果年末确认的累计折旧
和要求的累计税务折旧不同，资产的账面金额(成本减去累计折旧)将不同于其税基(成本
减去累计税务折旧)，因此产生应纳税暂时性差异。
EXAMPLE 1
A non-current asset costing $2,000 was acquired at the start of year 1. It is being depreciated
straight line over four years, resulting in annual depreciation charges of $500. Thus a total of
$2,000 of depreciation is being charged. The capital allowances granted on this asset are:
在第一年开始的时候，获得了一个价值 2,000 美元的非流动资产。它在四年内按照直线
法折旧，每年的折旧费为 500 美元。因此，总共会确认 2,000 美元的折旧费。该资产的
资本减免为:
Table 1 shows the carrying amount of the asset, the tax base of the asset and therefore the
temporary difference at the end of each year.
表 1(下方表格)显示了资产的账面价值、计税基础以及每年年末的暂时性差异。
As stated above, deferred tax liabilities arise on taxable temporary differences, ie those
temporary differences that result in tax being payable in the future as the temporary difference
reverses. So, how does the above example result in tax being payable in the future?
如上所述，递延税项负债产生于应纳税暂时性差异，即当暂时性差异转回时，会导致未
来应纳税的暂时性差异。那么，上面的例子是如何导致将来要缴税的呢？
Entities pay income tax on their taxable profits. When determining taxable profits, the tax
authorities start by taking the profit before tax (accounting profits) of an entity from their
financial statements and then make various adjustments. For example, depreciation is
considered a disallowable expense for taxation purposes but instead tax relief on capital
expenditure is granted in the form of capital allowances. Therefore, taxable profits are arrived
at by adding back depreciation and deducting capital allowances from the accounting
profits. Entities are then charged tax at the appropriate tax rate on these taxable profits.
公司是对根据应税利润来计算并缴纳所得税的。在确定应纳税利润时，税务机关首先从
财务报表中提取一个公司的税前利润(会计利润)，然后进行各种调整。例如，出于税收
目的，折旧是不允许抵扣的费用，但资本支出的税收减免是以资本减免的形式给予的。
因此，应纳税利润是通过加回折旧并从会计利润中扣除资本减免得出的。接着，对这些
应纳税利润按适当的税率向公司征税。
In the above example, when the capital allowances are greater than the depreciation expense
in years 1 and 2, the entity has received tax relief early. This is good for cash flow in that it
delays (ie defers) the payment of tax. However, the difference is only a temporary difference
and so the tax will have to be paid in the future. In years 3 and 4, when the capital allowances
for the year are less than the depreciation charged, the entity is being charged additional tax
and the temporary difference is reversing. Hence the temporary differences can be said to be
taxable temporary differences.
在上面的例子中，当资本减免(税收折旧)大于第 1 年和第 2 年的折旧费用时，公司相当
于提前获得了税收减免，这对现金流动是有好处的，因为它推迟了纳税时间。然而，这
种差异只是暂时性的，因此将来是需要缴税的。在第 3 年和第 4 年，当当年的资本减免
小于已计提的折旧时，公司将被征收额外的税款，暂时性差异将被冲销。因此，暂时性
差异可以说是应纳税暂时性差异。
Notice that overall, the accumulated depreciation and accumulated capital allowances both
equal $2,000 – the cost of the asset – so over the four-year period, there is no difference
between the taxable profits and the profits per the financial statements.
总的来说，累计折旧和累计资本减免都等于 2000 美元——资产的成本——因此在四年
期间，应纳税利润和财务利润之间没有差异。
At the end of year 1, the entity has a temporary difference of $300, which will result in tax
being payable in the future (in years 3 and 4). In accordance with the concept of prudence, a
liability is therefore recorded equal to the expected tax payable.
在第 1 年年末，公司有一笔 300 美元的暂时性差异，这将导致在未来(在第 3 年和第 4
年)会有对应的应付税款。根据谨慎性原则，负债金额应确认为预期应付的税款。
Assuming that the tax rate applicable to the company is 25%, the deferred tax liability that
will be recognised at the end of year 1 is 25% x $300 = $75. This will be recorded by
crediting (increasing) a deferred tax liability in the Statement of Financial Position and
debiting (increasing) the tax expense in the Statement of Profit or Loss.
假设公司税率为 25%，则在第 1 年年末确认的递延所得税负债为 25% x 300= 75。这将
通过在资产负债表中贷记(增加)递延税款负债和在损益表中借记(增加)税费来记录。
(Dr Tax expense 75 Cr Deferred tax liability 75)
By the end of year 2, the entity has a taxable temporary difference of $400, ie the $300
brought forward from year 1, plus the additional difference of $100 arising in year 2. A
liability is therefore now recorded equal to 25% x $400 = $100. Since there was a liability of
$75 recorded at the end of year 1, the double entry that is recorded in year 2 is to credit
(increase) the liability and debit (increase) the tax expense by $25.
到第 2 年年末，公司累积有 400 美元的应纳税暂时性差异，即第 1 年的 300 美元，加上
第 2 年产生的 100 美元的额外差异。因此，现在记录的负债等于 25% x 400= 100。由于
递延所得税负债属于资产负债表科目，记录的是一个时点的累计数，第 1 年年末已经记
录 l75 美元的负债，因此在第 2 年末记录的负债应为第一年到第二年的变动值也就是 25
美元，复式记账法是贷记(增加)负债并借记(增加)25 美元的税费。(Dr Tax expense 25 Cr
Deferred tax liability 25)
At the end of year 3, the entity’s taxable temporary differences have decreased to $260 (since
the company has now been charged tax on the difference of $140). Therefore in the future, the
tax payable will be 25% x $260 = $65. The deferred tax liability now needs reducing from
$100 to $65 and so is debited (a decrease) by $35. Consequently, there is now a credit (a
decrease) to the tax expense of $35.
在第 3 年年末，公司的应纳税暂时性差异减少到 260 美元(因为该公司现在已就 140 美
元的差异征税)。因此，在未来，应付税款将为 25% x 260= 65。递延所得税负债现在需
要从 100 减少到 65，因此借记(减少)35 美元。对应的，贷记(减少)一笔 35 美元的税费。
(Dr Deferred tax liability 35 Cr Tax expense 35)
At the end of year 4, there are no taxable temporary differences since now the carrying
amount of the asset is equal to its tax base. Therefore the opening liability of $65 needs to be
removed by a debit entry (a decrease) and hence there is a credit entry (a decrease) of $65 to
the tax expense. This can all be summarised in the following working.
在第 4 年年末，没有应纳税暂时性差异了，因为现在资产的账面金额已经等于税基了。
因此，65 美元的期初负债需要通过借记(减少)来消除，对应有一个 65 美元的税收费用
贷记(减少)。(Dr Deferred tax liability 65 Cr Tax expense 65)
The movements in the liability are recorded in the Statement of Profit or Loss as part of the
taxation charge（税收费用的变动是随着递延所得税负债的变化而变化的）
The closing figures are reported in the Statement of Financial Position as part of the
deferred tax liability.
上图中最后一行的期末数字，将是出现在资产负债表里的递延所得税负债的金额。
Proforma
Example 1 provides a proforma, which may be a useful format to deal with deferred tax
within a published financial statements question. The movement in the deferred tax liability in
the year is recorded in the Statement of Profit or Loss where:
 an increase in the liability, increases the tax expense
 a decrease in the liability, decreases the tax expense.
The closing figures are reported in the Statement of Financial Position as the deferred tax
liability.
例 1 提供了一个有用的格式来处理已公布的递延所得税问题。当年递延税负债的变动影
响着计入损益表中的税费的金额，其中:
- 负债的增加会增加税费
- 负债的减少会减少税费。
期末数字在资产负债中列为递延所得税负债。
The Statement of Profit or Loss
As IAS 12 considers deferred tax from the perspective of temporary differences between the
carrying amount and tax base of assets and liabilities, the standard can be said to take a
‘balance sheet approach’. However, it will be helpful to consider the effect on the Statement
of Profit or Loss.
由于 IAS 12 从资产和负债的账面金额和计税基础之间的暂时性差异的角度考虑递延税，
该准则可以说是采取了“资产负债表方法”（也就是常说的资产负债表观）。但是，考虑
对损益表的影响也是有帮助的。
Continuing with the previous example, suppose that the profit before tax of the entity for each
of years 1 to 4 is $10,000 (after charging depreciation). Since the tax rate is 25%, it would
then be logical to expect the tax expense for each year to be $2,500. However, income tax is
based on taxable profits not on the accounting profits.
继续上一个例子，假设企业从第 1 年到第 4 年每年的税前利润都是 10,000 美元(扣除折
旧后)。由于税率为 25%，因此每年的税费预计为 2,500 美元。然而，所得税是基于应税
利润而不是会计利润。
The taxable profits and so the actual tax liability for each year could be calculated as in Table
2.
每年的应纳税利润和实际纳税义务如表 2 所示计算。
The income tax liability is then recorded as a tax expense. As we have seen in the example,
accounting for deferred tax then results in a further increase or decrease in the tax expense.
Therefore, the final tax expense for each year reported in the Statement of Profit or Loss
would be as in Table 3.
所得税负债随后被记录为税费。正如案例所示，递延所得税的会计处理会导致税费的进
一步增加或减少。因此，损益表中记录的每年的最终税项费用如表 3 所示。
It can therefore be said that accounting for deferred tax is ensuring that the matching principle
is applied. The tax expense reported in each period is the tax consequences (ie tax charges
less tax relief) of the items reported within profit in that period.
可以说递延所得税的会计处理，就是为了确保会计配比的原则。每个报告期间的税务费
用是该期间利润中所报告的项目的税务后果(即税费减去税收减免)。
The FR exam
Deferred tax is consistently tested in the published financial statements question in the FR
exam. Here are some hints on how to deal with the information in the question.
- The deferred tax liability given within the trial balance or draft financial statements will be
the opening liability balance.
- In the notes to the question there will be information to enable you to calculate the closing
liability for the SFP or the increase/decrease in the liability.
递延所得税属于 FR 的高频考点，以下是关于如何处理相关问题的一些提示：
- 试算平衡表或财务报表草稿中给出的递延所得税负债一般代表的是期初负债余额。
- 在问题的注释中，将有信息帮助计算 SFP 的期末负债或负债的增加/减少。
It is important that you read the information carefully. You will need to ascertain exactly what
you are being told within the notes to the question and therefore how this relates to the
working that you can use to calculate the figures for the answer.
你需要明确在 notes（题干信息）中被告知了什么，以及这与可以用来计算答案数字的
workings 之间的关系。
Consider the following sets of information – all of which will achieve the same ultimate
answer in the published financial statements.
EXAMPLE 2
The trial balance shows a credit balance of $1,500 in respect of a deferred tax liability.
The notes to the question could contain one of the following sets of information:
1. At the year-end, the required deferred tax liability is $2,500.
2. At the year-end, it was determined that an increase in the deferred tax liability of $1,000
was required.
3. At the year-end, there are taxable temporary differences of $10,000. Tax is charged at a
rate of 25%.
4. During the year, taxable temporary differences increased by $4,000. Tax is charged at a
rate of 25%.
试算表显示递延所得税负债的贷方余额为 1,500 美元。
题干信息中可以包含下列信息之一:
1. 年底，所需的递延所得税负债为 2,500 美元。
2. 年底，确定需要增加 1,000 美元的递延所得税负债。
3. 年底，应纳税暂时性差异为 10,000 美元。税率为 25%。
4. 年内，应纳税暂时性差异增加了 4,000 美元。税率为 25%。
Situations 1 and 2 are both giving a figure that can be slotted straight into the deferred tax
working. In situations 3 and 4 however, the temporary differences are being given. These are
then used to calculate a figure which can be slotted into the working. In all situations, the
missing figure is calculated as a balancing figure. Table 4 shows the completed workings.
情况 1 和 2 都给出了一个可以直接归入递延税款工作的数字。然而，在情况 3 和 4 中，
给出了暂时性的差异。这些之后会被用来计算。在所有情况下，缺失的数字都都是通过
倒挤获得的轧差数。表 4 显示了完整的计算过程。
Revaluations of non-current assets
Revaluations of non-current assets (NCA) are a further example of a taxable temporary
difference. When an NCA is revalued to its current value within the financial statements, the
revaluation surplus is recorded in equity (in a revaluation reserve) and reported as other
comprehensive income. While the carrying amount of the asset has increased, the tax base of
the asset remains the same and so a temporary difference arises.
非流动资产重估(NCA)是应纳税暂时性差异的另一个例子。当非流动资产在财务报表中
被重估到当前价值时，重估增值会被记录在资产负债表权益中(计入 revaluation reserve)
并作为其他综合收益记录。虽然资产的账面金额增加了，但资产的计税基础保持不变，
因此产生了暂时性差异。
Tax will become payable on the surplus when the asset is sold and so the temporary difference
is taxable. Since the revaluation surplus has been recognised within equity, to comply with
matching, the tax charge on the surplus is also charged to equity. Suppose that in Example 1,
the asset is revalued to $2,500 at the end of year 2, as shown in Table 5.
当资产被出售时，增值的部分将被征税，因此暂时性差异是应纳税的性质。由于重估增
值已在权益中确认，为符合匹配要求，相应的税费也将计入权益。假设在例 1 中，资产
在第 2 年末被重估为 2,500 美元，如表 5 所示。
The carrying amount will now be $2,500 while the tax base remains at $600. There is,
therefore, a temporary difference of $1,900, of which $1,500 relates to the revaluation surplus.
This gives rise to a deferred tax liability of 25% x $1,900 = $475 at the year-end to report in
the Statement of Financial Position. The liability was $75 at the start of the year ( Example 1)
and thus there is an increase of $400 to record.
账面金额现在为 2,500 美元，而税基仍为 600 美元。因此，暂时性差异为 1,900 美元，
其中 1,500 美元与重估增值有关。这就产生了 25% x 1,900= 475 的递延所得税负债，并
在年末在资产负债表中报告。年初的负债为 75 美元(例 1)，因此要记录的负债共增加了
400 美元(475-75)。
However, the increase in relation to the revaluation surplus of 25% x $1,500 = $375 will be
charged to the revaluation reserve and reported within other comprehensive income. The
remaining increase of $25 will be charged to the Statement of Profit or Loss as before.
但是，与重估增值相关的 25% x 1,500= 375 将记入其他综合收益并在年底结转计入
revaluation reserve。剩余增加的 25 美元则将像以前一样记入损益表。
The overall double entry is: 记账分录如下:
The SBR exam
It is important to appreciate that deferred tax can arise in respect of many different types of
asset or liability and not just non-current assets as discussed above. Therefore, for SBR it is
more important that candidates understand the principles behind deferred tax so that they can
be applied to any given situation. Some of the situations that may be seen are discussed below.
In all of the following situations, assume that the applicable tax rate is 25%.
对于 SBR 考试，有一点比较重要的是，递延所得税可以来源于多种不同类型的资产或
负债，而不仅仅是上面提到的非流动资产。因此，对于 SBR 来说，更重要的是要理解
递延所得税税背后的原则，以便可以应用于任何既定的情况。下面讨论一些可能出现的
情况。在以下所有情况中，假设适用税率都为 25%。
Deferred tax assets
It is important to be aware that temporary differences can result in needing to record a
deferred tax asset instead of a liability. Temporary differences affect the timing of when tax is
paid or when tax relief is received. While normally they result in the payment being deferred
until the future or relief being received in advance (and hence a deferred tax liability) they can
result in the payment being accelerated or relief being due in the future.
需要注意的是，暂时性差异可能导致递延所得税资产的出现，而不是负债。暂时性差异
会影响纳税或获得税收减免的时间。虽然通常情况下，它们会导致付款延期至未来或提
前收到减免(因此是延期纳税义务)，但它们也可能导致付款提前或减免在未来才生效。
In these latter situations the temporary differences result in a deferred tax asset arising (or
where the entity has other larger temporary differences that create deferred tax liabilities, a
reduced deferred tax liability).
在后一种情况下，暂时性差异会导致递延税项资产的产生(或者，如果该公司有其他更
大的暂时性差异而产生递延税项负债，则会相应减少递延税项负债)。
Whether an individual temporary difference gives rise to a deferred tax asset or liability can
be ascertained by applying the following rule:暂时性差异是否会产生递延所得税资产或负
债，可通过以下公式计算:
If the temporary difference is positive, a deferred tax liability will arise. If the temporary
difference is negative, a deferred tax asset will arise.
如果暂时性差异为正数，将产生递延所得税负债。如果暂时性差异为负数，将产生递延
所得税资产。
EXAMPLE 3
Suppose that at the reporting date the carrying amount of a non-current asset is $2,800 while
its tax base is $3,500, as shown in Table 6 above.
根据上述案例，假设在报告日，非流动资产的账面金额为 2,800，税基为 3,500，如上表
所示。
In this scenario, the carrying amount of the asset has been written down to below the tax base.
This might be because an impairment loss has been recorded on the asset which is not
allowable for tax purposes until the asset is sold. The entity will therefore receive tax relief on
the impairment loss in the future when the asset is sold.
在这种情况下，资产的账面金额低于税基。这可能是因为资产上记录了减值损失，但该
损失出于税务目的，在资产出售前是不承认的。因此，该公司将在未来出售资产时获得
减值损失的税收减免。（也就是多了个未来少交税的权利，因此多了个 DTA）
The deferred tax asset at the reporting date will be 25% x $700 = $175.
报告日的递延所得税资产将为 25% x 700= 175。
It is worth noting here that revaluation gains, which increase the carrying amount of the asset
and leave the tax base unchanged, result in a deferred tax liability. Conversely, impairment
losses, which decrease the carrying amount of the asset and leave the tax base unchanged,
result in a deferred tax asset.
值得注意的是，重估增值增加了资产的账面金额，而税基依然保持不变，这导致了递延
所得税负债。相反，减值损失会减少资产的账面金额，而税基保持不变，从而导致递延
税项资产。
EXAMPLE 4
At the reporting date, inventory which cost $10,000 has been written down to its net realisable
value of $9,000. The write down is ignored for tax purposes until the goods are sold.
在报告日，成本为 10,000 美元的存货已减记至其可变现净值 9,000 美元。在货物售出之
前，存货减值损失在税务方面不被承认。
The write off of inventory will generate tax relief, but only in the future when the goods are
sold. Hence the tax base of the inventory is not reduced by the write off. Consequently, a
deferred tax asset of 25% x $1,000 = $250 as shown in Table 8 should be recorded at the
reporting date.
存货的跌价会产生税收减免，但只是在将来货物售出时才可以使用减免。因此，存货的
计税基础不会因为跌价而减少。所以如表 example 4 所示，应在报告日当天记录 25% x
1,000= 250 的递延所得税资产。
EXAMPLE 5
At the reporting date, an entity has recorded a liability of $25,000 in respect of pension
contributions due. Tax relief is available on pension contributions only when they are paid.
在报告日，公司记录了 25,000 美元的应付养老金，而养老金缴款只有在支付时才能享受
税收减免。
The contributions will only be recognised for tax purposes when they are paid in the future.
Hence the pension expense is currently ignored within the tax computations and so the
liability has a nil tax base, as shown in Table 8. The entity will receive tax relief in the future
and so a deferred tax asset of 25% x $25,000 = $6,250 should be recorded at the reporting
date.
由于只有在未来支付时，才能出于纳税目的确认缴款享受税收减免。因此，养老金费用
目前在税收计算中被忽略，因此负债的税基为零，如表 example 5 所示，公司将在未来
获得税收减免，因此应在报告日记录 25% x 25,000= 6,250 的递延所得税资产。
Group financial statements 集团报表
When dealing with deferred tax in group financial statements, it is important to remember that
a group does not legally exist and so is not subject to tax. Instead, tax is levied on the
individual legal entities within the group and their individual tax assets and liabilities are
cross-cast in the consolidation process. To calculate the deferred tax implications on
consolidation adjustments when preparing the group financial statements, the carrying amount
refers to the carrying amount within the group financial statements while the tax base will
be the tax base in the entities’ individual financial statements.
在集团财务报表中处理递延所得税项时，需要记住的是，集团在法律上其实并不存在，
因此是不需要纳税的。相反地，集团内的单个法律实体是需要缴税的，并且在合并过程
中对单独的税务资产和负债进行合并处理。在编制集团财务报表时，为了计算递延所得
税对合并调整的影响，账面金额是指集团财务报表中的账面金额，而税基将是企业的单
家财务报表中的税基。
Fair value adjustments 公允价值调整
At the date of acquisition, a subsidiary’s net assets are measured at fair value. The fair value
adjustments may not alter the tax base of the net assets and hence a temporary difference may
arise. Any deferred tax asset/liability arising as a result is included within the fair value of the
subsidiary’s net assets at acquisition for the purposes of calculating goodwill.
在收购日当天，子公司的净资产按照公允价值计量。公允价值调整不会改变净资产的计
税基础，但账面价值发生了变化，因此会产生暂时性差异。由此产生的任何递延税项资
产/负债都将包含在收购时子公司净资产的公允价值中，并影响商誉的计算。
Goodwill 商誉
Goodwill only arises on consolidation – it is not recognised as an asset within the individual
financial statements. Theoretically, goodwill gives rise to a temporary difference that would
result in a deferred tax liability as it is an asset with a carrying amount within the group
financial statements but will have a nil tax base. However, IAS 12 specifically excludes a
deferred tax liability being recognised in respect of goodwill.
商誉仅在合并时产生，在单家财务报表中是不被确认为资产的。理论上来讲，商誉会产
生暂时性差异，从而导致递延所得税负债，因为它是一项在集团财务报表中具有账面价
值的资产，但其税基为零。然而, IAS 12 明确排除了商誉所产生的递延所得税负债的确
认。
Provisions for unrealised profits (PUPs) 未实现利润
When goods are sold between group companies and remain in the inventory of the buying
company at the year-end, an adjustment is made to remove the unrealised profit from the
consolidated financial statements. This adjustment also reduces the inventory to the original
cost when a group company first purchased it. However, the tax base of the inventory will be
based on individual financial statements and so will be at the higher transfer price.
Consequently, a deferred tax asset will arise. Recognition of the asset and the consequent
decrease in the tax expense will ensure that the tax already charged to the individual selling
company is not reflected in the current year’s consolidated Statement of Profit or Loss but
will be matched against the future period when the profit is recognised by the group.
当货物在集团内出售，并在年末保留在买方公司的库存中时，需要进行合并抵消调整，
在合并报表中抵消未实现利润。这种调整也将存货降低到集团公司首次购买时的原始成
本。但是，存货的计税基础是基于单独的财务报表，也就是按照更高的转让价格。因此，
会产生暂时性差异，进而产生递延所得税资产，同时会产生税费的减少。当未来货物销
售给第三方实现利润的时候，会将递延所得税资产所产生的少交税的权利，进行相应确
认。
EXAMPLE 6
P owns 100% of the equity share capital of S. P sold goods to S for $1,000 recording a profit
of $200. All of the goods remain in the inventory of S at the year-end. Table 9 shows that a
deferred tax asset of 25% x $200 = $50 should be recorded within the group financial
statements.
P 拥有 S 100%的股份。P 以 1000 美元的价格向 S 出售商品，利润 200 美元。所有的货
物在年末都留在 S 的存货中。下表显示，25% x 200= 50 的递延所得税资产应记录在集
团财务报表中。
Measurement of deferred tax
IAS 12 states that deferred tax assets and liabilities should be measured based on the tax rates
that are expected to apply when the asset/liability will be realised/settled. Normally, current
tax rates are used to calculate deferred tax on the basis that they are a reasonable
approximation of future tax rates and that it would be too unreliable to estimate future tax
rates.
IAS 12 规定，递延所得税资产和负债应根据资产/负债变现/结算时预期适用的税率进行
计量。通常情况下，使用当前税率计算递延税项，因为它们相对来说，比较合理接近未
来税率，因为未来税率估计起来并不十分可靠。
Deferred tax assets and liabilities represent future taxes that will be recovered or that will be
payable. It may therefore be expected that they should be discounted to reflect the time value
of money, which would be consistent with the way in which other liabilities are measured.
IAS 12, however, does not permit or allow the discounting of deferred tax assets or liabilities
on practical grounds.
递延税资产及负债代表未来将会收回或应付的税项。因此，理论上来说需要考虑货币的
时间价值进行折现处理，这将与衡量其他负债的方式相一致。但是，IAS 12 不允许基于
实际理由对递延所得税资产或负债进行折现处理，因此不需要考虑折现的问题。
The primary reason behind this is that it would be necessary for entities to determine when
the future tax would be recovered or paid. In practice this is highly complex and subjective.
Therefore, to require discounting of deferred tax liabilities would result in a high degree of
unreliability. Furthermore, to allow but not require discounting would result in inconsistency
and so a lack of comparability between entities.
这背后的主要原因是，每个公司都有必要确定何时收回或者支付未来的税款。实际上，
这是非常复杂和主观的。因此，要求对递延所得税负债进行折现将导致高度的不可靠性。
此外，允许但又不要求折现会导致不一致，从而导致公司之间缺乏可比性。
Deferred tax and the framework
As we have seen, IAS 12 considers deferred tax by taking a “balance sheet” approach to the
accounting problem by considering temporary differences in terms of the difference between
the carrying amounts and the tax values of assets and liabilities – also known as the valuation
approach. This can be said to be consistent with the approach taken to recognition in the
International Accounting Standards Board’s Conceptual Framework for Financial
Reporting® (the Conceptual Framework). However, the valuation approach is applied
regardless of whether the resulting deferred tax will meet the definition of an asset or liability
in its own right.
正如我们已经看到的，IAS 12 通过采用“资产负债表观”来考虑递延税项，通过考虑资
产和负债的账面金额与税务价值之间的暂时差异来解决会计问题，也称为估值方法。这
与概念性框架中的却惹条件其实是一致的。然而，无论由此产生的递延税项本身是否符
合资产或负债的定义，估值方法均适用。
Thus, IAS 12 considers the overriding accounting issue behind deferred tax to be the
application of matching – ensuring that the tax consequences of an item reported within the
financial statements are reported in the same accounting period as the item itself.
因此，IAS 12 认为递延所得税背后的首要会计问题是会计 matching 的应用——确保在
财务报表中报告的税项与项目本身在同一个会计期间报告。
For example, in the case of a revaluation surplus, since the gain has been recognised in the
financial statements, the tax consequences of this gain should also be recognised – that is to
say, a tax charge. In order to recognise a tax charge, it is necessary to complete the double
entry by also recording a corresponding deferred tax liability.
比如，在有重估增值的情况下，由于增值的部分已经确认在在财务报表中，相应的税务
影响也要得到确认，也就是税费。为了确认税款，需要通过记录相应的递延税款负债来
完成记账。
However, part of the Conceptual Framework’s definition of a liability is that there is a
‘present obligation’. Therefore, the deferred tax liability arising on the revaluation gain should
represent the current obligation to pay tax in the future when the asset is sold. However, since
there is no present obligation to sell the asset, there is no present obligation to pay the tax.
然而，概念框架对负债的部分定义是存在“当前义务”。因此，重估增值产生的递延所
得税负债应代表未来出售资产时所产生的”当前纳税义务”。但是，由于目前没有出售资
产的义务，因此也没有纳税的义务。
Therefore, it is also acknowledged that IAS 12 is inconsistent with the Conceptual
Framework to the extent that a deferred tax asset or liability does not necessarily meet the
definition of an asset or liability.
所以其实 IAS 12 月概念性框架并不是完全一致的，因为递延所得税资产或负债不一定
符合资产或负债的定义。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:Section C - Deferred Tax.pdf

stem_zh:
IFRS 13, Fair Value Measurement
IFRS 13 has required a significant amount of work by entities to simply understand the
nature of the principles and concepts involved.
IFRS® 13, Fair Value Measurement was issued in May 2011 and defines fair value,
establishes a framework for measuring fair value and requires significant disclosures relating
to fair value measurement. The International Accounting Standards Board (the Board) wanted
to enhance disclosures for fair value in order that users could better assess the valuation
techniques and inputs that are used to measure fair value. There are no new requirements as to
when fair value accounting is required but rather it relies on guidance regarding fair value
measurements in existing standards.
IFRS 13 公允价值计量于 2011 年 5 月发布，定义了公允价值，同时建立了公允价值计
量框架，并要求对于与公允价值计量相关的信息进行重大披露。国际会计准则委员会希
望加强对公允价值的披露，以便报表使用者能够更好地采用估值技术和输入值计量公允
价值。
The guidance in IFRS 13 does not apply to transactions dealt with by certain standards. For
example share based payment transactions in IFRS 2, Share-based Payment, leasing
transactions in IFRS 16, Leases, or to measurements that are similar to fair value but are not
fair value – for example, net realisable value calculations in IAS® 2, Inventories or value in
use calculations in IAS 36, Impairment of Assets. Therefore, IFRS 13 applies to fair value
measurements that are required or permitted by those standards not scoped out by IFRS 13. It
replaces the inconsistent guidance found in various IFRS standards with a single source of
guidance on measurement of fair value.
IFRS 13 公允价值准则并不适用于已经有特定准则规范的交易。比如 IFRS 2 股份支付，
IFRS 16 租赁，或者是针对类似公允价值但并不是公允价值的计量——比如 IAS 2 净存
货里可变现净值的计算，或者是 IAS 36 资产减值里 VIU 使用价值的计算。因此，IFRS 13
适用于未规定范围的公允价值计量，消除了不同准则对于
Historically, fair value has had a different meaning depending on the context and usage. The
Board’s definition of fair value is the price that would be received to sell an asset or paid to
transfer a liability in an orderly transaction between market participants at the measurement
date. Basically it is an exit price. Consequently, fair value is focused on the assumptions of
the market place, is not entity specific and so takes into account any assumptions about risk.
This means that fair value is measured using the same assumptions used by market
participants and takes into account the same characteristics of the asset or liability. Such
conditions would include the condition and location of the asset and any restrictions on its
sale or use.
从历史上看，公允价值根据上下文和用法有不同的含义。国际会计准则委员会对公允价
值的定义是，在计量日，在市场参与者之间的有序交易中，因出售资产而收到的或为转
移负债而支付出去的价格。基本来说属于一个退出价格。因此，公允价值主要基于市场
的假设而不是以公司为准，同时还会考虑到相关风险假设。公允价值是站在市场参与者
的角度来进行计量的，同时考虑到资产或负债的相同特征。这些条件将包括资产的状况
和所在地以及对其出售或使用的任何限制。
Interestingly an entity cannot argue that prices are too low relative to its own valuation of the
asset and that it would be unwilling to sell at low prices. The prices to be used are those in ‘an
orderly transaction’. An orderly transaction is one that assumes exposure to the market for a
period before the date of measurement to allow for normal marketing activities to take place
and to ensure that it is not a forced transaction. If the transaction is not ‘orderly’ then there
will not have been enough time to create competition and potential buyers may reduce the
price that they are willing to pay. Similarly if a seller is forced to accept a price in a short
period of time, the price may not be representative. Therefore, it does not follow that a market
in which there are few transactions is not orderly. If there has been competitive tension,
sufficient time and information about the asset, then this may result in an acceptable fair
value.
有趣的是，公司并不能辩称价格相对于其自身的资产估值太低便不愿意以低价出售。交
易中要使用的价格是“有序交易”中的价格。有序交易指的是在计量日期之前的一段时间
内已经进入市场，并允许正常的营销活动发生，同时确保交易并不是强制性的。如果交
易不“有序”，那么就没有足够的时间来创造竞争，同时潜在买家也可能会降低愿意支付
的价格。同样地，如果卖方被迫在短时间内接受一个价格，那么该价格可能并不具有代
表性。因此，一个交易很少的市场并不意味着就是无序的。如果存在竞争紧张，足够的
时间和关于资产的信息，那么这可能会使得公允价值变得可被接受。
IFRS 13 does not specify the unit of account that should be used to measure fair value. This
means that it is left to the individual standard to determine the unit of account for fair value
measurement. A unit of account is the single asset or liability or group of assets or liabilities.
The characteristic of an asset or liability must be distinguished from a characteristic arising
from the holding of an asset or liability by an entity. An example of this is where an entity
sells a large block of shares, and it has to sell them at a discount price to the market price.
This is a characteristic of holding the asset rather than a characteristic of the asset itself and
should not be taken into account when fair valuing the asset.
IFRS 13 并没有指定计量公允价值应使用的记账单位。这意味着，确定公允价值计量的
记账单位，取决于独立标准。记账单位指的是单一的资产或负债或一组资产或负债。资
产或负债的特征必须与公司持有的资产或负债所产生的特征区别开来。比如一个公司出
售大量股票，它必须以低于市场价格的折扣价出售，这是持有资产的特征，而不是资产
本身的特征，在对资产进行估价时不需要考虑在内。
Fair value measurement assumes that the transaction to sell the asset or transfer the liability
takes place in the principal market for the asset or liability or, in the absence of a principal
market, in the most advantageous market for the asset or liability. The principal market is the
one with the greatest volume and level of activity for the asset or liability that can be accessed
by the entity.
公允价值计量的前提假设是，出售资产或转移负债的交易发生在主要市场，当主要市场
缺失的情况下，则会在最有利市场上交易。主要市场指的是交易量和交易水平最大的市
场。
The most advantageous market is the one, which maximises the amount that would be
received for the asset or paid to extinguish the liability after transport and transaction costs.
Often these markets would be the same.
最有利市场是在扣除运输和交易成本后，最大化资产收益或清偿债务的市场，也就是价
格最好的市场。这些市场往往是相同的。
Sensibly an entity does not have to carry out an exhaustive search to identify either market
but should take into account all available information. Although transaction costs are taken
into account when identifying the most advantageous market, the fair value is calculated
before adjustment for transaction costs because these costs are characteristics of the
transaction and not the asset or liability. However, if location is a factor, then the market price
is adjusted for the costs incurred to transport the asset to that market. Market participants
must be independent of each other and knowledgeable, and able and willing to enter into
transactions.
一个公司不必进行详尽的搜索来识别确定任何一个市场，但应该考虑所有可用的信息。
虽然在确定最有利市场时，会考虑交易成本(也就是将其扣除)，但公允价值是在扣除交
易成本之前计算的，因为这些成本是交易的特征，是以交易为导向的，不同的交易对应
的手续费也不同，但这并不是资产或负债的特征，因此计量公允价值时不需要考虑。但
是如果地点是一个因素，与资产特征相关的话，那么市场价格需要根据将资产运输到对
应市场(最主要市场或者最有利市场)的成本进行调整。市场参与者必须相互独立，知识
渊博，能够并愿意进行交易。
This is a complex process and so IFRS 13 sets out a valuation approach, which refers to a
broad range of techniques, which can be used. There are three approaches based on the
market, income and cost. When measuring fair value, the entity is required to maximise the
use of observable inputs and minimise the use of unobservable inputs. To this end, the
standard introduces a fair value hierarchy, which prioritises the inputs into the fair value
measurement process.
公允价值计量是一个复杂的过程，因此 IFRS 13 规定了一种估值方法，此方法涉及一系
列可以使用的估值技术 - 基于市场、收入和成本的三种方法。在计量公允价值时，要
求公司最大限度地使用可观察的输入数据，最小限度地使用不可观察的输入数据。为此，
准则引入了公允价值输入层级，将公允价值计量过程中的输入数据进行优先排序。
Fair value measurements are categorised into a three-level hierarchy, based on the type of
inputs to the valuation techniques used, as follows: 一共分为 3 个层级：
 Level 1 inputs are unadjusted quoted prices in active markets for items identical to the
asset or liability being measured. As with current IFRS standards, if there is a quoted
price in an active market, an entity uses that price without adjustment when measuring
fair value. An example of this would be prices quoted on a stock exchange. The entity
needs to be able to access the market at the measurement date. Active markets are ones
where transactions take place with sufficient frequency and volume for pricing
information to be provided. An alternative method may be used where it is expedient.
The standard sets out certain criteria where this may be applicable. For example where
the price quoted in an active market does not represent fair value at the measurement
date. An example of this may be where a significant event takes place after the close of
the market such as a business reorganisation or combination.
第一层级是活跃市场中与被计量的资产或负债相同的项目的未经调整的报价。与当前的
国际会计准则一样，如果活跃市场中已经有公开报价了，那么在计量公允价值时可以直
接使用此价格，无需调整。股票交易所的报价就是一个例子。公司需要能够在计量日进
入市场。活跃市场是指交易发生的频率和数量足以提供定价信息的市场。某些情况下也
会使用替代方法。比如，活跃市场中的报价并不代表计量日的公允价值，此类情况一般
可能在收盘后发生了重大事件，如业务重组或合并。
The determination of whether a fair value measurement is based on level 2 or level 3 inputs
depends on (i) whether the inputs are observable inputs or unobservable and (ii) their
significance.
确定公允价值计量是否基于第二或第三层级输入数据取决于: (i)输入数据是可观察的还
是不可观察的，以及(ii)重要性。
 Level 2 inputs are inputs other than the quoted prices in determined in level 1 that are
directly or indirectly observable for that asset or liability. They are likely to be quoted
assets or liabilities for similar items in active markets or supported by market data. For
example interest rates, credit spreads or yields curves. Adjustments may be needed to
level 2 inputs and, if this adjustment is significant, then it may require the fair value to be
classified as level 3.
第二层级输入值是除 l 第一层级中确定的报价之外的输入数据，可直接或间接观察到该
资产或负债。它们可能是活跃市场中类似项目的报价，或者有市场数据支持。比如利率、
信用利差或收益率曲线。第 2 级输入值可能需要相应调整，如果这种调整很大，则可能
需要归类为第 3 层级。
 Finally, level 3 inputs are unobservable inputs. These inputs should be used only when it
is not possible to use Level 1 or 2 inputs. The entity should maximise the use of relevant
observable inputs and minimise the use of unobservable inputs. However, situations may
occur where relevant inputs are not observable and therefore these inputs must be
developed to reflect the assumptions that market participants would use when
determining an appropriate price for the asset or liability. The general principle of using
an exit price remains and IFRS 13 does not preclude an entity from using its own data.
For example cash flow forecasts may be used to value an entity that is not listed. Each
fair value measurement is categorised based on the lowest level input that is significant
to it.
第三层级输入值是不可观察的输入值。只有当 1 级或 2 级输入值无法使用的时候，才应
使用 3 级输入值。公司应最大限度地使用相关的可观察输入，最小限度地使用不可观察
输入。但是，可能会出现无法观察到相关输入值的情况，因此这些输入值必须要能反映
市场参与者在确定资产或负债的适当价格时所使用的假设。使用退出价格的一般原则仍
然存在，IFRS 13 并不排除公司使用自己的内部数据。比如，现金流预测可用于评估未
上市公司的价值。
IFRS 13 also sets out certain valuation concepts to assist in the determination of fair value.
For non-financial assets only, fair value is determined based on the highest and best use of
the asset as determined by a market participant. Highest and best use is a valuation concept
that considers how market participants would use a non-financial asset to maximise its benefit
or value. The maximum value of a non-financial asset to market participants may come from
its use in combination with other assets and liabilities or on a standalone basis. In determining
the highest and best use of a non-financial asset, IFRS 13 indicates that all uses that are
physically possible, legally permissible and financially feasible should be considered. As such,
when assessing alternative uses, entities should consider the physical characteristics of the
asset, any legal restrictions on its use and whether the value generated provides an adequate
investment return for market participants.
IFRS 13 还提出了某些估值概念，以帮助确定公允价值。对于非金融资产，公允价值是
根据市场参与者所确定的该资产的最佳用途来确定的。最佳用途假设是一种估值概念，
它考虑了市场参与者将如何使用非金融资产来最大化其利益或价值。非金融资产对市场
参与者的最大价值可能来自于其与其他资产和负债的结合使用，或基于独立的使用。在
确定非金融资产的最佳用途时，IFRS 13 指出，应考虑所有物理上的可能性、法律上的
不禁止和经济上的可行性。因此，在评估替代用途时，公司应考虑资产的物理特征、对
其用途的任何法律限制，以及所产生的价值是否为市场参与者提供了足够的投资回报。
The fair value measurement of a liability, or the entity’s own equity, assumes that it is
transferred to a market participant at the measurement date. In many cases there is no
observable market to provide pricing information and the highest and best use is not
applicable. In this case, the fair value is based on the perspective of a market participant who
holds the identical instrument as an asset. If there is no corresponding asset, then a
corresponding valuation technique may be used. This would be the case with a
decommissioning activity. The fair value of a liability reflects the non performance risk based
on the entity’s own credit standing plus any compensation for risk and profit margin that a
market participant might require to undertake the activity. Transaction price is not always the
best indicator of fair value at recognition because entry and exit prices are conceptually
different.
对于负债或公司的自身权益的公允价值计量，一般是假定其在计量日被转移给另一个市
场参与者的情况下进行的。大部分情况下，没有可观察到的市场来提供价格信息，最佳
用途假设也并不适用。在这种情况下，公允价值是基于持有相同工具作为资产的市场参
与者的观点。如果没有相应的资产，则可以使用相应的估值技术。弃置成本便是用于此
情况。负债的公允价值反映了基于公司自身信用状况的未履约风险，加上市场参与者开
展活动可能需要的任何风险补偿和利润率。交易价格并不总是用于确认公允价值的最佳
指标，因为进入价格和退出价格在概念上是不同的。
The guidance includes enhanced disclosure requirements that include:
- information about the hierarchy level into which fair value measurements fall
- transfers between levels 1 and 2
- methods and inputs to the fair value measurements and changes in valuation techniques,
and
- additional disclosures for level 3 measurements that include a reconciliation of opening and
closing balances, and quantitative information about unobservable inputs and assumptions used.
公允价值的披露要求包括:
- 有关公允价值计量所属层级的信息
- 一级和二级之间的转移
- 公允价值计量的方法和输入值以及估值技术的变化，以及
- 3 级输入值计量的额外披露，包括期初和期末余额的对账，以及关于所使用的不可观
察输入和相关的假设信息。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:Section C - IFRS 13, Fair Value Measurement.pdf

stem_zh:
IFRS 2, Share-based payment
International Financial Reporting Standard (IFRS®) 2, Share-based Payment, applies
when a company acquires or receives goods and services for equity-based payment.
IFRS 2 主要适用于公司以股权为基础的支付方式收购商品和服务的情况。
These goods can include inventories, property, plant and equipment, intangible assets, and
other non-financial assets. There are two notable exceptions: shares issued in a business
combination, which are dealt with under IFRS 3, Business Combinations; and contracts for
the purchase of goods that are within the scope of International Accounting Standard (IAS®)
32 and IAS 39. In addition, a purchase of treasury shares would not fall within the scope of
IFRS 2, nor would a rights issue where some of the employees are shareholders.
这些商品可以包括存货、房地产、厂房和设备、无形资产以及其他非金融资产。这里有
两个需要注意的例外: 企业合并中发行的股票，根据 IFRS 3 企业合并准则处理；以及 IAS
32 和 IAS 39*(IFRS 9 的前身)。此外，购买库藏股不属于 IFRS 2 的范围，针对部分股东
的配股也不属于这一范围。
Examples of some of the arrangements that would be accounted for under IFRS 2 include call
options, share appreciation rights, share ownership schemes, and payments for services made
to external consultants based on the company’s equity capital.
根据 IFRS 2，需要考虑的情况包括:认购期权、股票增值权、股票所有权计划，以及根
据公司的股本向外部顾问支付的服务费用。
Recognition of share-based payment 股份支付的确认
IFRS 2 requires an expense to be recognised for the goods or services received by a company.
The corresponding entry in the accounting records will either be a liability or an increase in
the equity of the company, depending on whether the transaction is to be settled in cash or in
equity shares. Goods or services acquired in a share-based payment transaction should be
recognised when they are received. In the case of goods, this is obviously the date when this
occurs. However, it is often more difficult to determine when services are received. If shares
are issued that vest immediately, then it can be assumed that these are in consideration of
past services. As a result, the expense should be recognised immediately.
IFRS 2 要求当公司收到货物或服务时确认相应的费用。同时根据交易是以现金结算还是
以股票结算，来决定对应的贷方分录是公司的负债还是权益的增加。在股份支付交易中
获得的商品或服务应在实际收到时确认。就货物而言，显然是在货物发生(实际收到)的
日期。但是，一般更难确定的是何时收到服务。如果股票立即授予并发行，那么可以假
设这些股票是考虑到过去的服务。因此，可以在发行时点立即确认费用。
Alternatively, if the share options vest in the future, then it is assumed that the equity
instruments relate to future services and recognition is therefore spread over that period.
但如果股票是在未来才可行权的，则假定权益类工具与未来的服务有关，因此是在区间
段内确认。
Equity settled transactions 权益结算交易
Equity-settled transactions with employees and directors would normally be expensed and
would be based on their fair value at the grant date. Fair value should be based on market
price wherever this is possible. Many shares and share options will not be traded on an active
market. If this is the case then valuation techniques, such as the option pricing model, would
be used. IFRS 2 does not set out which pricing model should be used, but describes the
factors that should be taken into account. It says that ‘intrinsic value’ should only be used
where the fair value cannot be reliably estimated. Intrinsic value is the difference between
the fair value of the shares and the price that is to be paid for the shares by the counterparty.
与员工和董事的股权结算交易通常是被记为费用，并根据其在授予日当天的公允价值进
行计量。公允价值应尽可能地以市场价格为基础。许多股票和股票期权并不会在活跃市
场上交易。如果是这样的话，需要借用估值技术，比如期权定价模型来进行估值。IFRS
2 并没有规定应该使用哪种定价模式，而是给出了需要考虑的因素。准则指出“内在价值”
只能在公允价值无法可靠估计的情况下使用。内在价值指的是股票的公允价值与交易对
方应支付的股票价格之间的差额。
The objective of IFRS 2 is to determine and recognise the compensation costs over the
period in which the services are rendered. For example, if a company grants share options to
employees that vest in the future only if they are still employed, then the accounting process
is as follows:
IFRS 2 的目标是确定和确认提供服务期间的补偿成本。例如，如果一家公司向员工授予
股票期权，这些股票期权只有在他们仍然受雇的情况下才能在未来可行权，那么会计处
理如下:
 The fair value of the options will be calculated at the date the options are granted.
 This fair value will be charged to profit or loss equally over the vesting period, with
adjustments made at each accounting date to reflect the best estimate of the number of
options that will eventually vest.
 Shareholders’ equity will be increased by an amount equal to the charge in profit or loss.
The charge in the income statement reflects the number of options vested. If employees
decide not to exercise their options, because the share price is lower than the exercise
price, then no adjustment is made to profit or loss. On early settlement of an award
without replacement, a company should charge the balance that would have been
charged over the remaining period.
 期权的公允价值将在期权授予日当天计算。
 该公允价值将在等待期内平摊计入损益，并在每个报告日进行调整，以便对于最终
授予的期权数量给到最佳估计。
 股东权益也相应增加(金额等于计入损益表内的金额)。损益表中的费用反映了所授
予期权的数量。如果员工因为股价低于行使价格而决定不行使期权，则不会对损益
进行调整。在没有替换计划的情况下提前结算(加速行权)，公司应该立即确认本应
于剩余等待期内确认的服务金额。
EXAMPLE 1
A company issued share options on 1 June 20X6 to pay for the purchase of inventory. The
inventory is eventually sold on 31 December 20X8. The value of the inventory on 1 June
20X6 was $6m and this value was unchanged up to the date of sale. The sale proceeds were
$8m. The shares issued have a market value of $6.3m.
How will this transaction be dealt with in the financial statements?
某公司于 20X6 年 6 月 1 日发行股票期权，以支付购买存货。存货最终在 20X8 年 12 月
31 日售出。20X6 年 6 月 1 日的存货价值为 600 万美元，这一价值直到出售日都没有变
化。销售收入为 800 万美元。发行的股票市值为 630 万美元
在财务报表中如何处理这笔交易？
Answer
IFRS 2 states that the fair value of the goods and services received should be used to value the
share options unless the fair value of the goods cannot be measured reliably. Thus equity
would be increased by $6m and inventory increased by $6m. The inventory value will be
expensed on sale.
IFRS 2 规定，除非货物的公允价值无法可靠计量，否则应按照收到的商品和服务的公允
价值对股票期权进行估值。因此，权益将增加 600 万美元，存货对应增加 600 万美元。
存货价值将在出售时被计入费用(结转成本)。
Performance conditions 行权条件
Schemes often contain conditions which must be met before there is entitlement to the shares.
These are called vesting conditions. If the conditions are specifically related to the market
price of the company’s shares then such conditions are ignored for the purposes of estimating
the number of equity shares that will vest. The thinking behind this is that these conditions
have already been taken into account when fair valuing the shares. If the vesting or
performance conditions are based on, for example, the growth in profit or earnings per share,
then it will have to be taken into account in estimating the fair value of the option at the grant
date.
期权计划通常包含一些条件，在获得股份之前必须满足这些条件。这些被称为行权条件。
如果这些条件与公司股票的市场价格特别相关(市场条件)，那么在估计将授予的股票数
量时，这些条件将被忽略。这样做的原因是，在计量确定授予日公允价值的时候，其实
已经考虑过相关的市场条件了。如果可行权条件是基于利润或每股收益的增长(非市场
条件)，那么在估计授予日期权的公允价值时，需要考虑条件达成的可能性。
EXAMPLE 2
A company grants 2,000 share options to each of its three directors on 1 January 20X6,
subject to the directors being employed on 31 December 20X8. The options vest on 31
December 20X8. The fair value of each option on 1 January 20X6 is $10, and it is anticipated
that on 1 January 20X6 all of the share options will vest on 30 December 20X8. The options
will only vest if the company’s share price reaches $14 per share.
The share price at 31 December 20X6 is $8 and it is not anticipated that it will rise over the
next two years. It is anticipated that on 31 December 20X6 only two directors will be
employed on 31 December 20X8.
How will the share options be treated in the financial statements for the year ended 31
December 20X6?
某公司于 20X6 年 1 月 1 日向其三名董事每人授予 2,000 份股票期权，条件是这些董事
在 20X8 年 12 月 31 日依然在职。期权将于 20X8 年 12 月 31 日开始可行权。20X6 年 1
月 1 日，每个期权的公允价值为 10 美元，并且在 20X6 年 1 月 1 日预计所有股票期权都
将于 20X8 年 12 月 30 日可行权。只有当公司股价达到每股 14 美元时，这些期权才可以
被行权。
20X6 年 12 月 31 日的股价为 8 美元，预计在未来两年内不会上涨。预计到 20X6 年 12
月 31 日，只有两名董事在 20X8 年 12 月 31 日依然在职。
截至 20X6 年 12 月 31 日该如何处理股票期权？
Answer
The market-based condition (ie the increase in the share price) can be ignored for the purpose
of the calculation. However the employment condition must be taken into account. The
options will be treated as follows:
2,000 options x 2 directors x $10 x 1 year / 3 years = $13,333
Equity will be increased by this amount and an expense shown in profit or loss for the year
ended 31 December 20X6.
基于市场条件的行权条件(即股价上涨)在计算时可以忽略不计。但是服务条件需要考虑
在内。期权处理如下:
2，000 份期权 x 2 名董事 x 10 美元 x 1 年/ 3 年(等待期 3 年，需在 3 年间分摊)= 13,333
美元
截至 20X6 年 12 月 31 日止年度，Dr Expense 13,333 Cr Equity 13,333。
Cash settled transactions 现金结算交易
Cash settled share-based payment transactions occur where goods or services are paid for at
amounts that are based on the price of the company’s equity instruments. The expense for
cash settled transactions is the cash paid by the company.
以现金结算的基于股票的支付交易，是指商品或服务以公司权益工具的价格为基础进行
支付。现金结算交易的费用基于公司支付出去的现金金额。
As an example, share appreciation rights entitle employees to cash payments equal to the
increase in the share price of a given number of the company’s shares over a given period.
This creates a liability, and the recognised cost is based on the fair value of the instrument at
the reporting date. The fair value of the liability is re-measured at each reporting date until
settlement.
例如，股票增值权使员工有权获得现金支付，现金支付金额相当于特定时期内特定数量
的公司股票的股价涨幅。当期确认的成本是基于报告日当天的公允价值，同时计入负债。
结算前，每个报告日都需要对于负债的公允价值进行重新的计量，变动需要计入当期损
益。
EXAMPLE 3
Jay, a public limited company, has granted 300 share appreciation rights to each of its 500
employees on 1 July 20X5. The management feel that as at 31 July 20X6, the year end of Jay,
80% of the awards will vest on 31 July 20X7. The fair value of each share appreciation right
on 31 July 20X6 is $15.
What is the fair value of the liability to be recorded in the financial statements for the year
ended 31 July 20X6?
Jay 是一家上市有限公司，于 20X5 年 7 月 1 日授予其 500 名员工每人 300 股股票增值
权。管理层认为，截至 20X6 年 7 月 31 日，即公司的会计年底，80%的 SAR 将于 20X7
年 7 月 31 日到期。20X6 年 7 月 31 日，每股股票增值权的公允价值为 15 美元。
问：年底 20X6 年 7 月 31 日应记录的负债的公允价值是多少？
Answer
300 rights x 500 employees x 80% x $15 x 1 year / 2 years = $900,000(企业支付多少现金取
决于年底最终的股价)
Deferred tax implications 递延所得税的影响
In some jurisdictions, a tax allowance is often available for share-based transactions. It is
unlikely that the amount of tax deducted will equal the amount charged to profit or loss under
the standard. Often, the tax deduction is based on the option’s intrinsic value, which is the
difference between the fair value and exercise price of the share. A deferred tax asset will
therefore arise which represents the difference between a tax base of the employee’s services
received to date and the carrying amount, which will effectively normally be zero. A deferred
tax asset will be recognised if the company has sufficient future taxable profits against which
it can be offset.
在一些司法管辖区，股份交易通常可以获得免税额。扣除的税额并不等于计入损益的金
额。通常，税收减免是基于期权的内在价值，也就是股票的公允价值和行权价格之间的
差额(公司可以根据实际行权时该股票的公允价格与激励对象实际行权支付价格的差额
和数量，计算确定作为当年上市公司工资薪金支出，依照税法规定进行税前扣除)。这
将产生一项递延所得税资产，因为迄今所获得的员工服务的计税基础（会计上在等待期
内累计确认的费用）与税法上允许扣除的金额之间是存在差异的，应付职工薪酬（股份
支付）账面金额（通常为零）与到截止到目前为止获得的职工服务的计税基础（税务部
门允许在未来期间抵扣的金额）与之间的差异是一项导致产生递延所得税资产的可抵扣
暂时性差异。如果公司未来有足够的应纳税利润可以抵消，将可以确认递延所得税资产。
For cash settled share-based payment transactions, the standard requires the estimated tax
deduction to be based on the current share price. As a result, all tax benefits received (or
expected to be received) are recognised in the profit or loss.
对于以现金结算的股份支付交易，准则要求根据当前股价扣除预计税款。因此，收到（或
预计收到）的所有税收优惠均计入损益。
EXAMPLE 4
A company operates in a country where it receives a tax deduction equal to the intrinsic value
of the share options at the exercise date. The company grants share options to its employees
with a fair value of $4.8m at the grant date. The company receives a tax allowance based on
the intrinsic value of the options which is $4.2m. The tax rate applicable to the company is
30% and the share options vest in three-years’ time.
公司在某个国家经营，其获得等于行使日股票期权内在价值的税收减免。公司向其员工
授予股票期权，授予日的公允价值为 480 万美元。公司根据期权的内在价值获得 420 万
美元的税收减免。适用于公司的税率为 30%，股票期权等待期三年。
Answer
A deferred tax asset would be recognised of:
$4.2m @ 30% tax rate x 1 year / 3 years = $420,000
The deferred tax will only be recognised if there are sufficient future taxable profits available.
DTA 将被确认为:
420 万美元 x 30%税率 x 1/ 3 年= 420,000 美元
只有当未来有足够的应纳税利润时，才可以确认 DTA。
Disclosure 披露
IFRS 2 requires extensive disclosures under three main headings:
 Information that enables users of financial statements to understand the nature and extent
of the share-based payment transactions that existed during the period.
 Information that allows users of financial statements to understand how the fair value of
the goods or services received, or the fair value of the equity instruments which have
been granted during the period, was determined.
 Information that allows users of financial statements to understand the effect of expenses,
which have arisen from share-based payment transactions, on the entity’s profit or loss in
the period.
The standard is applicable to equity instruments granted after 7 November 2002 but not yet
vested on the effective date of the standard, which is 1 January 2005. IFRS 2 applies to
liabilities arising from cash-settled transactions that existed at 1 January 2005.
SBR candidates need to be comfortable with the above accounting principles and be able to
explain them in the context of some accounting numbers.
IFRS 2 要求披露以下内容:
 使财报使用者能够了解该期间存在的股份支付交易的性质和程度。
 让财报使用者了解所收到的商品或服务的公允价值，或在该期间所授予的权益工具
的公允价值是如何确定的。
 使财报使用者能够了解股份支付交易产生的费用对企业当期损益的影响。
该标准适用于 2002 年 11 月 7 日之后授予但在该标准生效日期（即 2005 年 1 月 1 日）
尚未行权的权益工具。IFRS 2 适用于在 2005 年 1 月 1 日存在的现金结算交易所产生的
负债。
SBR 考生需要对上述准则驾轻就熟，并且能够给出相应的解释。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:Section C - IFRS 2, Share-based Payment.pdf

stem_zh:
IFRS for SMEs
The principal aim when developing accounting standards for small and medium-sized
enterprises (SMEs) is to provide a framework that generates relevant, reliable and
useful information which should provide a high quality and understandable set of
accounting standards suitable for SMEs.
为中小企业制定会计准则的主要目的，是为了提供一个框架，以便提供相关、可靠和有
用的信息，从而给中小企业提供一套合适的、质量高且易懂的会计准则。
In July 2009, the International Accounting Standards Board (the Board) issued the IFRS for
SMEs Standard (the SMEs Standard) and amended in 2015. This standard provides an
alternative framework that can be applied by eligible entities in place of the full set of
International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS® standards).
2009 年 7 月，IASB 发布了中小企业准则，并于 2015 年进行了修订。该准则提供了一
个可供选择的框架，符合条件的公司，可以用其来代替整套的国际会计准则。
The SMEs Standard is self-contained, incorporating accounting principles based on extant
IFRS Standards which have been simplified to suit the entities that fall within its scope. There
are a number of accounting standards and disclosures that may not be relevant for the users of
SME financial statements. As a result the standard does not address the following topics:
- earnings per share
- interim financial reporting
- segment reporting
- insurance (because entities that issue insurance contracts are not eligible to use the
standard), and
- assets held for sale.
中小企业准则是独立的，基于现有的国际会计准则，将相关准则进行简化处理，以便适
合相关的中小型企业。有一些会计准则和披露可能与中小企业财报使用者无关。因此，
中小企业准则不涉及以下部分:
- 每股收益
- 中期财务报告
- 分部财务报告
- 保险(因为签发保险合同的实体没有资格使用该标准)，以及
- 持有待售资产。
In addition, there are certain accounting treatments that are not allowable under the SMEs
Standard. An example of these disallowable treatments is the capitalisation of borrowing
and development costs – under IFRS for SME’s they would be expensed to profit or loss.
Generally, there are simpler methods of accounting available to SMEs than those accounting
practices required by full IFRS Standards.
此外，有些会计处理是中小企业准则不允许的。比如借款利息以及开发成本，在中小企
业准则下,它们将不再资本化处理，而是直接作为费用，计入损益。总的来说，中小企
业可以采用比国际会计准则所要求的会计实务更简单的会计处理方法。
Additionally the SMEs Standard requires that all basic financial instruments are measured at
amortised cost using the effective interest method except for investments in non-convertible
and non-puttable ordinary and preference shares that are publicly traded or whose fair value
can otherwise be measured reliably are measured at fair value through profit or loss. All
amortised cost instruments must be tested for impairment. At the same time the standard
simplifies the derecognition and disclosure requirements. The SME Standard separates basic
and other financial instruments eg hedging instruments, swaps, options. However, the SME
Standard still offers significant simplifications even for more complex financial instruments.
SMEs can choose to apply the recognition and measurement requirements of IAS 39 if they
so wish.
此外,中小企业准则要求所有基本金融工具均按照实际利率，采用摊余成本法进行计量，
但公开交易的或者公允价值可以可靠计量的不可转换和不可质押的普通股和优先股，需
按照 FVTPL 来进行计量。所有摊销成本计量的工具必须进行减值测试。同时，准则简
化了终止确认和披露的要求。中小企业准则将基础金融工具和其他金融工具(如对冲工
具、互换、期权)分开。但是，即使针对更复杂的金融工具，中小企业准则仍然提供了
重要的简化。如果中小企业愿意，他们可以选择按照 IAS 39 (金融工具旧准则 - IFRS 9
的前身) 的确认和计量条件进行处理。
The standard also contains a section on transition, which allows all of the exemptions in IFRS
1, First-time Adoption of International Financial Reporting Standards. It also contains
'impracticability' exemptions for comparative information and the restatement of the opening
statement of financial position although these must be disclosed.
该准则还包含一个关于过渡章节，其中允许首次采用 IFRS 1 首次采用国际会计准则中
的所有豁免。准则还包含了对于比较信息和资产负债表期初报表重述的“不可行性”的豁
免，尽管这些必须被披露。
As a result of the above, the SMEs Standard requires SMEs to comply with less than 10% of
the volume of disclosure requirements applicable to listed companies complying with the full
set of IFRS Standards.
基于上述原因,中小企业准则对于中小企业的披露要求，要比采用完整 IFRS 的上市公司，
少 10%。
There is no universally agreed definition of an SME. No single definition can capture all the
dimensions of small and medium-sized business, or cannot be expected to reflect the
differences between firms, sectors, or countries at different levels of development.
对于中小企业并没有普遍一致的定义。没有一个单一的定义能够涵盖中小型企业的所有
维度，也不能期望其反映出不同发展水平的公司、部门或国家之间的差异。
Most definitions based on size use measures such as number of employees, net assets total, or
annual revenues. However, none of these measures apply well across national borders. The
SMEs Standard is intended for use by entities that have no public accountability (ie its
debt or equity instruments are not publicly traded or holds assets in a fiduciary capacity eg
most banks and financial institutions).
大多数基于规模的定义是使用员工数量、净资产总额或年收入等指标来进行计量的。但
是，当中没有任何一项计量方法能够很好地跨越国界。中小企业准则适用于没有公共问
责制的企业（即其债务或股权工具没有进行公开交易或以信托身份持有资产，如大多数
银行和金融机构）。
Ultimately, the decision regarding which entities should use the SMEs Standard stays with
national regulatory authorities and standard setters. These bodies will often specify more
detailed eligibility criteria. If an entity opts to use the SMEs Standard, it must follow the
standard in its entirety - it cannot cherry pick between the requirements of the SMEs Standard
and those of full IFRS Standards.
最终，哪些企业可以使用中小企业准则，是由国家监管机构和准则制定者来决定的。这
些机构通常会规定更详细的资格标准。如果一个企业选择使用中小企业准则，它必须完
全遵循该准则----不可以在中小企业准则和完整的国际会计准则的要求之间进行挑选。
The Board makes it clear that the prime users of IFRS Standards are the capital markets.
This means that IFRS Standards are primarily designed for quoted companies and not SMEs.
The vast majority of the world's companies are small and privately owned, and it could be
argued that IFRS Standards are not relevant to their needs or to the needs of their users. It is
often thought that small business managers perceive the cost of compliance with accounting
standards to be greater than their benefit.
国际会计准则委员会明确表示，国际会计准则的主要用户是资本市场。这就意味着国际
会计准则主要是为上市公司而不是中小企业设计的。世界上绝大多数公司都是小型的私
营公司，可以说国际会计准则与这些公司的需求或用户的需求无关。人们通常认为，小
企业管理者认为遵守国际会计准则的成本要远大于其收益（因为合规的成本很高）。
To this end, the SMEs Standard makes numerous simplifications to the recognition,
measurement and disclosure requirements in full IFRS Standards. Examples of these
simplifications are:
为此，中小企业准则对于完整的国际会计准则的确认、计量和披露要求进行了大量简化。
比如:
- goodwill and other indefinite-life intangibles are amortised over their useful lives, but if
useful life cannot be reliably estimated, then 10 years
- a simplified calculation is allowed if measurement of defined benefit pension plan
obligations (under the projected unit credit method) involves undue cost or effort
- the cost model is permitted for investments in associates and joint ventures.
- 商誉和其他不可确定使用寿命的无形资产在其使用寿命内摊销，如果使用寿命无法可
靠估计，一般按照 10 年进行；
- 如果既定福利养老金计划义务的计量(根据预计单位积分法)涉及不适当的成本或工
作，则允许进行简化计算；
- 成本模型适用于联营企业和合资企业的投资。
The main argument for the SMEs Standard is the undue cost burden of reporting, which is
proportionately heavier for smaller firms. The cost burden of applying the full set of IFRS
Standards may not be justified on the basis of user needs. Further, much of the current
reporting framework is based on the needs of large business, so SMEs perceive that the full
statutory financial statements are less relevant to the users of SME accounts. SMEs also use
financial statements for a narrower range of decisions, as they have less complex transactions
and therefore less need for a sophisticated analysis of financial statements. Thus, the
disclosure requirements in the SMEs Standard are also substantially reduced when compared
with those in full IFRS Standards partly because they are not considered appropriate for users'
needs and for cost-benefit considerations. Many disclosures in full IFRS Standards are more
relevant to investment decisions in capital markets than to the transactions undertaken by
SMEs.
中小企业准则的主要讨论点是过度的报告成本负担，对于较小的企业来说，这种负担相
对更重。从用户需求的角度来看，采用完整的国际会计准则的成本负担其实是不合理的。
此外，目前的报告框架大部分是基于大企业的需要，因此中小企业认为完整的法定财务
报表与中小企业账户的使用者不太相关。中小企业也将财务报表用于较小范围的决策，
因为它们的交易不太复杂，因此不太需要对财务报表进行复杂的分析。所以，与完整的
国际会计准则相比,中小企业准则的披露要求也大幅降低，部分原因是由于它们不适合用
户的需要和成本效益。国际会计准则中的许多披露与资本市场的投资决策更为相关，与
中小企业的交易并不是十分相关。
There are arguments against different reporting requirements for SMEs in that it may lead to a
two-tier system of reporting. Entities should not be subject to different rules, which could
give rise to different 'true and fair views'.
但也有人反对对中小企业实行不同的准则要求，因为这可能导致双重报告制度。企业不
应该受制于不同的规则，这可能会产生不同的“真实和公允的观点”。
The SMEs Standard is a self-contained set of accounting principles that are based on full
IFRS Standards, but that have been simplified so that they are suitable for SMEs. The
standard has been organised by topic with the intention that the standard would be
user-friendly for preparers and users of SME financial statements.
中小企业准则是一套独立的会计原则，它基于完整的国际会计准则，但简化处理过了，
更适用于中小企业。该准则是按主题进行的，目的是使该准则对于中小企业财务报表的
编制者和使用者更加便捷。
The SMEs Standard and full IFRS Standards are separate and distinct frameworks. Entities
that are eligible to apply the SMEs Standard, and that choose to do so, must apply that
standard in full and cannot chose the most suitable accounting policy from full IFRS
Standards or the SMEs Standard.
中小企业准则和国际会计准则是独立且不同的框架。有资格使用中小企业准则并选择这
样做的企业必须全面适用该准则，而不能从国际会计准则或中小企业准则中选择最合适
的会计政策。
The Board are expected to limit revisions to the SME Standard to once every three years
although the process means in reality the time frames are longer. The IASB are currently
(2021) in the process of consulting on whether and how to align the IFRS for SME standard
with the full IFRS standards to better serve the users and preparers of financial statements
without causing undue cost and effort to SMEs.
国际会计准则委员会预计将对中小企业标准的修订限制在每三年一次，尽管这个过程实
际上意味着时间框架会更长。IASB 目前（2021 年）正在咨询是否以及如何将中小企业
准则与完整的 IFRS 标准相结合，以更好地服务于财务报表的使用者和编制者，并且不
会不给中小企业造成过度的成本和工作量。
However, the SMEs Standard is naturally a modified version of full IFRS Standards, and not
an independently developed set of standards. They are based on recognised concepts and
pervasive principles and they will allow easier transition to full IFRS Standards if the SME
decides to become a public listed entity. In deciding on the modifications to make to IFRS
Standards, the needs of the users have been taken into account, as well as the costs and other
burdens imposed upon SMEs. Relaxation of some of the measurement and recognition criteria
in IFRS Standards had to be made in order to achieve the reduction in these costs and burdens.
Some disclosure requirements are intended to meet the needs of listed entities, or to assist
users in making forecasts of the future. Users of financial statements of SMEs often do not
make such kinds of forecasts. Small companies pursue different strategies, and their goals
are more likely to be survival and stability rather than growth and profit maximisation.
中小企业准则本身是基于完整的国际会计准则的修改版本，并不是独立制定的一套准则。
它们基于公认的概念和普遍原则，如果中小企业决定成为上市公司，它们允许更容易地
过渡到完整的国际会计准则。在决定对国际会计准则进行修改的时候，就有考虑用户的
需求，以及中小企业的成本和其他负担。为了减少这些成本和负担，必须放宽国际会计
中的一些计量和确认标准。一些披露要求旨在满足上市公司的需要，或帮助报表使用者
对未来做出预测。中小企业财务报表的使用者往往不作这种预测。小规模公司追求不同
的战略，他们的目标更侧重在生存和稳定，而不是增长和利润最大化。
The stewardship function is often absent in small companies, with the financial statements
playing an agency role between the owner-manager and the bank.
小公司通常没有管理职能，财务报表在所有者-管理者和银行之间扮演着代理人的角色。
Where financial statements are prepared using the SMEs Standard, the basis of presentation
note and the auditor's report will refer to compliance with the SMEs Standard. This reference
may improve access to capital. The SME Standard also contains simplified language that is
easily translatable, and explanations of the standards.
如果财务报表是使用中小企业准则编制的，那么附注列报的基础和审计报告也将参考中
小企业准则进行。这种参考可以改善获得资本的机会。中小企业准则还包含易于翻译的
简化语言，以及对于标准的解释。
In the absence of specific guidance on a particular subject. An SME may, but is not required
to, consider the requirements and guidance in full IFRS Standards dealing with similar issues.
The Board has produced full implementation guidance for SMEs.
在缺乏特定主题的具体指导的情况下，当处理类似事件的时候，中小企业可以，但不是
必须，去借鉴完整国际会计准则的要求以及指导。国际会计准则委员会为中小企业制定
了全面的实施指南。
There may be some important tax issues arising for SMEs that adopt the SMEs Standard and
this has been cited as one of the main reasons why some SMEs have not adopted the SME
Standard.
采用中小企业准则的中小企业可能会遇到一些重要的税务问题，这被认为是一些中小企
业没有采用中小企业准则的主要原因之一。
The SMEs Standard is a response to international demand from developed and emerging
economies for a rigorous and common set of accounting standards for smaller and
medium-sized businesses that is much simpler than full IFRS Standards. The SMEs Standard
should provide improved comparability for users of financial statements while enhancing the
overall confidence in the financial statements of SMEs, and reducing the significant costs
involved of maintaining standards on a national basis.
中小企业准则主要是为了回应发达经济体和新兴经济体对于一套严格且通用的中小企
业会计标准的国际需求，该标准比完整的国际会计准则要简单得多。中小企业准则应当
为财务报表使用者提供更好的可比性，同时增强对中小企业财务报表的整体信心，并减
少在国家层面上为了维持准则而发生的重大成本。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:Section C - IFRS for SMEs.pdf

stem_zh:
Impairment of financial assets
The impairment of financial assets – the expected credit loss (ECL) approach
IFRS 9 requires that credit losses on financial assets are measured and recognised using the
'expected credit loss (ECL) approach. Credit losses are the difference between the present
value (PV) of all contractual cash flows and the PV of expected future cash flows. This is
often referred to as the ‘cash shortfall’. The present values are discounted at the original
effective interest rate. ECLs are then calculated using the weighted average of credit losses
with the respective risks of a default occurring as the weights.
IFRS 9 要求金融资产的信用损失需要使用“预期损失法(ECL)”进行计量和确认。信用损
失是所有合同现金流的折现值(PV)与预期未来现金流的折现值之间的差额。这通常被称
为“现金短缺”。现值按原始实际利率贴现。ECL 根据违约风险发生的可能性，使用加权
平均的办法计算预期信用损失。
The ECL approach results in the early recognition of credit losses because it includes, not
only losses that have already been incurred, but also expected future credit losses – it is a
forward looking model. Arguably, this method is prudent as both financial assets and profits
will be reduced. It is however open to the criticism that, by requiring the estimation of future
credit losses, which will necessarily involve judgment, it will allow some companies to
engage in profit smoothing. Consequently, IFRS 9 has included definitions to provide clarity
as to what (and what is not) permitted. The ECL approach also impacts on the calculation of
interest revenue recognised from the financial asset (see below).
ECL 一般在初始确认的时候已经计算信用损失了，因为它不仅包括已经发生的损失，还
包括预期的未来信用损失——这属于一个前瞻性模型。这么处理的原因主要由于会计谨
慎性原则，计提损失准备进而金融资产和利润都会减少(Dr Loss Cr Allowance)。但此处
理方式也存在一定争议，因为当需要估计未来信用损失的时候，必然会涉及一些主观判
断，一些公司会利用此点进行平滑收益的操作(也就是公司高管为了隐瞒公司实际业绩
会通过一些会计的计提调整操控利润)。因此，IFRS 9 给出明确的定义，以明确什么是
允许的(什么是不允许的)。ECL 法也影响从金融资产中确认的利息收入的计算。
Before we look in detail at the ECL process required by IFRS 9, consideration of two further
definitions will be helpful. ECLs are further classified into (i) lifetime ECLs and (ii)
12-month ECL. The former are those that result from all possible default events over the
expected life of a financial instrument. The latter are those that result from default events that
are possible within 12 months after the reporting date.
ECL 被进一步分类为(i)存续期的 ECL 和(ii) 12 个月的 ECL。前者是金融工具预期寿命
内所有可能违约事件的结果。后者是由报告日后 12 个月内可能发生的违约事件造成的。
Under the approach required by IFRS 9, it is no longer necessary for a loss event to have
occurred but instead an entity is required to account for ECLs on initial recognition of the
余思之 893803493@qq.com
financial asset (the ECL could be nil) and then separately account for changes in the ECL at
each reporting date. Therefore, the impairment of financial assets is recognised in stages:
IFRS 9 并不要求损失事件已经发生了再确认损失，而是在初始确认金融资产的时候就已
经要计量相应的预期损失(ECL 也有可能是 0)，然后分别在每个报告期计量 ECL 的变动
情况。因此，金融资产的减值将分阶段确认：
 Stage 1—as soon as a financial instrument is originated or purchased, a 12-month ECL is
recognised in profit or loss and a loss allowance is established (may be nil). For financial
assets, interest revenue is calculated on the gross carrying amount (ie without deduction
for ECLs).
第 1 阶段 - 初始创建或购买金融工具的时候，12 个月的 ECL 便已经需要在损益中确认
(也有可能是零)，并计提相应的损失准备计入资产负债表。对于金融资产，利息收入是
根据总账面金额计算的(即不扣除 ECLs)。
 Stage 2 at each reporting date, the ECL is remeasured:
(i) if the credit risk has not increased significantly, continue to recognise a 12 month
ECL. The calculation of interest revenue is the same as for Stage 1.
(ii) if the credit risk increases significantly and is not considered low, full lifetime ECLs
are recognised in profit or loss. The calculation of interest revenue is the same as for Stage 1.
(iii) if the credit risk of a financial asset increases to the point that it is considered
credit-impaired, interest revenue is calculated based on the amortised cost (ie the gross
carrying amount less the loss allowance). Financial assets in this stage will generally be
assessed individually. Lifetime ECLs are recognised on these financial assets.
第 2 阶段，在每个报告日，需要重新计量 ECL:
(I)如果信用风险没有显著增加，则继续确认 12 个月的 ECL。利息收入的计算与第一阶
段相同(依然根据 gross carrying amount 进行计算)。
(ii)如果信贷风险显著增加且被认为不低，则需要确认整个存续期的 ECL 在利润表中。
利息收入的计算与第一阶段相同(依然根据 gross carrying amount 进行计算)。
(iii)如果金融资产的信用风险增加到被视为信用受损的程度，利息收入根据摊销成本(即
净值，也就是总账面金额减去损失准备金)计算。这个阶段的金融资产一般会单独评估。
存续期的 ECL 继续确认。
Example of the expected credit loss approach
Bale Co has a portfolio of $50,000 financial assets (debt instruments) that have two years to
maturity and are correctly accounted for at amortised cost. Each asset has a coupon rate of
10% as well as an effective rate of 10%.
余思之 893803493@qq.com
Bale Co 有一个价值 50，000 美元的金融资产(债务工具)组合，该投资为期两年，并正确
地按照摊销成本法进行核算。每项资产的票面利率为 10%，实际利率也为 10%。
No previous loss allowance has been recognised as the 12 month ECL was assessed to be nil
and there had been no significant change in the credit risk since the portfolio had been
acquired (this is Stage 1).
由于 12 个月的 ECL 被评估为零，且自投资组合被收购以来，信贷风险没有发生重大变
化(也就是第 1 阶段)，因此以前并没有确认相应的损失准备金。
At the year-end (this is Stage 2), information has emerged that the sector in which the
borrowers operate is experiencing tough economic conditions. It is now felt that a proportion
of loans will default over the remaining loan period and therefore the credit risk has increased
significantly. After considering a range of possible outcomes, the overall rate of return from
the portfolio is expected to be approximately 6% per annum for each of the next two years.
The debt instruments are not, however, considered credit impaired.
在年底(也就是第二阶段)，有信息显示借款人经营的行业正经历着艰难的经济状况。现
在发现，在剩余的贷款期限内，一部分贷款将会违约，因此信贷风险大大增加。在考虑
了一系列可能的结果后，投资组合的总体回报率预计在未来两年内每年约为 6%。但是，
债务工具并没有被认为是信用受损了。
Required:
Calculate the lifetime expected credit losses and the loss allowance required.
Answer
The lender was expecting an annual return of $5,000 a year ($50,000 × 10%) but is now only
expecting an annual return of $3,000 a year ($50,000 × 6%). There is therefore a cash
shortfall – ie an ECL of $2,000 per year. A loss allowance should be calculated at the present
value of the shortfalls over the remaining life of the asset.
出借人预计每年的回报应为 5,000 美元(50,000 × 10%)，但现在只预期每年能够收回 3,000
美元(50,000 × 6%)。因此存在现金短缺，即每年 2,000 美元的 ECL。损失准备金应按资
产剩余寿命期间短缺的现值计算。
The discount rate used should be the effective discount rate ie 10%.
折现率为 10%(根据准则要求，折现率需使用原始利率)。
余思之 893803493@qq.com
Thus, the ECL is $3,471. This is recognised as a loss allowance creating an expense to be
charged to profit or loss and offset against the carrying amount of the financial asset on the
statement of financial position. You should note IFRS 9 is not prescriptive about the
presentation in the statement of financial position and the loss allowance may be presented as
a liability instead of offset against the asset.
因此，ECL 算得 3,471 美元，Dr Loss 3471 Cr Loss allowance3471。Allowance 为资产备
抵项计入资产负债表，会抵减金融资产的账面价值，loss 则将计入损益。需要注意的是，
IFRS 9 并未对资产负债表中的列报做出规定，损失准备金也可以作为负债列示，而不是
去递减资产。
