Financial assets subject to impairment
If deemed necessary, a loss allowance for ECLs should be recognised for the following
financial assets: 如果认为有必要，应该为以下金融资产确认 ECLs 损失准备金:
- those measured at amortised cost and at fair value through other comprehensive income
(OCI)
- lease receivables
- contract assets
- irrevocable loan commitments, and
- financial guarantee contracts that are not accounted for at fair value through profit or loss
under IFRS 9.
- 以摊余成本和公允价值计量且其变动计入其他综合收益的资产(OCI)
- 应收租赁款
- 合同资产
余思之 893803493@qq.com
- 不可撤销的贷款承诺，以及
- 根据 IFRS 9，不以 FVTPL 计量的财务担保合同。
Therefore, this includes debt instruments such as loans, debt securities and trade receivables
(but see later for simplified approach).
因此，这包括债务工具，如贷款、债务证券和应收账款。
The recognition of ECLs is required for these financial assets by creating a loss
allowance/provision based on either 12-month or lifetime ECLs. Some entities would
recognise a loss allowance whilst others may choose to present ECLs as a liability.
这些金融资产需要确认 ECL，方法是根据 12 个月或存续期的 ECL 计提损失准备。一些
公司可能确认损失准备金，其他公司也有可能选择将 ECLs 作为负债。
Financial assets with a low credit risk would not meet the lifetime ECL criterion. An entity
does not recognise lifetime ECL for financial assets that are equivalent to 'investment grade',
which means that the asset has a low risk of default. There is a rebuttable presumption that
lifetime expected losses should be provided for if contractual cash flows are more than 30
days overdue (‘backstop indicator’). If the credit quality subsequently improves and the
lifetime ECL criterion is no longer met, the credit loss reverts back to a 12-month ECL basis.
Therefore a financial asset can move from 12 month ECL to lifetime ECL and back again if
there is evidence that there is no longer a significant increase in credit risk and there should
not be an assumption that a financial asset with a lifetime ECL will default. The assessment of
significant increases in credit risk can be performed on a collective basis, rather than on an
individual basis, if the financial instruments share the same risk characteristics. However if
any assets are deemed credit impaired they will generally be assessed on an individual basis.
信用风险低的金融资产不符合存续期 ECL 的计提条件。等同于“投资级别”的金融资产
视同违约风险低的资产，不需要计提存续期的 ECL。有一个可反驳的假设是，如果合同
现金流逾期超过 30 天了，则视同信用风险显著增加，需要计提存续期的预期损失。如
果信贷质量随后有所改善，且不再符合存续期 ECL 计提标准的，则信贷损失可以再恢
复为 12 个月的 ECL。因此，如果有证据表明信用风险不再显著增加，并且并没有假设
金融资产将违约，则金融资产可以从 12 个月的 ECL 转移到存续期的 ECL 再进行转回。
如果金融工具具有相同的风险特征，则可以根据整体基础对信贷风险的显著增加进行评
估，而不是根据单个情况进行。但是，如果有任何资产被视为信贷受损，则通常需要根
据个体情况进行评估。
Summary of the two-stage approach:
Stage 1 - on initial recognition
An entity would recognise a loss allowance based on the 12-months' ECL. This may be
assessed as nil.
第一阶段——初始确认时，将根据 12 个月的 ECL 确认损失准备金。这可能被评估为零。
余思之 893803493@qq.com
Stage 2 - each reporting date
Where there is no evidence that the credit quality of a financial asset has deteriorated
significantly since initial recognition, then the loss allowance continues to be based on the 12
months ECL (which could continue to be nil).
如果没有证据表明金融资产的信贷质量自初始确认以来已经严重恶化，那么损失准备金
继续基于 12 个月的 ECL(可能继续为零)。
Where there is evidence that the credit quality of a financial asset has deteriorated
significantly since initial recognition, then the impairment loss is based on the lifetime ECL.
If the asset is considered credit impaired then there is a further impact as the interest revenue
is calculated on the carrying amount net of the loss allowance.
如果有证据表明金融资产的信用质量自初始确认以来已显著恶化，则减值损失基于存续
期的 ECL。如果资产被视为信用受损，那么会有进一步的影响，因为利息收入是根据扣
除损失准备金后的账面净额计算的。
Simplified approach
For trade receivables there is a simplified approach in that no credit loss allowance is
recognised on initial recognition. Any loss allowance will be the present value of the expected
cash flow shortfalls over the remaining life of the receivables. This approach uses the
conventional matrix method (aged receivables list) of considering historically observed
default rates and adjusted for forward-looking estimates.
对于应收账款，有一个简化的方法，即在初始确认时不确认信用损失准备金。任何损失
准备金将是应收款项剩余期限内预期现金流短缺的现值。这种方法使用传统的矩阵方法
(账龄应收账款列表), 考虑历史观察到的违约率，并根据前瞻性估计进行调整。
Conclusion
The ECL model will require judgment carrying amount of financial assets and assessment of
impairment is dependent on forward-looking information which can be subjective. IFRS 9 has
attempted to limit this subjectivity by providing detailed definitions. IFRS 9 addressed the
criticism that losses were recognised too late, only after a credit event, and by requiring a
considered forward looking approach to impairment assessment it will make the financial
reporting of financial assets more relevant and useful to users of financial statements.
ECL 模型要求判断金融资产的账面价值，而减值评估则依赖于存在主观判断的前瞻性信
息。IFRS 9 试图通过提供详细的定义来限制这种主观性，从而解决对于预期损失的批评，
即在信用事件后才承认损失，因此 IFRS 9 要求一种考虑前瞻性的减值评估方法，它将
使金融资产的财务报告对财务报表的使用者更相关和更有用。
余思之 893803493@qq.com
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:Section C - Impairment of financial assets.pdf

stem_zh:
Measurement
The relevance of information provided by a particular measurement method depends on how
it affects the financial statements. The cost should be justified by the benefits of reporting that
information to existing and potential users. The different measures used should be the
minimum necessary to provide relevant information and there should be infrequent changes
with any necessary changes clearly explained. Further it makes sense for comparability and
consistency purposes, to use the same method for initial and subsequent measurement unless
there is a good reason from not doing so.
特定计量方法提供信息的相关性取决于它如何影响财务报表。应根据向现有和潜在用户
报告该信息的好处来证明成本的合理性。不同的计量标准应该是提供相关信息所必需的
最低计量标准，并且应该很少发生变化，任何必要的变化都需要详细解释清楚。此外，
出于可比性和一致性的目的，在初始和后续计量中使用同样的方法是有意义的，除非有
充分的理由证明可以不这样做。
The existing Conceptual Framework for Financial Reporting® (the framework) provides very
little guidance on measurement, which constitutes a serious gap in the Framework. A single
measurement basis may not provide the most relevant information to users and therefore
IFRS® standards adopt a mixed measurement basis, which includes fair value, historical cost,
and net realisable value. Different information from different measurement bases may be
relevant in different circumstances. A particular measurement bases may be easier to
understand, more verifiable and less costly to implement. However, if different measurement
bases are used, it can be argued that the totals in financial statements have little meaning.
Those that prefer a single measurement method favour the use of current values to provide the
most relevant information.
现有的概念性框架在计量方面提供的指导很少，这就构成了框架中的一个严重缺口。单
一的计量基础可能无法向用户提供最相关的信息，因此 IFRS 采用了混合的计量基础，
其中包括公允价值、历史成本以及可变现净值。来自不同计量基础的不同信息可能在不
同的情况下是相关的。一个特定的计量基础可能更容易理解，更可验证，应用成本更低。
然而，如果使用不同的计量基础，那么财报中显示出来的总数则被认为可能没有什么意
义。那些喜欢单一计量方法的人会倾向于使用当前价值来提供最相关的信息。
A business that is profit orientated has processes to transform market input values (inventory
for example) into market output values.(sales of finished products).Thus it makes sense that
current values should play a key role in measurement. Current market value would appear to
be the most relevant measure of assets and liabilities for financial reporting purposes.
以利润为导向的企业有将市场投入价值(比如库存)转化为市场产出价值的过程 (销售产
成品)。因此，当前价值在计量中发挥关键作用是有道理的。出于财务报告的目的(提供
有用信息给到报表使用者，帮助其做决策)，当前价值似乎是对资产和负债最相关的计
量标准。
The International Accounting Standards Board favour a mixed measurement approach
whereby the most relevant measurement method is selected. It appears that investors feel that
this approach is consistent with how they analyse financial statements and that the problems
of mixed measurement are outweighed by the greater relevance achieved. In recent standards,
it seems that the Board felt that fair value would not provide the most relevant information in
all circumstances. For example, IFRS 9 requires the use of cost in some cases and fair value
in other cases, while IFRS 15 essentially applies cost allocation.
国际会计准则委员会倾向于采用混合计量方法，即选择最相关的计量方法。投资者似乎
认为，这种方法与他们分析财务报表的方式是一致的，而且混合计量的问题被所获得的
更大相关性抵消了。在最近的准则中，国际会计委员会似乎认为公允价值不会在所有情
况下都提供最相关的信息。例如, IFRS 9 要求在某些情况下使用成本进行计量，而在其
他情况下则使用公允价值，而对于成本分配 IFRS 15 更适配。
A factor to be considered when selecting a measurement basis is the degree of measurement
uncertainty. The Exposure Draft on the Conceptual Framework states that for some estimates,
a high level of measurement uncertainty may outweigh other factors to such an extent that the
resulting information may have little relevance. Most measurement is uncertain and requires
estimation. For example, recoverable value for impairment, depreciation estimates and fair
value measures at level 2 and 3 under IFRS 13.Consequently, the Board believes that the level
of uncertainty associated with the measurement of an item should be considered when
assessing whether a particular measurement basis provides relevant information.
选择计量基础时要考虑的一个因素是计量的不确定性。概念性框架指出，对于一些估计
数，高度的计量不确定性可能会超过其他因素，以至于由此产生的信息可能没有什么相
关性。大多数的计量方法都是不确定的，需要进行会计估计。例如，减值下的可收回金
额、折旧估计以及 IFRS 13 下 2 级和 3 级输入值对于公允价值的计量。因此，IASB 认
为，在评估某一特定计量基础是否提供有关信息时，应考虑与项目计量有关的不确定性
程度。
Measurement uncertainty could be considered too great with the result that the entity may not
recognise the asset or liability. An example of this would be research activities. However,
sometimes a measure with a high degree of uncertainty provides the most relevant
information about an item. For example, financial instruments for which prices are not
observable. The Board thinks that the level of measurement uncertainty that makes
information lack relevance depends on the circumstances and can only be decided when
developing particular standards.
计量的不确定性可能被认为太大，其结果是公司可能不会去确认资产或负债。比如 R&D
当中的 research 调查活动。然而，有时一个具有高度不确定性的计量反而也可以提供关
于某个项目最相关的信息。比如价格无法观察到的金融工具。IASB 认为，使信息缺乏
相关性的计量不确定性水平取决于具体情况，只有在制定特定准则时才能做决定。
It would be easier if measurement bases were categorised as either historical cost or current
value. The Exposure Draft on the Conceptual Framework describes these two categories but
also states that cash-flow-based measurement techniques are generally used to estimate the
measure of an asset or a liability as part of a prescribed measurement basis. Cash-flow-based
measurement can be used to customise measurement bases, which can result in more relevant
information but it may also be more difficult for users to understand. As a result the Exposure
Draft does not identify those techniques as a separate category.
如果将计量基础划分为历史成本或当前价值，或许会更容易些。概念框架对于这两类基
础都做了描述，但也指出，基于现金流的计量技术通常用于估计资产或负债。基于现金
流的计量可以用于定制计量基础，这将产生更多的相关信息，但用户也可能更难理解。
因此，征求意见稿没有将这些技术确定为一个单独的类别。
There are several areas of debate about measurement. For example,should any discussion of
measurement bases include the use of entry and exit values, entity-specific values and the role
of deprival value. Again should an entity’s business model affect the measurement of its
assets and liabilities. Many would advocate that different measurement methods should be
applied that are dependent both on the nature of assets and liabilities and also, importantly, on
how these are used in the business. For example, property can be measured at historical cost
or fair value depending upon the business model.
关于计量有几个方面的争论。例如，关于计量基础的任何讨论是否包括进入和退出值、
公司特定价值和剥夺价值的的使用。同样，一个公司的商业模式是否也会影响其资产和
负债的计量。许多人主张采用不同的计量方法，这既取决于资产和负债的性质，重要的
是，也取决于它们在业务中如何被使用。例如，房地产可以根据商业模式选择以历史成
本或公允价值进行计量。
In order to meet the objective of financial reporting, information provided by a particular
measurement basis must be useful to users of financial statements. A measurement basis
achieves this if the information is relevant and faithfully represents what it essentially is
supposed to represent. In addition, the measurement basis needs to provide information that is
comparable, verifiable, timely and understandable. The Board believes that when selecting a
measurement basis, the amount is more relevant if the way in which an asset or a liability
contributes to future cash flows is considered. The Board considers that the way in which an
asset or a liability contributes to future cash flows depends, in part, on the nature of the
business activities.
为了达到财务报告的目标，由特定的计量基础所提供的资料必须对财报使用者有用。如
果信息是相关的，并且公允地陈述了它本质上应该呈现的内容，那么计量基础就可以实
现这一点。此外，计量基础还需要提供具有可比较、可验证、及时以及可理解的信息。
IASB 认为，在选择计量基础时，如果考虑到资产或负债对于未来现金流量的贡献，则
金额会更加相关。IASB 认为，资产或负债对未来现金流量的贡献方式，在一定程度上
取决于业务活动的性质。
There are many different ways in which an asset or liability can be measured. Historical cost
seems to be the easiest of these measures but even here, complexity can arise where there is a
deferred payment or a payment, which involves an asset exchange. Subsequent accounting
after initial recognition is not necessarily straightforward with historical cost as such matters
as impairment of assets have to be taken into account and the latter is dependent upon rules,
which can be sometimes subjective.
计量资产或负债的方法有很多种。历史成本法看起来是最简单的一种计量方法，但即使
这样，当有递延支付或涉及资产交换的时候，也可能会出现复杂性。初始确认后的后续
计量并不一定直接涉及历史成本，因为必须考虑资产减值等事项，而后者更依赖于规则，
有时可能是主观的(比如 VIU 的计算，涉及对于未来现金流的估计)。
Current values have a variety of alternative valuation methods. These include market value,
value-in-use and fulfilment value. Of these various methods, there is less ambiguity around
current market prices as with any other measure of current value, there is likely to be specific
rules in place to avoid inconsistency. In the main, the details of how these different
measurement methods are applied, are set out in each accounting standard.
现值有多种可供选择的估价方法。在这些不同的方法中，与任何其他计量现值的方法一
样，当前市场价格的模糊程度较低，因为会有具体的准则来避免不一致。总的来说，这
些不同的计量方法是如何应用的，在每个会计准则中都有详细规定。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:Section C - Measurement.pdf

stem_zh:
Sustainability reporting
In April 2021, the IFRS Foundation published two documents in relation to their
project on sustainability reporting. The first summarises the significant matters raised
by respondents to their Consultation Paper on Sustainability Reporting. The second
document was an Exposure Draft with proposed targeted amendments to the IFRS
Foundation Constitution to accommodate an International Sustainability Standards
Board (ISSB). This would allow the ISSB to set IFRS sustainability standards.
2021 年 4 月，有两份关于可持续性报告项目的文件发布。第一份文件总结了可
持续反战咨询报告当中的重要问题。第二份文件是征求意见稿，主要针对国际会
计准则基金委员会对于国际可持续性标准委员会(ISSB)的接纳。这将允许 ISSB
制定国际会计准则中的可持续性准则。
The IFRS Foundation reviewed the feedback on their Consultation Paper on
Sustainability Reporting and set out a strategy that proposed the creation of a new
board, the ISSB, under the Foundation’s current governance structure. The IFRS
Foundation reached the following conclusions:
- the new board would focus on information that is material to the decisions of
investors and other participants in the world’s capital markets
- the new board would initially focus on climate-related reporting while also moving
quickly to work towards meeting the information needs of investors on other
environmental, social and governance (ESG) matters
- the new board would build on the well-established work of the Financial Stability
Board’s Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures (TCFD), as well as
work by the alliance of leading standard-setters in sustainability and integrated
reporting focused on enterprise value
- by working with standard-setters from key jurisdictions, standards issued by the new
board would provide a globally consistent and comparable sustainability reporting
framework.
- 新成立的 ISSB 将重点关注对于投资人以及资本市场其他参与者的决策至关重
要的信息上；
- 将侧重于气候变化相关的报告以及与 ESG 有关的信息需求上
- 新的委员会将以 TCFD 以及可持续性和整合报告准则制定者的工作基础上，重
点关注企业价值
- 通过与标准制定者合作，提供一个全球一致和可比的可持续性报告框架。
There is broad stakeholder support for globally recognised sustainability reporting
standards. Currently it can be argued that there are diverse approaches to and
objectives for sustainability standard-setting which could result in increasing global
fragmentation. This demonstrates the need to promote comparable reporting and
reduce the complexity in approaches and objectives.
利益相关方广泛支持全球认可的可持续发展报告标准。目前来说，可持续性标准
制定的方法和目标是多种多样的，这可能会导致全球发展更加不一致。因此促进
报告可比性，降低方法和目标的复杂性，非常必要。
Because of the International Accounting Standards Board’s (IASB®) relationships
with global governance bodies and industries, and its expertise in international
standard-setting ,it could play an important role in global sustainability reporting by
creating a new board that would operate under its existing governance structure.
鉴于 IASB 与全球治理机构及行业的关系，以及其国际准则制定的专业度，其可
以通过现有治理架构的基础下，成立新的委员会，在全球可持续性报告中发挥重
要作用。
A set of comparable and consistent standards would allow companies to create public
trust through greater transparency of their sustainability initiatives, which would be
helpful to investors. Investors require better disclosure of such information as climate
risks and sustainability indicators.
一套可比和一致的标准将使公司能够通过提高其可持续性举措的透明度来建立
公众信任，这将有助于投资者做决策。投资者要求更好地披露气候风险和可持续
性指标等信息。
The objective of the ISSB would be to develop and maintain a global set of
sustainability reporting standards. Such standard setting could make use of existing
sustainability frameworks and standards. The development of a framework for
sustainability reporting could be coherent with IFRS standards and the IASB’s
mission to serve investors and primary users of financial statements. The ISSB could
adapt the existing standard setting process and use the experience of the IASB in
promoting the consistent use and application of sustainability standards. The IFRS
Foundation has established expertise in standard-setting which would benefit both the
new ISSB and investors. This would help investors to use sustainability reporting to
inform their decisions by giving them comparable and verifiable information.
ISSB 的目标是制定和维护一套全球性的可持续性报告准则。这种标准的制定可
以利用现有的可持续性框架和标准。制定可持续性报告框架符合 IFRS standards
和 IASB 为投资者和财务报表使用者服务的使命。ISSB 可以调整现有的标准制定
程序，并借鉴 IASB 的经验，促进可持续性标准的一致性。IASB 在准则制定方面
积累了专业知识，这对 ISSB 和投资者都有好处。这将有助于投资者利用可持续
发展报告提供的可比较和可验证的信息，为他们做决策提供信息。
The standards would benefit from the interconnectedness between financial reporting
and sustainability reporting. In addition, investors would benefit if a single
organisation developed requirements in financial reporting and sustainability
reporting.
The IFRS Foundation is well positioned to develop an appropriate institutional and
governance framework. However, there is a risk of reducing the current momentum
created by other frameworks and standard setting bodies. As an alternative, the IFRS
Foundation could encourage regulators to mandate the use of sustainability reporting
standards globally. In addition, it could be argued that the GRI standards already have
created global sustainability reporting standards that regulatory authorities could
mandate. Also, as the EU is already taking the lead in developing sustainability
standards and has a very ambitious timescale to develop and issue them, the IASB
could contribute its expertise in financial reporting to find consistency between
financial and sustainability reporting.
IFRS Foundation 完全有能力制定一个适当的机构和治理框架。然而，其他框架
和标准制定机构目前创造的势头有可能被减弱。因此，IFRS Foundation 可以鼓
励监管机构在全球范围内强制使用可持续性报告准则。此外，由于欧盟已经率先
制定可持续性标准，IASB 可以贡献其在财务报告方面的专业知识，以找到财务
报告和可持续性报告之间的一致性。
There is demand from investors for international coordination of an agreed set of
sustainability reporting standards. Currently, investors are often struggling with
incomplete and inconsistent data on companies. The ISSB would assist in providing a
level playing field for companies that prepare reports and also international
comparability for investors.
投资者经常被不完整和不一致的公司数据所困扰。ISSB 将协助编写报告的公司
提供一个公平的竞争环境，加强信息可比性。
SBR candidates need to be aware of progress in this area of business reporting as it is
a dynamic, fast-paced and developing subject area.
Notes：文章对可持续发展报告的背景，已经 ISSB 的成立作了介绍。除了了解这
些以外，对于可持续发展报告的后续进展，也是考生需要实时关注的部分。
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:Section E - Sustainable report.pdf

stem_zh:
Climate-related disclosures and investor focus
Climate change is impacting both society and companies alike. Corporations are
responding to its impact, and one of the reasons is that investors are demanding
actions. Investors need to know how a company is considering the impact of climate
change on its business model, risk strategy, and also the effect on its financial
statements. Investors want to understand the future challenges that the company faces,
and what the company’s plans are to deal with these challenges.
气候变化既对社会也对公司有影响。投资者想知道一家公司是如何考虑气候变化
对其商业模式、风险战略以及对其财务报表的影响的。投资者希望了解该公司未
来面临的挑战，以及该公司应对这些挑战的计划。
The Paris Agreement (United Nations) is a legally binding international treaty on
climate change which will require a significant reallocation of company resources if
the agreed goals are to be met. Therefore, companies could be exposed to a wide
range of risks and opportunities as they aim to meet these goals. Companies will need
to disclose the financial implications of climate-related challenges that face them.
An increasing number of companies are providing narrative reporting on
climate-related issues. Where minimum legal requirements are being met, investors
are calling for additional disclosure to inform their decision making. Some companies
have set strategic goals such as ‘net zero’ (or carbon neutral), but it is often unclear
from their reporting how progress towards these goals will be achieved, monitored or
assured. Climate-related narrative reporting requirements and expectations cover both
the potential impact on the future of a business and the company’s impact on the
environment.
As the demand for climate-related disclosure by investors and other stakeholders
increases, many companies are developing their climate governance in line with
reporting frameworks, principally ‘The Task Force on Climate-related Financial
Disclosures’ (TFCD).
Some of the information that investors may require is set out below:
- the arrangements in place and strategy for assessing and considering climate-related
issues
- the metrics used to monitor climate-related goals and targets
- the opportunities and risks concerning climate-related issues which are most relevant
and material to the company’s business model and strategy
- the potential effects on the company’s profitability, net assets, products, customers,
suppliers etc of different climate scenarios
- are the risks and opportunities reflected in the financial statements, for example the
effect of assumptions used in impairment testing, depreciation rates, decommissioning
etc
- the assessment of the company’s viability over the longer-term taking into account
climate-related issues
- the viability of the company’s business and business model.
投资者需要的信息：
- 评估和考虑与气候有关的问题的适当安排和战略
- 用于监测与气候相关的目标和目标的指标
- 与公司的商业模式和战略最相关和最重要的与气候相关的机遇和风险
- 不同气候情景下对公司盈利能力、净资产、产品、客户、供应商等的潜在影响
- 财务报表中是否反映的风险和机会，例如在减值测试、折旧率、退役等中使用
的假设的影响
- 对公司的长期可行性的评估，并考虑到与气候相关的问题
- 该公司的商业模式和商业模式的可行性。
以下涉及到对于财报、会计准则的影响（重要）：
Climate change and International Financial Reporting Standards
(IFRS® standards)
There is no single IFRS standard which addresses climate change. However, IFRS
standards provide a framework for incorporating the risks of climate change into
companies’ financial reporting. Companies must consider climate-related matters
when the effect is material on the financial statements.
IAS® 1, Presentation of Financial Statements
IAS 1 requires disclosure of information not specifically required by IFRS standards
and not presented elsewhere in the financial statements, but that is relevant to an
understanding of the financial statements. In addition, IAS 1 requires a company to
consider whether any material information is missing from its financial statements
such as the impact of climate-related matters on the company’s financial position and
performance.
Disclosure of assumptions about climate-related matters may be required, where
assumptions have been affected by climate change. For example, estimates of future
cash flows for impairment testing purposes or the calculation of decommissioning
obligations. The disclosure may include the nature of the assumptions or the
sensitivity of the calculations.
In addition, IAS 1 requires disclosure of the judgements that have a significant effect
on the amounts recognised in the financial statements.
IAS 1 requires management to assess a company’s ability to continue as a going
concern. Climate-related matters may create material uncertainties that cast significant
doubt upon a company’s ability to continue as a going concern. IAS 1 requires
disclosure of those uncertainties.
IAS 2, Inventory
Climate-related matters may cause a company’s inventories to become obsolete, or
the value to decline or costs of completion to increase. IAS 2 requires inventories to
be valued at the lower of cost and net realisable value (NRV). NRV is the estimated
selling price in the ordinary course of business, less the estimated cost of completion
and the estimated costs necessary to make the sale. Estimates of NRV will be based
on the most reliable evidence available of the amount which the inventories are
expected to realise.
气候变化会影响对于存货的估值。
IAS 12, Income Taxes
IAS 12 requires companies to recognise deferred tax assets for deductible temporary
differences and unused tax losses and credits, to the extent it is probable that future
taxable profit will be available against which those amounts can be utilised.
Climate-related matters may affect a company’s estimate of future taxable profits
which may result in potential deferred tax assets not being recognised or the
derecognition of already recognised deferred tax assets.
气候相关事项可能会影响公司对未来应税利润的估计，从而可能导致潜在的 DTA
未被确认或已确认的 DTA 被终止确认。
IAS 16, Property, Plant and Equipment
IAS 16 requires companies to review the residual value and the useful life of an asset
at least at each financial year end and, if expectations differ from previous estimates,
any change should be accounted for prospectively as a change in estimate.
Climate-related matters may affect the estimated residual value and expected useful
lives of assets because of obsolescence or legal restrictions on their use.
气候变化，会导致因资产过时或使用有限制，从而影响对于残值和剩余使用寿命
的估计。
IAS 36, Impairment of Assets
Climate-related matters may give rise to an indication that assets are impaired. A
decline in demand for products that are not environmentally friendly could indicate
impairment of that product or the manufacturing unit making the product. An adverse
change in the business environment of a company is an indication of impairment.
In assessing value in use, a company is required to calculate cash flow projections
based upon reasonable and supportable assumptions that are the best estimate of the
future economic conditions. Thus, companies will need to consider whether
climate-related matters affect those assumptions.
Companies are required to disclosure the events, circumstances and assumptions that
led to the recognition of an impairment loss, which could include climate-related
events.
气候的变化影响会衍生出相关的一些减值迹象，计算 VIU 时，需要将与气候相
关的问题考虑进来，评估这些问题是否会影响对于未来现金流的假设。
IAS 37, Provisions, Contingent Liabilities and Contingent Assets
Climate-related matters may affect the recognition, measurement and disclosure of
liabilities related to such things as penalties imposed by governments for not meeting
climate-related targets or causing environmental damage. In addition, contracts may
become onerous due to a change in inventory purchasing strategy or redesign of
products.
Companies should disclose major assumptions about any future events that have
affected a provision or contingent liability.
气候相关事项可能会影响相关负债的确认、计量和披露，比如没能履行政府要求
的气候相关目标或者造成环境损害而需要支付的罚金等。此外，由于库存采购策
略的改变或者需要重新进行产品设计，很有可能会出现亏损合同。
公司需要披露所有会影响预计负债或者是或有负债的未来事项。
IFRS 9, Financial Instruments
Climate-related matters may affect a lender’s exposure to credit losses, caused by
environmental disasters or regulatory change, and also a borrower’s ability to meet its
debt obligations to the lender. Climate-related matters may, therefore, affect the
calculation of expected credit losses if there is an impact on the different potential
future economic scenarios or the assessment of a significant increase in credit risk.
The classification and measurement of loans may be affected as lenders may include
terms linking contractual cash flows to an entity’s achievement of climate-related
targets. The lender would need to assess whether the contractual terms of the financial
asset give rise to cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest on the
principal amount outstanding. Additionally, climate-related targets may create an
embedded derivative that needs to be separated from the host contract.
Climate change may reduce the probability of a hedged forecast transaction occurring
or affect its timing. In this case, the hedge accounting relationship may need to be
terminated or there may be hedge ineffectiveness. Similarly, a reduction in the volume
of highly probable forecast transactions may lead to partial termination under IFRS 9.
- 影响信贷损失的计算
- 对 loan 的划分以及计量会有影响，需要考虑合同现金流的性质
- 影响套期会计关系，因为对冲交易发生的可能性或者发生的时间点会受到影响
IFRS 13, Fair Value Measurement (FVM)
When making the critical assessments and judgements for measuring fair value, the
entity should consider what conditions and the corresponding assumptions were
known or knowable to market participants. The impact of climate change on FVM
would depend on the evaluation of whether the climate change would have impacted
market participants’ valuation assumptions at the reporting date.
计量公允价值时，需要考虑气候变化对于市场参与者估值假设的影响。
The information such as climate-related legislation available to the market at the
reporting date may be relevant in making this evaluation. This would include any
corroborative or contrary evidence such as the timing and trajectory of observable
market price movements of related assets in the relevant markets, as well as
information from other sources of market data up to the reporting date.
报告日当天气候相关法规的确立对于公允价值估值的影响，比如市场上可观察的
相关资产的市场价格波动，其他市场来源的数据信息，都会影响对于公允价值的
估计。
Depending on the facts and circumstances of each case, disclosure may be needed to
enable users to understand whether or not climate change has been considered for the
purpose of FVM. Users should understand the basis for selecting the assumptions and
inputs that were used in the FVM and the related sensitivities.
所有基于的假设，输入值等相关信息都需要被了解。
The above examples from IFRS standards are not exclusive but are indicative of the
far-reaching impact climate change will have on business reporting. This area of
business reporting is evolving as the investor, and wider stakeholder, demand for both
financial and non-financial disclosures increases generally.
source_doc: ACCA:sbr:essay:Section F - Climate-related disclosures and investor focus.pdf

stem_zh:
The Sustainable Development Goals
可持续发展目标
可持续发展目标是 193 个联合国成员国同意在 2030 年前实现的 17 个目标，旨在解决重大的
世界问题。这些目标包括零饥饿、体面工作和经济增长，以及减少不平等。
The SDGs apply to all countries and set the priorities for governments. Demographic and social
change, shifts in global economic power, urbanisation, climate change, resource scarcity,
inequality and technological breakthroughs demand a corporate response. The SDGs can provide
insights for companies on how they can create economic, social and environmental value for their
investors and other stakeholders. The goals will allow business to understand and better respond to
the risks and opportunities created by rapid change across the various sectors.
SDG 适用于所有国家，并为政府设定了优先级。人口和社会变化、全球经济实力的转移、
城市化、气候变化、资源稀缺、不平等和技术突破等，都在要求企业做出回应。可持续发展
目标可以为公司提供关于如何为投资者和其他利益相关者创造经济、社会和环境价值的见解。
这些目标将使企业了解并更好地应对各个部门快速变化所带来的风险和机遇。
There is increasing interest by the investors in understanding how businesses are developing
SDGs. Investors seek information on the relevance of the SDGs to overall strategies, and thus
entities providing relevant SDG data will help investors make informed decisions which can lead
to capital being channelled to responsible businesses. Companies are developing business
strategies that embrace the growth potential of responsible environmental and societal policies.
投资者越来越有兴趣了解企业是如何制定可持续发展目标的。投资者寻求关于可持续发展目
标与总体战略相关性的信息，因此，提供相关可持续发展目标数据的企业，将有助于投资者
做决策，从而将资本引入负责任的企业。很多公司正在制定业务战略，以利用环境责任和社
会政策抓住潜在的发展机遇。
There are several reasons why companies should focus on sustainable business practices, and they
include: 公司关注可持续商业实践的原因包括:
- the increased future government focus on sustainable business
- such business practices often improve performance as they lower operational, reputational and
regulatory risk
- there are significant business growth opportunities in products and services that address the SDG
challenges
- the fact that short term, profit based models are reducing in relevance. Companies and their
stakeholders are changing how they measure success and this is becoming more than just about
profit.
- 政府关注度提高
- 运营、声誉和监管风险更低
- 产品和服务具有巨大的业务增长机会
- 公司及其利益相关者衡量成功的方式正在改变，不再单纯以利润为导向。
Investors realise that the SDGs will not all be equally relevant to all companies, with boilerplate
disclosures having little relevance at all. Good disclosure will qualitatively show how the
company’s SDG related activities affect the primary value drivers of the business. It would be
natural to assume that SDG reporting should be based around the disclosure of information to
mitigate business risk and the drive for improved predictability of investment decisions. However,
if there is to be fair presentation, then there should also be disclosure of any negative and positive
impacts on society and the environment.
投资者意识到，可持续发展目标并不适用于所有公司。良好的披露可以更好地展示公司 SDG
相关活动是如何影响业务主要价值驱动因素的。人们很自然地会认为，可持续发展目标报告
应该基于信息披露以降低商业风险，并提高投资决策的可预测性。然而，如果要有公平的陈
述，那么任何对社会和环境负面和正面影响也应该披露。
Investors’ expectations will still be focused on companies realising their core business activities
with financial sustainability as a prerequisite for attracting investment. However, institutional
investors have a fiduciary duty to act in the best interests of their beneficiaries, and thus have to
take into account environmental, social and governance (ESG) factors, which can be financially
significant. Companies utilising more sustainable business practices provide new investment
opportunities.
投资者仍然集中在公司心业务的实现上，并将财务可持续性作为吸引投资的先决条件。然而，
机构投资者有信托义务，要站在利益相关方的最大利益角度行事，所以必须考虑到环境、社
会和治理因素，这些因素在财务上可能很重要。利用更可持续的商业实践的公司会获得新的
投资机会。
Investors screen companies as regards their ESG policies and integrate these factors into their
valuation models. Additionally there is an increased practice of themed investing, whereby
investors select a company for investment based upon specific ESG policy criteria such as clean
technology, green real estate, education and health. Investors are increasingly factoring impact
goals into their decision making whereby they evaluate how successful the company has been in a
particular area for example, the reduction of educational inequality. This approach can help
optimise financial returns and demonstrate their contribution to the SDGs through their portfolios.
Investors are increasingly incentivised to promote sustainable economies and markets to improve
their long-term financial performance.
投资者会根据公司的 ESG 政策对公司进行筛选，并将这些因素整合到他们的估值模型中。
此外，公司投资的方法越来越多，投资者会根据特定的环境、社会和公司治理政策标准(如
清洁技术、绿色房地产、教育和健康) 选择被投资公司。投资者越来越多地将可持续发展目
标纳入到他们的决策制定中，会去评估公司在特定领域(比如，减少教育不平等)的成功程度。
这种方法有助于优化财务回报，并通过投资组合展示它们对可持续发展目标的贡献。投资者
越来越有动力推动可持续的经济和市场，以改善他们的长期财务业绩。
Institutional investors realise that environmental events can create costs for their portfolio in the
form of insurance premiums, taxes, and the physical cost related with disasters. Social issues can
lead to unrest and instability, which carries business risks which may reduce future cash flows and
financial returns.
机构投资者意识到，环境事件是会通过保险费用、税收和与灾害相关的实务成本的方式，增
加其投资组合的成本。社会问题会导致动荡和不稳定，从而带来商业风险，这可能会减少未
来的现金流入和财务回报。
Investors seek SDG information produced in line with widely-accepted recommendations. The
Global Reporting Initiative (GRI) and the UN Global Compact amongst others, have developed
guidance documents that mutually complement each other and create a reference point for
companies.
投资者寻求根据广泛建议所以产生的 SDG 信息。全球报告倡议(GRI)和联合国全球契约（UN
Global Compact）等机构已经制定了相互补充的指导文件，为公司提供可参考的点。
Companies should disclose to investors how they have decided on their SDG strategy, philosophy
and approach. The approach should be capable of measurable impacts and have a clear description
of the material issues and a narrative that links the sustainability issues back to the business model
and future outlook of the entity.
公司应该向投资者披露他们是如何决定其 SDG 战略、理念和方法的。这种方法应该能够产
生可衡量的影响，并对实质性问题有一个明确的描述，能够将可持续性问题与企业的商业模
式和未来前景联系起来。
For investors, it is important that SDG-related reporting is presented in the context of the strategy,
governance, performance and prospects of the entity. Stakeholders should be engaged from the
beginning in order to identify any potential impact with some investors expecting companies to
have a stakeholder dialogue that goes beyond financial matters. Investors often require an
understanding of how the entity feels about the relevance of the SGDs to the overall corporate
strategy, and this will include a discussion of any risks and opportunities identified and changes
that have occurred in the business model as a result.
对于投资者来说，在企业战略、治理、绩效和前景的背景下呈现与可持续发展目标相关的报
告非常重要。利益相关者应该从一开始就参与进来，以便确定任何潜在的影响，因为一些投
资者希望公司与利益相关者进行超越财务事项的对话。投资者通常需要了解该企业对 SGD
与整体公司战略的相关性的看法，并包括对于任何已识别的风险和机会，以及在业务模式中
发生的变化等一系列的讨论。
The SDGs and targets are likely to present some of the greatest business risks and opportunities
for companies who should publish material SDG contributions, both positive and negative, as part
of their report. For example, an inability to address negative social and environmental impacts
may also be directly detrimental to short-term financial value for a business. Investors are
increasingly seeking investment opportunities that can make a credible contribution to the
realisation of the SDGs.
可持续发展目标可能会给公司带来一些巨大的商业风险和机会，这些公司应该在报告中公布
其对 SDG 的实质性贡献，包括正面和负面的。例如，无法解决负面的社会和环境影响也可
能会直接损害企业的短期财务价值。投资者越来越多地寻求能够为实现可持续发展目标做出
可信贡献的投资机会。
However, if an investor wants to have a positive impact on working conditions for example, they
cannot assume that any investment in this area is relevant. The investor would need to be provided
with additional information such as data on the lowest income workers, any potential income
increase and how confident the company is that an increase in income will occur.
但是，如果一个投资者想要对工作状况产生积极的影响，他们不能假定在这一领域的任何投
资都是相关的。投资者还需要额外的信息，比如工人最低收入的数据、任何潜在的收入增长
以及公司对收入增长的信心。
Investors can choose not to invest in, or to favour, certain investments. Alternatively, they can
actively engage in new or previously overlooked opportunities that offer an attractive impact and
financial opportunity, even though these may involve additional risk.
投资者可以选择不投资或按照各自偏好进行某些投资。或者，他们可以积极参与新的或以前
被忽视的投资机会，这些机会可以提供有吸引力的影响和财务机会，即使会产生额外的风险。
There is an assumption that the disclosure of ESG factors will ultimately affect the cost of capital;
lowering it for sustainable businesses and increasing it for non-sustainable ones. It may also affect
cash flow forecasts, business valuations and growth rates. Investors employ screening strategies,
which may involve eliminating companies that have specific features, for example, low pay rates
for employees and eliminating them on a ranking basis. They may also be eliminated on the basis
of companies who are contributing or not, to a range of SDGs and targets. Investors will use
SDG-related disclosures to identify risks and opportunities on which they will, or will not, engage
with companies. Investors will see potential business opportunities in those companies that
address the risks to people and the environment and those companies that develop new beneficial
products, services and investments that may mitigate the business risks related to the SDGs.
有一个假设是，ESG 因素的披露最终会影响资本成本；降低可持续业务的成本，提高不可
持续业务的成本。它还可能影响现金流预测、企业估值和增长率。投资者会采用筛选战略，
淘汰具有特定特征的公司，比如，员工工资水平低的公司，并根据排名进行淘汰。也可以根
据公司是否对一系列可持续发展目标和具体目标做出贡献来排除它们。投资者将通过 SDG
相关的披露来来决定是否愿意与公司合作。 投资者将在那些应对人类和环境风险，以及开
发新的有益产品、服务和投资以缓解与可持续发展目标相关的商业风险的公司中，寻找潜在
的商业机会。
Notes：中文解析只是辅助考生更好地理解这篇文章的内容，但考生更多地还是要理解投资
者对于公司可持续发展的重视程度。全球化发展下，可持续发展报告已经变得更加广泛及有
意义。监管要求以及市场对于企业披露透明度的要求，都报告质量和范围的提高有着更高要
求。“可持续投资”描述了一种投资方法，将环境、社会或治理(ESG)因素，结合财务，共同
指导投资者做决策。2020 MJ Q4(a)考过相关的知识点，且答案均来源于此篇文章，希望考
生能够重视。

术语部分和回答技巧总结
考经分享-万字笔记
常识部分
1. Management Stewardship
: 管理层使用资源的能力/规避风险的能力/合规合法
: 报表体现客观公正的信息，能用于判断管理层是否高效履行了他们的职责
2. Information qualitative characteristics (benefit > cost)
Fundamental:
Relevance (material for decision making)
Faithful representation (true and fair/prudence/substance over form)
Enhancing:
Comparability/Consistent (other entities/another period)
Verifiability
Timeliness
Understandability
3. Asset/Liability/Equity
Asset
: present economic resource controlled by the entity
: right that has potential to produce economic benefits
Liability
: present obligation to transfer economic resource
: no way to avoid
Equity
Residual interest
准则部分
1.IAS 16 PPE
资本化原则
: probable that future economic benefits will inflow
: measured reliably
: replacement and regular major inspection 若满足条件可以资本化
: routine maintenance costs 则费用化
: 资本化占总成本的重要部分，则单独折旧
(初始以成本计量)
包括 purchase price/与达到可使用状态直接相关 cost/dismantling cost/borrowing cost
资产交换 (具有 commercial substance)
Dr: 得到的资产 (FV of asset given-up > FV of asset received > BV of asset given-up)
Cr: 放弃的资产(BV of asset given-up)
Cr: P/L
Cost model
每年对残值、使用年限和折旧方法进行 review
达到可使用状态就要开始计提折旧
不能用 revenue based depreciation
考经分享-万字笔记
买卖折旧减值
Revaluation model
当账面价值和公允价值有重大差异时重估
Once an item is revalued, the entire class of assets also required to be revalued
向上重估-OCI 向下重估-P/L 反向重估先冲抵 ，OCI（RS）随折旧政策 transfer RE
买卖折旧重估
De-recognition
OCI (RS) transfer RE, 损益进 P/L
2. IAS 40 Investment Property
: land or a building held to earn rentals or for capital appreciation or for undetermined future use
(初始以成本计量)
包括 transaction cost
Cost model
同 PPE
若使用 cost model 仍要披露 FV
Fair value model
期初一个值，期末一个值，相差进入 P/L (不折旧)
后续计量模型须适用于所有 IP
不能改成 cost model
用途 transfer
Transfer at FV
3.IAS 23 Borrowing costs
资本化原则
Qualifying asset:
takes a substantial period of time to get ready for its intended use
Qualifying periods:
commences when expenditures are being incurred/borrowing cost are being incurred/activities
are in progress/suspend during interruption of development/cease when substantially complete
若 funds are borrowed generally, 用 WA borrowing cost
若 funds are borrowed specifically, 注意建造期间的 temporary investment 需要扣除
考经分享-万字笔记
4.IAS 36 Impairment of assets (PPE/IA/CGU)
每年需要测试减值的情况:
intangible assets with an indefinite useful life/IA not yet available for use/goodwill
其他减值测试则需要 Impairment indicators:
significant decline in market value/changes in tech, market, economic, legal
environment/changes in interest rates/obsolescence or physical damage/discontinuance,
disposal or restructuring/declining asset performance
若发现 Carrying amount > Recoverable amount 则发生减值 (Recoverable amount = higher of FV
- CTS and VIU)
The impairment is treated as a revaluation decrease, the reversal of the impairment is treated as
a revaluation increase
CGU 减值顺序：obviously damaged or destroyed > goodwill > pro-rata basis (1.CA 不参与 CGU
减值 2.减值最多不超过 recoverable amount)
Reversal of impairment loss: 年底 review the indication/reversal 上限: depreciated historical cost
(正常折旧后的值)/Goodwill 减值不能 reverse
5.IAS 38 Intangible assets
ICE
Identifiable when
: capable of being separated and sold or used
: arise from contractual or other legal rights
Control
Estimated reliably
R & D:
Research: expensed
考经分享-万字笔记
Development: capitalized if PIRATE criteria are met (only directly related costs)
Probable future economic benefits
Intention to complete
Resources to complete
Ability to use or sell the IA
Technical feasibility of completion of IA
Expenditures are measured reliably
Finite useful life:
Cost model: 使用年限、摊销方法、残值一般为 0 (每年复核), revenue based amortization is
inappropriate (除非两者高度相关)
Revaluation model: 很少用（没有公开市场），重估增值则进 OCI
Indefinite useful life:
No foreseeable limit to future expected economic benefits
Not amortized, test for impairment annually
Review annually
6.IFRS 5 NCAHFS and DO
NCAHFS: NCA or disposal group (可以包括负债)
确认条件：
Available for immediate sale
Management plans to sale
Actively locating buyer
Reasonable price
Highly probable sell in 1 year
It is unlikely the plan will have significant changes or withdrawal
确认过程 (reclassification)
1. Impairment test (因为卖定了，所以只用 FV - CTS)
2. Reclassify, then cease depreciation
3. 年底再进行 Impairment test (因为卖定了，所以只用 FV - CTS)
4. 可以 reverse impairment, 但上限是已确认的 impairment loss, 不能重估
考经分享-万字笔记
DO (Discontinued operation):
Represents a separate major line of business or geographical area of operations
A single co-ordinated plan to dispose
Subsidiary acquired exclusively for resale
Lose control of subsidiary
在 P/L 中单独体现 DO 的经营利润
理解：属于 NCAHFS 但不属于 DO 的情况：出售寥寥几个资产、负债
理解：属于 DO 但不属于 NCAHFS 的情况：年中已出售 DO，利润表占部分利润，但资产、
负债已处置
7.IAS 2 Inventory
Held for sale in ordinary course of business (FG)
In the process of production for such sale (WIP)
In the form of materials to be consumed in the process of production (Material)
以成本计量
Cost of purchase, non-recoverable taxes, transport and handling
Cost of conversion
Cost of bringing inventory into its present condition and location
Exclude: storage costs (unless necessary for the production process)
For non-interchangeable items: Specific identification
For interchangeable items: FIFO/WACO
Inventories are measured at the lower of cost and net realizable value
(NRV):
FG: Selling price - CTS
WIP: Selling price - Cost of completion - CTS
RM: Replacement cost
当减值迹象消失或 NRV 回升时，存货减值可以转回（转回最大到 cost）
8.IAS 41 Agriculture
Biological asset: a living animal or plant
Agricultural produce: the harvested produce of biological asset
Harvest: the process of detaching produce from a biological asset or cessation of its life
考经分享-万字笔记
Biological asset 初始以 FV - CTS 计量，FV 改变进 P/L（物理改变或价格改变），后续有折旧、
减值
Agricultural produce 初始以 FV - CTS 计量, 后续属于存货
9.IAS 37 Provisions, Contingent Liabilities and Contingent Assets
预计负债确认条件：
Present legal or constructive obligation
Probable that an outflow or economic benefits
Reliable estimate
预计负债确认方法：
一次性事件：best estimate, take into account all related risks and uncertainties
重复性事件：EV
亏损合同 Onerous contracts
预计负债取 lower of: cost of fulfilling the contract/penalties incurred in cancelling the contract
若发生亏损合同，其中标的资产出现减值，则先进行减值测试，再确认预计负债
Before a separate provision for an onerous contract is established, and entity recognises any
impairment loss which has occurred on assets dedicated to that contract
重组 Restructuring 确认预计负债的条件：
A detail formal plan: business/locations/number of employees to be compensated
A valid expectation (implement/announcing)
只包括直接费用（redundancy，onerous contracts）
或有负债
Possible obligation depending on whether some uncertain future event occurs
时间或金额不确定
或有负债披露内容
Nature/estimate of financial effect/uncertainty to amount or timing/expected reimbursement
10.IAS 8 Accounting Policies, Changes in Accounting Estimates and Errors
Accounting policies 的选择顺序
Standard > other standard > conceptual framework > practice
考经分享-万字笔记
Accounting policies 举例
FIFO/WACO
Capitalised/expensed
FVTPL/FVTOCI
Cost model/Revaluation model/Fair value model
Accounting policies 在两种情况下能改变：
1. Standard requires
2. Change will provide more relevant and reliable information
这种改变将使用追溯调整法 (retrospective application)
Accounting estimates 举例：
折旧相关
坏账准备
这种改变将使用未来调整法 (prospective application)
Errors (Prior period errors)发生原因
Could have been reasonably expected to be taken into account
Change was available before Financial Statements were issued
Errors 举例：
Mathematical mistakes
Mistakes in applying accounting policies
Oversights and misinterpretation of facts
Fraud
这种改变将使用追溯调整法 (retrospective application)，注意，对过去年份的利润调整直接
进今年 RE
11.IAS 10 Event after the Reporting Period
: Period from year end to the issue date of Financial Statements
常见调整事项：
Going concern assumption/break up basis
Court case before year end finalized
Bad debt due to bankruptcy of a customer
存货年前已减值（由降低售价 indicate）
预估的数值在期后得到验证，例如暂估入账
Fraud and errors
常见非调整事项：
自然灾害
期后分红 declaration
期后合并、重组（看年底是否存在推定义务）
期后税率调整
重大非调整事项需要披露 nature, financial effect (amount)
12.IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers
五步法
考经分享-万字笔记
第一步：识别合同
书面、口头甚至暗示、双方同意、付款条件和权利义务明确、有商业实质
有权取得的对价很有可能收回（collectability assessment）
第二步：识别履约义务
履约义务：可以明确区分（distinct）的商品和服务：
客户能从中获益
可以分开
第三步：识别对价
重大融资成分 Significant financing component
折现、计息（长期应收款）（首选 incremental borrowing rate）
收入应为客户取得商品控制权（或商品被转移）当天的对价
可变对价 Variable consideration
EV (大量重复事件)
Most likely amount (只有两种结果)
限定条件：不超过当相关不确定性消除时，已确认对价极不可能发生重大转回的金额
Consideration payable to the customer
Accounted for as a reduction in the transaction price
Non-cash consideration
Accounted for at FV
第四步：Allocate the transaction price to each performance obligation (PO)
If the standalone selling price is not observable, estimation may include:
Adjusted market assessment (参考竞争对手的价格)
Expected cost plus a margin
Allocate a discount
Discount relates to specific PO > on a proportionate basis
第五步：Recognise revenue as each performance obligation is satisfied
控 制 权 转 移 的 标 志 ： ability to direct the use/ability to obtain substantially all the remaining
考经分享-万字笔记
benefits
收入确认条件：overtime
Customer simultaneously receives and consumes all of the benefits
Enhances an asset controlled by the customer
Enforceable right to payment for completed performance
收入确认条件：at a point of time
若上述三个条件不满足
收入确认方法：overtime
Output methods: sales to date 除以 total sale（已转移的商品对客户的价值）
Input methods: cost to date 除以 total cost（人力，时间，资源等）
收入确认方法：at a point of time
At the point of time at which the entity transfers control to the customer
合同修改 Contract modification (scope or price)
单独售价（standalone selling price）
可明确区分的
商品或服务
（distinct）
YES NO
YES
1.单独合同（Modification
accounted for as a
separate contract）
2.和原合同剩余部分合成
（Modification accounted
for prospectively）
NO
3.和原合同合成重新计算履约进度（Modification
accounted for through a cumulative catch-up
adjustment）
考经分享-万字笔记
13.Revenue-application guidance
关于特定交易的会计处理
Contract cost 合同成本
Cost to obtain a contract
为取得合同付出的增量成本，且该成本预期能收回
计入合同资产（例如佣金）
Cost to fulfill a contract
Directly relate to the contract (人工，原材料，费用等)
增加了未来履约的资源
且上述成本预期能收回（expected to be recovered）
计入合同资产
合同资产
摊销与收入确认模式一致
减值，若对价大于成本（类似 NRV）
Sale with a right of return 附有销售退回条款的销售
只确认预期不会退回的收入
Refund liability (provision for refund)
只确认预期不会退回的销货成本
Return asset (return cost receivable)
考经分享-万字笔记
Warranties 附有质量保证条款的销售
Assurance type (1PO)
Good or service will function as specified
This warranty is not allowed to buy separately
Legal requirements
Service type (2PO)
Service is provided in addition to assurance
Coverage time is longer than the assurance type
考经分享-万字笔记
Customer options for additional goods or service 附有客户额外购买选择权的销售
Gives rise to a performance obligation, only if the option provides a material right
Material right: a discount that is incremental to those discounts typically for the same class of
customer in that geographical area or market
属于 option 部分的对价只有在 option 商品或服务转移时，以及 option 过期时确认收入
理解：只考虑第一次交易的数字
Contract asset versus Receivables, Contract liability
Contract asset:
Transferring goods or services to a customer before consideration payment
Receivables:
Unconditional right to consideration
考经分享-万字笔记
Contract liability
Obligation to transfer goods or services as the consideration is already received
Customer’s unexercised rights 客户未行使的权利（如礼品卡）
A customer’s non-refundable prepayment that gives a right to receive goods or services in the
future
记为合同负债，当行使权利，确认后续收入
若放弃行使权利（breakage），按可变对价的限定条件处理（当相关不确定性消除，已确认
对价极不可能发生重大转回时确认收入）:
若不能预估 breakage 的金额，则只有权利到期（lapse）时才能确认收入
若 能 够 预 估 breakage 的 金 额 ， 则 recognises the expected breakage amount as revenue in
proportion to the pattern of rights exercised by the customer
举例说明：若 100 元礼品卡存在 30 breakage，则每购买 1 元商品，确认 1.43 元收入
Non-refundable upfront fees 不可退回的初始费
（如会员费等）算销售收入
Licensing (of an entity’s intellectual property)授予知识产权许可
A license is not distinct and should be accounted for together with other goods or services as a
single PO, when：
It is an integral component to the functionality of a tangible good
Only benefit comes from the licence in conjunction with a related service
A distinct license is accounted for as a single PO
收入确认方式：Overtime, 当且仅当：
卖方会从事对知识产权有重大影响的活动
上述活动将对拥有此知识产权的买方产生积极、消极的影响
上述活动不涉及商品或服务的转移
例如：动漫形象
收入确认方式：Point of time, 当：
不满足上述条件
考经分享-万字笔记
例如：歌曲版权
Sales-based or usage-based royalities （版税）
收入确认时间：the later of:
Subsequent sale or usage occurs
PO has been satisfied
Repurchase agreements 售后回购
Sell an asset and also promises or has the option to repurchase
三类：
Obligation to repurchase the asset (forward)
Right to repurchase the asset (call option)
Obligation to repurchase the asset at the customer’s request (put option)
Forward or call option:
客户不能指导用途，不能获得大部分经济利益，因而不具有控制权
若回购价格<出售价格，交易实质是 lease（对方以物为主的融资）
若回购价格>=出售价格，交易实质是 financing（我方以钱为主的融资）
Put option:
Whether the customer has a significant economic incentive to exercise that right
If yes, 交易实质是 lease
If no, 交易实质是 sale with a right of return
Principal versus agent consideration 主要责任人和代理人
代理人 arranges for third party to transfer goods/services 如下
Consignment arrangements 寄售/委托代销安排
同上，在代理过程中并未发生销售
寄售合同 indicators:
在最终销售前，主要责任人实际控制商品
主要责任人有权要求代理人退回或转移给第三方
代理人可能像主要责任人支付押金，但无责任买下商品
Bill-and-hold arrangements 售后代管商品安排
销售未发货
以下条件满足时，客户才具有商品控制权，因而也可以确认收入：
具有商业实质（应客户要求而代管）
属于客户的商品能够单独被识别（identified separately）
随时准备交付（physical transfer）
企业不能使用该商品或转给其他顾客
例如：越是通用的商品越不满足，而越是定制的商品越满足

13.IAS 20 Accounting for Government Grants and Disclosure of Government Assistance

14.IFRS 13 Fair Value Measurement
定 义 ： price would be received to sell an asset or paid to transfer a liability in an orderly
transaction between market participants at the measurement date
Active market: frequent and large volume of transactions occur on an ongoing basis
Market participants: when measuring the FV, an entity shall use the assumptions that most
market participants act in their economic best interest
FV hierarchy
Level 1 inputs
Unadjusted quoted prices in active markets for identical assets or liabilities
Level 2 inputs
Other than quoted prices that are observable/not active markets/similar assets or liabilities
Level 3 inputs
Using unobservable, or internal data (DCF)
Valuation techniques
Market approach
Use price or other relevant information generated by market transactions involving identical or
similar assets, liabilities
Cost approach
Current replacement cost
Income approach
DCF
Principal market&Most advantageous market
Principal market
Market with the greatest volume and level of activity
Principal market should be given priority
考经分享-万字笔记
Most advantageous market
Market that maximises the received amount from sell assets or minimises the paid amount from
transfer liabilities

判断 FV 时，却只扣运输费用，不扣交易费用（理解：运输费用代表减少资产的位置价值，
而交易费用不属于资产的价值）
Application to non-financial assets

18.IAS 12 Income Taxes
Current tax
Current tax liability 是为预估，因着今年的税还没有交，下一年多退少补
因此，若有余额，那是高估或低估了
Deferred tax
Temporary differences: differences between the carrying amount of asset or liability in the
statement of financial position and its tax base
Taxable temporary differences 未来要多交（deferred tax liability）
Deductible temporary differences 未来可抵扣（deferred tax asset）
递延所得税资产、负债金额计算永远等于税率*temporary differences，并在最后一年（CV 与
TB 双消）冲回

stem_zh:
道德题的套路，你一定得掌握！
道德题是 SBR 考试中必考的一个知识点，一般出现在 Section A-Q2 中，通常考核 8-10 分左
右的道德困境分析，同时包含 2 分的 professional marks。SBR 是目前 ACCA SP 阶段考试中，
唯一的一门专业分尚且维持在 4 分的科目，2 分给到道德题（这也是唯一我们努力一下能够拿到
的专业分），另外 2 分一般出现在 Section B 中，对于大家的分析要求会更高，拿分的可能性
却没那么高了。因此掌握了道德题的答题套路，多拿 10 分不是梦！
其实道德题无外乎是给大家一段案例，案例中会存在不同的道德困境、矛盾点、处理方式不合适
的地方，涉及 1 个或多个主人公，而我们需要做的事情，就是去讨论到底存在哪些的道德困境？
面临哪些的道德威胁？是由于什么原因造成的？解决方案有哪些？
话不多说，上干货！分析步骤如下：
Step 1：分析案例。识别案例中的相关事实，都有哪几个主人公，存在哪些矛盾冲突的情况，
可以通过考试页面 Highlight 高亮功能，帮助分析。分析的时候，随着案例事件发生的顺序来即
可。当出现一些负面/否定/极端的词语，如：not/only/without authority 等，都是在提醒我们
有可能存在问题。
Step 2：识别道德困境。根据 step 1 分析出的矛盾冲突情况，按照各个击破的方法，逐一分析
是否存在不道德/不合理的地方，有没有违背五大基本原则：
JENNY 简妮
Step 3：是否存在道德威胁。分析完道德困境之后，紧接着分析一下是否存在道德威胁：
Step 4：考虑可以缓解道德问题的 safeguards 措施。措施的部分需要紧密结合案例信息，给
出一些正确的做法建议即可，当然也是存在万金油式的答案的，包括但不限于：
 consider contacting ACCA for advice and guidance
 approach colleagues with whom he can discuss his position
 involving the board of directors in its resolution
 should document the substance of the discussions held
 Seek legal advice
接下来，我们通过一个栗子来看一下，如何套用模板吧~
题目截选自：2023 MJ - Q2b
Requirement
(b) Discuss the ethical issues faced by the accountant (Mr Ebrima) and the internal auditor
(Mr Franklin) in Herding Co and any actions they should take to address these issues. (6
marks)
Professional marks will be awarded in part (a) and (b) for the quality of the ethical discussion
and identification of appropriate actions. (2 marks)
题目要求我们分别分析会计师、内部审计师面临的道德问题，并给出相应的解决措施，共 6
分。根据下述给分点，可以看出考官整体的思路也是按照我们给到大家的步骤来的，同时识
别出一个 issue 给 1 分，能够关联到相关的道德原则分析给 1 分，给出正确的 action 解决
措施给 1 分。
JENNY 简妮
案例信息
New procurement system
In 20X8, Herding Co upgraded its computerised systems and centralised its procurement
(purchasing) processes. All divisions of Herding Co were required to use the same
procurement system. However, on 1 August 20X8, the divisional head of the Cutherd division
(Mr Bookman) 【第一个人物出现】purchased a procurement system without authority,
which is incompatible with the other systems in Herding Co. The new system is specific to
the Cutherd division and cannot be used by other divisions in Herding Co. Mr Bookman has
stated that the Cutherd division will use this new system to make purchases in the future
rather than the company's centralised system. As a result of using the new system, the Cutherd
division has developed a poor purchasing strategy which has resulted in unfavourable pricing
decisions. Even though the Cutherd division has made purchases with the new system, it is
not fully operational because it is incompatible with the company's centralised systems.【红笔
标注的都多少包含着负面信息】
Accountant and internal auditor
The accountant of Herding Co (Mr Ebrima) 【第二个人物-也是题目的主人公出现】is
considering the impact of the poor purchasing decisions and how to improve the financial
control over the Cutherd division. However, he does not wish to introduce controls which Mr
Bookman may feel are 'excessive' because Mr Bookman is a dominant individual who often
puts pressure on him. 【重 点 关 注 他 的 举 措 以 及 所 遭 受 的 事 项】Mr Bookman is on the
management committee of Herding Co to whom the internal auditor (Mr Franklin) reports
【第三个人物-也是题目的另一位主人公出现】. Mr Ebrima has reported his concerns to the
internal auditor who is a qualified member of ACCA and known to be a personal friend of Mr
Bookman.【看到亲密关系就要能联想到道德威胁之一：Familiarity threat】
上模板
以 Mr Ebrima 为例
Step 1: The divisional head (Mr Bookman) has bypassed the centralised procurement system
and a division specific system can result in inefficient procurement which does not meet the
needs and opportunities for the organisation as a whole.(识别案例，英文总结能力较差的同学
可以适当抄题干背景）
Step2: Mr Ebrima and the internal auditor must assess why and how a purchasing strategy
JENNY 简妮
has been allowed to occur which has resulted in a system which is incompatible with the
company’s centralised systems. The impact of the unfavourable pricing of the system on the
company’s financial position must also be assessed. (在识别某些行为不道德时，顺便将将正确
的做法阐述出来会更好。如果时跟会计准则相关，那就要表述一下正确的会计处理）
Step 3: As the divisional head, Mr Bookman is a very dominant figure and a personal friend
of the internal auditor. There is an intimidation threat as Mr Ebrima could be deterred from
acting objectively by pressure exerted by Mr Bookman.(表达相关的道德威胁，以及有可能影
响哪些基本原则)
Step 4: Mr Ebrima may also consult with a professional body or discuss with an independent
non-executive director in the company to take some safeguards to ensure the threat is at an
acceptable level.(如果不知道写什么措施，可以参考这句万金油答案)
JENNY 简妮

术语部分和回答技巧总结
考经分享-万字笔记
常识部分
1. Management Stewardship
: 管理层使用资源的能力/规避风险的能力/合规合法
: 报表体现客观公正的信息，能用于判断管理层是否高效履行了他们的职责
2. Information qualitative characteristics (benefit > cost)
Fundamental:
Relevance (material for decision making)
Faithful representation (true and fair/prudence/substance over form)
Enhancing:
Comparability/Consistent (other entities/another period)
Verifiability
Timeliness
Understandability
3. Asset/Liability/Equity
Asset
: present economic resource controlled by the entity
: right that has potential to produce economic benefits
Liability
: present obligation to transfer economic resource
: no way to avoid
Equity
Residual interest
准则部分
1.IAS 16 PPE
资本化原则
: probable that future economic benefits will inflow
: measured reliably
: replacement and regular major inspection 若满足条件可以资本化
: routine maintenance costs 则费用化
: 资本化占总成本的重要部分，则单独折旧
(初始以成本计量)
包括 purchase price/与达到可使用状态直接相关 cost/dismantling cost/borrowing cost
资产交换 (具有 commercial substance)
Dr: 得到的资产 (FV of asset given-up > FV of asset received > BV of asset given-up)
Cr: 放弃的资产(BV of asset given-up)
Cr: P/L
Cost model
每年对残值、使用年限和折旧方法进行 review
达到可使用状态就要开始计提折旧
不能用 revenue based depreciation
考经分享-万字笔记
买卖折旧减值
Revaluation model
当账面价值和公允价值有重大差异时重估
Once an item is revalued, the entire class of assets also required to be revalued
向上重估-OCI 向下重估-P/L 反向重估先冲抵 ，OCI（RS）随折旧政策 transfer RE
买卖折旧重估
De-recognition
OCI (RS) transfer RE, 损益进 P/L
2. IAS 40 Investment Property
: land or a building held to earn rentals or for capital appreciation or for undetermined future use
(初始以成本计量)
包括 transaction cost
Cost model
同 PPE
若使用 cost model 仍要披露 FV
Fair value model
期初一个值，期末一个值，相差进入 P/L (不折旧)
后续计量模型须适用于所有 IP
不能改成 cost model
用途 transfer
Transfer at FV
3.IAS 23 Borrowing costs
资本化原则
Qualifying asset:
takes a substantial period of time to get ready for its intended use
Qualifying periods:
commences when expenditures are being incurred/borrowing cost are being incurred/activities
are in progress/suspend during interruption of development/cease when substantially complete
若 funds are borrowed generally, 用 WA borrowing cost
若 funds are borrowed specifically, 注意建造期间的 temporary investment 需要扣除
考经分享-万字笔记
4.IAS 36 Impairment of assets (PPE/IA/CGU)
每年需要测试减值的情况:
intangible assets with an indefinite useful life/IA not yet available for use/goodwill
其他减值测试则需要 Impairment indicators:
